description: Your saved grocery lists — check off ingredients as you shop.
title: My Grocery List

description: Your personal collection of favorited Bell family recipes and AI-generated creations.
title: My Recipe Book

description: Tell us what ingredients you have and we'll find matching Bell family recipes — or let AI create a brand new one just for you!
title: What's In My Fridge?

content: Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 1/2 cup granulated sugar 1 tbsp baking powder 1/2 tsp salt 1 cup fresh or frozen blueberries 1 cup milk 1/4 cup vegetable oil or melted butter 1 large egg 1 tsp vanilla extract Instructions Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners. In a bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk milk, oil, egg, and vanilla until combined. Stir wet ingredients into dry just until combined, then gently fold in blueberries. Divide batter evenly between muffin cups and bake 18–22 minutes, or until golden and a toothpick comes out clean. Cool slightly before serving. Notes This is a placeholder version recreated from memory; feel free to update ingredients and instructions to match your preferred blueberry muffin recipe.
description: Tender homemade blueberry muffins with a soft crumb, bursts of juicy berries, and a golden bakery-style top.
title: Blueberry Muffins

content: Ingredients 1/4 cup Garlic cloves, peeled (approx. 10–12 cloves) 1/2 tsp Kosher salt (plus more to taste) 1 Large Egg white (at room temperature) 1 cup Vegetable oil (or other neutral oil like Canola or Grapeseed) 1.5 tbsp Fresh lemon juice Instructions Prep the Garlic: Slice the garlic cloves in half and remove any green sprouts (the germ) to prevent bitterness. Create the Paste: Place the garlic and salt in a food processor. Pulse until finely minced. Scrape down the sides and process on High until it forms a smooth, thick paste. Note: If the blades aren&rsquo;t catching the garlic, add 1 tsp of oil to help it move. Incorporate the Egg: Add the egg white to the processor. Run on High for about 30 seconds until the mixture is bright white and frothy. The Slow Emulsion (The Secret): With the processor running on High, begin adding the vegetable oil in an extremely slow, steady stream (think droplets or a thin thread). Take your time; this should take about 2–3 minutes for the full cup. Balance with Lemon: Once you have added about half of the oil and the sauce is thickening, drizzle in half of the lemon juice. Continue with the remaining oil, and finish with the rest of the lemon juice. Final Whip: Once all ingredients are in, process for another 15–20 seconds until the sauce is stiff, airy, and holds its shape on a spoon.
description: The legendary thick, white "garlic fluff" found in Middle Eastern shawarma shops. This version uses a single egg white to ensure a stable, foolproof emulsion.
title: Garlic Sauce (George's)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups flour ½ tsp salt 2 tsp baking powder ¼ cup margarine Instructions Mix all the above ingredients together to form a cornmeal-like mixture.
Put this mixture in plastic bag for transporting over portages.
When needed, mix with water in a bowl, then dump it in frying pan and cook till brown. (Not too fast.)
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with ½ tsp salt and ¼ cup margarine that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: “bush Bread” (bannock) for 1 Scone

content: Ingredients 1 pkg (4-serving size) Jello Chocolate 1 cup cold milk instant pudding 1 1-L Cool Whip, thawed 20 chocolate sandwich cookies 1-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows chopped 1 graham crumb crust Gummy worms for decoration Instructions Prepare pudding according to package directions reducing milk to 1 cup. Fold in whipped topping. Stir in 2/3 of the cookies and all the marshmallows. Spoon into crust. Sprinkle top with remaining cookies. Decorate with gummy worms. Freeze until firm – about 4 hours. Remove from freezer 10 minutes before serving for easy slicing. Makes 6 to 8 servings. For best results, use only Jello instant pudding and Cool Whip topping.
description: A beautiful decadent pie with jello chocolate and cold milk with a buttery crust and a filling so good you'll want to eat it by the spoonful.
title: “dirt” Pie

content: Ingredients Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil. Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds. Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes. Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot. Graham wafers l/2 pkg flaked almonds ¾ cup brown sugar l cup butter
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with line cookie sheet with foil. grease foil. and put graham wafers on foil. top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Almond Bars

content: Ingredients Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil. Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds. Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes. Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot. Graham wafers l/2 pkg flaked almonds ¾ cup brown sugar l cup butter
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with line cookie sheet with foil. grease foil. and put graham wafers on foil. top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Almond Bars

content:
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 1 heaping Tbsp baking powder ½ tsp table salt water to mix and oil or lard to fry in Instructions METHOD: In a large bowl combine all dry ingredients. Then slowly add cold water as needed. The texture is a thick texture, thicker than pancake batter. Mix well.
Flour the area where you will be rolling out the dough. Drop the whole dough chunk onto flour, and freely sprinkle more flour on this dough. Begin to knead as though making bread, making sure dough is not sticking to your hands. If that happens, just ad a little more flour.
Now roll out dough till about ½” thick, and cut into 2 . 2” squares. Tear holes in each piece – this allows for even heat when frying. Prepare your frying pan with oil – oil should be at least ½” deep. Temperature should be med/hot – deep frying temperature. Drop each piece one by one till evenly distributed in oil. Use thongs to turn bread over when cooking and fry till each side is golden brown. TIP: When taking bread out of hot pan, stand wach bread up to allow for any drippings. Do this in a pan with paper towel at bottom of pan. ENJOY!
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with heaping tbsp baking powder and ½ tsp table salt that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Ann’s Indian Fry Bread

content:
Instructions Preheat over to 375 degrees.
For each pie, I combine slightly more than ½ cup of sugar (more if apples are tart) with ½ tsp cinnamon and a dash of salt.
Prepare pastry and line a 9” pie plate with the rolled pastry..Roll out the top pastry.
Peel and core apples. (I usually used 6 medium-sized apples). I half fill the pastry-lined pie plate with thinly sliced apples. Sprinkle on half of the sugar mixture. Finish filling the pie plate with thinly sliced apples, heaping them in the center. Sprinkle the rest of the sugar mixture on top. Dot with 1 Tablespoon of butter.
Moisten edge of pastry. Place top crust in position. Seal edge and flute. Bake in preheated oven for 40 to 50 minutes, or until pastry is golden brown and the apples are tender.
NOTE: I usually freeze these pies before baking. To bake, I put the unbaked pie in a cold oven, then turn on the oven to 375 degrees and bake for 1 hour or until pie crust is golden brown.
description: Classic melt-in-your-mouth pie — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Apple Pie – (purity Cookbook)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 1 cup whole wheat flour 2 Tbsp sugar (can use Splenda) 1 Tbsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda 1/3 cup margarine 1/3 cup grated Asiago cheese 2 Tbsp chopped herbs (rosemary, thyme, sage, oregano – these can be fresh or dried herbs) Instructions Combine dry ingredients. Cut in margarine. Stir in cheese and herbs. Pour buttermilk over flour mixture and stir only enough to form rough batter (don’t over mix)
Drop batter by tablespoonful on greased baking sheet.
Bake at 400 deg. F. for 12 to 15 minutes (don’t over cook – these do not necessarily have to brown.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with whole wheat flour and grated asiago cheese that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Asiago Drop Biscuits – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)

content: Ingredients 1 Tbsp butter 3 small onions, chopped 3 large carrots, sliced 3 celery stalks, diced 1 lb ground beef 1 – 16 oz can diced tomatoes 3 medium potatoes, diced 1/3 cup macaroni 6 cups water Salt and pepper to taste Instructions Melt butter, add ground beef and cook slightly. Add onions, tomatoes, salt, pepper and water. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer for 1 hour. Add vegetables and simmer 1 hour longer. Stir in macaroni during the last 15 minutes.
description: Rich, comforting robust soup with small onions and large carrots that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Aunt Gert’s Hamburg Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)

content:
Ingredients 5 cups milk 2 level Tablespoon butter 6 eggs 1 tsp nutmeg 1 tsp ginger 1 cup Indian meal (Cornmeal) 1 cup powdered sugar (Fruit) Instructions Scald 4 cups milk. Mix Indian Meal with 1 cup cold milk and stir into hot milk. Add butter. Continue to stir until mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat.
Beat eggs until fluffy with the sugar and add nutmeg and ginger, then stir the whole mixture together. Pour into a buttered casserole or deep 12 x 8” baking dish.
Bake at 400 F. until nicely browned, approximately 30 minutes. Serve hot with Wine Sauce.
WINE SAUCE: Beat 1 cup fruit sugar and ½ cup soft butter to a cream. Flavour with ¼ to ½ tsp nutmeg and 2 to 4 Tbsp wine or brandy. Chill and Serve.
description: Creamy, dreamy heavenly pudding with level tablespoon butter and nutmeg that's simple to make but tastes absolutely luxurious. Spoon after heavenly spoon.
title: Baked Indian Pudding – Aboriginal Cooking

content:
Ingredients 3 cups flour (730 ml) 3 level Tbsp baking powder 1 Tbsp sugar 1 cup cold water 1 Tbsp (heaping) lard ¾ tsp salt Instructions Mix together the flour, baking powder, salt and sugar in large bowl. Add the lard, cut into small pieces. Mix and make a well in the middle. Add water and mix again. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour. Pat and form into a cake. Deep fry in enough lard or oil so the mixture floats OR bake over coals on a stick like hot dogs. Cook about 20 minutes. Tastes great alone or buttered, with molasses, honey or corn syrup.
NOTE: I haven’t tried this recipe yet. It was given to me by a good friend who neglected to tell me how much salt to put in. I took a guess. You might have to adjust it to your taste.
description: Freshly baked toasty bread with level tbsp baking powder and cold water that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Bannock

content:
Ingredients ½ cup prepared barbecue sauce ¼ cup vegetable oil ¼ cup Realemon lemon juice 1 Tbsp packed brown sugar Instructions In small bow, combine barbecue sauce, oil, lemon juice and sugar. Pour over meat or poultry. Cover and refrigerate six hours or overnight, turning occasionally. Remove meat from marine; grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade. Makes about 1 cup.
BEER MARINADE – For beef or pork
1 large onion, finely chopped	2 cloves garlic, crushed, chopped ½ cup vegetable oil, divided	½ cup Realemon lemon juice 1 cup beer	2 Tbsp packed brown sugar 2 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
In medium frypan, cook onion and garlic in ¼ cup oil until tender. Remove from heat. Add beer, lemon juice, remaining oil, sugar and Worcestershire sauce. Pour over meat. Cover and refrigerate six hours or overnight, stirring occasionally. Remove meat from marinade.Grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with ½ cup prepared barbecue sauce and ¼ cup vegetable oil. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Barbecue Marinade – for Meat or Poultry

content:
Ingredients ½ cup Canola oil ½ cup chili sauce 1 cup vinegar 1 Tbsp chopped onion 1 clove garlic 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce ½ tsp chili powder (optional) ¼ cup brown sugar Instructions Measure all ingredients into bowl. Mix until thoroughly blended.
YIELD: 2 cups
NOTE: Use Sauce for Barbecued Frankfurters, Chicken, Fish, Pork Chops or Ribs.
description: A pan of melt-in-your-mouth squares with ½ cup canola oil and ½ cup chili sauce that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Barbecue Sauce

content: Ingredients Boil, peel and dice beets to make 16 cups. ½ cup flour 1 Tbsp dry mustard 2-1/2 cups white vinegar 2 cups white sugar 1 cup water 1 Tbsp salt Instructions Mix until well blended and bring to a boil. Boil until sauce is thickened. Add diced beets. Pour hot mixture into hot sealer jars. Seal and let cool. Vacuum seal is accomplished when jar lids “pop” inward.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with boil, ½ cup flour, and dry mustard. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Beet Salad

content:
Ingredients 2-1/2 to 3 cups flour Pinch of salt 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder 2 Tbsp margarine – melted 1-1/2 cups milk (enough milk to moisten – like dumplings) Instructions Bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes until golden brown.
Use a cast iron frying pan or an 8 x 8 cake pan.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with to 3 cups flour and pinch of salt that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Betty Lance’s Award-winning Banic

content:
Ingredients ¾ cup salad oil ¼ cup lemon juice 1 egg 2 garlic cloves Salt and pepper Instructions Place these ingredients in a blender and blend thoroughly. Chill.
Iceberg lettuce or Romaine lettuce	Croutons Bacon, cooked crispy and crumbled
Tear lettuce in bite-sized pieces into salad bowl. Add dressing, bacon bits and Caesar salad mix to taste. Top with croutons.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with ¾ cup salad oil, ¼ cup lemon juice, and garlic cloves. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Betty’s Caesar Salad Dressing

content: Ingredients 1 box chopped spinach or broccoli l/2 chopped onion l/2 cup bell pepper l cup cheese ¾ cup Bisquick 3 eggs l/2 tsp salt l/4 tsp pepper Instructions Spray Pam in pan. Put first 4 ingredients (spinach, onion, bell pepper, cheese) in bottom of pie plate. Mix rest with mixer to pour over other ingredients. Bake at 400 degrees for 35 minutes.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with box chopped spinach or broccoli and l/2 chopped onion — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Beverly’s Spinach Quiche

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup sugar 3 Tbsp cornstarch ¼ tsp salt 2-1/4 cup milk Instructions Mix sugar, starch and salt in top of double boiler. Add milk, mix and cook until thickened.
Yum, Yum!
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with cornstarch and ¼ tsp salt that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Blanc Mange

content: Ingredients 3 cups blueberries 1 cup water ½ cup sugar 3 Tbsp lemon juice Pinch of cinnamon Pinch of cloves ½ tsp coriander 1 cup yogurt or cream Instructions In a blender, blend blueberries until pureed. Place in cooking pot and add remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer 5 minutes (covered). Remove from heat and cool. Once cooled, add one cup of yogurt or one cup of cream.
description: A soul-warming bowl of savory soup with blueberries and ½ cup sugar — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Blueberry Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)

content: Ingredients 2 cups flour 1 cup bran 1 tsp salt 1 tsp soda ½ tsp cream of tartar 1 Tbsp melted shortening 1 cup sour milk Instructions Mix dry ingredients, add the melted shortening, then sour milk. Pour into a greased baking pan and bake in a moderate oven for about 40 minutes.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with bran and soda that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Bran Bannock

content:
Ingredients 24 cukes, sliced, let stand in salt 2 hrs. Rinse in cold water, drain. 12 onions, sliced 4 cups vinegar 2-1/2 cups white sugar 1 tsp ginger 1 tsp turmeric 1 tsp mustard seed 1 tsp celery seed Instructions Put cukes and onions in vinegar solution and just bring to a boil. Take off heat immediately and pack cukes &amp; onions in jars.
Bring solution to a boil again and pour into jars. Seal
description: Homemade fragrant bread with cukes and rinse in cold water that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Bread & Butter Pickles (colette Tremblay)

content: Ingredients 4 cups bread crumbs ½ cup white sugar ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp cinnamon or nutmeg ½ cup seeded raisins 2 eggs 2 cups milk ¼ cup of butter Instructions Scald milk and add butter. Beat eggs slightly and add to milk with sugar, salt, spice and raisins. Pour over bread crumbs and mix well. Pour into a greased deep casserole and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 minutes. Serve with cream or lemon sauce.
description: Freshly baked toasty bread with bread crumbs and ½ cup white sugar that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Bread Pudding

content:
Ingredients ½ cup raisins ½ cup shelled sunflower seeds 1 medium red onion, chopped ½ lb crisp, crumbled bacon 3 heads broccoli florets Instructions Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.
Dressing: ½ cup Miracle Whip 2 tsp rice vinegar 1/3 cup white sugar (or Splenda)
Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with ½ cup raisins and ½ cup shelled sunflower seeds that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Broccoli Salad – (bill & Heather’s Cookbook)

content:
Ingredients 4 cups granulated sugar 2 cups white vinegar 2 Tbsp coarse pickling salt 1 tsp turmeric 1 tsp celery seed 1 tsp mustard seed 1 lg red pepper, seeded and cut in slices 1 lg green pepper, seeded and cut in slices 1 lg Spanish onion, cut in slices 5 pickling cucumbers with peel, thinly sliced Instructions Measure the first 6 ingredients in a 4 quart ice cream bucket. Stir.
Add red pepper, green pepper and onion. Add more or less cucumber, depending on size, to fill bucket up to level of liquid. Let stand about 2 hours. Stir. It will shrink down a bit so you may be able to add a few more cucumber slices. Cover. Store in refrigerator for up to 6 months. Makes about 2-1/2 quarts.
description: Homemade rustic preserves with granulated sugar and white vinegar that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Bucket Pickles

content: Instructions BASE: 1/3 cup butter	1 cup lightly packed brown sugar 1 egg	1 tsp vanilla ¾ cup all-purpose flour	1 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt	½ cup chopped pecans (I used walnuts) FILLING: 2-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows TOPPING: ¼ cup butter	½ cup lightly packed brown sugar 2 Tbsp milk	1 cup sifted icing sugar 32 pecan halves (optional) BASE: Melt butter in large saucepan. Stir in brown sugar, egg and vanilla. Blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Stir dry ingredients into butter mixture along with pecans or walnuts. Spread batter in greased 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 – 30 minutes. Immediately scatter KRAFT marshmallows over entire surface of hot cake. Press gently to flatten. TOPPING: Melt butter in saucepan. Add brown sugar and simmer over low heat, stirring constantly for 2 minutes. Add milk, stirring until mixture just return to boil. Remove from heat. Gradually add icing sugar, beating with a wooden spoon after each addition until smooth. If necessary, thin icing with an additional few drops of milk. Spread quickly over top of marshmallows. Decorate with pecans, if desired. Cool before cutting into squares. Makes 20 squares. NOTE: I found squares are easier to cut and remove from pan if they are still a bit warm when I cut them.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food . The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Butter Mallow Squaraes

content:
Ingredients Base: 1-1/4 cup flour ¼ cup brown sugar ½ cup butter Filling: 1/3 cup butter 1 egg 2 tbsp cream 1/2 tbsp flour 1 tsp vanilla 1/2 cup raisins (or walnuts) 1 cup brown sugar Instructions Grease pan lightly, especially around the edges to make it easier to remove edges pieces. Mix base ingredients together and press firmly in a 9-inch square pan. Mix filling together. Pour over base and bake at 350 degrees until done. (25 to 30 minutes)
NOTE: I found it easier to cream butter or margarine with brown sugar first in the Filling. It made for a smoother filling.
description: Homemade luscious pie with base: and ¼ cup brown sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Butter Tart Squares

content: Ingredients 1/3 cup soft butter 1 cup brown sugar 2 tbsp milk 1 egg 1 tsp vanilla raisins or walnuts (optional) Instructions Cream butter and brown sugar. Add milk, vanilla and egg, and beat well, preferably with an electric beater. Half fill pastry-lined tart tins and bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with soft butter and brown sugar — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Butter Tarts

content:
Ingredients 3 tbsp butter 1-1/2 cups of milk 1-1/2 cups brown sugar 6 tbsp flour ¾ cup boiling water pinch of salt 4 egg yolks Instructions Melt butter, add sugar and allow to brown. Gradually add boiling water. In medium size bowl, beat egg yolks; add a small amount of the measured milk and the flour. Beat until smooth. Then add remainder of milk and salt. Pour this slowly into sugar mixture and cook until thick, stirring constantly.
MERINGUE; 4 egg whites	1 tsp lemon juice ¼ tsp salt	6 tbsp white sugar ¼ tsp vanilla
Beat egg whites until stiff. Add remaining ingredients slowly as you beat. Spread on pies and put in oven to brown.
description: Classic heavenly pie with of milk and brown sugar — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Butterscotch Pie

content:
Ingredients 1 9”baked pie shell 2-1/2 cups milk 1 cup dark brown sugar 3 egg yolks, slightly beaten 3-1/2 tbsp cornstarch 1 tsp vanilla ½ tsp salt 3 tbsp butter Instructions Combine sugar, cornstarch and salt in top of a double boiler. Stir in cold milk. Cook over boiling water until thickened – stirring constantly. Cover and cook 15 minutes longer.
Stir a little of the hot mixture into slightly beaten egg yolks. Add to remaining mixture in double boiler and cook for 2 minutes over hot, not boiling, water, stirring constantly. Add the butter and vanilla and let cool. Pour into the baked pie shell.
Top with meringue made from the 3 egg whites or Dream Whip or whipped cream.
description: Homemade luscious pie with ”baked pie shell and dark brown sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Butterscotch Pie (carmen Zimmerman)

content: Ingredients 2 egg yolks ½ clove garlic ½ tsp salt ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce 2/3 cup olive oil Instructions Combine all ingredients in a blender. Mix well. Serve with romaine lettuce, croutons and bacon. Makes 1-1/2 cup dressing.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with egg yolks and ½ clove garlic — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Caesar Salad Dressing – No. 2

content: Ingredients ½ tsp salt ½ tsp ground pepper ½ tsp dry mustard 1 cup (1/4 lb) grated Romano ½ tsp crushed oregano or Parmesan cheese or a blend ½ tsp M.S.G. (optional) or Parmesan and Romano Instructions Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Store in a covered jar at room temperature. Use as the seasoning in Caesar Salad. It may also be sprinkled over other tossed salads and used as a topping for casseroles and creamed soups.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp ground pepper, and ½ tsp dry mustard. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Caesar Salad Seasoning Mix

content:
Ingredients Preparation: 10 minutes Cook: 22 minutes 2 Tbsp margarine or butter 1 Tbsp all-purpose Flour 2 cups milk 1 pouch Loney’s Onion Soup mix ½ cup parmesan cheese, grated 1 tsp Dijon mustard ½ tsp black pepper Pinch garlic powder 4 cups peeled potatoes, sliced Instructions In large, deep microwaveable casserole, melt margarine or butter at high. Blend in flour, microwave at High 30 seconds. Whisk in milk and Soup Mix; microwave at High 4 to 6 Minutes, stirring twice, until mixture thickens slightly. Stir in cheese, mustard, pepper, garlic powder and potatoes. Microwave at High, covered, 12 to 16 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Stir twice during cooking.
For a crisp golden topping, sprinkle these microwave-cooked potatoes with grated Parmesan cheese and place until broiler a few minutes. Garnish with fresh parsley.
Makes 4 servings.
description: Crispy, golden hearty potatoes with preparation: 10 minutes and cook: 22 minutes — crunchy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and gone way faster than you'd expect.
title: Caesar Scalloped Potatoes

content:
Ingredients 1 tbsp sweet paprika 1 tsp salt 1 tsp onion powder 1 tsp garlic powder 1 tsp cayenne pepper ½ tsp white pepper 1 tsp black pepper ½ tsp dried thyme ½ tsp dried oregano Instructions Spread melted butter on cold fish that has been wiped dry. Sprinkle spices on fish. Cook fish in ungreased very hot iron frying pan. NOTE: Fish should be sliced fairly thin so that it will cook right through without burning on the outside. Fish done this way cooks very rapidly.
FOR LARGER QUANTITY: ½ cup sweet paprika	¼ cup salt ¼ cup onion powder	¼ cup garlic powder ¼ cup cayenne powder	1/8 cup white pepper ¼ cup black pepper	1/8 cup dried thyme 1/8 cup dried oregano Makes 2-1/8 cups. NOTE: I omitted the salt when I made it.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with sweet paprika and onion powder — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Cajun Spice

content:
Instructions EASY HERBED RICE – Just substitute Campbell’s Chicken Broth for the water when cooking rice. During the simmering time, add your favourite fresh or dried herbs, such as oregano, thyme or parsley.
EASY MUSHROOM RICE – Cook your rice with Campbell’s Chicken or Beef Broth instead of water. Stir fry fresh sliced mushrooms in a saucepan. Add to simmering rice at the end of cook time.
EASY SAVOURY RICE PILAF – Simply cook rice in Campbell’s Chicken or Beef Broth, instead of water. During the last 10 minutes of the simmering time, add fresh or frozen diced vegetables.
description: Steaming bowls of comforting soup packed with simple, honest ingredients. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Campbell’s Broth for Savoury Rice

content:
Ingredients 2 tbsp butter or margarine 1-1/2 cups packed brown sugar 1-1/2 cups water Instructions DUMPLINGS; 1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour	½ cup sugar 2 tsp baking powder	½ tsp salt ½ cup milk	2 tbsp butter or margarine, softened 2 tsp vanilla extract	½ cup coarsely chopped peeled apple (optional)
In a skillet, heat the unsoftened butter, brown sugar and water to boiling. Reduce heat to simmer. Meanwhile, mix together all dumpling ingredients. Drop by tablespoonfuls into the simmering sauce. Cover tightly and simmer 20 minutes. Do not lift lid. Serve warm with cream or ice cream, if desired.
Yield: 6 – 8 servings.
description: Sweet, homemade rich candy with butter or margarine and packed brown sugar — the kind of treat that makes you feel like a confectioner. Wrap them up for gifts or keep the whole batch.
title: Caramel Dumplings

content: Ingredients 2/3 cup (Small can) undiluted Carnation evaporated milk 1-2/3 cup sugar 1-1/2 cup (6 medium) diced marshmallows ½ tsp salt 1-1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips 1 tsp vanilla ½ cup chopped nuts Instructions Mix Carnation, sugar and salt together in sauce pan over low heat. Heat to boiling; cook 5 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Add marshmallows, chocolate chips, vanilla and nuts. Stir 1 to 2 minutes or until marshmallows melt. Pour into buttered pan (9” square). Cool. Then cut into squares. Indeed it is wonderful to know the secret of making fudge that is smooth and creamy – perfect every time.
description: Velvety heavenly fudge with undiluted carnation evaporated milk and diced marshmallows — rich, dense, and indulgent. Cut into tiny squares because a little goes a long, long way.
title: Carnation “can’t Fail” 5-minute Fudge

content: Ingredients 1-2/3 cup granulated sugar 2/3 cup evaporated milk 2 tbsp butter ½ tsp salt 2 cups miniature marshmallows 1-1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips ½ cup chopped walnuts 1 tsp vanilla Instructions In medium saucepan, combine sugar, evaporated milk, butter and salt. Stir over medium heat until mixture comes to a full boil. Boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat Stir in marshmallows, chocolate chips, nuts and vanilla. Stir vigorously until marshmallows melt and blend. Pour into foil-lined 8” square pan. Chill until set. Cut into 1-inch squares to serve. Store in refrigerator in sealed container. Makes 64 squares. Variations: Omit chopped walnuts and substitute one of the following: 1 cup chopped toasted hazelnuts 1 cup chopped toasted almonds, plus 1 tsp almond extract 1 cup chopped well-drained maraschino cherries plus ½ cup chopped nuts ¾ cup finely chopped candied ginger
description: Melt-in-your-mouth sweet fudge loaded with granulated sugar, evaporated milk, and ½ tsp salt. A holiday tradition that disappears faster than you can box it up.
title: Carnation Five-minute Fudge

content:
Ingredients ½ cup melted butter 1 6-oz pkg chocolate chips 1 6-oz pkg butterscotch chips Instructions Melt above ingredients in a saucepan.
ADD: 1 cup peanut butter and cool
Add to cooled mixture the following: 1 cup coconut	1 pkg colored mini marshmallows ½ cup finely chopped nuts
Mix together and pack in a buttered pan and keep in fridge.
description: Homemade comforting goodness with ½ cup melted butter and pkg chocolate chips — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Carnival Chip Dainties

content:
Instructions Combine in a mixing bowl 2 cups shredded carrots ½ cup diced celery ½ cup raisins
Mix together 1/3 cup mayonnaise or salad dressing 1 Tbps vinegar
Toss lightly with two forks and turn into lettuce-lined serving bowl.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Carrot & Raisin Salad

content: Ingredients 6 carrots, sliced (Boil 10 min.) 1 medium onion ¼ cup margarine 1/8 cup flour 1 cup milk 1/6 tsp dry mustard 1/8 tsp celery salt 1/8 tsp pepper ¾ tsp salt grated cheese Instructions Cook onions in margarine in microwave for 5 min. Add flour, spices and mix. Add milk. Cook milk till thick 5 – 10 minutes. Layer carrots, onion-milk mixture, then cheese. Top with cracker crumbs, then cheese. Bake for 30 minutes at 350 degrees F.
description: Homemade luscious pie with carrots and medium onion. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Carrot Casserole (barb Pierce)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups baby carrots 1 20-oz tin pineapple chunks 4 tsp cornstarch ½ tsp ground cinnamon ½ cup packed brown sugar 1 tbsp butter or margarine Instructions In a saucepan, bring 1” of water to a boil; place carrots in a steamer basket over water. Cover and steam for 8 – 10 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain pineapple, reserving juice; set pineapple aside.
In a saucepan, combine cornstarch and cinnamon. Add the brown sugar, butter and reserved juice. Bring to a boil, cook and stir for 2 minutes or until thickened. Stir in the carrots and pineapple. Heat through. YIELD: 4 servings
description: Savory, colorful vibrant vegetables with baby carrots and tin pineapple chunks — proof that the humble veggie side can absolutely steal the show.
title: Carrots and Pineapple (cw)

content: Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 tsp crushed garlic 1 cup chopped onions 1 medium cauliflower (separated into florets) 4 cups chicken stock (broth) 2 small potatoes, peeled and chopped ¼ cup shredded cheddar cheese 2 Tbsp chopped fresh chives In large non-stick saucepan, heat oil, brown the garlic and onions until softened (approx. 5 minutes). Add cauliflower, stock and potatoes. Bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat and simmer for 25 minutes or until tender. Transfer to food processor and blend until creamy and smooth. Return to saucepan and thin with more stock of desired. Serve with a sprinkle of cheese and chives.
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with vegetable oil, crushed garlic, and chopped onions. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Cauliflower Potato Soup

content:
Ingredients INGREDIENTS: 2 oz Mozzarella cheese, shredded 1 oz onion, diced 1 oz red pepper, diced 1 oz green pepper, diced DIPPING SAUCE: ¼ cup mayonnaise ¼ cup sour cream 1 finely chopped garlic clove 1 Tbsp chopped fresh dill Instructions Combine ingredients for dipping sauce and place in serving dish. Cook Cavendish Farms FlavourCrisp Crispy Coated or Spicy fries according to package directions. Combine the cheese, onions and peppers. Sprinkle over fries. Broil or bake at 450 degrees until the cheese is melted.
Serves 1.
description: Creamy, comforting bold potatoes with ingredients: 2 oz mozzarella cheese and onion — the ultimate side dish that makes everything else on the plate better.
title: Cavendish Farms Flavourcrisp Fries

content: Ingredients ¼ cup butter or margarine 1-1/4 tsp seasoned salt 4-1/2 tsp Wooster sauce 8 cups Chex cereals 1 cup peanuts – of course! 1 cup small pretzels Instructions Melt and mix seasonings in a large saucepan. Dump in remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly. 250 degree oven for 1 hour stirring well every 20 minutes.
description: Crunchy, addictive hearty snack mix with ¼ cup butter or margarine and seasoned salt — once you start munching, there's no stopping. Perfect for parties or movie nights.
title: Charlie Brown Party Mix

content: Ingredients 13 cups water 5 cups vinegar 1 cup pickling salt 1 tsp alum Instructions Bring to a boil. Put plenty of dill in jars and 2 garlic buds. Pour hot mixture over cukes to ½” from top of jar. Seal tightly, cool and store.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with vinegar and pickling salt — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Chassie’s Dills

content: Ingredients 4-1/2 cups sliced zucchini 2 – 3 tbsp oil salt &amp; pepper to taste 1 large onion, chopped 2 tbsp minced garlic 1 10-3/4 oz tin tomato puree 1 6-oz tin tomato paste 3 tbsp sugar 1 tsp Italian seasoning 1 tsp dried basil 2 2-1/4 oz tins of drained sliced ripe olives 3 cups (12 oz) shredded mozzarella cheese 6 eggs, lightly beaten 1-1/2 cups grated Parmesan cheese Instructions In a large skillet, sauté zucchini in oil until tender. Sprinkle with salt and pepper; stir; transfer to an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2 baking dish. In same skillet, sauté onion until crisp-tender. Add garlic; sauté 3 minutes longer. Stir in tomato puree, tomato paste, sugar, Italian seasoning and basil. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Simmer, uncovered, for 10-15 minutes or until slightly thickened. Stir in olives. Pour over zucchini. Sprinkle with mozzarella. Combine the eggs and Parmesan cheese; pour over zucchini. Bake, uncovered, at 375 degrees for 25-30 minutes or until a knife inserted near he center comes out clean. Let the dishstand for 15 minutes before serving. YIELD: 12 to 16 servings.
description: Hearty rustic casserole packed with sliced zucchini, large onion, and minced garlic — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Cheesy Zucchini Bake (cw)

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs 1 cup chocolate chips 1 cup butterscotch chips 1 cup chopped walnuts 11-oz sweetened condensed milk Instructions Measure all ingredients. Stir together well. Grease a 9 x 9 pan. Smooth with back of spoon. Bake for 30 – 35 minutes in a 350 degree oven. Cool for 4 – 5 minutes. Cut into 36 squares
NOTE: I tried this in a well-greased pan and it was a real struggle to get out of the pan. The next time I will grease the pan, line it with tin foil and grease this very generously and keep my fingers crossed that it will come out easy.
description: Irresistible rich squares with graham cracker crumbs and chocolate chips — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Chewy Chip Squares

content: Ingredients 1 tin cream of chicken soup 1 cup cubed cooked chicken ½ cup milk or water Mashed potatoes Instructions Place ingredients in a pan. Then cover with mashed potatoes. Heat in oven at 325 degrees for 30 minutes.
description: Homemade indulgent pie with tin cream of chicken soup and cubed cooked chicken. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Chicken Pie – (merit)

content:
Instructions Prep. Time 15 min. - Cooking Time – 20 min.
2 Tbsp cornstarch	1 lb skinless boneless chicken, cut into 1 tin condensed chicken broth	strips 1 Tbsp soya sauce	1 onion, sliced ¼ tsp ground ginger	5 cups cut-up veggies (broccoli, carrots 2 Tbsp vegetable oil	mushroom, celery &amp; green onion)
Stir together cornstarch, broth, soya sauce and Ginger – set aside. In large skillet, heat half of the oil. Stir fry chicken with onion till browned. Remove from skillet; season lightly with salt and pepper and set aside. In same skillet, heat remaining oil. Stir fry veggies until tender crisp. Add reserved cornstarch mix. Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly. Return chicken to skillet. Heat thru. Serve over rice, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned rustic chicken — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Chicken-veggie Stir-fry

content:
Ingredients Melt 6-oz pig (1 cup) Chipits chocolate chips and 6-oz pkg (1 cup) Chipits butterscotch chips in a 1-1/2 quart bowl over hot (not boiling) water. Stir in 1 cup chow mein noodles and 1 cup peanuts. Instructions Drop with 2 teaspoons onto wax paper. Chill until firm. Makes 2 dozen.
For an interesting variation, substitute (for noodles) one cup broken thin pretzel sticks, corn flakes or rice crispies.
description: A savory bold snack with melt 6-oz pig chipits chocolate chips and 6-oz pkg chipits butterscotch chips in a 1-1/2 quart bowl over hot water. stir in 1 cup chow mein noodles and 1 cup peanuts. that vanishes by the handful. Make a big batch because a small one won't last.
title: Chipits Crunchies

content: Ingredients Soda crackers 1-1/2 cups chocolate chips 1 cup butter 1 cup chopped almonds 1 cup brown sugar Instructions Grease cookie sheet. Line bottom of sheet with soda crackers. Boil butter and brown sugar for 5 minutes; keep stirring. Pour hot mixture over crackers. Bake in oven for 5 minutes at 375 degrees. Pour chocolate chips and almonds on top of hot crackers. Refrigerate to harden. Break into pieces. ENJOY!
description: Rich, chewy sweet squares packed with soda crackers, chocolate chips, and chopped almonds. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Chocolate Almond Bark

content: Ingredients 1 cup margarine 1 cup brown sugar 1 tsp vanilla 2 cups sifted flour 1 cup chocolate chips 1 cup chopped walnuts Instructions Cream margarine well. Add sugar and vanilla and mix well. Add flour a little at a time and mix well. Then add chips and walnuts. Pack in a jelly roll pan and bake at 350 degree oven for 20-25 minutes. Cut while warm.
description: Sweet, satisfying sweet squares with brown sugar and sifted flour. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Chocolate Chip Bars

content:
Ingredients 8 oz white chocolate, divided 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate chips ½ cup each salted peanuts, mini marshmallows, crispy rice cereal, coconut, red &amp; green cherries Instructions In a heavy saucepan or top or a double boiler, melt 6 oz white chocolate and all the chocolate chips. Stir in peanuts, marshmallows and cereal. Pour onto a greased 10”pizza pan or a 10” circle of cardboard covered with foil. Spread to even out top. Sprinkle with coconut. Top with cherries. Melt remaining white chocolate and drizzle over pizza. Chill.
Yield: 16 - 20 servings.
description: Crispy, bubbly robust pizza with white chocolate and semi-sweet chocolate chips — homemade crust, bold toppings, and the kind of flavor that puts delivery to shame.
title: Chocolate Pizza - Cw

content: Ingredients 1/3 cup margarine l/2 cup cocoa 1/3 cup honey 3 cups small marshmallows 4 cups rice krispies l cup peanuts (optional) Instructions Melt margarine in large pan, add honey, cocoa and marshmallows. Stir till marshmallows are melted. Remove from heat and add rice krispies and peanuts. Press into a buttered 9”square pan.
description: Homemade luscious pie with l/2 cup cocoa and honey. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Chocolate Rice Krispies Squares

content:
Ingredients 1 cup white sugar 1 cup peanut butter 1 cup corn syrup Instructions Mix and melt together in a large deep pan, then remove from stove. Add six cups Rice Krispies gradually and mix. Spread in a buttered 8” x 8”pan. Cool.
MELT TOGETHER;: 1 6-oz pkg chocolate chips	1 6-oz pkg butterscotch chips
Spread on top of above mixture and let cool. Cut in squares. Kids just love it.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with peanut butter and corn syrup that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Chocolate Scotcharews

content:
Ingredients 8-1/2 or 9-oz pkg cream-filled sugar wafer coolies 24 vanilla caramels 2 tbsp milk 6-oz pkg (1 cup) semi-sweet choc.chips 3 tbsp margarine or butter Instructions Lightly grease 8”or 9” square pan. Arrange single layer of wafer cookies side by side over bottom of greased pan. Reserve half of wafer cookies for second layer. In small saucepan, melt caramels with milk over low heat, stirring occasionally until well blended.
Spread evenly over wafer cookies in pan. Arrange reserved cookies side by side in single layer over caramel mixture in pan.
In small saucepan, melt chocolate chips, stirring occasionally. Spread evenly over wafer cookies. Cool until set. Cut into bars. Makes 36 bars.
description: A pan of indulgent squares with or 9-oz pkg cream-filled sugar wafer coolies and vanilla caramels that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Chocolate-caramel Squares

content:
Ingredients 15 cups of cherries to 1 gallon of hot water Acid Blend 2 tsp per gallon Grape Tannin ¼ tsp per gallon Pectic Enzyme ½ tsp per gallon Yeast Nutrient ½ tsp per gallon Sugar 3 lbs per gallon - Stir well. Wine Yeast 1 pkg per 1 to 5 gallons Instructions Cool mixture and add 1 pkg of wine yeast. Stir daily for two weeks. After two weeks, extract liquid. NOTE: Use same cherries to make a second brew. (Make half the amount of first batch.)
When brew stops working (fermenting), check for sweetness and if too dry, add more sugar (1 cup or so per 5 gallons). Brew will start to work again.
When this stops working, check again and repeat same procedure as before.
NOTE: It is necessary to rack the brew several times in order to clarify it.
description: A refreshing savory drink with of cherries to 1 gallon of hot water and acid blend that's perfect for gatherings, holidays, or just a treat on a warm afternoon.
title: Choke Cherry Wine

content: Ingredients 1 cup ketchup 1.5 tsp lemon juice 1 Tbsp finely chopped onion ¼ cup horseradish Add Tabasco sauce, salt and pepper to taste
description: Homemade rustic sauce with ketchup and lemon juice — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Cocktail Sauce

content:
Instructions If you have a microwave and need a nice potato salad or cole slaw dressing in a hurry, here is one. In a medium glass bowl, with wire whip or rotary beater, thoroughly combine
½ cup water	1/3 cup vinegar ¼ to 1/3 cup sugar	1 egg 1 tbsp flour	½ tsp salt ½ tsp dry mustard
Heat on high 4 – 5 minutes until mixture is thickened, stirring twice; stir in
1 tbsp butter or margarine.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with simple, honest ingredients. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Cole Slaw Dressing

content:
Ingredients 4 qts finely shredded cabbage 1 tbsp coarse salt (salad salt) 1 cup peeled and shredded carrot ½ cup mayonnaise ½ cup commercial sour cream 1 tsp celery salt 1 tbsp vinegar Salt and pepper 3 tsp sugar Instructions In a large colander over a large bowl, layer the shredded cabbage and coarse salt. Place a plate over the cabbage and weight it down with a heavy object. Allow cabbage to stand at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Press out as much moisture as possible and place cabbage and the shredded carrot in a large bowl. In a small bowl blend the mayonnaise, sour cream, celery salt, sugar and vinegar. Add the mayonnaise mixture to the cabbage and stir until well combined. Chill for several hours or overnight. Taste the coleslaw after it has been chilled and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
Makes 2 – 3 quarts.
description: A vibrant zesty salad with qts finely shredded cabbage and coarse salt that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Coleslaw

content: Ingredients 1-1/3 cup small marshmallows 3-1/4 cup graham crumbs ½ cup red cherries (chopped) ½ cup green cherries (chopped) ½ cup chopped walnuts 1 can Eagle Brand Instructions Mix well and roll into balls or shape into rolls for slicing.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with small marshmallows and graham crumbs that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Colette’s Marshmallow Rolls

content: Ingredients 2 heaping tsp cornstarch 2 eggs 1 cup white sugar 1 cup vinegar and 4 tsp water 1 level tsp dry mustard 1 tsp salt 4 tbsp canned milk Instructions Combine above in double boiler and cook. After it starts to thicken, boil for 5 minutes. Chill before using.
description: A vibrant zesty salad with heaping tsp cornstarch and vinegar and 4 tsp water that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Cooked Salad Dressing

content:
Ingredients 1 cup dry bread crumbs 1 cup powdered milk 1 cup cornmeal 1 cup all-purpose flour 3 tbsp salt 1 tbsp dried thyme 1 tsp pepper 1 tsp garlic salt 1 tsp celery salt 1 tsp onion salt 1 tsp cried oregano Instructions Combine all the ingredients. To use, place 1 cup mixture in a plastic bag. Shake chicken pieces until coated. Place on a baking pan and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Store unused mixture in an airtight container.
Yield: Recipe makes enough coating for three chickens, each 2-1/2 to 3 lbs.
description: Juicy, flavorful comforting chicken with dry bread crumbs and powdered milk — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Cornmeal and Herb Chicken Coating

content:
Ingredients 1 16-oz package cranberries 1 cup brown sugar 1 cup water 1 cup orange juice 1 cup raisins 1 medium chopped granny smith apple 1 Tbsp ground ginger ¼ tsp ground cloves ½ tsp ground nutmeg Instructions In a 3-quart saucepan, combine cranberries, water, raisins and shopped apple. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer. Cook, covered for 15 minutes or until cranberries pop.
Stir in sugar, orange juice, ginger, nutmeg and cloves. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Ladle into jars or freezer containers. Refrigerate or freeze until ready to use.
description: Homemade bold preserves with package cranberries and brown sugar that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Cranberry Chutney – (aboriginal Cooking)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups cooked codfish, fresh or frozen 3 cups of milk 4 Tbsp of butter or margarine 4 Tbsp of flour Instructions Salt and pepper to taste
Melt butter or margarine in top of double boiler, blend in flour, add milk gradually and cook, stirring constantly until sauce thickens.
Add flaked fish. Add salt and pepper.
Serve on toast, or with hot baking powder biscuits or over broiled potatoes.
Enjoy!
description: Crispy on the outside, melt-in-your-mouth tender inside — this satisfying fish with cooked codfish and of milk is weeknight fancy at its absolute best.
title: Creamed Codfish – (from Metis Cookhouse by Gail Lebland_

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 cup brown sugar 2-1/2 cups Rice Krispies 2//3 cup corn syrup 1 cup coconut 2/3 cup peanut butter ½ cup chopped walnuts Instructions Combine brown sugar, corn syrup and peanut butter over low heat. Remove and add remaining ingredients. Make small balls and put them on wax paper. Chill for ½ hour.
description: Warm, heavenly dessert with brown sugar and rice krispies — golden and bubbling on top, tender and fruity underneath. Best served with a scoop of ice cream.
title: Crispy Chews

content:
Instructions Prep: 10 min. Total: 35 minutes
2 pkg (300 g each) frozen chopped spinach,	1 tub (250 g) Philadelphia Chives &amp; Onion thawed and well drained	Spreadable Cream Cheese ½ cup Draft Rancher’s Choice Dressing	2 eggs, lightly beaten 1-1/2 cups Draft Double Cheddar Shreds, 1 cup crushed Ritz Crackers, divided divided
PREHEAT oven to 375 F. Mix spinach, cream cheese spread, dressing, eggs and ¾ cup of the shredded cheese in large bowl. Stir in ½ cup of the crushed crackers.
SPOON spinach mixture evenly into greased 2-L ovenproof casserole dish. Sprinkle with remaining ½ cup crushed crackers and remaining ¾ cup shredded cheese.
BAKE 20 to 5 minutes or until heated through and cheese on top is melted.
Makes 12 servings. To thaw spinach, place in refrigerator overnight, or unwrap; place in microwaveable bowl and thaw in microwave as directed on package. Be sure to squeeze well after thawing to remove as much water as possible.
description: Simple, delicious garden-fresh vegetables . Sometimes the best meals are built around what's growing in the garden.
title: Crispy Topped Creamy Spinach (kraft What’s Cooking)

content: Ingredients 2 cups dry sifted toasted breadcrumbs oil for brushing ½ cup flour 2 tsp M.S.G. (Accent) 1 tsp salt ½ tsp fruit sugar ¼ to ½ tsp garlic salt ½ tsp flavor salt Instructions To make the crumb coating brush sparingly with oil (or melted butter) the slices from half a standard loaf of bread. Arrange overlapping a little on broiler pan and dry out until crisp in oven at 200 degrees F. Buzz in blender until extremely fine. To the two cups add all remaining crumb-coating ingredients and mix well.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with dry sifted toasted breadcrumbs and oil for brushing — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Crumb Coating

content: Ingredients 1 6-oz pkg (1 cup) Butterscotch Morsels 1 6-oz (1 cup) Chocolate Chips ½ cup peanut butter ½ cup sifted icing sugar 4 cups Rice Krispies 2 tbsp soft butter or margarine 1 tbsp water Instructions Melt Butterscotch Morsels with peanut butter in heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring until well blended. Remove from heat. Add Rice Krispies; stir until well coated with butterscotch mixture. Press half of cereal mixture into buttered 8 x 8 x 2 pan. Chill in refrigerator while preparing fudge mixture. Set remaining cereal mixture aside. Combine chocolate chips, sugar, butter and water in top of double boiler; place over hot water and stir until chocolate melts and mixture is well blended. Spread over chilled cereal mixture. Spread remaining cereal mixture evenly over top. Press in gently. Chill. Remove from refrigerator for about 10 minutes before cutting into squares.
description: Chilled, Crunchy Fudge Sandwiches, easy to make and impossible to resist
title: Crunchy Fudge Sandwiches

content: Ingredients 2 cups whole wheat flour 2 cups cornmeal l cup parmesan or cheddar cheese l/2 cup water or broth ¾ cup vegetable oil, drained tuna oil or bacon/chicken fat Instructions Preheat oven to 300 degrees. Combine flour, cornmeal and cheese in a large bowl. Stir in oil until mixture is crumbly. Stir in water/broth until well mixed. Roll dough on lightly floured surface to a thickness of l/2”. Biscuits can be cut out with a 2”or 4”dog bone cookie cutter. Transfer to an ungreased baking sheet. Bake for 45 minutes. Transfer to rack to cool completely.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with whole wheat flour and cornmeal that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Dandy Dog Bone Biscuits

content:
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 2 cups rolled oats 1 cup brown sugar ½ cup coconut 1-1/4 cup shortening ½ tsp soda ½ tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 1-1/2 lb dates Instructions Rub first 8 ingredients together until crumbly. Put 2/3 mixture in bottom of a 9 x 12 cake pan. Cook dates with 1-1/2 cups water until soft. Add ¼ to ½ cup white sugar and 2 tbsp butter. Mix well. Spread date mixture over crumbs in bottom of pan. Top with remaining crumb mixture. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
NOTE: This square can also be filled with mincemeat in place of the dates.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with rolled oats and brown sugar. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Date Squares

content: Ingredients 1 pkg. marshmallows 1 small tin Carnation milk Graham wafers or Graham wafer crumbs Instructions Soak marshmallows in the milk for 3 to 4 hours. Stir often without breaking them up. Roll out graham wafers to a powder. Drop one marshmallow at a time on or into the graham wafer crumbs and roll. Place on a plate to dry.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with marshmallows and small tin carnation milk that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Do-das

content:
Instructions Mix 2 cups Bisquick, ¾ cup milk well with fork. Drop by tablespoonfuls gently onto boiling stew. Cook over low heat 10 minutes uncovered and 10 minutes covered. Let stew bubble gently.
Makes 10 – 12 dumplings.
description: A tried-and-true satisfying recipe that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Dumplings (made with Bisquick)

content:
Instructions In a bowl, whisk together one 35 gr packet of Old El Paso Seasoning Mix, 1 cup of light sour cream and ½ cup of light mayonnaise until thoroughly combined.
Stir in 2/3 cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese.
Service with vegetable sticks and chicken wings.
Makes 2-1/2 cups
description: Smooth, tangy garden-fresh dip — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Dunkin’ Crunchin’ Dip

content:
Ingredients ½ cup boiling water 3 tbsp margarine ¾ cup sugar 1-1/2 cup instant powdered milk Instructions Put in blender and mix. No cooking. Can be used for all recipes that call for Eagle Brand milk.
Eagle Brand has a measure of 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons in a 15 oz can.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with ½ cup boiling water and ¾ cup sugar that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Eagle Brand Milk Substitute

content:
Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour ¼ cup granulated sugar ½ cup butter 1/3 cup raspberry jam (or your choice) 2 eggs 1 pkg (212 g) Shirriff Lemon Pie Filling ½ cup ground almonds ¼ cup sliced almonds Instructions Combine flour and sugar, then butter until the mixture clumps together. Press firmly in 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees 15 – 18 minutes or until slightly browned. Remove from oven and spread with jam. Beat eggs until light and creamy. Beat in pie filling and ground almonds. Pour over jam layer. Sprinkle with sliced almonds. Bake 20 –25 minutes or until set and firm to touch. Cool. Cut into squares. Makes 25 squares.
NOTE: Grease pan and especially the sides as the squares really stick to the sides.
HINT: For Lemon Coconut Squares Substitute 1-1/2 cups flaked coconut for the ground almonds and sliced almonds.
description: A pan of heavenly squares with ¼ cup granulated sugar and ½ cup butter that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Easiest Ever Lemon Squares

content:
Instructions No –Bake Double Peanut Butter Bars Prep: 20 Min. Total: 20 Min.
1-1/2 cup Draft Smooth Peanut Butter	2/3 cup honey 2/3 cup sugar	3 cups Post Honey Bunches of Oats Cereal 1 cup peanuts	4 squares Baker’s Semi-Sweet Chocolate, melted.
MICROWAVE peanut butter, honey and sugar in large microwaveable bowl on HIGH 2 or 3 min. or until sugar is melted and mixture is smooth, stirring every minute.
POUR over cereal and peanuts in large bowl; mix to coat well. Press into greased foil-lined 13” x 9” pan.
SPREAD melted chocolate evenly over cereal mixture. Let stand or refrigerate until chocolate is firm. Cut into 24 bars. Store in tightly covered container in refrigerate.
Makes 24 servings, 1 bar each.
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food . The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Easiest Ever Peanut Butter Treats (kraft What’s Cooking)

content: Ingredients 1 cup white sugar ½ cup milk 1 cup brown sugar ¼ cup corn syrup ¼ cup melted butter 2 tbsp cocoa Instructions Mix in small saucepan. Bring to a boil (soft ball stage). Five minutes after it begins to boil, remove from heat. Add vanilla and nuts. Less cocoa will give a caramel fudge.
description: Smooth, creamy melt-in-your-mouth fudge with ½ cup milk and brown sugar that melts on your tongue and sets perfectly every time. The candy thermometer's finest hour.
title: Easy Fudge Recipe

content: Ingredients 2.5 l (10 cups) coarsely chopped peeled peaches 1.25 l (5 cups) chopped peeled pears (about) 3 kg/6-1/2 lbs) (about 1.2kg/2-3/4 lbs) 4 limes 60 to 90 ml (4 to 6 Tbsp) liquid honey 1 – 115 g pkg commercial salsa mix Instructions PREPARATION: Place 6 clean 500-ml (1 cup) Mason jars on a rack in a boiling water canner; cover jars with water to heat to a simmer (82C/180F). Set screw bands aside. Heat snap lid sealing discs in hot water, not boiling (82C/180F). Keep jars &amp; sealing discs hot until ready to use. 2. In a large, deep stainless-steel saucepan, combine peaches and pears. Grate peel from limes and squeeze juice. Add grated peel, 125 ml (1/2 cup) lime juice, honey and salsa mix to fruit. Stirring continuously until well mixed, bring mixture to a full rolling boil. Cook and stir for 2 minutes. 3. Ladle hot salsa into a hot jar to within 1cm (1/2”) of top of jar (headspace). Using a non-metallic utensil, remove all bubbles and adjust headspace, if required, by adding more salsa. Wipe jar rim, removing any food residue. Centre hot sealing disc on clean jar rim. Screw band down until resistance is met, then increase to finger tip tight. Return filled jar to rack in canner. Repeat for remaining salsa. 4. When canner is filled, ensure that all jars are covered by at least 2.5 cm (1”) of water. Cover canner and bring water to a full rolling boil before starting to count processing time. At altitudes of up to 305 m (1000 ft), process (boil filled jars) for 15 minutes for 250-ml (1-cup) jars, 20 minutes for 500-ml (2-cup) jars. 5. When processing time is complete, remove canner lid, wait for 5 minutes, then remove jars without tilting and place them upright on a protected work surface. Let cool upright, undisturbed, for 24 hours; do not retighten screw bands. 6. After cooling, check jar seals. Sealed discs curve downward and do not move when pressed. Remove screw bands; wipe and dry bands and jars. Store screw bands separately or replace loosely on jars, …
description: Smooth, tangy garden-fresh dip with l coarsely chopped peeled peaches and l chopped peeled pears — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Easy Peach-pear Salsa – (nugget Thursday Food Page)

content:
Ingredients 2 lb (about 8 cups) yellow beans, cut in 1” pieces ½ cup flour 1-1/2 tsp salt ¼ cup dry mustard 2 cups brown sugar 2 tsp turmeric 1 Tbsp celery seed 3 cups Heinz white vinegar 1 cup water Instructions Cook beans in lightly salted water until just tender-crisp; drain. In large heavy saucepan, mix flour, mustard, turmeric, salt, sugar and celery seed. Stir in vinegar and water gradually. Bring to a boil over low heat and cook, stirring constantly until thickened, about 3 to 4 minutes. Add beans to sauce and bring to boiling. Pack into hot, sterilized jars and seal.
Makes 4 pints.
description: Crispy, tender, Edna Nadon’s Mustard Beans, a classic homemade preserve
title: Edna Nadon’s Mustard Beans

content:
Ingredients (Tastes lie Kraft Catalina) 1 tsp salt l tsp paprika l/3 cup sugar 2 tbsp lemon juice l/4 cup vinegar 2/3 cup salad oil l/3 cup catsup (ketchup) Instructions Measure ingredients into bottle or jar. Cover tightly and shake until well mixed. Makes l-l/4 cups.
NOTE: Catsup is easily measured if you measure the oil then add the catsup to make l cup. When measured this way, the catsup doesn’t stick to the measuring cup.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with l tsp paprika, l/3 cup sugar, and lemon juice. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Emergency Salad Dressing

content:
Ingredients 8 oz (8 squares) Baker’s semi-sweet chocolate 2/3 cup sweetened condensed milk 1 tsp vanilla ¼ tsp salt ½ cup chopped nuts (optional) Instructions Melt chocolate with milk over hot water. Remove from heat; blend in vanilla, salt and nuts. Spread in buttered 8”x 4”loaf pan. Flatten with buttered spoon. Chill until firm, then cut into squares. Makes 18 candies.
NOTE: Recipe may be doubled and spread in 8-inch square pan.
description: Velvety luscious fudge with baker’s semi-sweet chocolate and sweetened condensed milk — rich, dense, and indulgent. Cut into tiny squares because a little goes a long, long way.
title: Fabulously Flawless Chocolate Fudge

content: Ingredients 1 qt club soda 1 6-oz can pink lemonade 1 6-oz can orange juice 4 cups cranberry juice Instructions combine all ingredients and chill.
description: Mix up this bold beverage with qt club soda and can pink lemonade and watch it become everyone's favorite. Simple to make, impossible to stop sipping.
title: Festive Punch

content:
Ingredients 2 cups hot or cold mashed potatoes 1 small onion, finely chopped 1 8-oz pkg cream cheese, softened 2 eggs 2 Tbsp all-purpose flour Salt and pepper to taste Instructions Put the potatoes into the large bowl of your electric mixer. Add the cream cheese, chopped onion, eggs, and flour. Beat at medium speed until the ingredients are blended, then bet at high speed until light and fluffy. Taste and add salt and pepper, if needed. Spoon into a greased 9-inch square baking dish. Bake, uncovered, in a 300 degree oven for about 35 minutes.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
NOTE: I usually make it in a regular casserole dish and bake at 350 for about 1 hour.
description: Warm, satisfying robust casserole with hot or cold mashed potatoes and small onion that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Fluffy Potato Casserole

content: Ingredients 7 cups sliced pickling cucumber, with peel 1 large onion, sliced 1 cup thinly sliced peppers, seeded, all green or half red 1 Tbsp coarse pickling salt ½ tsp celery seed 1 cup white vinegar 2 cups granulated sugar Instructions Combine all ingredients in bowl. Stir well. Cover. Refrigerate for 3 days. Stir every day. Pack into freezer containers, covering with brine to within 1 inch of top. Freeze. Thaw before serving. Keeps in refrigerator at least 8 weeks. Keeps in freezer at least 1 year. Makes 3 pints.
description: Crunchy, tangy comforting pickles with sliced pickling cucumber and large onion — brined to briny perfection. Open a jar and taste summer all year long.
title: Freezer Pickles

content:
Ingredients 1 cup white sugar 1 Tbsp Crisco 2 Tbsp butter 3 Tbsp water Instructions Cook 3 minutes. Dip pieces of bread in mixture (cut 2” or 3” pieces) and place on wax paper.
Sprinkle with coconut.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with crisco — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Frontier Kitchen Dolly Drops

content:
Ingredients 6 cups flour ½ tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 1 lb Tenderflake lard 1 cup boiling water Instructions Combine dry ingredients. Cut lard into it until grainy. Add water. Mix thoroughly. Wrap and set 24 hours. Take out 6 hours before use. Will last 3 months in fridge, 6 months in freezer.
Makes 4 2-crust pies.
NOTE: originated in England approx. 150 years ago. If you want to make it fussy for fruit pies, roll top crust in sugar.
description: Tender, Frontier Kitchen Never Fail Hot Water Partry, with a perfectly flaky crust
title: Frontier Kitchen Never Fail Hot Water Partry

content: Ingredients 2 eggs 2 cups brown sugar 2 Tbsp cream or milk 3 Tbsp melted butter 1 Tbsp cornstarch 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Mix all together. Then place in uncooked tart shells and bake at 400 F. until pie is golden brown.
description: Homemade luscious pie with brown sugar and cream or milk. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Frontier Kitchen Sugar Tarts

content: Ingredients 1 pkg (3 oz) Jell-O Gelatin (any flavor) 1 envelope Kool-Aid (any flavour) 1 cup sugar 2 cups boiling water 2 cups cold water Instructions Dissolve Jell-O Gelatin, instant soft drink mix and sugar in boiling water. Add cold water. Pour into ice cube trays, small paper cups, or frozen pop molds. Insert wooden sticks or paper spoons diagonally in each ice cube section or at an angle in colds or cups for handles. (If desired, pops may be partially frozen before handles are inserted.) Freeze until firm – 2 to 3 hours. Makes 20 to 24 pops.
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with jell-o gelatin and envelope kool-aid. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Frozen Pops

content:
Ingredients Variety of Fruit Topping: Crust: 12 oz cream cheese, softened 2 cups all-purpose flour ½ cup granulated sugar ½ cup brown sugar 1 tsp vanilla ¼ cup icing sugar Apricot Glaze: 1 cup butter, softened ¼ cup apricot jam or orange marmalade 1 Tbsp water Instructions Crust: Mix all ingredients together in a bowl until it forms a ball. Press onto bottom of pizza pan. Bake at 350 degree F about 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Cool
Topping: Beat cheese, sugar and vanilla together. Spread over crust.
Arrange fruit in pattern of your choice. Make your own colour arrangement. Glaze fruit.
Apricot Glaze: Mix apricot jam or orange marmalade with water.
description: Homemade satisfying pizza loaded with variety of fruit, topping:, and crust:. Golden crust, melty cheese, and the satisfaction of making it yourself.
title: Fruit Pizza

content:
Ingredients 1 cup cubed bread ½ cup chopped peeled apples ¼ cup dried cranberries, cherries or apricots 1 egg ¼ cup white sugar 1 tsp vanilla ½ cup milk 1 tsp butter Cinnamon Instructions In lightly greased 2-cup baking dish, toss together bread, apples and cranberries.
In small bowl, whisk together egg, sugar and vanilla. In small saucepan, heat milk with butter just until steaming; gradually whisk into egg mixture in slow, steady stream. Pour over bread mixture. Let stand for 5 minutes, press gently to loisten bread evenly. Sprinkle with cinnamon to taste.
Bake in 350 degree oven for about 30 minutes or just until puffed and knife inserted in center comes out clean. Broil for about 2 minutes or until golden and crusty.
Makes 2 servings.
description: Warm, homemade golden bread with cubed bread and ½ cup chopped peeled apples — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Fruity Bread Pudding for Two

content:
Ingredients ½ cup vegetable, chicken or beef stock 1/3 cup couscous 2 tsp vegetable oil ¼ cup chopped onion 2 tsp minced gingerroot 2 cups packed fresh spinach, chopped ½ cup each diced sweet red and green peppers ½ cup sugar snap peas, trimmed 2 tsp soya sauce ¼ cup sliced radishes Pinch each salt and pepper Instructions In saucepan, bring stock to boil. Add couscous; cover and remove from heat. Let stand for 5 minutes; fluff with fork.
Meanwhile, in non-stick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat; stir-fry onion and ginger for about 3 minutes or until softened.
Add spinach, red and green peppers and peas; stir-fry for about 2 minutes or just until spinach is wilted. Drizzle with soy sauce, cover and cook for 1 minute. Fold in couscous and radishes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Makes 1 serving.
description: Fresh, vibrant bright vegetables with ½ cup vegetable and couscous — cooked just right so they're tender but still have that satisfying snap.
title: Garden Vegetable Stir-fry for One – (good Times)

content: Ingredients 1 baked 9- or 10-inch pie shell 5 cups blackberries, strawberries, raspberries 3 tbsp cornstarch or blueberries 1 cup sugar ¾ cup water 1 tsp lemon juice 1 cup whipping cream Instructions Crush and sieve 1 cup of the berries to remove seeds. Combine cornstarch, sugar, sieved berries, and water. Cook over medium-low heat until thickened and clear, about 10 minutes. Cool slightly, then add lemon juice and remaining berries. Mix lightly and pile into baked pie shell. Cool and chill about 2 hours. At serving time, whip the cream and pipe or spoon around the rim.
description: Classic heavenly pie with baked 9- or 10-inch pie shell and blackberries — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Glazed Berry Pie

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup light corn syrup 1 tsp vanilla 2 tsp margarine or butter 1 10-oz pkg golden grahams 1 11-oz pkg chocolate chips 3 cup mini marshmallows Instructions Butter 13”x 9”x 2” baking pan. Heat syrup, butter and chocolate chips just to boiling in a 3-quart saucepan, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal in large bowl; toss quickly until coated. Fold in marshmallows, 1 cup at a time. Press in pan. Let stand until firm – about 1 hour.
Yield: 48 squares.
description: Rich, chewy decadent squares packed with light corn syrup, margarine or butter, and pkg golden grahams. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Golden Graham Bars

content: Ingredients ¾ cup light corn syrup 1 tsp vanilla 3 Tbsp margarine 9 cups Golden Grahams cereal (1-12 oz box) 1 pkg (11.5 oz) milk chocolate chips 3 cups miniature marshmallows Instructions Grease rectangular pan, 13 x 9 x 2”. Heat corn syrup, margarine and chocolate chips to boiling in 3-quart saucepan, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal in large bowl. Toss until completely coated with chocolate. Fold in marshmallows, 1 cup at a time. Press mixture evenly in pan with buttered back of spoon. Let stand until firm, at least 1 hour. Cut into 2-inch squares. Store loosely covered at room temperature no longer than 2 days. Makes 24 squares.
description: Savory, golden satisfying pork with ¾ cup light corn syrup and golden grahams cereal that's ridiculously easy to make and even more ridiculously delicious to eat.
title: Golden Grahams S’mores

content:
Instructions Mix together ½ cup softened butter ½ cup white sugar 1 cup molasses Add 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp ginger 2-1/2 cups white flour
Mix well then shape i8nto small balls and press down with a fork. Add candy decorations if you wish. Bake in 350 F. Makes 3 dozen cookies.
description: Warm from the oven and loaded with simple, honest ingredients, these luscious cookies are the ones everyone reaches for first at the bake sale.
title: Grandma’s Molasses Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ head lettuce, torn bite size 1 Romaine lettuce 2 tomatoes, cut bite size 1 cucumber, cut bite size ¼ cup sliced green onions ¼ cup sliced ripe olives ¾ cup Feta cheese, cubed ½ cup salad oil ¼ cup red wine vinegar ¼ cup chopped parsley ½ tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 1/8 tsp oregano 1/8 tsp garlic powder Instructions Layer lettuce in large salad bowl or on a large platter. Spread with tomatoes, cucumber, onions, olives and cheese. Beat next seven ingredients together well. Shake before serving, Serve with salad.
NOTE: A few sardines or anchovies may be arranged over top of salad. Feta cheese may be exchanged with 8 oz (250 gr) cream cheese.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with ½ head lettuce, romaine lettuce, and tomatoes. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Greek Salad

content: Ingredients Louise Beaudry’s Grandmother’s recipe 5 cups brown sugar 8 onions, sliced (5 cups) 34 cups (24) lg green tomatoes, sliced 4 cups white vinegar 2 cups water 4 tbsp pickling spice Instructions Soak tomato and onion slices overnight in 1 cup of coarse salt and 12 cups water. In the morning, rinse tomato and onion slices well. Add brown sugar, vinegar, water and cheese cloth bag of pickling spices. Boil gently until tomato slices start to fall apart. Pour hot into sterilized jars and seal.
description: Old-fashioned savory pickles with louise beaudry’s grandmother’s recipe and brown sugar — crisp, tangy, and made the way grandma did it. Fill the pantry and count the days until they're ready.
title: Green Tomato Ketchup

content: Ingredients 8 oz. Cream cheese, softened 16 oz powdered sugar 2 pkg (6 squares each white baking 1-1/2 tsp vanilla chocolate squares, melted 1 cup toasted chopped nuts 8 drops yellow food coloring 4 drops red food coloring multi-colored sprinkles Instructions Beat cream cheese in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Gradually add sugar, beating until well blended. Stir in white chocolate, vanilla, nuts and food coloring until well mixed. Spread on foil-lined 8” square pan. Top with sprinkles Refrigerate 1 hour or until firm. Cut into squares.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with cream cheese and powdered sugar. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Hallowe’en Fudge – Makes 48 Squares

content:
Ingredients ¼ cup vegetable oil ¼ cup Realemon lemon juice ¼ cup chopped green onions 1 Tbsp water ½ tsp garlic salt ¼ to ½ tsp dried dill weed Instructions In small bow, combine oil, lemon juice, green onions, water, garlic salt and dill weed. Pour over fish, steaks, scallops or shrimp. Cover and marinate 1 hour. Remove fish from marinade. Grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade. Makes about ¾ cup.
VERSATILE MARINE – For poultry, meat or vegetables
½ cup Realemon lemon juice	½ cup vegetable oil 2 cloves garlic, crushed, chopped	1 tsp salt ½ tsp dried rosemary, thyme or	¼ tsp pepper tarragon leaves, crushed
In small bowl, combine lemon juice, oil, garlic, salt , rosemary and pepper. Pour over poultry, meat or vegetables. Cover and refrigerate for six hours or overnight, turning frequently. Remove from marinade, grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade. Makes about 1-1/2 cups.
description: A rich, flavorful savory sauce with ¼ cup vegetable oil and ¼ cup realemon lemon juice that transforms anything it touches. The secret weapon every kitchen needs.
title: Herbed Lemon Marinade – for Fish, Steaks, Scallops or Shrimp

content:
Instructions Place in large crock: 6 cups soft water
Into this put: 1 tin lye ½ lb Borax
When completely dissolved, put in 9 cups lukewarm fat, constantly stirring with a wooden stick until mixture begins to thicken. Set away in a warm place until next day, then cut into bars. Let ripen for 3 weeks before using.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food . The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Homemade Soap – (from the Hazelwood Cookbook)

content:
Ingredients 7 ml Canola Gold Oil 25 ml light soy sauce 50 ml liquid honey 2 cloves garlic Instructions Combine Canola Gold, soy sauce, honey and garlic in a small bowl. Mix well.
Marinade for 2 to 4 hours in the refrigerator. Excellent for chicken, turkey or pork. While cooking, turn frequently to avoid burning.
description: Tangy, savory comforting sauce with canola gold oil and light soy sauce. Drizzle it, dip it, pour it — this one makes everything taste better.
title: Honey Garlic Marinade

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup liquid honey ¼ cup each of water or maple syrup 7 to 8 cups air-popped popcorn Instructions Place honey, water and maple syrup in a medium-size saucepan set over medium-high heat. Boil, uncovered, without stirring, until thick, about 8 to 9 minutes. Meanwhile, place popcorn in a large bowl. Pour syrup over popcorn. Toss using 1 or 2 large spoons until popcorn is evenly coated. Using lightly buttered hands, form popcorn into 10 3” balls. Gobble up right away or wrap in waxed paper and store at room temperature.
Makes 10 balls.
description: Fresh, vibrant fresh vegetables with liquid honey and ¼ cup each of water or maple syrup — cooked just right so they're tender but still have that satisfying snap.
title: Honey-maple Popcorn Balls

content:
Ingredients 3-1/2 cups sliced or baby carrots 2 tbsp oil 1 tbsp honey 1 tbsp maple syrup 1 tbsp Dijon mustard 2 tsp dried parsley flakes Instructions In a saucepan, bring 1” of water to a boil. Place carrots in a steamer basket over water. Cover and steam for 8 – 10 minutes or until crisp-tender.
In a saucepan, combine the oil, honey, syrup and mustard; bring to a boil. Stir in carrots and parsley.
YIELD: 4 – 5 servings.
