description: Simple, delicious garden-fresh vegetables with sliced or baby carrots and honey. Sometimes the best meals are built around what's growing in the garden.
title: Honey-mustard Carrots (cw)

content: Ingredients 1 box (8 qts) non-fat dry milk 1 6-oz non-dairy coffee creamer 1 16-oz container dry chocolate ½ cup confectioners’sugar milk mix Instructions Place all ingredients in a very large bowl or kettle. Stir until well blended. Store in airtight containers or pack into small gift containers. To serve, add ¼ cup chocolate mix to 2/3 cup hot water.
description: Mix up this satisfying beverage with box non-fat dry milk and non-dairy coffee creamer and watch it become everyone's favorite. Simple to make, impossible to stop sipping.
title: Hot Chocolate Mix - Cw

content: Ingredients 1 lb bacon l can green chili (chopped) l cup grated cheese (Jack) l-l/2 cups milk l cup Bisquick 6 oz sour cream 3 eggs Salt and pepper to taste Instructions Brown, drain and crumble bacon. Put in bottom of ll”x 7”greased dish. Top with onion and cheese. Pour rest in blender for l minute. Pour into pan. Bake at 350 degrees until tests done with a knife. (35 – 45 minutes.)
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a melt-in-your-mouth filling with bacon and l can green chili — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Impossible Bacon Breakfast Pie (quiche)

content: Ingredients 3 eggs ¼ tsp salt ½ cup Bisquick Dash pepper ½ cup butter – melted ½ cup sausage cooked and crumbled 1-1/2 cups milk Instructions Blend all but cheese and meat for 15 seconds in blender. Sprinkle with cheese and meat on top. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Cool for 5 minutes.
description: Homemade luscious pie with ¼ tsp salt and ½ cup bisquick. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Impossible Breakfast Quiche

content:
Ingredients 1 10-oz pkg frozen chopped broccoli 1-1/3 cup shredded Cheddar Cheese 8 oz. 1-1/2 cup cooked chopped chicken 2/3 cup chopped onion 1-1/3 cup milk 3 eggs ¾ cup Bisquick ½ tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper Instructions Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate 10 x 1-1/2”. Rinse broccoli in cold water to thaw; drain thoroughly. Mix broccoli, cheese, the chicken and onion in pie plate. Beat milk, eggs, baking mix, salt and pepper 15 seconds in blender on high speed, 1 minute with wire whisk or hand beater or until smooth. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes.
Makes 6 servings.
description: Classic indulgent pie with pkg frozen chopped broccoli and shredded cheddar cheese 8 oz. — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Impossible Chicken ‘n Broccoli Pie

content:
Ingredients 2 cups cut-up chicken ½ - 1 can mushrooms, drained ½ cup diced green onions ½ tsp salt 1 cup shredded Swiss or brick cheese 1-1/2 cup milk ¾ cup Bisquick Instructions Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate and sprinkle with flour. Into pie plate, place the chicken, mushrooms, onions, salt and cheese. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth. Pour over mixture in pie plate. Bake until knife is clean when inserted into center of pie – 30 to 35 minutes. If you wish you can sprinkle top of pie with Parmesan cheese or corn flake crumbs.
NOTE: The Bisquick does to the bottom to form a crust.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with cut-up chicken and ½ - 1 can mushrooms — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Impossible Chicken Pie

content: Ingredients 2 cups milk ¾ cup sugar ½ cup biscuit mix 4 eggs ¼ cup butter or margarine 1-1/2 tsp vanilla 1 cup flaked coconut Instructions Combine milk, sugar, biscuit mix, eggs, butter and vanilla in electric blender container. Cover and blend on low speed for 3 minutes. Pour into greased pie plate. Let stand about 5 minutes, then sprinkle with coconut. Bake at 350 degrees for 40 minutes. Serve warm or cold.
description: A beautiful decadent pie with ¾ cup sugar and ½ cup biscuit mix with a buttery crust and a filling so good you'll want to eat it by the spoonful.
title: Impossible Coconut Pie

content: Ingredients 6 cups sliced pared tart apples ¾ cup milk 1-1/4 tsp ground cinnamon ½ cup Bisquick ¼ tsp ground nutmeg 2 eggs 1 cup sugar 2 tbsp soft margarine or butter Streusel (Below) Instructions Heat oven to 325 degrees. Grease pie plate 10xl-l/2”. Mix apples and spices, turn into plate. Beat remaining ingredients except Streusel until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or l minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Sprinkle with Streusel. Bake until knife inserted in center comes out clean, 55 to 60 minutes. STREUSEL;Mix 1 cup Bisquick baking mix, ½ cup chopped nuts, 1/3 cup packed brown sugar and 3 tbsp firm margarine or butter until crumbly.
description: A beautiful melt-in-your-mouth pie with sliced pared tart apples and ¾ cup milk with a buttery crust and a filling so good you'll want to eat it by the spoonful.
title: Impossible French Apple Pie

content: Ingredients 2 cups cut-up fully cooked smoked ham 4 eggs 1 cup shredded natural Swiss cheese 2 cups milk 1/3 cup chopped green onion or onion 1 cup Bisquick ¼ tsp salt, if desired 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate, 10 x 1-1/2”. Sprinkle ham, cheese and onion in plate. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or 1 minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Bake until golden brown and knife inserted in center comes out clean, 35 to 40 minutes. Cool 5 minutes. 6 servings.
description: Homemade melt-in-your-mouth pie with cut-up fully cooked smoked ham and shredded natural swiss cheese. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Impossible Ham ‘n Cheese Pie

content: Ingredients 1/2 cup creamed cottage cheese 1 lb ground beef 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese ½ tsp salt ½ tsp dried oregano 1 cup milk 1 can (6 oz) tomato paste 2 eggs ½ cup tea biscuit mix Instructions Spread cottage cheese in greased 9” pie plate. Cook ground beef, drain. Stir in ½ cup mozzarella cheese, salt, oregano and tomato paste. Spoon over cottage cheese. Stir milk, eggs and biscuit mix with fork until blended. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30-35 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake 1 – 2 minutes more until cheese is melted. What could be simpler? And follow this up with a dessert that’s just as easy. IMPOSSIBLE PUMPKIN PIE1
description: Classic sweet pie with creamed cottage cheese and ground beef — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Impossible Lasagne Pie

content: Ingredients ¾ cup sugar 1 can (16 oz) pumpkin ½ cup tea biscuit mix 2-1/2 tsp pumpkin pie spice 2 tbsp margarine 2 tsp vanilla 1 can evaporated milk 3 eggs Instructions Put everything in a blender. Mix well. Grease a 9”pie plate. Fill. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 45 – 50 minutes.
description: Classic heavenly pie with ¾ cup sugar and pumpkin — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Impossible Pumpkin Pie

content:
Ingredients 3-1/2 cups unbleached flour 2 cups whole wheat flour l cup rye flour 2 cups bulgar (cracked wheat) l cup cornmeal l/2 cup instant nonfat dry milk powder 4 tsp salt l tbsp (l envelope) dry yeast l/4 cup warm water 3 cups chicken broth l egg, slightly beaten with l sp milk Instructions Heat oven to 300. Mix the first 7 dry ingredients with a wooden spoon in a large bowl. Dissolve yeast thoroughly in warm water in glass measuring cup. Add to dry ingredients. Add chicken broth to flour mixture. Stir until dough forms. Roll our dough until it is l/4”thick. Using a large dog biscuit for model, cut out bone shapes from dough. Place on prepared cookie sheets. Brush dough with egg glaze. Bake bones for 45 minutes. Turn oven off. Biscuits should remain in oven overnight to harden.
Makes 30 large bones.
description: Soft, golden buttery rolls with unbleached flour and whole wheat flour that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Jason’s Doggie Biscuits

content: Ingredients 1 can pink lemonade ½ bottle cranberry juice ½ can pineapple juice 1 bottle gingerale Wine or champagne (optional)
description: A refreshing savory drink with pink lemonade and ½ bottle cranberry juice that's perfect for gatherings, holidays, or just a treat on a warm afternoon.
title: Judy Lafreniere’s Punch

content: Ingredients 5 cups water 2 28-oz tins tomatoes 2 green peppers, diced 1 medium cabbage, shredded 1 large onion, finely chopped 1 stalk celery, diced 1 35-gram pkg onion soup mix Instructions In large pot, combine water, tomatoes, peppers, cabbage, onion, celery and onion soup mix. Bring to boil, reduce heat and simmer 45 minutes, adding more water if desired. Makes about 24 cups.
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with tins tomatoes, green peppers, and medium cabbage. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Judy’s Miracle Soup

content:
Ingredients 6 cups Kellogg’s Crispix Cereal 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 5 tsp Club House SALADS ‘N DIPS Dressing &amp; Dip Mix - approx ½ pkg – any variety Instructions In large mixing bowl, using two spatulas, gently toss cereal with oil until well coated. Spread on cookie sheet.
Bake at 250 degrees for 20 minutes, stirring after 10 minutes. Remove from oven.
Place dry mix in 8-cup or larger container. Add warm cereal, close container securely and gently shake and turn until cereal is thoroughly coated with mix. Serve warm, or cool on tray and store in container. Yield: 6 cups.
NOTE:- You can change the flavour of this easy snack to suit your taste by varying the amount of dry mix. Use the complete package if you enjoy highly seasoned food.
description: This sweet dessert with kellogg’s crispix cereal and vegetable oil is proof that simple ingredients, done right, create something truly magical.
title: Kellogg’s Crispix Recipe #3

content:
Ingredients 4 envelopes Knox Unflavoured Gelatine 3 pkgs. (3 oz each) flavoured gelatine 4 cups boiling water Instructions In a large bow, combine Knox Unflavoured Gelatine and flavoured gelatine; add boiling water and stir until gelatine dissolves. Pour into jelly roll pan (l5-1/2 “ x 10-1/2”) or large shallow baking pan. Chill until firm. Using cookie cutters, cut out shapes and remove carefully. If desired, decorate with glossy decorating gel (available in 11/16 oz tubes) or with chocolate or multi-coloured long sprinkles as pictures. (Note:- other varieties of sprinkles may melt – test before using.)
Chop gelatine trimmings and use in parfaits layered with ice cream or sherbet, or fold into whipped topping.
description: A tried-and-true comforting recipe with envelopes knox unflavoured gelatine and flavoured gelatine that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Knox Blox

content: Ingredients Potatoes, peeled Corn flakes l-l/2 tsp paprika 2 tsp salt Bacon fat Instructions Crush corn flakes and mix with paprika and salt. Cut peeled potatoes into serving size pieces, roll in melted bacon fat, then roll in corn flake mixture. Place in a greased covered casserole and bake in a 350 degree oven for l hour. This is delicious and different when you are tired of boiled, mashed or baked potatoes. Be sure to prepare enough – the family will eat twice as many prepared this way.
description: A tried-and-true comforting recipe with potatoes and corn flakes that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Korny Spuds

content: Ingredients 12 slices white bread Butter (see below) ¼ cup dried red &amp; green sweet pepper flakes ¼ cup parsley flakes 2 tsp cornmeal ½ tsp onion salt ½ tsp celery seed ½ tsp paprika ½ tsp salt 1/8 tsp garlic powder ¼ tsp pepper 1 tsp sugar 1 tbsp whole wheat flour (optional) 2 tsp flavor salt 1 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese Instructions Butter bread on one side, arrange, buttered side up, on large cookie sheets and toast at 250 F. until golden and crisp (about 1 hour). Buzz toasted bread slices, pepper flakes and parsley in blender until as fine as possible. In bowl combine thoroughly the toasted buzzed crumbs with all remaining ingredients. It is now ready to use. NOTE: Leftover coating should be stored in plastic bag or tightly covered Tupperware container in refrigerator. If you want to use this coating on chicken legs, thighs or breasts, do not dry them. Shake on coating and place on buttered shallow pan and bake at 325 F. for 60 to 70 minutes. OR Bake at 350 F. for 45 minutes turning chicken pieces over once.
description: A tried-and-true satisfying recipe with slices white bread and ¼ cup dried red & green sweet pepper flakes that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Kote-n-roast

content:
Ingredients 1 cup shortening 2 cups flour ½ cup icing sugar Instructions Mix until all crumbly. Lightly grease a 9”x 13” pan. Press in pan and bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees. (Looks watery).
Topping:
4 eggs	2 cups white sugar ½ tsp salt	1 tsp baking powder 4 tbsp lemon juice
Mix (not cook) – pour on crust and bake 18 minutes at 350 degrees.
description: Rich, chewy decadent squares packed with ½ cup icing sugar. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Lemon Squares

content:
Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour ½ cup butter or margarine, softened ¼ cup confectioners’ sugar 2 eggs 1 cup granulated sugar ½ tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt 2 Tbsp lemon juice Instructions Heat oven to 350 F. Mix thoroughly flour, butter and confectioners’ sugar. Press in ungreaased square pan, 8x8x2 inches building up ½” edge. Bake 20 minutes.
Beat remaining ingredients about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. Pour over hot crust.
At 350 F. bake about 25 minutes longer or until no imprint remains when touched lightly in center. Cool, cut into squares.
description: Irresistible rich squares with ½ cup butter or margarine and ¼ cup confectioners’ sugar — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Lemon Squares - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse

content:
Ingredients 3 medium potatoes, peeled and cubed 2 Tbsp butter 1 small onion, chopped 2 Tbsp flour 1 cup milk Celery salt and garlic to taste (optional) 2 slices processed cheese Instructions Boil potatoes with seasonings in enough water to cover, until cooked. Mash potatoes with their cooking water. They should be slightly liquefied. In small saucepan, melt butter. Add flour to form a smooth paste. Stir butter mixture into mashed potatoes. Over low heat, gradually stir milk into potato mixture. Add cheese; stir until melted. Makes 3 to 4 servings.
If soup becomes too thick when cooled, a little more milk can be added when reheated.
Keeps in refrigerator for 3 days.
Recipe doubles easily.
description: Hearty, comforting soup loaded with medium potatoes, small onion, and slices processed cheese. Ladle it into big bowls, grab some crusty bread, and settle in for the evening.
title: Lena Langevin Creamy Potato Soup

content:
Ingredients 6 cups shredded cabbage 1 cup shredded carrot ¼ cup chopped green pepper 1 cup mayonnaise 2 Tbsp sugar 2 Tbsp vinegar 1 tsp prepared mustard 1 tsp celery seed ½ tsp salt Instructions Combine veggies and chill. Mix with slaw mix and toss well.
Serves 12.
description: A bright, crisp fresh salad with shredded cabbage and shredded carrot — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Liz’s Cabbage Slaw

content:
Instructions Mix together: 1 tsp garlic powder	½ tsp dried oregano ½ tsp dried thyme	½ tsp dried lemon peel ¼ tsp slt	Pinch cayenne Pinch white pepper
Pop ½ cup popping corn
Melt 2 Tbsp butter
Pour melted butter over popped corn and toss until well coated. Sprinkle mixed seasoning over corn and toss again until well coated.
Makes 12 – 16 cups – 35 cal. Per cup
description: Savory, colorful crisp vegetables — proof that the humble veggie side can absolutely steal the show.
title: Low-cal Cajun Corn

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup brown sugar ½ tsp vanilla ½ cup flour 1 pie shell, uncooked 1-1/4 cup cream Instructions Mix all ingredients. Pour in pie crust. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake another 25 – 30 minutes until filling is set. Cool before serving
Tastes great with scoop of ice cream.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with brown sugar and ½ tsp vanilla — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: M.n.r. Sugar Pie

content: Ingredients 3 cups (one 500-gr pkg) semi-sweet chocolate chips 1 can Eagle Brand Milk Dash salt 1-1/2 tsp vanilla extract ½ cup chopped nuts, optional Instructions In top of double boiler, melt chocolate over boiling water, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat, stir in sweetened condensed milk, salt, vanilla and nuts. Spread mixture evenly onto waxed paper lined 8 x 8” baking pan. Chill 2 hours or until firm. Turn fudge onto cutting board. Peel off paper and cut into squares. Tightly cover any leftovers.
description: Melt-in-your-mouth sweet fudge loaded with semi-sweet chocolate chips, eagle brand milk, and dash salt. A holiday tradition that disappears faster than you can box it up.
title: Magic French Fudge

content:
Ingredients 1 lb ground beef 1 – 28 oz can tomatoes 1 medium onion 2 – 5-1/2 oz cans tomato paste ½ cup diced celery 6 cups hot water ½ green pepper, chopped 2 beef or chicken bouillon cubes 2 tbsp oil 3 tbsp chopped parsley 2 tsp salt 2 cups raw potatoes, diced 2 tbsp sugar 1 cup raw carrot, diced 1/8 tsp pepper ¼ tsp baking soda 6 cups cabbage, coarsely chopped Instructions In a very large soup pot, sauté ground beef, onion, celery and green pepper in the oil until redness is gone from meat. Break meat up as it sautés. Add remaining ingredients, except cabbage, and combine thoroughly. Simmer uncovered for 1 hr, stirring occasionally. Add cabbage and simmer covered, for 1 hr. longer. If soup is too thick, more water may be added.
Serve generous amounts in large soup bowls with whole grain bread. Serves 8 – 10.
description: Rich, comforting rustic soup with ground beef and – 28 oz can tomatoes that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Main Course Cabbage Soup

content:
Ingredients CAKE: 1 cup sugar 1 egg 1 tbsp apricot jam 1 cup flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp baking powder generous pinch of salt 1 tbsp butter 1 tsp vinegar 1 cup milk SAUCE 1 cup cream 6 oz butter 1 cup sugar ½ cup hot water Instructions Beat the egg and sugar well in a mixer. Add the jam, sift flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Melt the butter and add the vinegar. Add liquids to egg mixture alternately with the flour. Beat well and bake in a covered dish at 350 degrees F. for 45 minute to 1 hour. It is important to use a covered dish otherwise sauce won’t sink into the cake.
Melt together the ingredients for the sauce and pour over the pudding as it comes out of the oven.
This is a delicious dessert, but rich!!
description: A luscious indulgent pudding with cake: 1 cup sugar and apricot jam — smooth, rich, and the perfect ending to any meal.
title: Malva Pudding (claire Massicotte)

content: Ingredients 1 recipe pastry 1/3 cup butter or margarine ½ cup maple syrup ¼ tsp nutmeg ½ cup brown sugar ½ cup raisins or walnuts 2 eggs, slightly beaten Instructions Prepare pastry. Line medium tart tins. Combine remaining ingredients. Mix well. Fill tart tins 2/3 full. Bake at 350 degrees for about 25-30 minutes. Makes about 16 medium tarts.
description: Homemade luscious pie with recipe pastry and butter or margarine. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Maple Butter Tarts

content:
Ingredients ½ cup pure maple syrup 2 cups 15% cream 2 tsp cornstarch firm fruits and berries cut into pieces Instructions Gently heat maple syrup in a pan for 5 minutes. Dilute the cornstarch in 2 tsp of cream. Bring the remaining cream to a boil and add to the maple syrup. Blend the cornstarch into the syrup mixture and stir continuously over gentle heat until thickened.
Serves four to six. Exquisite!
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with ½ cup pure maple syrup and % cream that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Maple Creamy Fondue

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp dry white wine 5 Tbsp olive oil 1 tsp thyme 1 tsp tarragon 1 tsp chopped parsley Salt &amp; pepper to taste Instructions Mix all ingredients of the marinade together. Place steaks in a shallow dish and pour marinade over. Let stand for at least 2 hours, turning steaks once.
Remove steaks from the marinade and season with salt and pepper. Cook on a very hot grill to your liking.
Enough marinade for 6 steaks.
description: Homemade bold sauce with dry white wine and olive oil — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Marinade for Venison Steaks – (aboriginal Cooking)

content: Ingredients 30 lg or 120 mini colored marshmallows 1-1/2 cup Eagle Brand milk 2 cups graham cracker crumbs or vanilla wafers 2/3 cup maraschino cherries (drained) 1 tsp vanilla 2-1/2 cup coconut (fine cut) Few grains salt Instructions Blend marshmallows, milk, crumbs or wafers, salt, vanilla and cherries. Butter your hands and form ingredients into rolls. Roll rolls in coconut. Place in wax paper. Refrigerate to cure for 24 hours. Freeze and slice off in just the right size rounds.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with lg or 120 mini colored marshmallows and eagle brand milk that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Marshmallow Rolls

content:
Ingredients 1 tsp salt 1 tsp paprika 1/3 cup sugar 2 Tbsp lemon juice ¼ cup vinegar 1/3 cup ketchup ½ cup Canola oil Instructions Measure all ingredients into a bottle or jar.
Cover tightly and shake well, chill thoroughly.
Shake well before serving.
Yield: 1-1/4 cups.
description: Creamy, zesty crisp dressing with paprika and lemon juice — whisk it together in minutes and taste the difference homemade makes.
title: Melody Dressing

content:
Ingredients 2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces 1 medium green pepper, chopped 1 small onion, chopped 2 tbsp vegetable oil 1 8-3/4 oz tin kernel corn, drained 1 cup chicken broth 1 cup salsa ½ to 1 tsp salt ½ to 1 tsp chili powder, optional ¼ tsp pepper 1-1/2 cups uncooked instant rice ½ to 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese Instructions In large skillet, sauté the chicken, green pepper and onion in oil until chicken is no longer pink and vegetables are crisp-tender. Add the corn, broth, salsa, salt, chili powder if desired and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Stir in the rice. Cover and remove from the heat. Let stand for 5 minutes or until the cheese is melted.
YIELD: 6 servings.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned satisfying chicken with boneless and medium green pepper — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Mexican Chicken and Rice (cw)

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 lbs carrots, shredded ¼ cup water 1 10-3/4 oz tin condensed cream of celery soup (undiluted) 1 cup (4 oz) shredded cheddar cheese 1 medium onion, chopped ¼ cup milk ½ tsp ground mustard 2 tbsp dried bread crumbs 1 tbsp melted butter or margarine Instructions In a 1-1/2 qt microwave-safe dish, combine carrots and water. Cover and microwave on high for 6 – 8 minutes or until crisp-tender, stirring once; drain and return to dish.
In a bowl, combine the soup, cheese, onion, milk and mustard. Pout over carrots; stir to coat. Cover and microwave on high for 2 minutes. Toss the bread crumbs and butter. Sprinkle over the top.
Cover and microwave on high 2 minutes longer. Let stand for 2 minutes.
YIELD: 4 servings.
NOTE: This recipe was tested in an 850-watt microwave.
description: Hearty bold casserole packed with carrots, ¼ cup water, and tin condensed cream of celery soup — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Microwave Carrot Casserole (cw)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups light brown sugar 1 cup boiling water ½ tsp maple flavoring Instructions Combine sugar and water, bring to boiling point and simmer 5 minutes. Remove from heat, add flavouring and stir well. Store in covered jar.
Makes about 2-1/2 cups.
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with light brown sugar and boiling water. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Mock Maple Syrup

content: Ingredients 2 cups brown sugar 2 cups white sugar 2 cups water 1 tsp margarine or butter ½ tsp maple extract Instructions Combine brown sugar, white sugar and water. Bring to a boil and simmer over medium heat – stirring constantly – for half an hour. Stir in margarine or butter and maple extract. Ready to serve over your pancakes.
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with brown sugar and margarine or butter. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Mom’s Mock Maple Syrup – Aboriginal Cooking

content:
Ingredients About 5 lbs beets 1-1/2 tbsp pickling spices 2 cups white vinegar 2 cups white sugar 2 cups water 1-1/2 tbsp coarse salt Instructions Prepare beets by boiling till tender. Mix and bring to a boil the vinegar, sugar, salt, water and spices (tied in a bag if used). Boil for 5 minutes and keep hot.
Peel beets and, if they are large, cut them into bite-size chunks into hot sterilized jars. Pour hot brine solution on top, filling to ½ inch from top. Wipe rim of jars and put on prepared lids. Tighten screw top just fingertip tight.
Makes enough brine for 4 quarts.
description: Homemade rustic preserves with about 5 lbs beets and pickling spices that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Momma’s Pickled Beets

content:
Ingredients 3 lbs moose, cut in stewing pieces 2 carrots ¼ lb butter or margarine 2 parsnips 6 cups water 1 small turnip Salt &amp; Pepper to taste 10 medium potatoes Instructions Brown moose meat in hot butter or margarine. Add water, salt &amp; pepper. Simmer 30 minutes then add chopped onions and cook on low for one hour.
But up and add 2 carrots, 2 parsnips, 1 small turnip and 10 medium potatoes and cook for 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
description: A thick, hearty hearty stew with moose and carrots — slow-simmered until everything is fall-apart tender and the gravy is rich as velvet.
title: Moose Stew - (metis Cookhouse by Gail Leblanc

content:
Instructions Wash and trim ends from 1 (6-quart) basket young yellow string beans. Cut into 1” lengths. This amount will measure about 16 cups prepared beans. Cook in boiling salted water until just tender. MUSTARD SAUCE: Mix together in a large sauepan or kettle 3 cups sugar, 1 Tbsp turmeric 2 tsp salt ½ cup dry mustard ½ cup all-purpose flour Blend in ½ cup vinegar and 1 cup water.
Heat to boiling point 2-1/2 cups vinegar and 1 tsp. celery seeds.
Stir hot liquid slowly into flour mixture. Place over moderate heat, stirring until thickened and smooth (about 5 – 10 minutes. Add drained beans; bring mixture to boiling point. Ladle into hot sterilized sealers and seal.
Yield: About 6 pints.
description: Homemade bold preserves that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Mustard Bean Pickles (purity Cook Book)

content:
Ingredients 250 gr. Cream cheese, softened ½ cup sour cream ¼ cup white sugar ¼ cup packed brown sugar 1 – 2 Tbsp real maple syrup Instructions Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Garnish with maple sugar leaves, if desired.
JUDY’S ESPECIALLY EASY FRUIT DIP
250-gr. Pkg. Cream cheese, softened container of marshmallow cream for sundaes (microwave for about 10 seconds to soften, if necessary.
Mix both ingredients thoroughly.
description: A rich, flavorful vibrant dip loaded with cream cheese, ½ cup sour cream, and ¼ cup white sugar. The appetizer that everyone parks themselves next to and never leaves.
title: Nancy’s Special Fruit Dip

content:
Ingredients 250 gr. Cream cheese, softened ½ cup sour cream ¼ cup white sugar ¼ cup packed brown sugar 1 – 2 Tbsp real maple syrup Instructions Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Garnish with maple sugar leaves, if desired.
JUDY’S ESPECIALLY EASY FRUIT DIP
250-gr. Pkg. Cream cheese, softened container of marshmallow cream for sundaes (microwave for about 10 seconds to soften, if necessary.
Mix both ingredients thoroughly.
description: A rich, flavorful vibrant dip loaded with cream cheese, ½ cup sour cream, and ¼ cup white sugar. The appetizer that everyone parks themselves next to and never leaves.
title: Nancy’s Special Fruit Dip

content:
Ingredients Prep. Time - 15 minutes Microwave: - 2 minutes 2 Tbsp butter or margarine 3 cups miniature marshmallows 4 cups Post Fruity, Cocoa or Cinna-Crunch Pebbles Cereal Baker’s Angel Flake Coconut Instructions Grease Muffin Pan. Put butter or margarine in a 4-quart microwave-able bowl sprayed with no-stick spray on HIGH 3 seconds or until melted. Add marshmallows, Stir to coat. Microwave 1-1/2 minutes or until marshmallows are melted and smooth, stirring after 45 seconds. Add cereal. Stir with wooden spoon to coat well.
Press cereal mixture firmly into prepared muffin pan to form 24 nests. Cool completely. Remove nests from pan.
Fill nests with coconut and candies.
description: Homemade sweet candy studded with prep. time - 15 minutes, microwave: - 2 minutes, and butter or margarine — prettier than store-bought and a hundred times more delicious.
title: Nests with Coconut and Candies – Makes 24

content: Ingredients 5 cups flour 2 tbsp white sugar 1 tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 2 tbsp vinegar 1 egg 1 lb lard Instructions Sift dry ingredients. Add lard and cut into the flour until lard is in very small pieces. In an 8 oz cup, beat egg. Add vinegar and fill cup with cold water. Combine wet and dry mixtures. Use plenty of flour when rolling pie crust. Store unused pie crust in covered container in the refrigerator or separate into smaller batches and freeze.
description: Homemade indulgent pie with vinegar and lard. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Never-fail Pie Crust

content:
Ingredients 1 lb tiny new potatoes (about 20) 1 Tbs chopped chives 1 Tbs chopped fresh tarragon/basil 1 Tbs lemon juice 2 Tbs olive oil or vegetable oil Salt and pepper to taste Instructions In saucepan, boil unpeeled potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain. Add tarragon, chives, lemon juice and oil. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Makes 4 servings.
description: Creamy, comforting bold potatoes with tiny new potatoes and tbs chopped chives — the ultimate side dish that makes everything else on the plate better.
title: New Potatoes with Herbs – (aboriginal Cooking – Gail Leblanc

content:
Ingredients ½ cup butter or margarine ½ cup lightly-packed brown sugar ½ cup corn syrup. Instructions Mix and bring to a boil.
Add: 1 cup peanut butter chocolate chips. Melt over medium heat til smooth. Remove from heat and add 6 cups corn flakes. Mix well.
Pour into a greased 9 x 13 pan. Melt rest of chipits and add some chocolate chipits to make a cup. Spread on squares.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with ½ cup butter or margarine and ½ cup lightly-packed brown sugar. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: No-bake Peanut Butter Squares

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 cups rolled oats 2 cups 2% milk ½ cup white flour ½ cup whole wheat flour 1-1/2 Tbsp brown sugar 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder ½ tsp salt ½ tsp cinnamon 3 eggs (beaten) ¼ cup melted butter/margarine/Crisco Instructions Mix oats and milk. Set aside until milk is absorbed. Flour mixture: combine flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. Mix well. Add eggs and melted butter to oats mixture. Beat well. Add oat mixture to flour mixture, mixing until smooth. Pour batter by ¼ cupfuls onto preheated lightly greased griddle. Bake until fluffy and bubbles break on surface. Pour natural maple syrup over and enjoy. Makes 12 – 15 pancakes.
description: Light, airy heavenly breakfast with rolled oats and % milk that puffs up on the griddle and melts in your mouth. Short stack? Never.
title: Oatmeal Pancakes – (aboriginal Cooking)

content:
Ingredients Preparation: 5 minutes Cook: 20 minutes 3 Tbsp Margarine or butter 4 large potatoes, cut into chunks 1 pouch Loney’s onion soup mix 1/3 cup water 2 Tbsp fresh parsley, chopped 1 Tbsp lemon juice 1 tsp lemon rind, grated ½ tsp dried rosemary leaves ¼ tsp black pepper 1 Tbsp vegetable oil Instructions In large, nonstick skillet, melt margarine or butter. Add potatoes; cook, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender and golden. In small bowl, combine onion soup mix, water, parsley, lemon juice, lemon rind, rosemary and pepper. Add to potatoes; stir and cook over high heat until po0tatoes are coated and liquid evaporated. Stir in oil and cook until potatoes are crisp.
A zesty menu accompaniment – perfect with meat, chicken or burgers. For added colour, use unpeeled potatoes.
Makes 4 servings.
description: Creamy, comforting rustic potatoes with preparation: 5 minutes and cook: 20 minutes — the ultimate side dish that makes everything else on the plate better.
title: Onion Sauteed Potatoes

content:
Ingredients 2 85-gr pkg Ramen noodles 3 Tbsp vinegar (the ones w/the flavour packets) 1 Tbsp sugar 1 cup almonds 2 cups cooked chicken 2 Tbsp butter 2 green onions, chopped ½ cup oil 1 small cabbage, shredded Instructions Drop noodles into boiling water for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes. Drain and cool. Saute almonds in butter until toasted. Toss all ingredients together.
Sounds weird; tastes good!
description: Irresistible melt-in-your-mouth squares with pkg ramen noodles and vinegar — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Patty Bartlett’s Noodle Salad

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup margarine ½ cup corn syrup or honey ¾ cup peanut butter 1 tsp vanilla ½ tsp salat 1 lb or 3-3/4 cups icing sugar Instructions Mix first 5 ingredients and gradually add icing sugar. Spread in greased or buttered 8 x 8” pan, cut and refrigerate.
I haven’t tried this recipe yet but Lucia assures me it’s a prize-winner. And no cooking!
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with ½ cup corn syrup or honey and ¾ cup peanut butter. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Peanut Butter “sucre a La Crème” – Lucia Beaudoin

content: Ingredients 1 cup peanut butter 1 cup Rice Krispies 1 Tbsp butter or margarine 1 cup icing sugar ½ cup nuts (optional) 1 cup coconut so roll balls into Instructions Cream peanut butter, icing sugar and butter. Add Rice Krispies and nuts. Shape into balls and roll in coconut.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with peanut butter and rice krispies. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Peanut Butter Balls - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)

content:
Ingredients Mix ½” slice off 1# butter 1 cup icing sugar ¾ cup peanut butter ¾ cup chopped walnuts. Instructions Chill for some time; then make small balls. Return to freezer.
MELT: 4 squares semi-sweet chocolate and ½ block Para wax in double boiler.
DIP: chocolate balls in mixture and cool.
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with mix ½” slice off 1# butter and icing sugar. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Peanut Butter Chocolate Balls – Isabelle Rainville

content:
Ingredients 1 cup margarine, melted 4 cups icing sugar Instructions 1-1/2 cups peanut butter 1 cup graham wafer crumbs
Topping: 1 pkg semi-sweet chocolate chips
Mix the above ingredients together. Mixture will be rather dry. Spray cookie sheet with Pam, then press onto the cookie sheet. Melt 1 pkg of semi-sweet chocolate chips and spread on top. Chill for 10 minutes. Remove and cut into squares. Chill for about 2 hours before removing from cookie sheet.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with icing sugar. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Peanut Butter Cups (native Cuisine)

content: Ingredients 1 cup peanut butter ½ cup butter of margarine 12-oz pkg butterscotch chips ½ bag coloured mini marshmallows Instructions Melt first 3 ingredients. Cool and add marshmallows. Place in a lightly buttered 9”x 9”pan. Chill in refrigerator. Cut into 1 inch squares.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with peanut butter and ½ cup butter of margarine — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Peanut Butter Marshmallow Goodies

content: Ingredients ½ cup maple syrup ½ cup peanut butter ¼ cup sugar 3 cups Kellogg’s corn flakes ¼ cup brown sugar Instructions Combine first 3 ingredients and bring to a boil over moderate heat. Stir in peanut butter, Remove from heat and stir in corn flakes. Using a heaped tablespoon, drop onto greased cookie sheet. Chill until firm.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with ½ cup maple syrup and ½ cup peanut butter — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Peanut Crackers

content:
Ingredients Peel and remove seeds from cucumbers if desired and put through food chopper. Should make about 12 – 13 cups of cukes and onions mixed. Add 3 tbsp salt and let stand for 4 hours. Press water out – Mix 3 cups white sugar, ½ to 2/3 cup flour, ½ tsp cayenne pepper, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp dry mustard, 1-1/2 cups water, 2 cups vinegar and heat mixture to a boil. Boil on low for 10 minutes. Instructions NOTE: ½ tsp cayenne makes it pretty peppy! But Good!
Makes 6-1/2 pints.
description: Homemade rustic preserves with peel and remove seeds from cucumbers if desired and put through food chopper. should make about 12 – 13 cups of cukes and onions mixed. and add 3 tbsp salt and let stand for 4 hours. that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Pearl’s Cucumber Relish

content:
Ingredients 6-qt basket string beans (to make 16 cups) cut into 1” pieces 3 cups vinegar 3 cups brown sugar ½ cup dry mustard ½ cup flour (1/4 cup for thinner sauce) 1-1/2 tsp turmeric 1 tsp celery seed 1 tsp salt Instructions Wash, cut and cook beans in boiling salted water for 15 minutes. Drain.
Make a sauce of the other ingredients and add cooked beans. Cook for 5 minutes. Bottle and seal.
Yield: 6 Pints.
description: Crunchy, tangy robust pickles with qt basket string beans cut into 1” pieces and vinegar — brined to briny perfection. Open a jar and taste summer all year long.
title: Pickled Beans (nugget Cook Book)

content:
Ingredients 3 cups white vinegar 1-1/2 cups water 1-1/2 tsp salt 1 tsp pepper berries ½ tsp celery seed Instructions Boil vinegar solution for 10 minutes and cool. Hard-boil 1 dozen eggs, dash into cold water to cool rapidly and remove shells. Pack eggs in quart jars. Pour cold vinegar solution over to cover, then seal.
Let stand at least 2 days before using. These keep well at room temperature for a few days but should be refrigerated if kept more than 1 week.
description: Homemade rustic preserves with white vinegar and pepper berries that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Pickled Eggs

content: Ingredients 1 tin 213-ml tomato sauce ½ tsp dried basil ½ tsp dried oregano ½ tsp minced garlic ¼ tsp dried thyme Pinch of rosemary Pinch of dried dill Instructions In a bowl or a jar, combine all ingredients.
description: Homemade satisfying sauce with tin 213-ml tomato sauce and ½ tsp dried basil — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Pizza Sauce

content:
Instructions 1ST WEEK 4 quarts of dandelion flowers, cleaned of ALL their green 4 quarts of boiling water
2ND WEEK 4 oranges cut in ¼” slices 4 lemons cut in ¼” slices 1 cup of white raisins, finely chopped 6 cups of cane sugar 1 package of dry granulated yeast
Put dandelion blossoms in canner kettle and pour the boiling water over them. Let sand in a warm place for one week. Stir twice a day if possible.
At the end of the 1st week, strain the blossoms through a jelly bag, squeezing the pulp very dry to extract all of the liquid and flavor. Return liquid to the canner kettle and add the sliced oranges and lemons, and the raisins. Stir in the sugar; be sure to stir long enough to dissolve every grain. Sprinkle the dry granulated yeast over the surface. Set in a warm place to ferment for two weeks. Stir every day, inverting the fruit which rises to the surface. At the end of this two-week period, strain through several thicknesses of cheesecloth, and return to canner kettle to settle for two days more. When the wine has settled, siphon off carefully into clean sterilized bottles. Put corks in lightly until all fermentation is over. Fermentation has stopped when small bubbles no longer cling to the sides of the bottles.. Then tighten the corks securely and dip in hot paraffin. Let wine age at least six months; it is best at the end of a year. In aging dandelion wine, your resistance to the temptation of sampling will govern the quality of the wine. In the spring when dandelions start to bloom again, the wine will go through a slight fermentation in the bottle; it will become a little turbid and cloudy. Don’t be alarmed, fir it will settle back after the dandelions are through blooming.
description: Mix up this rustic beverage and watch it become everyone's favorite. Simple to make, impossible to stop sipping.
title: Plain Dandelion Wine

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp salt 2 cups white sugar ½ cup (8 tbsp) cocoa 2 tsp vanilla 1 cup oil 4 eggs ¼ cup cold water ½ cup (or more) chopped walnuts Instructions Put all ingredients in beater bowl in order given ending with walnuts and beat on low speed or by hand until smooth. Do not beat too long. Grease a 9 x 13 pan. Smooth batter in it and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes – no longer. Ice if desired.
description: A tried-and-true robust recipe with ½ cup cocoa and ¼ cup cold water that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Popular Brownie Recipe

content:
Ingredients Cook Time:- 1 Hr. &amp; 15 Min. Prep. Time:- 10 Min. 2 lbs. Pork loin or sirloin roast, boneless 1 Tbsp vegetable oil Salt &amp; Pepper 1 large onion, chopped 2 garlic cloves, minces 3 cups cranberry juice cocktail 2 – 19 oz/540 ml) cans white kidney beans 2 Tbsp tomato paste 1 Tbsp dry mustard ½ tsp thyme 1 bay leaf fresh parsley, chopped 1 cup fresh or frozen cranberries, chopped (optional) Instructions Cut pork into ¾” cubes. Heat oil in large Dutch oven over medium heat. Season Pork cubes with salt and pepper and brown. Transfer to a plate and set aside. Brown onions and garlic until soft, then add cranberry cocktail. Stir to scrape up browned bits; bring to a boil and simmer for 5 – 7 minutes to reduce liquid.
In a small bowl, mix together tomato paste, mustard and thyme. Add to onion and garlic mixture in Dutch oven, along with the Pork, white beans and the by leaf. Cover and bake in a 350 F oven for 30 minutes. Remove from oven and stir in cranberries, if using. Return to oven and bake 30 minutes longer.
Top with fresh parsley and serve with French Bread.
description: Rustic rustic stew loaded with cook time:- 1 hr. & 15 min., prep. time:- 10 min., and pork loin or sirloin roast, simmered until the kitchen fills with the most incredible aroma. Serve it with crusty bread and call it a perfect night.
title: Pork Cassoulet (from Metis Newspaper)

content:
Instructions This recipe is for microwave ovens but could be prepared in the same manner and used in a conventional oven.
1-1/2 cups mashed potatoes	½ cup chopped green pepper ¼ cup dry bread crumbs	¼ lb bacon slices, chopped 1 tsp dried mixed Italian herbs	1 14-oz tin tomatoes, drained Salt and pepper	1-1/2 cups grated cheese 1 small tin anchovy fillets(optional)	Few ripe olives (optional)
Mix together the mashed potato, bread crumbs and herbs. Season lightly and press into a round shallow glass pan. Mix together the onion, green peppers, bacon and cook in the microwave for 5 minutes (until vegetables are softened and bacon is lightly cooked). Arrange this mixture on the potato base. Top with drained tomatoes and grated cheese. Arrange the anchovy fillets in a lattice pattern on top and cook in microwave for 4 minutes or until heated thoroughly and the cheese has melted. Place an olive in each square of the pizza before serving.
description: Homemade rustic pizza loaded with simple, honest ingredients. Golden crust, melty cheese, and the satisfaction of making it yourself.
title: Potato Pizza (serves 4)

content: Ingredients 1/3 cup butter 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup brown sugar 1 egg, beaten 2 tbsp milk Instructions Melt butter, mix all ingredients and put in tart shells. Bake at 350 degrees until pastry is browned – approx. 30 minutes.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with brown sugar — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Prize Butter Tarts

content:
Instructions Prep. Time: 15 min. Total: 1 hour 35 Min.
1 pkg (2-layer size) yellow cake mix	2 cups cold milk 1-1/4 cup water	2 pkg (4-serving size each) Jell-o Lemon 1/3 cup sugar (optional)	Instant Pudding 2 Tbsp icing sugar (optional)
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Prepare cake mix as directed on package. Pour batter into greased 13 x 9-inch baking dish. Pour milk and water into large bowl. Add dry pudding mixes and granulated sugar, if using. Beat with wire whisk 2 minutes or until well blended. Pour over cake batter in dish. Place baking dish on baking sheet to catch dripping, as mixture does bubble. Bake 55 min. to 1 hour or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pan 20 minutes. (Mixture will thicken slightly as it cool.). Sprinkle with icing sugar, if using. Spoon onto serving dishes. Serve warm; garnish with raspberries, if desired. Makes 16 servings. Serving suggestions: There’s no need to reheat leftovers – this cake is also delicious as a cold treat. The lemon filling transforms from saucy and gooey to thick and custard-like. Yummy!
description: Homemade heavenly cake that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Pudding-cake (kraft Winter 2006)

content:
Ingredients 3-1/2 cups flour 2 tsp cinnamon 1 tsp cloves 1 tsp nutmeg 2-2/3 cups sugar 1 tsp salt 2 tsp baking powder ½ tsp baking soda 2 cups canned pumpkin 2.3 cup cooking oil 4 eggs – beaten 2/3 cups water 1 cup walnuts, optional Instructions Mix all dry ingredients. Add all wet ingredients and mix well together. Pour into 2 greased 2-pound load pans. Bake approx. 1 hour at 350 degrees F.
Makes 2 – 2 pound loaves.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with cinnamon and cloves that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Pumpkin Bread – Gail Leblanc

content:
Ingredients ½ cup brown sugar l tbsp pumpkin pie spice l tbsp all-purpose flour l/2 tsp salt 2 eggs l-l/2 cups pumpkin l-l/2 cups milk (can be part cream) Instructions Mix sugar, spice, flour and salt. Beat eggs until light and fluffy. Stir in pumpkin. Add dry ingredients, mixing well. Gradually stir in milk. Blend thoroughly. Bake in pie pastry in a 375 degree oven for 45 – 55 minutes.
NOTE: I mix this all together in the blender. Much easier and faster.
NOTE: If I don’t have pumpkin pie spice on hand, I use l-l/2 tsp cinnamon with l/2 tsp ginger and l/4 tsp ground cloves.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a melt-in-your-mouth filling with ½ cup brown sugar and l tbsp pumpkin pie spice — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Pumpkin Pie

content: Ingredients l-l/2 cups cooked brown rice 2 tbsp butter, softened 2 tbsp chopped onion l/4 tsp basil l egg, slightly beaten Instructions Combine ingredients. Press into bottom and sides of greased 9” pie plate. Use any quiche filling. Tuna tastes especially good in this crust.
description: Classic heavenly pie with l-l/2 cups cooked brown rice and chopped onion — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Quiche Crust (rice)

content:
Ingredients 1 14-oz Eagle Brand 1 pkg (4-oz) instant chocolate pudding mix 1 cup cold water Additional whipped cream 1 cup whipping cream, whipped Instructions In a large mixing bowl, combine milk, pudding mix and water. Beat until well mixed. Chill 5 minutes. Fold in whipped cream. Spoon into individual serving dishes and garnish with additional whipped cream.
Yield: 4 – 6 servings
description: Silky, rich pudding with eagle brand and instant chocolate pudding mix — pure comfort in a bowl. The kind of dessert that makes everyone feel like a kid again.
title: Quick Chocolate Mousse - Cw

content:
Instructions For hamburger, pork, chicken, etc.
2 oz catsup	6 oz sugar 8 oz water	2 tsp corn starch 2 oz vinegar
Boil first four ingredients until clear. Mix cornstarch in 2 oz water. Add to sauce to thicken.
description: Homemade satisfying sauce — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Quick Sweet and Sour Sauce

content:
Ingredients 1 quart chicken broth or stock 1 16-oz can tomatoes ½ cup chopped onion ½ cup chopped cabbage 1 10-oz pkg frozen mixed veggies, ½ tsp dried basil thawed 1/8 tsp pepper Dash sugar ¼ cup uncooked elbow macaroni 1 cup leftover cubed cooked chicken or roast beef Instructions In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, bring broth, tomatoes, onion, cabbage, vegetables and seasonings to a boil. Add macaroni and simmer 10 minutes or until tender. Add meat and heat through.
Yield: 6 servings.
description: A soul-warming bowl of savory soup with quart chicken broth or stock and can tomatoes — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Quick Vegetable Soup

content:
Instructions These decadent treats have soft coconutty centers, a touch of raspberry and a luscious layer of chocolate on top.
1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour	1/3 cup granulated sugar ½ cup butter	1 egg yolk 1/3 cup raspberry jam	1 can (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk	2 cups sweetened flaked coconut	5 oz semisweet chocolate 1 tbsp butter
In bowl, mix together flour and sugar, using pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in butter until mixture is roughly the size of peas. Stir in egg yolk, mix thoroughly.
Press into bottom of 9-inch square baking pan. Bake in 350 F oven for 18 – 20 minutes or until starting to brown. Spread jam over warm base. Combine condensed milk and coconut; gently spread evenly over jam. Return to oven, bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown and set. Let cool completely on rack.
In small saucepan, stir together chocolate with butter until melted. Spread over coconut layer, smoothing top with knife. Cover and refrigerate until chocolate is set. Let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes. Cut into squares. Makes 36 squares.
description: Sweet, satisfying melt-in-your-mouth squares . The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Raspberry Chocolate Squares

content:
Instructions INGREDIENTS 1 cup virgin olive oil ½ cup raspberry vinegar 2 tsp dry mustard 3 Tbsp dried , finely crushed onion flakes 4 Tbsp liquid honey (more or less to suit taste) Fresh herbs to taste (basil, dill, mint or your choice) Salt &amp; pepper to taste
DIRECTIONS Put ingredients in a bottle and shake well before using. Store dressing in fridge.
This vinaigrette is wonderful sprinkled on green salads or dribbled over steamed vegetables.
description: Creamy, zesty zesty dressing — whisk it together in minutes and taste the difference homemade makes.
title: Raspberry Vinaigrette (community Voices)

content: Ingredients 1 pint raspberries 2 cups champagne vinegar ¼ cup sugar Instructions Set aside ¼ cup berries. Place remaining in medium bowl. With fork, crush berries slightly. In medium saucepan, bring to a boil vinegar and sugar. Pour over crushed berries, cover and steep 48 hours. Place whole berries in sterilized bottles. Strain vinegar through cheesecloth, pour into bottles.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with pint raspberries and champagne vinegar — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Raspberry Vinegar

content:
Instructions If you like your summer salads light and refreshing, all you need to do s splash on a dash of raspberry vinegar. Sprinkle it straight from the bottle or use it as a base for Raspberry Vinaigrette.
INGREDIENTS: 5 cups apple cider vinegar (or red wine vinegar) 5 handfuls of raspberries, washed
DIRECTIONS: Put berries into bottle or jar. Heat vinegar until boiling point. Pour over raspberries, leaving about one inch head space. Cork or cap and set in a cool dark place for several days before using.
This allows vinegar to draw out the berry flavour. After setting time is up, you can strain the vinegar and discard well-squeezed berries, if you wish.
Keeps up to six months in refrigerator.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Raspberry Vinegar #2 (community Voices)

content:
Ingredients About ½ cup olive oil 2 large onions, sliced 2 large cloves garlic, minced 1 medium eggplant, cut in ½” cubes 6 medium zucchini, sliced thick 2 green or red peppers, seeded,cut in chunks About 2 tsp salt 1 tsp basil leaves ½ cup minced parsley 4 large tomatoes, cut in chunks Instructions Heat ¼ cup of the oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Add onions and garlic and cook, stirring until onions are limp but not browned. Stir in the eggplant, zucchini, peppers, 2 tsp salt, basil and minced parsley. Add a little of the oil as needed to keep the vegetables from sticking. Cover pan and cook over moderate heat (about 30 minutes). Stir occasionally, using a large spatula and turning the vegetables to help preserve their shape. If mixture becomes quite soupy during this time, remove cover to allow some of the moisture to escape.
Add the tomatoes to the vegetables in the pan and stir to blend. Add more oil if vegetables are sticking. Cover and cook over moderate heat for 15 minutes. Stir occasionally. Again, if mixture becomes quite soupy during this period, remove cover and allow moisture to evaporate. Ratatouille should have a little free liquid but still be of a good spoon-and-serve consistency. Add more salt if desired. Makes 8 – 10 servings.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with about ½ cup olive oil and large onions — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Ratatouille

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cups sugar 4 tbsp flour ¼ tsp nutmeg 1 tbsp butter 2 beaten eggs 3 cups cut-up rhubarb 1 unbaked pie shell Instructions Blend sugar, flour, nutmeg and butter. Add beaten eggs. Beat until smooth. Pour over rhubarb in a 9-inch pastry lined pan. Top with pastry if desired. Bake 10 minutes at 450 degrees, then reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake for 30 minutes.
NOTE: This is the original recipe which I copied. However, I bake this pie for about one hour at 375 degrees.
NOTE: Be sure to use a large deep pie plate. It has a tendency to run over.
description: Homemade sweet pie with ¼ tsp nutmeg and beaten eggs. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Rhubarb Cream Pie

content:
Ingredients 3 cups rhubarb ½ cup white sugar 1 tsp cinnamon Instructions Mix and place in bottom of buttered dish.
1 cup rolled oats	½ cup margarine ½ cup flour	1 cup brown sugar
Mix till crumbly and place on top of rhubarb.
Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.
Yum, Yum delicious!
description: A pan of indulgent squares with rhubarb and ½ cup white sugar that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Rhubarb Crisp

content:
Ingredients 1 egg, beaten 1 cup flour 1-1/2 cup white sugar 3-1/2 tsp baking powder 2 tbsp shortening ½ tsp salt ¼ cup milk Instructions Mix the above ingredients and put in 13 x 9 inch pan. Cover with 4 or 5 cups cut rhubarb. Sprinkle with 1 cup sugar and pour 2 cups boiling water over top. Bake at 350 to 400 degrees F. for 30 minutes (or until top is golden brown). Serve with whipped cream.
Note: I baked this for 45 minutes.
description: A pan of indulgent squares with ½ tsp salt and ¼ cup milk that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Rhubarb Dessert

content: Ingredients ½ cup melted butter or margarine 1 tsp baking powder 1 cup brown sugar, packed 2 cups quick cooking oatmeal ½ tsp vanilla Instructions Mix last four ingredients, add butter and mix. Place on cookie sheet – spread out so sheet is evenly. Bake for approximately 5 minutes at 350 degrees – or until mixture is bubbling. Remove and let sit for 5 minutes. Cut in squares.
description: Rich, chewy decadent squares packed with ½ cup melted butter or margarine, brown sugar, and quick cooking oatmeal. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Ribbon Squares

content:
Instructions Excellent with beef, turkey or ham. Can be reheated many times.
2 cups cooked rice	6 medium zucchini, sliced 2 large sliced onions	½ lb sharp cheese, grated 2 cans mushroom soup
Cook onion and zucchini over low heat until soft with butter of margarine in fry pan (cover and stir occasionally). Put a layer of rice, layer of vegetable mixture, some of the cheese and part of mushroom soup. Repeat in layers. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes, or until bubbly and top is browned. Serves 12
description: A bubbly, golden hearty casserole — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Rice and Zucchini Casserole

content: Ingredients ½ cup butter or margarine 10 cups miniature marshmallows 3 tsp vanilla extract (optional) or 80 regular (2 250g pkgs) 11 cups Rice Krispies Instructions In large saucepan over low heat, melt margarine. Add marshmallows. Stir until melted and well blended. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Add cereal, stirring until coated. Using lightly-buttered spatula, press into buttered 13 x 9 pan. Cool.
description: Classic heavenly pie with ½ cup butter or margarine and miniature marshmallows — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Rice Krispie Squares (large Quantity)

content:
Ingredients ¼ cup margarine or butter 5 cups miniature marshmallows (or 40 regular marshmallows) (250 gram pkg) l/2 tsp vanilla 6 cups rice crispies Instructions In large saucepan over low heat, melt margarine. Add marshmallows, stir until melted and well blended. Remove from heat Stir in vanilla. Add cereal, stirring until well coated. Using lightly buttered spatula, press into buttered 13 x 9 “pan. Cool.. Cut into squares. YIELD; 24 – 2”squares.
Microwave Directions:- In large microwaveable bowl microwave margarine at HIGH 30-60 seconds or until melted. Add marshmallows, tossing to coat. Microwave at HIGH 1 to 1-1/2 minutes or until smooth when stirred, stirring after 45 seconds. Follow Steps 2 and 3 above.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a melt-in-your-mouth filling with ¼ cup margarine or butter and miniature marshmallows — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Rice Krispies Squares

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 cup cooked rice 1 Tbsp butter 1 cup milk 1 tsp vanilla 2 eggs ½ cup raisins 1/3 cup sugar Instructions Combine rice, milk, eggs and sugar in double boiler. Heat until mixture coats metal spoon. Add remaining ingredients and let sit for 30 minutes.
description: A luscious indulgent pudding with cooked rice and ½ cup raisins — smooth, rich, and the perfect ending to any meal.
title: Rice Pudding, Mom’s Creamy – (hazelwood Cookbook)

content:
Ingredients 1 250-gr whipping cream ¼ - ½ cup of Carnation milk 1 full cup brown sugar ¼ cup flour Vanilla and nutmeg Instructions Mix all together at minimum speed and then at full speed until well blended.
Pour into uncooked pie shell and bake at 350 until “you decide it’s cooked”.
P.S. I have not made this yet but I understand it’s a prize winner. When I do make this pie, I will time it and enter it on this sheet.
description: Homemade luscious pie with whipping cream and ¼ - ½ cup of carnation milk. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Richard Belanger’s Sugar Pie

content:
Ingredients 1 lb butter 1 lg tin Carnation (whole milk) 1 tsp vanilla 1 kg brown sugar 1 kg icing sugar Instructions Mix well, Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring a lot. Boil for no more than 12 minutes. Take off heat. Add 1 kg icing sugar. Mix well and quickly pour into a well-buttered 9 x 13 pan INSTRUCTIONS AS WRITTEN BY RICHARD BELANGER:- In a big pot on stove, let melt 1 lb of butter. When melted, add 1 big can of Carnation and a little bit of vanilla.
Add 1 kg of brown sugar. Mix well and when it starts to boil good, lower your stove as long as it boils for 12 minutes only, not more.
Take off the stove and mix in with a wooden spoon 1 kg of icing sugar. Mix well, but finish with mixer at high speed. When you see that it is very smooth, put fudge in a big enough pan or a big one and small one.
description: Velvety luscious fudge with lg tin carnation and kg brown sugar — rich, dense, and indulgent. Cut into tiny squares because a little goes a long, long way.
title: Richard’s Excellent Fudge

content:
Ingredients 2 cups (12-oz pkg) chocolate chips 2 tsp vanilla 1 can Eagle Brand milk 2 cups peanuts 2 tbsp margarine 1 10-oz pkg mini marshmallows Instructions MELT: Chips with Eagle Brand and margarine over low heat, or in top of a double boiler. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and nuts. Cool to lukewarm.
Place marshmallows in a large bowl; fold in chocolate mixture. Spread in a waxed paper-lined 9”x 13”pan. Chill until firm, about 2 hours. Remove from pan and peel off waxed paper. Cut into squares.
description: Velvety heavenly fudge with chocolate chips and eagle brand milk — rich, dense, and indulgent. Cut into tiny squares because a little goes a long, long way.
title: Rocky Road Fudge

content: Ingredients 2 lbs soup bones 8 cups water (to start with) 1 cup barley ¾ tsp savory 3 large carrots, diced Pinch garlic salt 3 medium onions, diced ½ tsp oregano 4 stalks celery with leaves, diced 28-oz tin stewed tomatoes 2 tsp parsley Salt &amp; Pepper to taste Instructions Boil water and bones for about ½ hour. Add barley and spices; let boil for about 1 hour. Add more water, if needed. Add vegetables and tomatoes; let cook until vegetables are ready. Sit and enjoy.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with soup bones and barley. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Roly’s Barley Soup

content: Ingredients 2 cups orange Tang ½ cup dry lemonade mix 1 cup instant tea 2 tsp cinnamon ½ cup sugar 2 tsp cloves
description: A refreshing hearty drink with orange tang and ½ cup dry lemonade mix that's perfect for gatherings, holidays, or just a treat on a warm afternoon.
title: Russian Tea

content: Ingredients ¼ cup vinegar 1 or 2 Tbsp sugar ½ tsp salt ¼ tsp celery seed 1 large cucumber, thinly slice (about 3 cups) ½ cup thinly sliced onions Instructions Combine marinade ingredients. Add cucumber and onions and toss to coat. Cover and chill in fridge for four hours or up to five days. Stir occasionally. Enjoy!
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food with ¼ cup vinegar and or 2 tbsp sugar. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Ruth’s Marinated Cucumbers and Onions – (gail Leblanc)

content:
Ingredients 2 cups flour ¼ cup sugar 1 Tbsp baking powder 1 tsp salt ¼ cup oil or shortening ¾ cup milk Instructions Mix flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Cut in the oil; add milk and stir with a fork to make a soft dough.
Gather into a ball and knead several times until smooth. Roll out to ½” thick and cut with a small round cookie cutter ( about 2” diameter).
Bake at 420 F. for about 13 minutes (depends on your oven.)
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with ¼ cup sugar and ¼ cup oil or shortening that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Ruth’s Tea Biscuits - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)

content: Ingredients 1 can Eagle Brand milk 2 eggs 1 can vinegar salt and pepper 1 tsp dry mustard Instructions Mix 2 eggs, add Eagle Brand milk and dry mustard; mix thoroughly. Then add salt and pepper to taste. Mix thoroughly and add 1 can vinegar. This is an excellent dressing for potato salad and will keep in the fridge for quite a while.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with eagle brand milk and vinegar — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Salad Dressing

content:
Ingredients 1 tin 3-3/4 oz salmon 2 tbsp sweet green relish salt and pepper to taste 1 tbsp mayo Instructions HARD-COOKED EGG SANDWISH FILLING
4 hard-cooked eggs	½ cup mayo ½ tsp salt	2 tbsp green or red hot dog relish 1 tsp chopped green onion
description: Crunchy, tangy satisfying pickles with tin 3-3/4 oz salmon and sweet green relish — brined to briny perfection. Open a jar and taste summer all year long.
title: Salmon Relish Sandwich Filling

content: Ingredients For oven or BBQ MARINADE: 1 Tbsp brown sugar ½ tsp chopped dill 1 Tbsp Dijon mustard Juice of half a lemon Salt &amp; pepper to taste Half a lemon, thinly sliced 2 Tbsp soft margarine Aluminum foil 1/3 cup dry white or red wine Instructions Stir together brown sugar, dill, mustard, lemon juice, salt and pepper Brush over salmon and top each steak with lemon slices Brush margarine on half of two sheets of foil. On each sheet place 2 salmon steaks side by side and baste with wine. Wrap up and seal like a turnover. Cook either in your oven or on the BBQ at 375 F. for approx 15 minutes.
description: A robust comforting beef dinner with for oven or bbq and marinade: that's big on flavor and bigger on satisfaction. The entrée everyone remembers.
title: Salmon Steak “en Papillote” – (aboriginal Cooking)

content:
Ingredients 4 raw potatoes 3 Tbsp flour 1 can cream of mushroom soup or 2 to 4 Tbsp butter cream of chicken soup 2 cans of milk 1 onion Salt and pepper to taste Instructions Scrub, peel and thinly slice the potatoes. Place half of them in a thickly buttered baking dish, salt and pepper, and sprinkle with the flour. Add soup and onions, butter and the rest of the potatoes, salt and pepper and butter.
Pour the milk over all and bake in a 350 degree oven for 1-1.2 hours. Take the cover off 20 minutes before the time is up or until a fork goes into potatoes.
description: Rich, buttery comforting potatoes loaded with raw potatoes, cream of mushroom soup or, and to 4 tbsp butter. Comfort food doesn't get much more comforting than this.
title: Scalloped Potatoes – (elsie Smiley)

content: Ingredients 2 cans cream of celery soup 2 medium cooking onions, chopped ½ cup milk potatoes to fill a good-sized casserole dish Approx. l lb medium cheddar cheese Salt – approximately 1 tsp. Pepper to taste Instructions Peel and wash potatoes. Place alternate slices of potatoes and finely chopped onions in casserole. In a separate bowl, combine soup, milk, salt and pepper. Pour over potatoes. Grate cheese and sprinkle approximately ½ between layers. Top with remaining cheese and small bite-size pieces of butter, a dash paprika. Bake in a moderate oven at 350 degrees, covered, in casserole dish, for approximately 1 hour. Then remove cover and bake an additional ½ hour.
description: Crispy, golden satisfying potatoes with cream of celery soup and medium cooking onions — crunchy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and gone way faster than you'd expect.
title: Scalloped Potatoes, Cheezy

content: Ingredients 1 can 7-Up ½ bag marshmallows 1 large pkg lime Jello 1 19-oz can crushed pineapple 8 oz pkg cream cheese 1 pkg Dream Whip Instructions Dissolve over low heat marshmallows in one can of 7-Up. Add lime Jello and stir until dissolved. Add cream cheese and beat until smooth. Add pineapple. When partially set, beat Cream Whip and fold in. Mold and chill.
description: A vibrant zesty salad with ½ bag marshmallows and large pkg lime jello that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Seven-up Salad

content: Ingredients 1 cup flour 1 cup cornmeal 3 Tbsp white sugar 4 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 1 cup milk 1 beaten egg ¼ cup oil Instructions Mix. Put in hot greased frying pan. Lower heat and bake 20 – 25 minutes.
description: Soft, golden toasty rolls with cornmeal and beaten egg that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Shining Tree Corn Bread

content: Ingredients 1 tin Honeydew Melon or Strawberry Daiquiri 1 tin frozen orange juice 2 cups cold tea 12 oz Lemon Gin Instructions Mix. Freeze. Scoop out and mix with Gingerale, 7-up or Sprite.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with tin honeydew melon or strawberry daiquiri and tin frozen orange juice. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Slu8h

content:
Ingredients (1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans 1 Tbsp sugar ) ( 1 cup flour ½ cup butter ) 1st Part 1 large tub Cool Whip 1 cup icing sugar 8 oz (250 gr) cream cheese 1 small pkg instant chocolate pudding 1 small pkg instant vanilla pudding 4 cups milk (Separated) 1 Jersey milk chocolate bar Instructions Cream together first four ingredients: press in a 9” x 13” pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes. Cool well.
Cream icing sugar, cream cheese, and then add ½ of the Cool Whip. Spread over the bottom crust. Mix puddings in separate bowls with 2 cups of milk each. Place chocolate and then vanilla puddings in layers. Add second half of Cool Whip.
Then garnish with shredded chocolate bar on top.
description: A gorgeous rich dessert with (1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans and sugar ) that looks like it came from a bakery but tastes even better because it came from your kitchen.
title: Sonia’s Absolutely Fabulous Dessert

content:
Ingredients 1 cup dairy sour cream 1 cup granulated sugar 3 tbsp all-purpose flour ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla 1 egg 4 cups peeled, cored &amp; diced tart apples 1 9”unbaked pastry shell 1/3 cup packed brown sugar ½ cup all-purpose flour ¼ cup butter Instructions Beat together cream, sugar, flour, salt, vanilla and egg. Add apples. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in 375 degree oven for 25 minutes.
Mix brown sugar, ½ cup flour and butter - sprinkle on top. Bake 10 to 15 minutes more.
description: Homemade luscious pie with dairy sour cream and granulated sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Sour Cream Apple Pie

content: Ingredients 1 cup thick sour cream ½ cup sugar 1 cup raisin 1-1/2 tbsp flour 2 eggs, separated 1 tsp cinnamon Few grains nutmeg Few grains salt Instructions Mix together raisins, sugar, flour, salt and spices. Add to sour cream. Mix and add well beaten egg yolks. Whip egg whites until stiff; fold into mixture. Pour mixture into an unbaked pie shell. Bake in a moderate oven – 350 degrees – until filling is firm enough not to stick to a knife inserted into center of pie- about 50 minutes.
description: A beautiful sweet pie with thick sour cream and ½ cup sugar with a buttery crust and a filling so good you'll want to eat it by the spoonful.
title: Sour Cream Pie

content: Ingredients 3 eggs 1/3 cup melted butter or margarine 2/3 cups sugar 1 cup pecan halves 1 cup dark corn syrup 9-inch unbaked pastry shell Instructions Beat eggs thoroughly with sugar, dash of salt, corn syrup and melted butter. Add pecans. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 50 minutes or until knife inserted halfway between outside and center of filling comes out clean. Cool.
description: Classic heavenly pie with melted butter or margarine and pecan halves — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Southern Pecan Pie

content: Ingredients 2 pkg (3 oz ea.) raspsberry or strawberry- 2 cups boiling water flavoured Jello 3-4 cups frozen whole strawberries Whipped topping 1 prepared pie crust Instructions In a mixing bow, dissolve gelatin in water. Add frozen berries and allow to set a few minutes to thicken. When partially set, spoon into pie shell. Refrigerate. Garnish with whipped topping.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with raspsberry or strawberry- and boiling water — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Sparkling Strawberry Pie - Cw

content:
Ingredients ¾ cup sugar 2/3 cup vinegar 1/3 cup vegetable oil 1 tsp salt Dash pepper 1 15-1/2 oz kidney beans, rinsed &amp; 1 15-oz garbanzo beans, rinsed &amp; drained drained ½ medium onion, chopped 1 15-oz great northern beans, rinsed ½ cup chopped green pepper &amp; drained Instructions In a bowl, combine sugar, vinegar, oil, salt and pepper. Stir in all remaining ingredients. Let marinate until serving time.
Yield: 6 servings
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with ¾ cup sugar and vinegar that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Speedy Bean Salad - Cw

content: Ingredients ½ (12 oz pkg) semi-sweet chocolate chips ½ tsp salt 1 – 15 oz tin sweetened condensed milk ½ cup chopped nuts 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Melt chocolate over low heat stirring. Remove from heat and stir in sweetened condensed milk, salt, vanilla and nuts. Pour into a waxed paper lined 8”square pan. Spread evenly smoothing surface. Refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours. Turn candy out on board and peel off paper. With a sharp knife, cut fudge into desired size pieces.
description: Smooth, creamy decadent fudge with ½ semi-sweet chocolate chips and ½ tsp salt that melts on your tongue and sets perfectly every time. The candy thermometer's finest hour.
title: Speedy Chocolate Fudge

content:
Instructions Select small young beets 1” to 1-1/2” in diameter. Scrub thoroughly leaving root and 1” to 2” of stem. Cook until just tender. Dip in cold water and remove skins.
One 6-quart basket will yield about 12 cups of prepared beets. For this quantity, prepare the following syrup.
Combine in saucepan 2 cups vinegar (can be cider if desired) 2 cups sugar 2 cups water 1 Tbsp salt 1 tsp ground allspice
Bring to a boil and boil for 5 minutes. Keep syrup hot until needed. Pack hot sterilized sealers with prepared beets. Pour hot syrup over beets to completely cover them.
Seal, cool and store. YIELD: 6 pints.
description: Old-fashioned hearty pickles — crisp, tangy, and made the way grandma did it. Fill the pantry and count the days until they're ready.
title: Spiced Pickled Beets (purity Cook Book)

content:
Instructions To prepare dressing, whisk together 1/3 cup mayonnaise 2 Tbsp milk 2 Tbsp sugar 1 Tbsp white wine vinegar 1 Tbsp poppy seed 1/8 tsp salt
Combine in a bowl 8 cups baby spinach or torn romaine lettuce 2 cups sliced strawberries ¼ cup slivered almonds, toasted
Toss with dressing to coat. Serve immediately. Serves 8.
description: A bright, crisp fresh salad — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Spinach Salad W/strawberry & Poppy Seed Dressing

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cups brown sugar 1-1/2 cups white sugar 1 cup Carnation Evaporated Milk – undiluted Instructions Bring the Carnation milk and the sugars to a boil. Boil to 240 F., stirring continually till a soft ball stage is reached. (Dropping a few drops in cold water.) Let mixture cool slightly, then stir till mixture thickens – this happens quickly. Pour mixture in a buttered 8” square pan.
Cut into squares before it is completely cooled. ½ to ¾ cup of chopped nuts may be added before cooling.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with brown sugar and carnation evaporated milk – undiluted that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Sucre a La Crème

content: Ingredients 1 cup evaporated milk or cream 2 eggs 4 Tbsp flour 1 tbsp butter 2 cups brown sugar 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Beat eggs with milk. Add remaining ingredients and blend well. Pour into an unbaked 9-inch pie shell. Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 40 minutes
description: Homemade luscious pie with evaporated milk or cream and brown sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Sugar Pie

content:
Ingredients 1 lb each cashews, whole almonds and pecan halves ½ cup unsalted butter or margarine, melted 2 egg whites, lightly beaten 1 cup sugar 1 tbsp ground cinnamon Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees. In large bowl, combine nuts. In medium bowl, whisk together remaining ingredients, pour over nuts. Mix, spread on baking sheet. Bake 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until crisp. Cool before packing.
Makes 3 pounds.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with each cashews and ½ cup unsalted butter or margarine — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Sugared Nuts

content:
Ingredients These knotty tubers are the roots of a variety of the sunflower plant and were discovered by Champlain in 1605 in the gardens of the Indians in Cape Cod. They can be served raw or cooked. They are superb in salads and add an interesting touch to soups when thin slices are floated on top. Instructions They have a delicate, nutty flavour when served raw. They are an excellent diabetic food since they are high in food value and in the production of levulose sugars, which persons with diabetes can eat.
MASHED ARTICHOKES – Clean and peel artichokes. Boil or steam artichokes until tender. Mash and season with margarine, salt and pepper. The texture will be more moist and not as fluffy as mashed potatoes.
For a change, blend a cup of mashed artichokes with a cup of mashed cooked carrots, season to taste.
description: A delicious homemade comforting recipe with these knotty tubers are the roots of a variety of the sunflower plant and were discovered by champlain in 1605 in the gardens of the indians in cape cod. they can be served raw or cooked. they are superb in salads and add an interesting touch to soups when thin slices are floated on top. — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Sunchokes (jerusalem Artichoke) – (aboriginal Cooking)

content:
