Ingredients 1 pkg (350 gr) semi-sweet chocolate chips 1 can Eagle Brand milk 1-1/4 cup icing sugar pinch of salt 1 tsp vanilla ½ cup chopped nuts Instructions In heavy saucepan, over low heat and melt chocolate chips with Eagle Brand. Stir in remaining ingredients. Spread evenly in waxed paper-lined 8” square pan. Chill 2 to 3 hours or until firm. Turn fudge onto cutting board. Peel off paper. Cut into squares. Store loosely covered at room temperature. Makes bout ¾ pounds.
Microwave in 2-qt glass measure: combine chips with Eagle Brand. Microwave on full power (high) 2 minutes. Stir until chops melt and mixture is smooth. Stir in remaining ingredients. Proceed as above.
description: Velvety luscious fudge with semi-sweet chocolate chips and eagle brand milk — rich, dense, and indulgent. Cut into tiny squares because a little goes a long, long way.
title: Super Chocolate Fudge

content:
Ingredients ½ lbs (1-1/2 cups) blanches almonds ½ lb (2 cups) walnut halves 1 cup sugar dash salt 2 stiffly beaten egg whites ½ cup butter Instructions Toast nuts (325 degrees) until light brown. Fold sugar and dash of salt in egg whites. Beat until stiff, fold in nuts.
Melt butter on cookie sheet. Spread nut mixture in pan, baking at 300 degrees for about 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes until no butter remains. Cool. Makes about 4 cups.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with ½ lbs blanches almonds and ½ lb walnut halves — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Swedish Nuts

content:
Ingredients 1 cup shortening l cup brown sugar l egg l tsp vanilla l/4 tsp salt 2 cups all-purpose flour l/2 cup jam 2 tbsp icing sugar l/2 cup nuts, finely chopped Instructions Cream shortening and sugar until light and fluffy. Add egg, vanilla and salt and mix well. Add flour and combine. Place cookie batter on slightly greased cookie sheet. Spread and flatten until l/4”thick.
Baking time: l5 minutes at 350 degrees.
While giant cookie is still hot, spread thinly with jam and dust with icing sugar. Sprinkle with nuts. Cut immediately into small bars.
description: Flavorful, satisfying robust comfort food with l cup brown sugar and l egg. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Swedish Strips

content:
Ingredients Brine: 4 cups vinegar Onions 3 cups water Garlic 3 cups sugar Dill ½ cup pickling salt Green Cucumbers Instructions Slice onions (about 2 medium ones to 1 quart) and put 1 piece of garlic and dill in bottom of quart sealers. Then slice green cucumbers (about ¼” thick) into sealers. Bring brine (vinegar, water, sugar, salt) to a boil. Pour over cucumbers in jar and seal.
Enough for 5 quarts.
description: Old-fashioned hearty pickles with brine: 4 cups vinegar and onions — crisp, tangy, and made the way grandma did it. Fill the pantry and count the days until they're ready.
title: Sweet & Sour Polish Dill Pickles –(louise Chalifoux)

content: Ingredients Melt over heat: ½ cup corn syrup ½ cup peanut butter ½ cup brown sugar Add: 2 cups rice krispies 1 tbsp melted butter ½ cup peanuts Instructions Spread in 8”x 8”pan (slightly buttered). Ice with chocolate icing or melt semi-sweet chocolate chips and pour over bars.
description: Rich, chewy indulgent squares packed with melt over heat:, ½ cup corn syrup, and ½ cup peanut butter. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Sweet Marie Bars

content:
Ingredients 2 cloves of garlic 1 3-lb chicken 2 tsp dried tarragon ¼ cup white wine or apple juice 1 Tbs vegetable oil Instructions Cut 1 garlic clove in half. Rub cut sides on outside of chicken. Place garlic in chicken cavity. Sprinkle half of the tarragon in chicken cavity. Tie chicken with string and place in roasting pan.
Mince remaining garlic and combine with remaining tarragon, wine (or apple juice) and oil. Drizzle over chicken and roast in 350 F/180 C oven, basting frequently, for 1-1/4 hours or until juices run clear when chicken is pierced with a fork.
Makes 6 servings.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned robust chicken with cloves of garlic and chicken — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Tarragon Roasted Chicken – (aboriginal Cooking –gail Leblanc

content: Ingredients 3 cups brown sugar 2 eggs, beaten 2 tbsp margarine 3 tbsp flour 1 tsp vanilla 1-1/2 cups milk Instructions Mix together all ingredients and pour into a large uncooked pie shell or two small ones. Bake at 375 degrees for approx. 50 minutes.
description: Homemade luscious pie with brown sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Tarte Au Sucre (pauline Dupuis)

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 lb ground pork (lean or medium) ¾ cup water ¾ cup finely chopped onion ¼ cup finely chopped celery 2 cloves garlic, minced ¾ tsp salt ½ tsp dried savory ¼ tsp each thyme, pepper and ground cloves 1 bay leaf ½ cup mashed potato Pastry for double crust 9”pie Instructions In large heavy saucepan, combine pork, water, onions, celery and garlic. Cook over medium heat until bubbling, stirring to break up meat. Add salt, savory, thyme, pepper, cloves and bay leaf. Reduce heat, cover and simmer, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes. Remove bay leaf. Stir in mashed potato, mixing well. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed. Let cool, stirring occasionally (mixture will thicken as it cools). Line pie plate with pastry. Fill with meat mixture. Cover with remaining pastry, seal and crimp edges, cut a few steam vents. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 375 degrees and bake for 20 to 25 minutes longer or until golden brown.
description: Classic heavenly pie with ground pork and ¾ cup water — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: The Ultimate Tourtiere

content:
Instructions Many Aboriginal people know corn, beans &amp; squash as the “Three Sisters”. It is believed that these crops are watched over and kept safe by the spirits of the Three Sisters who are considered to be special gifts from the Creator and should be planted together, eaten together and celebrated together.
1 Tbsp vegetable oil	1 bay leaf l lb beef or wild meat	1 tsp dried basil 1 onion, chopped	6 cups beef stock * 1 cup carrots, diced	1 – 28 oz tin tomatoes 1 cup celery or fennel, chopped	3 cups chopped cabbage 1 cup acorn, butternut or hubbard	1 cup frozen corn squash, cubed	1 – 19 oz tin kidney beans, drained
Use 8 beef bullion cubes or sachets to make 6 cups of beef stock.
Cut meat into bite-sized pieces. Chop, dice and cube vegetables. To make squash easier to cut, pierce the whole squash with a knife and place it in the microwave. Cook on high for 5 minutes. Slice in half and spoon out the seeds. Peel and cut into small cubes. Turn on stove to medium heat. In a large saucepan heat oil and brown meat over medium-high heat. Add onions, carrots, celery and squash. Cook for 5 minutes. Add bay leaf, basil, beef stock, tomatoes and cabbage. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer until vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes. Add corn and kidney beans. Cook for another 5 minutes. Remove the bay leaf before serving
description: A soul-warming bowl of robust soup — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Three Sisters Soup – (from Take-5 Workshop by Lise Malette)

content: Instructions Place unopened can of evaporated milk in freezing compartment for 1 hour. Or pack in ice and salt. To whip, bowl and beaters must be ice cold.
description: Homemade rustic goodness — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: To Whip Evaporated Milk

content: Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour ½ tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt 1 cup Skor chips ¾ cup chocolate chips 1/3 cup sliced natural almonds 1/3 cup unsalted butter, melted ½ cup firmly packed light brown sugar 1 tsp almond extract ½ tsp vanilla extract 1 large egg Instructions Preheat over to 350 F. and coat an 8” pan with non-stick spray, or line with non-stick aluminum foil. Whisk flour, baking powder and salt together in a small bowl. Toss together toffee, chocolate chips and almonds in a small bowl. Put one cup of mixture into another small bowl and ½ cup into a third bowl. Whisk together melted butter, brown sugar and extracts in a medium bowl (or pot you melted butter in) until blended. Whisk in egg until well combined. Stir in flour mixture until a few streaks of it remain, then stir in first bowlful of toffee mixture until evenly combined. Spread in an even layer in baking pan and sprinkle top evenly with reserved 1 cup of toffee mixture. Bake until light golden brown around edges but soft in center, 30 minutes; a toothpick inserted in center should come out with wet batter attached. Sprinkle remaining ½ cup of toffee mixture evenly over top. Bake until toothpick inserted in center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, about 5 more minutes. Place pan on rack to cool completely. Cut into 16 bars. NOTE: Grease pan really well as this recipe tends to really stick, especially around the edges.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with ½ tsp baking powder and ¼ tsp salt. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Toffee Chocolate Chunk Almond Bars

content:
Ingredients 1 cup butter 1 cup brown sugar 2 cups flour 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup walnuts 1 cup chocolate chips Instructions Cream butter, sugar and vanilla. Stir in flour &amp; nuts and mix well.
Pat into an ungreased 9 x 13 pan. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 15 – 20 minutes, until lightly browned.
Remove from oven and sprinkle chocolate chips evenly over top. After about 5 minutes they’ll be soft – then spread evenly.
Cut in squares while still warm.
description: Homemade sweet candy studded with brown sugar, walnuts, and chocolate chips — prettier than store-bought and a hundred times more delicious.
title: Toffee Frost-ease

content: Ingredients 6 slices buttered bread 1 28-oz tin tomatoes ½ tsp salt 1 to 3 tbsp brown sugar ½ tsp dried basil 2 tbsp butter 2 tbsp or more chopped onion (or green onions) Instructions Cut the buttered slices of bread into 1”squares and dry them out in the oven at 250 degrees for about ¾ hr. Put half in bottom of large casserole (about 2-quart size). Melt butter in a saucepan and lightly sauté onions. Pour over bread cubes. Mix tomatoes, sugar, salt and basil and pour over bread. Top with rest of bread cubes, pushing them down a little. Bake uncovered at 350 degrees for 45 – 60 minutes.
description: Tangy, savory comforting sauce with slices buttered bread and tin tomatoes. Drizzle it, dip it, pour it — this one makes everything taste better.
title: Tomato Casserole - (cowboy Grub)

content:
Instructions The tuxedo of dessert bars, this dressed up sweet will become a holiday tradition at your house.
½ cup butter, melted	1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs 1 200-gr pkg flaked sweetened coconut	1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 1 350-gr pkg semisweet chocolate chips	½ cup smooth peanut butter
Combine butter with cracker crumbs in bowl; press evenly over bottom of 13 x 9 cake pan or glass baking dish lined with parchment paper. Top evenly with coconut, then condensed milk.
Bake in 350 F oven (or 325 F if using glass baking dish) for 25 minutes or until top is lightly browned. In small saucepan over low heat, stir together chocolate chips with peanut butter until melted. Spread evenly over hot coconut layer. Let cool in pan on rack for 30 minutes; refrigerate until chilled.
Cut into bars and remove parchment paper. (To make ahead, store bars in air-tight container at room temperature.) Makes 36 bars.
description: A pan of sweet squares that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Top Hat Triple-layer Bars

content:
Instructions If you don’t have your own favorite meat pie, here is a recipe you might like to consider
Pastry for a 2-crust 9”pie.
Filling: 1 Tbsp shortening	2 – 3 Tbsp boiling water 1 small onion, chopped fine	Salt and pepper ½ lb lean veal, minced	Celery leaves or parsley snipped ½ lb minced pork	Enough crumbs to thicken the meat
Method: Saute the onion in melted shortening until limp. Add pork, veal and water. Simmer 35 minutes or just until meat becomes very tender. Add salt, pepper, celery or parsley and crumbs. Bake at 375 degrees for about 45 – 50 minutes or until crust is nicely browned.
description: Classic heavenly pie — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Tourtiere

content:
Ingredients 4 eggs 1-2/3 cups granulated sugar 1 cup cooking oil 16 oz. Can pumpkin 2 cups all-purpose flour 2 tsp baking powder, ground cinnamon 1 tsp salt, baking soda Instructions In mixing bowl, beat together eggs, sugar, oil and pumpkin till light and fluffy. Stir together baking powder, cinnamon, salt and soda. Add to pumpkin mixture and mix thoroughly. Spread batter in 2 lightly greased 8” x8” pans.
Bake in 350 F oven for 25 minutes. Cool. Frost bars with your favourite cream cheese frosting.
description: Sweet, satisfying sweet squares with granulated sugar and cooking oil. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Uncle Paul’s Pumpkin Bars – (creation Unique)

content: Ingredients 1 lb ground beef ½ cup dry onion soup mix 1 can stewed tomatoes 1 tsp white sugar 8-oz can tomato sauce 1 cup diced potatoes 2 cups water 1 tbsp celery salt 10-oz pkg frozen mixed vegetables Instructions In Dutch oven or large pan, brown ground beef. Drain off fat. Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce, water, onion soup mix, vegetables, sugar and celery salt. Bring to boil; reduce heat, cover and simmer ½ hour. Add diced potatoes; simmer 20 minutes longer.
description: A soul-warming bowl of savory soup with ground beef and ½ cup dry onion soup mix — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Vegetable Burger Soup

content:
Ingredients 4 cups flour 1 tsp curry powder 3 tbsp salt ½ tsp poultry seasoning 1 tbsp pepper 1/16 tsp cloves 1 tbsp garlic powder 1/16 tsp turmeric 1 tbsp onion salt ½ tsp dry mustard 1 tsp mixed spice Instructions Mix well and store in a jar. For potatoes, beat 2 eggs with 1 tbsp of milk. Dip potatoes in egg mixture, roll in the prepared flour, then fry in oil until golden.
P.S. – I use this dry mixture to coat liver that I fry in bacon fat.
description: Crispy, golden hearty potatoes with curry powder and ½ tsp poultry seasoning — crunchy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and gone way faster than you'd expect.
title: Wedges (potatoes)

content:
Ingredients 6 cups finely shredded cabbage 1 cup finely shredded carrot 1 cup mayonnaise ½ cup milk ½ cup minced onion ¼ cup sugar ½ tsp salt 2 drops hot pepper sauce (optional) 1/8 tsp pepper ½ tsp celery salt Instructions Toss cabbage and carrot. Mix all other ingredients in a separate bowl. Toss with large fork until well coated. Refrigerate for at least 1-1/2 hr. before serving.
Makes one quart of coleslaw.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with finely shredded cabbage, finely shredded carrot, and mayonnaise. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Wendy Coleslaw Dressing

content: Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour 1 cup whole wheat flour 1 Tbsp granulated sugar 1 Tbsp baking powder ½ tsp baking soda ½ tsp salt ¼ cup soft margarine 1 cup blueberries 1 cup buttermilk or sour milk In bowl, combine all-purpose and whole wheat flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Using fingers, rub in margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in blueberries; add buttermilk and mix lightly. Drop by spoonfuls into 10 mounds. Bake in 425 degree (220 C) oven for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden. Makes 10 biscuits (5 g fat per biscuit)
description: Homemade toasty bread with whole wheat flour and granulated sugar that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Whole Wheat Blueberry Biscuits

content:
Instructions INGREDIENTS 2 handfuls of wild blueberries 2 single serving containers of about 6 oz of yogurt (vanilla or plain) 1 Tbsp of honey if plain yogurt is used 3 – 4 ice cubes
DIRECTIONS Blend well at high speed. Top with loose Wild Blueberries. Serve immediately. Serves 2.
description: Rich, chewy sweet squares packed with simple, honest ingredients. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Wild Bluebarry Smoothies (community Voices)

content:
Ingredients 1 cup wild rice 3 cups water ½ cup chopped onion 1 cup grated cheese ¼ tsp salt 3 cups sliced mushroom 3 Tbsp butter or margarine 1 – 19 oz tin stewed tomatoes Instructions Wash rice under cold water. Put the rice into 3 cups of water and boil for 5 minutes. Let soak in the same water for 1 hour. Cook rice in boiling water for an additional 20 minutes.
Drain rice if necessary. Saute onions and mushroom for about 5 minutes. Toss all ingredients in a 2-quart casserole and bake at 350 F. degrees for 1 hour.
description: Hearty rustic casserole packed with wild rice, ½ cup chopped onion, and grated cheese — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Wild Rice & Cheese Casserole – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc

content:
Ingredients 2 cups cooked wild rice 2 cups cream or 2% evaporated milk 6 eggs 1 tsp vanilla extract 1 cup blueberries ¾ cup maple syrup Instructions Whisk together the eggs, milk, vanilla and maple syrup. Over medium heat, stir together the wild rice and blueberries in a large sauce pan. Gradually add in milk mixture and stir well. Simmer over medium low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently until pudding thickens. Pour into bowls and cool slightly before serving. Serves 8
Tracy Bald is the Community Wellness Coordinator for the Georgian Bay Metis Council and she had this to say about this recipe. “This year I have been able to freeze a great deal of wild blueberries. I love to have them on hand for pancakes, oatmeal, and one of my favourites, wild rice pudding. I made this recipe for an event we held to honour our Elders back in June. The pudding was enjoyed by all and many asked for the recipe. I thought I would share it since I am not the only one that thinks it is delicious. Enjoy!
description: A luscious indulgent pudding with cooked wild rice and cream or 2% evaporated milk — smooth, rich, and the perfect ending to any meal.
title: Wild Rice Blueberry Pudding - (tracy Bald)

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup wild rice 2 chopped onions ¼ cup margarine or butter, melted ½ cup blanched almonds 1 cup sultana raisins 2 tsp poultry seasoing Salt &amp; pepper to taste Instructions Wash rice; soak overnight; in the morning drain; cook in boiling salted water until tender. Drain.
Saute onions in hot margarine or butter until golden brown. Add almonds, raisins, poultry seasoning, salt and pepper.
Lightly stuff fowl or game. Cover bird with oiled, heavy brown paper or foil paper, with no water, no cover and no basting, roast in 325 F. oven until done.
description: Fluffy, fragrant satisfying rice with wild rice and chopped onions — every grain perfectly cooked and packed with flavor. The side dish that steals the spotlight.
title: Wild Rice Stuffing for Game Birds – (by Gail Leblanc)

content:
Ingredients 2 28-0z cans best quality tomatoes (Drained 2 6-oz cans tomato paste 2 green peppers 4 medium cooking onions 6 lg outer celery stalks w/leaves ¾ cup sugar 2 tsp salt ¾ cup vinegar Spices tied in spice bag Instructions Spices tied in cheesecloth bag with long string: 12 whole cloves, 1 tsp mustard seed, 1 tsp celery seed, 3” cinnamon stick, ¼ tsp cayenne pepper (OR 2 Tbsp mixed pickling spices). Into rather heavy kettle measure the tomatoes from which excess juice has been drained to shorten boiling period. (Use juice for some other purpose.) Add the peppers, onions and celery that have been put through medium coarse knife of grinder. You will have to reverse the winding handle several times to unpack the celery strands that gather behind the knife. OR chop finely by hand on board. Add vinegar and salt and sugar and spices tied in bag. Bring to boil and stir very frequently to prevent sticking. Boil gently ½ hour. Add tomato paste and boil ½ hour longer. Remove spice bag. Pour into 3 sterilized pint jars and seal.
NOTE: The use of whole spices in a bag and addition of tomato paste give this chili sauce the good color and texture common to commercial chili sauce.
description: Bold, spicy savory chili loaded with z cans best quality tomatoes (drained 2 6-oz cans tomato paste, green peppers, and medium cooking onions — thick enough to stand a spoon in and packed with layers of smoky flavor.
title: Winter Chili Sauce – Using Canned Tomatoes

content:
Ingredients ¾ cup margarine 1-1/2 tsp Italian seasoning 1 16-oz pkg Quaker Oat Square Cereal 1-1/2 cups thin pretzel sticks ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese 1-1/2 cups peanuts Instructions Heat oven to 325 degrees. In 13 x 9” baking pan, heat margarine in oven until melted. Remove pan from oven. Stir in cheese and seasoning.
Add cereal, pretzels and peanuts, stirring until well coated. Bake 20 minutes, stirring occasionally during baking.
Cool. Store in tightly covered container. Makes approx. 11 cups.
description: A savory rustic snack with ¾ cup margarine and italian seasoning that vanishes by the handful. Make a big batch because a small one won't last.
title: Zesty Party Crunch

content:
Ingredients 2 cups thinly sliced zucchini 1 tomato, sliced thin 1 medium onion, thinly sliced 3 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese Salt and pepper to taste Instructions In greased 9 x 5 x 3 baking dish, layer half the zucchini, tomato and onions. Sprinkle with half the cheese; salt and pepper to taste. Repeat.
Bake uncovered in a 350 degree oven for 40 minutes. Cover and bake 20 minutes longer.
Makes 4 servings.
P.S. I always use much more vegetables than this as they really decrease in amount as they cook. Then I also alter the amount of cheese I use. This recipe is fool-proof. If you blow it it’s because you’re trying to.
description: Warm, satisfying robust casserole with thinly sliced zucchini and tomato that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Zucchini-tomato-onion Casserole

content:
Submitted by Isabelle Dorval
Friend of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1/3 cup rice vinegar 8 tsp reduced sodium soy sauce 2 tbsp sugar 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced 1 clove garlic, minced 1/2 tsp sesame oil 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes 4-3 oz boned and skinned chicken breast halves 2 cups broccoli florets 2 carrots, cut into 1 1/2 inch long matchsticks 1 cup (6 ozs.) pitted prunes, snipped into halves 3 green onions, sliced 4 lettuce leaves Instructions To make dressing/marinade, in medium bowl whisk vinegar, soy, cilantro, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and pepper flakes. Remove 1/4 cup of mixture into a shallow 8 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts in baking dish. Turn to coat both sides. Let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.
Place remaining ingredients, except lettuce, in bowl with dressing. Toss to coat completely. Let stand 30 minutes. Grill or broil chicken, basting with marinade, for about 10 minutes until chicken juices run clear, turning once. Cut each breast
into 1/4 inch slices across the grain. To serve, line each of 4 plates with 1 lettuce leaf. Top with vegetable mixture, dividing equally. Place 1 sliced chicken breast on each plate. Makes 4 servings.
Nutritional analysis per serving:
Calories: 276
gr of fibre 6mg
Grams of fat: 4
sodium 474
Mg of Cholesterol: 46 %
calories from fat 12
description: A bright, crisp fresh salad with rice vinegar and reduced sodium soy sauce — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Zesty Asian Chicken Salad

content:
Submitted by Emily and Robert Desbois
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients 1 lemon Jello
1 can tomato soup
1 small cream cheese
3 sticks celery, chopped
1 medium onion, chopped
¾ cup almonds, chopped
1 cup Miracle Whip
Instructions Heat Jello, soup and cream cheese until melted. Add other ingredients. Put green peppers on top if desired.
Place in fridge. Make a day ahead.
description: A soul-warming bowl of savory soup with lemon jello and tomato soup — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Tomato Soup Salad

content:
Submitted by Patti and Darren Henri
Cousins of the Bride
Spinach, washed and torn 1 pint of fresh strawberries, washed and sliced ½ cup slivered almonds Dressing:
Ingredients ¼ to ½ cup of sugar 2 tbsp sesame seeds 1 tbsp poppy seeds ¼ tsp Worcestershire sauce 1 ½ tsp chopped onion ¼ tsp paprika ½ cup vegetable oil ¼ cup cider vinegar Instructions Make the dressing the day before. Pour over salad, toss and serve.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with spinach, pint of fresh strawberries, and ½ cup slivered almonds. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Spinach Salad with Strawberries

content:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau
Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
2 cups cooked rice, cooled 1 ½ cup fresh, chopped spinach 3 stalks celery, cut diagonally ¼ cup raisins ½ cup green peppers, chopped ½ cup red peppers, chopped 3 sprigs fresh parsley ½ cup green onions, chopped 1 cup fresh, sliced mushrooms 2 cups fresh (not canned) bean sprouts ½ cup cashews Dressing:
Ingredients ¼ cup soya sauce ½ cup oil 2 garlic buds (or more if you like) Instructions Put dressing in a jar and add raisins. Let stand overnight in refrigerator. Mix all ingredients together and pour dressing. Let sit in refrigerator for one hour before serving.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with cooked rice, ½ cup fresh, and stalks celery. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Salade d'Amour

content:
Submitted by Lynn Sayer
Cousin of the Bride
2 cups cooked and cooled, Uncle Ben’s Converted Brand rice 3/4 cup English cucumber, cut into slices 3/4 cup tomatoes, diced 3/4 cup green and red peppers, diced 1/3 cup red onion, diced 1 cup Feta cheese, cut into large dice 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted Dressing:
Ingredients 1/3 cup olive oil 1/4 cup red wine vinegar 1 garlic, minced (about 1 tsp) 1 tsp rubbed oregano 1 tbsp lemon juice 1/2 tsp salt 1/2 tsp pepper Instructions In a large bowl, combine cooked rice with all diced vegetables, diced cheese and olives.
In another bowl, combine dressing ingredients. Pour dressing over rice/vegetable mixture. Toss well and refrigerate.
Salad may be served over a bed of mixed greens or as a side dish. Yields 6-1 cup servings.
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with cooked and cooled and english cucumber that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Rice Greek Salad

content:
Submitted by Deanna and Larry Shank
Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
Ingredients 1 can crushed pineapple 1 small package of lime Jello 1 cup whipping cream 1 cup chopped celery 6 ozs cream cheese, room temperature 1 cup chopped nuts Instructions Heat pineapple. Dissolve lime Jello in pineapple. Stir well. Set in refrigerator until partially set.
Whip cream. Beat in cream cheese. Add nuts and celery. Add cream mixture to pineapple mixture.
Set in refrigerator for about 3 hours before serving.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with crushed pineapple and small package of lime jello — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Party Lime Salad

content:
Submitted by Billy Bell
Groom
Dressing:
Ingredients ¼ cup oil ¼ cup vinegar 2 ½ tbsp sugar ¼ cup concentrated orange juice 2 cloves of garlic 1 tsp crushed red pepper ½ tsp basil black pepper salt Instructions Cook choice of pasta, drain and add the dressing. Add crushed feta cheese, parmesan cheese, grapes (red and green), red and green peppers, olives and celery.
Chill and serve.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with ¼ cup oil and ¼ cup vinegar — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Pasta Salad

content:
Submitted by Ellie and Mike Bell
Cousins of the Groom
1 green pepper 1 red pepper 2 green onions 2 celery stalks ¼ lb mushrooms ¼ lb spinach (1 bag) 1 cup cooked rice 4 ozs cashew nuts, optional 1 tbsp fresh parsley Dressing:
Ingredients ½ cup cooking oil ¼ cup soya sauce 1 garlic clove, minced Instructions Marinate dressing for one hour before using. Add to above salad 5-10 minutes before serving.
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with green pepper and red pepper that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Lovely Salad

content:
Submitted by Bev Bell and Lorne Mick
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
1 medium cabbage 1 medium onion 2 grated carrots 1/2 green pepper, optional 1/2 red pepper, optional 1 stick of celery, optional Dressing:
Ingredients 2/3 cup oil 1 cup white vinegar 2/3 cup sugar 2 tsp salt celery salt, a little honey and chopped garlic are optional Instructions Chop, shred, grate, hack the veggies into a bowl. Bring oil, vinegar, sugar and salt to a boil, stirring to dissolve. Cool a little and pour over veggies. Tip the bowl to allow dressing to run over cabbage, etc.
Refrigerate for a few hours. Keeps for at least a week in the fridge.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with medium cabbage and medium onion — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Good Coleslaw

content:
Submitted by Sandra and Steve Pigeau
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 1 lb ground beef, browned 1 large bag of Doritos, crushed into small pieces 2 cups grated cheddar cheese 1 medium onion, diced 1 green pepper, diced 1 tomato, diced 1 small head of lettuce, chopped 1 bottle of Kraft Catalina dressing Instructions Mix all ingredients together. If done ahead, do not add the Doritos or dressing until served.
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with ground beef and large bag of doritos that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Easy Taco Salad

content:
Submitted by Tracey and Pierre Beaudry
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride
1/2 cup raisins 1/2 cup shelled sunflower seeds 1 medium red onion, chopped 1/2 lb crisp, crumbled bacon 3 heads broccoli florets Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.
Dressing:
Ingredients 1/2 cup Miracle Whip 2 tsp rice vinegar 1/3 cup white sugar Instructions Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.
description: Crunchy, refreshing crisp salad tossed with raisins, shelled sunflower seeds, and medium red onion. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Broccoli Salad

content:
Submitted by Julie and Dave Pigeau
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 1 head of lettuce, chopped 3 ribs of celery, chopped 1 medium onion, chopped 2 cups partially thawed peas 2 cups Hellman&rsquo;s (must be Hellman&rsquo;s mayonnaise) 2 tbsp white sugar ¼ cup parmesan cheese bacon bits Instructions In a rectangle pan, layer all ingredients beginning with lettuce, celery, onions, and peas. Spread mayonnaise over top. Sprinkle with sugar, then parmesan cheese. Put in the fridge for 24 hours. Sprinkle with bacon bits just before serving.
description: A vibrant garden-fresh salad with head of lettuce and ribs of celery that brings crunch, color, and flavor to any table. This one earns its spot next to the main course.
title: Best Damn Salad

content:
Submitted by Naz and Brian Lloyd
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 3 ½ lbs of skinless chicken (cut up 2 whole chickens) 1 ½ cup rice 1-540 mL can black-eyed peas 1-400 mL can coconut milk 3 tbsp butter 3 tbsp soya sauce 2 tbsp vegetable oil 1 tsp basil 3 cloves of garlic, minced 1 large onion, minced pinch of black pepper salt to taste Instructions Wash chicken and cut into pieces (leave bones in). Place chicken in a large bowl. Blend in onion, garlic, soya sauce, basil, pepper and salt.
Heat oil in a stewing pot and add seasoned chicken. Stir occasionally until all pieces are browned. Stir in dry rice and cook for 3 minutes. Then, add peas, coconut milk and enough water to cover the entire mixture.
Simmer until rice is loose and stir in butter. Serve with your favorite pepper sauce.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with ½ lbs of skinless chicken and ½ cup rice — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: "Cook Up" Rice

content:
Submitted by Lori Girard
Friend of the Bride
Ingredients 1 can of tomatoes, or stewed tomatoes one onion, cut in long slivers 1 pkg beef bouillon ¾ cup water basil, garlic, salt and pepper Instructions Fry onion. Add other ingredients. Thicken sauce with corn starch. Fry breakfast sausage. Slice sausage and add to sauce.
Pour sauce with sausage over white rice.
description: Savory rustic rice studded with of tomatoes, one onion, and beef bouillon — simple, satisfying, and the perfect base for whatever else lands on the plate.
title: Sausage and Rice

content:
Submitted by Linda Desrochers
Cousin of the Groom
Ingredients 2 cups of Uncle Ben’s converted rice 4 cups water ½ cup soya sauce ½ cup oil 1 pkg onion soup mix 1 can drained mushrooms 1 can drained peas Instructions Mix all ingredients in roast pan. Place in oven for one hour at 350 degrees.
description: A bubbly, golden hearty casserole with of uncle ben’s converted rice and ½ cup soya sauce — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Baked Rice

content:
Submitted by Derek Forgie
Friend of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients Macaroni Cheese Dust Instructions Fill a metal container with a water-like substance. Find a way to make it hot enough for the water to make a boiling noise. Place several noodles into the noisy water.
Stir the noodles with a pointy or rounded stick until they become limpanized. Drain the leftover water into a plant that looks cold and thirsty.
Place cheese dust evenly onto noodles. Stir until drowsy. YIELD!
Eat dish while humming the A-Team theme song. Serves 30.
description: Tender pasta tossed with macaroni and cheese dust in a bold sauce that coats every strand. This is the dinner everyone asks for twice in one week.
title: Poor Man's Pasta

content:
Submitted by Marleen and Bill Tregenza
Godparents of the Groom
Ingredients 6 red bell peppers 14 ozs fresh mushrooms 8 ozs sausage, remove from casing 2 tbsp olive oil 1 tbsp oregano 1 tbsp thyme 2 garlic cloves ½ cup fresh parsley grated parmesan cheese salt and pepper Instructions Heat oven to 400 degrees. Cut peppers into ½ inch diagonal pieces. Place in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Drizzle with 1 tbsp of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste. Bake until charred (45 minutes), stir occasionally. Remove.
Finely chop parsley, oregano, thyme and garlic. Heat remaining oil, add sliced mushrooms and cook until golden brown. Add herb mixture. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and sauté.
Crumble sausage and cook until browned. Cook pasta. Set aside ¼ cup of pasta cooking liquid. Combine sausage, veggies and herbs.
Serve on pasta. Sprinkle with cheese and pasta cooking liquid.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with red bell peppers and fresh mushrooms. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Penné with Peppers, Mushrooms, and Sausage

content:
Submitted by Sue and Don Lloyd
Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
Ingredients 4 hot or medium Italian sausages 2 cups penné or corkscrew-shaped pasta 1 tsp vegetable oil 1 large onion, chopped 3 gloves garlic, minced 1-28 oz can of tomatoes, coarsely chopped 2 tsp dried basil 1 tsp dried oregano ½ cup fresh parsley, chopped pepper 2 tbsp freshly grated parmesan cheese 2-3 pinches of crushed red pepper Instructions In skillet, cook sausage over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes. Drain well. Cut into thin, round slices. Cook pasta until tender but firm. Drain and return to pot.
In non-stick pan, heat oil over medium heat. Cook onion until tender, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, tomatoes, basil and oregano. Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes. Add cooked sausage. Pour over pasta. Toss to mix. Sprinkle with parsley, pepper and parmesan, toss again.
Makes 6 servings.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with hot or medium italian sausages and penné or corkscrew-shaped pasta — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Penné with Italian Sausage, Tomato, and Herbs

content:
Submitted by Christine Shank
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 1 boiling fowl (5 ½ - 6 lbs) a few stalks of celery, chopped 3 small onions, chopped 1 quart milk ¼ lb butter 4 ozs hard flour 2 cups chicken stock 1 lb spaghetti 1 lb mushrooms ½ cup parmesan cheese salt and pepper to taste Instructions Boil fowl. Take meat from bones and put stalks of celery, onions and salt and pepper to taste into chicken stock. Simmer.
Make roux of butter and flour in saucepan. Bring milk to boiling point in double boiler. Thicken with roux. Add 2 cups strained chicken stock.
In separate pot, boil spaghetti in salt water until cooked. Wash in cold water. In separate pan, sauté washed and sliced mushrooms.
Grease good-sized casserole dish with butter and fill as follows in layers: one of spaghetti, one of mushrooms, one of chicken meat, one of strained celery/onions, one of white sauce. Repeat until casserole is filled, ending with white sauce with a topping of parmesan cheese (ordinarily, 2 layers of everything, ½ and ½).
Bake in 375-400 degree oven until top is golden brown. Can be made ahead and frozen.
description: Juicy, flavorful robust chicken with boiling fowl and a few stalks of celery — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Chicken Tetrazzini

content:
Submitted by Allison and Dave Rudback
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride
Ingredients 2 tbsp olive oil, divided 2 boneless chicken breasts, cubed 1 carrot, sliced diagonally 1 cup broccoli florets 2 cloves of garlic, minced 12 oz angel hair pasta 2/3 cup chicken broth 1/4 cup parmesan cheese 1 tsp dried basil Instructions Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a medium sized skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towel.
Heat remaining oil in same skillet. Begin heating water for pasta. Add carrots to skillet, stir while cooking for 4 minutes. Add broccoli and garlic to skillet, stir while cooking for 2 minutes longer.
Cook pasta according to package directions. While pasta is cooking, add chicken broth, basil and parmesan to skillet. Stir to combine. Return chicken to skillet. Reduce heat and simmer for 4 minutes.
Drain pasta and place in a large serving bowl. Top with chicken and vegetable mixture. Serve immediately.
description: A comforting bowl of hearty pasta with olive oil and boneless chicken breasts — saucy, satisfying, and ready to become a weeknight staple.
title: Angel Hair Pasta with Chicken

content:
Submitted by Heather Bell
Bride
Ingredients 1 ½ cup brown sugar 1 ½ tbsp flour Instructions Place the above ingredients in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of canned milk. Cook over low heat, stirring until dissolved. Remove from heat. Slowly add 1 beaten egg, stirring constantly. Pour into an 8 inch, unbaked pie shell. Bake at 400 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 300 degrees and continue baking for 30 minutes, or until thick.
description: Homemade luscious pie with ½ cup brown sugar and ½ tbsp flour. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Sugar Pie

content:
Submitted by Paulette and Mike Shank
Cousins of the Bride
In a large bowl, combine:
1 ½ cup warm water 1 tbsp yeast Let sit for 10-15 minutes. Mix well, then add:
Ingredients 1 tbsp oil 2 tbsp brown sugar 3 cups flour (+1 cup if needed) 1 tsp salt Instructions Mix well and knead to form a ball. Cover and let rise in oven (heat off) for 1 hour.
Divide in two. Roll out. Place on a greased pizza pan. Garnish with your favorite toppings. Bake at 425 degrees for 12-15 minutes.
Makes 2 large pizzas.
description: Crispy, bubbly savory pizza with ½ cup warm water and yeast — homemade crust, bold toppings, and the kind of flavor that puts delivery to shame.
title: Pizza Dough

content:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients 5 cups flour 1 tsp baking powder ¾ tsp salt 3 tbsp brown sugar 1 lb shortening 1 egg, slightly beaten cold water 2 tbsp vinegar Instructions Sift flour, baking powder and salt together. Add brown sugar. Cut in shortening until fine as meal. Slightly beat egg in measuring cup. Add cold water until egg and water mixture measures ¾ cup. Add vinegar. Drizzle egg mixture over flour mixture and stir with a fork until it is well mixed.
Divide into 8 equal portions This pastry can be used for dessert pies or meat pies.
description: A tried-and-true robust recipe with ¾ tsp salt and brown sugar that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Never Fail Pastry

content:
Submitted by Del Gosson
Aunt of the Bride
Filling:
3 lbs of beef, or cheaper cut of steak 2 lbs of pork, remove fat 2 double chicken breasts, cooked for about 20 minutes 3 large onions 1 tbsp salt 1 tsp pepper ½ cup flour Pie Crust:
Ingredients 1 lb of shortening Instructions Follow pastry recipe on shortening box. Add 2 extra cups of flour and enough water to hold the pastry together.
Finely chop all of the meat. Put in a pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil . Add 2 chopped onions and pepper. Let simmer for about 2 hours. Add the last chopped onion and the salt, simmer for another hour.
Mix flour and water in a separate bowl. Add to meat mixture until thick.
Let cool overnight. Prepare pie crust and fill pies or turnovers. Will make 20-25 turnovers. Bake at 375 degrees for about ½ hour.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a indulgent filling with of beef and of pork — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Meat Pies

content:
Submitted by Gisele and Ronnie Roy
Cousins of the Bride
18-20 graham wafers ¼ cup butter, melted ¼ cup shortening, melted ¼ tsp cinnamon Crush and roll wafers fine. Blend in butter, shortening and cinnamon. Line bottom and sides of a 9 inch pie plate. Reserve 1/8 cup for topping.
Filling:
Ingredients 4 eggs ½ cup sugar 1 ½ tbsp cornstarch 2 cups milk 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Separate eggs. Beat yolks until lemony in color. Add sugar and cornstarch and blend well.
Add milk and cook over direct heat, stirring constantly. When thickened, take off of heat and add vanilla.
Beat 4 egg whites until stiff. Add 8 tablespoons of sugar, 2 spoons at a time. Continue to beat.
Pour filling into pie shell. Spoon meringue over top and sprinkle with wafer mixture.
Bake at 250 degrees for 20 minutes. Cool well before cutting.
description: Homemade indulgent pie with graham wafers and ¼ cup butter. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Flapper Pie Special

content:
Submitted by Charleen and Jim Martin
Cousins of the Bride
Requires 2 uncooked pie shells.
1 large pkg cream cheese 1 egg ½ cup white sugar 1 tsp vanilla Beat the above ingredients together and spread in bottom of pie shells.
Pumpkin Layer:
Ingredients 2 cups pumpkin, canned 2 beaten eggs ½ cup sugar 1 tsp cinnamon 1 cup milk ½ tsp ginger Instructions Mix ingredients and spread carefully over cream cheese layer in pie shells.
Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes at bottom of oven. Continue cooking for 35 minutes at 350 degrees (or until pumpkin is set).
Garnish with whipped cream or crushed peanut brittle.
**The edges of the pies can be wrapped with foil to prevent the pastry from getting too dark before the pumpkin is cooked.
description: Homemade luscious pie with large pkg cream cheese and ½ cup white sugar. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Deluxe Pumpkin Cream Cheese Pie

content:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients 4 VERY large Granny Smith apples (6 cups) 2/3 to 1 cup white sugar, depending on tartness of apples 1 tsp cinnamon 1/8 tsp salt 1 tbsp cornstarch Instructions Prepare the “Never Fail Pastry” on page 92. Use 2 portions.
Mix sugar, cinnamon, salt and cornstarch together. Peel and thinly slice the apples. Toll out one portion of the pastry and line the bottom of a 9 inch pie pan. Place alternate layers of apples and sugar mixture in the pie pan. Roll out the second portion of pastry, cut vents and place top crust in position. Seal edge and flute.
Bake in a pre-heated, 400 degree oven for 40-50 minutes or until pastry is golden brown and apples are tender.
description: Classic heavenly pie with very large granny smith apples and to 1 cup white sugar — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Apple Pie

content:
Submitted by Murdena and Robert Burton
Cousins of the Groom
Ingredients 1-2 steaks 1-14 oz can of tomatoes 1 can mushroom pieces 1 can beef consommé 1 small onion, chopped salt, pepper and garlic salt to taste Instructions Brown steak and onion. Season with salts and add remaining ingredients in slow cooker.
Simmer for 2-3 hours.
description: Hearty, satisfying hearty beef with steaks and can of tomatoes — the kind of meal that brings everyone to the table before you've even finished plating.
title: Swiss Steak Slow Cooker

content:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell
Parents of the Groom
Ingredients 1 small onion, minced ¼ cup crushed crackers (or bread crumbs) 1-8 oz tin tomato sauce ¼ cup brown sugar, packed ¼ cup vinegar 1 tsp prepared mustard 1 egg 2 lbs ground chuck 1 ½ tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper Instructions Mix tomato sauce with sugar, vinegar and mustard until sugar is dissolved. Put ½ cup of sauce mixture aside.
With fork, slightly beat egg. Add onion, crackers, chuck, salt, pepper and remaining tomato sauce mixture. Combine lightly, but thoroughly.
Place in loaf pan and cover with the ½ cup of sauce mixture that was put aside.
Bake at 400 degrees for 45 minutes.
description: Hearty, satisfying hearty beef with small onion and ¼ cup crushed crackers — the kind of meal that brings everyone to the table before you've even finished plating.
title: Sweet 'n' Sour Meatloaf

content:
Submitted by Billy Bell
Groom
2 lbs hamburg 1 cup milk 1 tsp salt 1 cup bread crumbs 2 eggs Knead all ingredients together. Form meatballs and place on a cookie sheet. Cook at 350 degrees until brown.
Sauce:
Ingredients 1 ½ cup brown sugar 1 tsp dry mustard ¾ cup ketchup ¾ cup water ¾ cup vinegar Instructions Mix ingredients together in a sauce pan. Bring to a boil. Mix
1 ½ teaspoon of corn starch with water and add to boiling mixture.
Place meatballs in large casserole dish or roast pan and pour sauce over top. Cook at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
description: Hearty, satisfying hearty beef with hamburg and bread crumbs — the kind of meal that brings everyone to the table before you've even finished plating.
title: Sweet and Sour Meatballs

content:
Submitted by Kara MacDonald
Friend of the Bride
Ingredients 2 tbsp butter ½ pkg mushrooms, diced 1 red pepper 1 envelope of Lipton onion soup mix 1 cup bread crumbs ½ tsp pepper 4 thick pork chops Instructions Combine vegetables and butter in sauce pan and cook until mushrooms are done. Add the soup mix, bread crumbs and pepper. Allow the mixture to cool.
Cut the pork chops on the side and make a pocket on the inside of the pork chop. Do not remove excess fat from the pork chops. Stuff the pockets with the above mixture.
Arrange in low sided cookie sheet and cook for 45-60 minutes at 350 degrees. Serve with noodles or rice.
description: Savory, golden comforting pork with ½ pkg mushrooms and red pepper that's ridiculously easy to make and even more ridiculously delicious to eat.
title: Stuffed Pork Chops

content:
Submitted by Pauline and Jim Cunningham
Friends of the Groom’s Family (Uncle Jim)
Stuffing:
Ingredients 1/4 – 1/2 cup salt pork, diced 5 apples, diced 1/2 cup celery, chopped 1/3 cup sugar 1/2 cup onions, chopped 2 cups dried bread crumbs 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped Instructions Fry salt pork until crisp. Remove from skillet. Cook celery, onion and parsley in fat for 5 minutes. Remove from skillet. Place apples in skillet and sprinkle with sugar. Cook covered until tender. Remove cover and continue to cook until juice evaporates. Add bread crumbs and other ingredients and mix well.
Remove bone from shoulder of pork. Place slits in meat and add chopped garlic cloves to taste. Spread stuffing on meat.
Roll like a jelly roll. Secure with string. Place roast on rack in a pan. Add the bone that was removed. Cook at 350 degrees, 45 minutes to the pound.
Make lots, they ask for seconds!
description: Succulent rustic pork with – 1/2 cup salt pork and apples — slow-cooked (or quickly seared) to melt-in-your-mouth perfection. Every bite is a flavor bomb.
title: Roast Shoulder of Pork With Stuffing

content:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients 1 elephant roast ½ boxcar of bread crumbs 3 crates of cut celery 3 mailbags of chopped garlic cloves 2 trash cans of scalding hot water 50 lbs diced hot peppers 1 keg salt 2 oil drums of butter Instructions Blast off the rear end of one elephant to make large opening for stuffing. Mix ½ boxcar of bread crumbs, 3 mailbags of chopped garlic, 3 crates of cut celery and 2 trash cans of scalding hot water to moisten.
Add 50 lbs of diced hot peppers and 10 shovelfuls of salt. Place in an empty cement mixer and stir gently with a railroad
tie. Skewer elephant with one redwood tree (hickory can be used if you are a conservationist). Close rear end with telephone cable.
Place each end of redwood (hickory) on a 40 foot crane for support. Cook over bonfire for 3 months. (An additional month is needed for every ton over 10,000 lbs).
Remove from the fire and place in an airplane hangar for 2 weeks to cool. Baste with butter over the meat with a strong warehouse broom.
Slice with a chain saw and serve.
DELICIOUS!!!
description: A delicious homemade robust recipe with elephant roast and ½ boxcar of bread crumbs — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Roasted Bull Elephant

content:
Submitted by Anya and Rich Pagé
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1 pkg of ground beef ½ cup or so of bread crumbs 1 egg salt and pepper 2 green onions, chopped 1 tsp parsley flakes 2 cloves of garlic, chopped butter or oil to grease frying pan 1 jar of honey garlic sauce (available in Chinese food section of grocery store) Instructions In a large bowl, mix everything except honey garlic sauce and roll lovingly into balls. The balls like to be squeezed and played with so don’t hesitate to give them your full attention. If you drop a ball on the floor, throw it out, no one likes hairy balls.
Once the balls are happily formed, heat up the frying pan and add your oil or butter (because the balls like a little lube before they jump into the pan). Cook your balls slowly and turn them often to make sure they get cooked all around and right through. If your balls look blue, you should cook them longer.
When the balls are done, place them in a pot and pour the honey garlic sauce over them. Bring the sauce to a boil. Reduce heat and serve your balls with a yummy side dish: corn on the cob, Italian sausage, or baked whole zucchini are our favorites!
description: Homemade rustic goodness with of ground beef and ½ cup or so of bread crumbs — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Rich's Sweet Balls

content:
Submitted by Tina Sasyniuk and Kevin Long
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients ¾ lb lean, boneless pork 1 tbsp cornstarch 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce 1 tbsp cooking sherry 1 bunch broccoli 2 tbsp vegetable oil 2 tbsp minced garlic cloves 2 tbsp minced gingerroot ¼ cup water 2 tbsp hoisin sauce (optional) 25ml Instructions Slice pork thinly across the grain.
In a bowl, stir together cornstarch, soy sauce, and sherry. Add pork and stir to coat well. Separate broccoli into florets, peel stalks, then cut in 1 ½ inch pieces.
In wok or large nonstick skillet, heat oil over high heat. Add pork mixture, stir-fry for 2 minutes or until meat is lightly browned.
Stir in garlic, ginger, and broccoli. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add water. Cover and steam for 2 minutes or until broccoli is tender and crisp.
Stir in hoisin sauce if desired.
description: Savory, golden comforting pork with ¾ lb lean and cornstarch that's ridiculously easy to make and even more ridiculously delicious to eat.
title: Pork With Broccoli Stir Fry

content:
Submitted by Shelley Bzdell and Marc Robidas — Friends of the Groom
Ingredients Fish filet 7UP (enough to boil in) Garlic butter Instructions Boil fish in 7UP until the filet floats.
Fry in garlic butter.
Enjoy the fine taste of lobster!
description: Light, flaky savory fish with fish filet and garlic butter — perfectly cooked with a golden crust and tender, moist flesh inside. Elegant made easy.
title: Poor Man's Lobster

content:
Submitted by Lacey Pigeau
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 1 ½ cups white rice 1-10 oz can condensed celery soup 1-10 oz can condensed mushroom soup 1 pkg dried onion soup 1 cup milk 4-5 skinless chicken breasts Instructions Mix soup, milk and rice. Place mixture in a casserole dish. Lay chicken on top. Cover with foil and cook at 325 degrees for 2 hours. Do not peek!
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with ½ cups white rice and can condensed celery soup cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: No Peek Chicken

content:
Submitted by Stephanie Lemaire and John Breton
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 6 boneless, skinless chicken breasts 1 cup onion, finely chopped 1/3 cup white vinegar 1/3 cup soya sauce 1/2 cup water 2 tbsp granulated sugar 1/8 tsp garlic powder 1/4 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions Arrange chicken breasts in a single layer in a large saucepan. Mix remaining 8 ingredients in a small bowl. Pour over chicken. Cover. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for
15-20 minutes until tender.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned comforting chicken with boneless and onion — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Manilla Chicken

content:
Submitted by Patsy and Gord Bell
Sister-in-law and Brother of the Groom
Ingredients 1 ½ kg meat (any kind, no fat) 1 bottle soya sauce ½ bottle Worcestershire sauce ½ - 1 cup of brown sugar a few cloves of garlic Instructions Cut meat into 1/8-1/4 inch thick strips. Make sure there is no fat on the meat. Cut with the grain for chewier jerky.
Mix the rest of the ingredients and taste to your liking. Add meat strips, cover and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
A dehydrator works great for drying meat, but an oven set on the lowest setting with a broom handle wedging the door open an inch will work too.
The meat will take 6-10 hours to dry, depending on your meat size and setup.
Note:For oven method, you will need a mesh or heavy screen to put the meat on.
This is a good start. Be creative!
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with ½ kg meat and soya sauce that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Gord's Homemade Jerky

content:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson
Parents of the Bride
Ingredients 1 cup onions, chopped 1 cup celery, chopped 1 can kidney beans 1 tbsp chili powder 1 ½ tsp salt 1 tbsp vinegar 1 cup green peppers 1 lb ground beef 1 large can tomatoes (I use stewed whole tomatoes) ¼ tsp pepper ½ - 1 tsp crushed red pepper Instructions Brown beef, onion, celery, and green peppers. Add remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour stirring frequently.
description: Bold, spicy savory chili loaded with onions, celery, and kidney beans — thick enough to stand a spoon in and packed with layers of smoky flavor.
title: Dan's Chili

content:
Submitted by Tom Alexander
Friend of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 3 cups beans 6 cups water 1 tsp salt 1 onion, chopped ½ lb green bacon or regular bacon (I use a chunk of salt pork) ½ cup molasses 1 cup ketchup ¼ cup brown sugar 2 tsp dry mustard Instructions Place everything in the crock pot and let cook on low for 15-20 hours or on high for 5-7 hours. (7 hours seemed to work best)
Also note - Not a bad idea to soak the beans ahead of time if you like them softer.
description: Simple, delicious zesty vegetables with beans and onion. Sometimes the best meals are built around what's growing in the garden.
title: Crock Pot Beans

content:
Submitted by Charlene and Leonard Gillis
Cousins of the Groom
Ingredients 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts ¼ cup parmesan cheese 2 cups tomato sauce (or leftover spaghetti sauce) ½ - 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese Instructions Put ½ cup of sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place the chicken on top of the sauce and pour remaining sauce over chicken.
Sprinkle parmesan cheese over the sauce and chicken and place in a preheated 350 degree oven. Bake for about 1 ½ hours or until chicken is tender when poked with a fork.
In the last 15 minutes, sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top and let it melt. Serve with egg noodles or even spaghetti. Serves 4.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned robust chicken with boneless and ¼ cup parmesan cheese — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Chicken Parmesan

content:
Submitted by Rachelle and Tom Pigeau — Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 2 cups chopped, cooked chicken breast Frozen vegetables (optional) 1 can cream of chicken soup 1/2 can milk (or water) 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese, shredded 1 pouch Stove Top stuffing mix 1 cup water Instructions Layer chicken and frozen vegetables in a medium baking dish.
Mix cream of chicken soup with 1/2 can of milk (or water) and pour over top of chicken and vegetables.
Spread shredded mozzarella over the dish.
Mix stuffing mix with 1 cup water and spoon evenly over chicken and vegetables.
Bake at 375°F for 30–35 minutes or until dressing is slightly browned.
description: Succulent satisfying chicken with chopped and frozen vegetables that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Chicken à la King

content:
Submitted by Becky and Darren Shackleton
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1 large package of wings (about 25) ¾ cup of soya sauce ½ cup ketchup ½ cup water ½ cup brown sugar 2 tsp flour Instructions Take a large glass casserole dish and mix soya sauce, ketchup, water and brown sugar. Add the flour and mix well. Add the wings in the dish and place in oven for 40 minutes at 400 degrees.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with large package of wings and ¾ cup of soya sauce — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Becky's Sweet and Sour Chicken Wings

content:
Submitted by Darcy Lloyd
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 12 ozs of bacon, diced 4 medium white mushrooms, chopped 1 small onion, finely chopped 1 ½ lbs extra lean ground beef 1 egg ¼ cup evaporated milk or half and half 6 ozs shredded swiss cheese ½ cup corn flake crumbs Instructions In a large skillet, cook bacon until crisp but not burnt. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel to drain and cool. Remove all but a tablespoon of the bacon fat from the skillet and sauté the mushrooms and onions until tender. Allow to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, mix the beef, egg and milk. Add the mushroom/onion mixture, all but a few spoonfuls of the swiss cheese and all but one tablespoon of the bacon bits. Add the cornflake crumbs and mix until blended. Shape into a loaf and place in a large loaf pan.
Bake at 350 degrees for about an hour or until cooked thoroughly. Drain fat from the pan and sprinkle the reserved cheese and bacon bits on top. Bake an additional 5 minutes, or until cheese is bubbly. Let cool for approximately 10 minutes.
description: Rich, savory rustic beef with of bacon and medium white mushrooms — fork-tender and packed with deep, beefy flavor. Comfort food at its finest.
title: Bacon Mushroom Swiss Meatloaf

content:
Submitted by Kathy and Sean Demarce
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Take one clean industrial size olive or pickle jar (4 litre size would be perfect) with a lid.
Ingredients 3 lemons 3 limes 3 oranges Instructions Cut the fruit into quarters. Squeeze the juice into the jar and place the rinds into the jar as well. Add some different fruit like whole strawberries, quartered kiwi (2-3) is good. We recommend NOT using grapefruit, or bananas because they can get mushy. Add 1 bottle of flavoured Vodka (750ml) (Absolut Mandarin, Blueberry, Raspberry, whatever flavour sounds good). Fruit flavoured vodka is the best, although, Vanilla Vodka can be good.
Add ½ cup of sugar and 1 jar of marashino cherries (juice and all).
Pack jar with ice, close and cover jar with tea towel. It gets pretty frosty. Shake for 20 minutes (NO LESS). Pass around the group for everyone to have a turn shaking. If the ice gets low, just add more.
Once 20 minutes has passed, open and take a sip (or 2 or 3) right from the jar! Pass around to the other guests to enjoy.
There are some rules: You may NOT put Yuck-a-flux in a glass to drink, it must be from the jar and there will be NO use of straws!! Don&rsquo;t tempt the Yuck-a-flux Gods.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with lemons and limes — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Yuck-a-Flux (The Party Drink)

content:
Submitted by Susy Boulanger and Craig Lavigne
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1 big bucket 3 bags of ice 6 cans of concentrated fruit juice 60-100oz of Vodka 24 bottles of beer Instructions Pour beer and vodka into bucket. Add ice and concentrated juice. Mix well.
Dunk your cup in and enjoy!
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with big bucket and of ice — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Porch Climber

content:
Submitted by Jesse Beaudry
Nephew of the Bride
Ingredients ½ cup sliced strawberries 2 tbsp white sugar 1 tbsp cocoa 1 cup milk, divided ½ cup cold club soda ice cream or whipped cream fresh strawberries Instructions In a blender, combine the sliced strawberries, sugar, cocoa and ½ cup milk. Cover and blend until smooth. Add remaining ½ cup milk and club soda. Cover and blend. Pour into 2 glasses. Garnish with scoop of ice cream or whipped cream and whole strawberries.
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food with ½ cup sliced strawberries and cocoa. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Jesse's Strawberry Chocolate Shake

content:
Submitted by Francine Massicotte
Cousin of the Groom
Ingredients 1-8 oz pkg of cream cheese
1 cup sour cream
½ cup mayonnaise
1 cup seafood sauce
2 cups shrimp (optional)
½ cup diced green onions
½ cup diced tomatoes
½ cup green peppers
2 cups grated mozzarella and/or cheddar cheese
Instructions Mix the first 3 ingredients together and spread it on a pizza pan or large plate. Spread seafood sauce on top and add shrimp (optional). Cover with cheese. Sprinkle with green onions, tomatoes and green peppers.
description: Creamy, addictive bright dip with pkg of cream cheese and sour cream — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Yummy Dip

content:
Submitted by Brenda and Denis Arsenault
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 1 cup sour cream 1 cup Hellman’s mayonnaise 1 tsp beau monde spice 1 tsp minced onion 1 tsp accent 1 tsp dill weed 1 tsp parsley ½ tsp garlic salt 2 to 3 drops of Tabasco Instructions Mix all ingredients together and serve with vegetables or chips.
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with sour cream and hellman’s mayonnaise — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Vegetable/Chip Dip

content:
Submitted by Diana Bell
Cousin of the Groom
Ingredients 1 pkg of cream cheese 1 envelope of taco seasoning 1 jar of salsa 1 medium tomato, diced 1 can black olives, optional 1 small pkg of shredded cheddar cheese 1 small container of sour cream Instructions Blend cream cheese and taco seasoning. Spread on baking dish. Layer on sour cream and salsa. Top with diced tomato and black olives. Sprinkle cheddar cheese on top.
Serve with nacho chips.
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with of cream cheese and envelope of taco seasoning — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Taco Dip

content:
Submitted by Kelly and Kyle Jolley
Friends of the Groom
Ingredients 2-250 gr pkgs of Philly cream cheese 2 cups shredded, old cheddar cheese 1 tsbp each of chopped red peppers, green peppers and onions 3 tbsp mayonnaise 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce 1 tsp each of seasoning salt, onion salt and garlic salt 1 tsp real lemon juice dash of cayenne pepper pinch of salt Instructions Cream cheeses, add remaining ingredients and mix. Chill for one hour. Shape into ball and roll in chopped pecans.
Refrigerates well for 2 weeks. Freezes for up to 6 weeks.
description: A delicious homemade satisfying recipe with pkgs of philly cream cheese and shredded — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Party Cheese Ball

content:
Submitted by Kathy Genore and Paul Hamilton
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 1 package Philadelphia cream cheese 2 tbsp mayonnaise 1 small jar salsa 2 green onions, finely chopped 1 tomato, finely chopped approximately 2 cups finely grated cheddar cheese (marble works well) Instructions Cream Philly and mayonnaise together, spread over bottom of dish, not quite to the edges. Pour salsa over cream cheese mixture. Sprinkle chopped green onions and tomato over salsa. Cover with grated cheddar cheese.
Chill. Serve with tortilla chips.
description: A rich, flavorful vibrant dip loaded with philadelphia cream cheese, mayonnaise, and small jar salsa. The appetizer that everyone parks themselves next to and never leaves.
title: Nacho Dip

content:
Submitted by Margot Bell and Darryl Fleguel
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom
Ingredients 500 mL sour cream 1 pkg taco seasoning mix 1 tomato, diced shredded lettuce 1-2 green onions, chopped ½ lb grated cheddar cheese Instructions Layer:
Mix sour cream with taco seasoning mix. On top of sour cream mixture, add cheese, lettuce, diced tomato, and onions.
Serve with taco chips. Easyandyummy!
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with sour cream and taco seasoning mix — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Mexi-Dip

content:
Submitted by Mary and John Willemsen
Friends of the Bride
Ingredients 1/2 cup milk 1/3 cup salsa 3-8 oz packages of cream cheese, cubed 2-8 oz packages of mock crab, flaked 1 cup thinly sliced green onions 1-4 oz can of chopped green chillies assorted crackers Instructions Combine milk and salsa in a bowl. Transfer to a slow cooker coated with non-stick cooking spray. Stir in cream cheese, crab, onions and chillies. Cover and cook on low for 3-4 hours, stirring every 30 minutes. Serve with crackers. Yields 5 cups.
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with salsa and packages of cream cheese — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Hot Crab Dip

content:
Submitted by Margot Bell and Darryl Fleguel
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom
Ingredients 2 ripe avocados, peeled and mashed 2 tbsp lemon juice 2 tsp onion flakes 1 tsp salt (optional) ¼ tsp cayenne pepper ¼ tsp pepper ¼ tsp garlic powder 1 ripe tomato, diced Instructions Mix all ingredients together. Serve with corn chips, tortilla chips or fresh, raw veggies. Makes about 1 cup.
description: Smooth, tangy garden-fresh dip with ripe avocados and lemon juice — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Guacamole Dip

content:
Submitted by Stephanie and Shawn Thomas
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 5 to 5 ½ cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp salt 2 tsp ginger 2 tsp cinnamon 1 tsp nutmeg 1 tsp cloves 1 cup shortening 1 cup sugar 1 ¼ cups unsulphured molasses 2 eggs, beaten Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Thoroughly mix flour, soda, salt and spices. Melt shortening in large saucepan. Cool slightly. Add sugar, molasses and eggs. Mix well. Add four cups of dry ingredients and mix well. Turn mixture onto lightly floured surface. Knead in remaining dry ingredients by hand. Add a little more flour, if necessary, to make a firm dough. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to ¼ inch thickness for cut-out cookies. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet (small and medium-sized cookies for 6-10 minutes, large cookies for 10-15 minutes). One recipe of this dough will yield 2 to 3 dozen average-sized cookies.
Note:If you&rsquo;re not going to use the dough right away wrap it in plastic and refrigerate. Refrigerated dough will keep for a week, but be sure to remove it 3 hours prior to rolling so it softens and is workable.
Decorative Icing:
Mix egg whites and icing sugar to make icing, then pipe on to gingerbread men shaped cookies to bring them to life.
P.S. mini-M&amp;M&rsquo;s are a must for their buttons!
description: A classic melt-in-your-mouth cookie recipe with to 5 ½ cups all-purpose flour and ginger — chewy centers, golden edges, and that irresistible homemade smell that fills every room in the house.
title: Traditional Gingerbread Man Cookies

content:
Submitted by Buddy Pierce
Nephew/Godson of the Groom
Ingredients 1 can of Eaglebrand 3 cups miniature marshmallows ½ cup maraschino cherries, chopped 1 tsp vanilla 2 ½ cups graham cracker crumbs Instructions Mix all ingredients together. Butter hands and roll into 1 inch balls. Roll in fine coconut.
Refrigerate. Makes about 60.
Uncle Billy’s favorite!!
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with of eaglebrand and miniature marshmallows that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Refrigerator Balls

content:
Submitted by Audrey and John Gosson
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 3 eggs ½ cup sugar ½ cup corn syrup 4 ½ cups flour 5 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 1 tsp nutmeg 3 tbsp melted shortening or salad oil ½ cup milk apples chopped in small pieces, raisins and candied fruit- should equal 2 cups total Instructions Beat egg yolks until thick. Add sugar gradually and then corn syrup. Mix shortening or oil with milk and stir into the egg and sugar mixture.
Add all but ¼ cup of dry ingredients. Stir just enough to blend. Do not beat. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites, and remaining dry ingredients. Add fruit.
Drop by heaping tablespoonfuls into hot, deep frying fat (about 370 degrees) until golden brown. They will turn by themselves.
Drain on paper towel. Yields 4 dozen.
description: Homemade comforting goodness with ½ cup sugar and ½ cup corn syrup — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Oile Bolen (Dutch Donut)

content:
Submitted by Heather Bell
Bride
Ingredients 2 cups flour ¼ tsp salt 2 tsp ginger ¼ cup molasses ¾ cup margarine 1 cup brown sugar 1 egg 1 ½ tsp baking soda ½ tsp cinnamon Instructions In a large bowl, cream sugar, margarine, egg and molasses. In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients. Blend dry ingredients into cream mixture.
Roll batter into balls and place on a greased cookie sheet. Using a fork, gently press down on balls to flatten slightly. Sprinkle cookies with sugar. Bake at 375 degrees for 8-10 minutes.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with ¼ tsp salt and ginger — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Granny Gosson's Ginger Sparklers

content:
Submitted by Michelle and Jesse Webster
Cousins of the Groom
Ingredients 1 cup soft butter ¾ cup icing sugar 1 tbsp vanilla 2 cups flour &amp; ¾ tsp salt sifted together ½ cup rolled oats ½ cup semi sweet chocolate pieces ½ cup milk sprinkles of your choice Instructions Mix butter, sugar and vanilla. Cream well. Add flour mixture (sifted) and rolled oats. With hands, make into balls, roll into logs or use cookie cutters. Cook at 325 degrees for 20 minutes.
Melt chocolate and milk. Stir until smooth. Dip cookies in chocolate and add sprinkles to decorate.
Freezes well.
description: Homemade heavenly cookies with soft butter and ¾ cup icing sugar that snap, crumble, and melt exactly the way a great cookie should. Pure comfort in every bite.
title: Cookie Candies

content:
Submitted by Jenny Navid
Aunt of the Bride
2 cups raisins 1 cup water Boil together for 5 minutes.
Ingredients 4 cups flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 2 cups white sugar 1 cup shortening 3 eggs 1 tsp nutmeg 1 tsp allspice ½ cup chopped nuts 2 tsp cinnamon Instructions Mix dry ingredients. Add beaten eggs with the raisins and water. Drop by the tablespoon onto a cookie sheet.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes.
description: Buttery, golden melt-in-your-mouth cookies with raisins and nutmeg that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Boiled Raisin Cookies

content:
Submitted by Hazel Pigeau
Grandmother of the Bride
Ingredients 1 lb hamburg 1 medium onion, chopped 5 or 6 medium potatoes, sliced 1 can Heinz tomato soup Instructions Brown hamburg and onions. Place potatoes in a greased casserole dish. Add hamburg and onions. Mix in tomato soup.
Cook at 350 degrees for 1 hour, or until potatoes are tender.
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with hamburg, medium onion, and or 6 medium potatoes. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Tomato Soup Potatoes

content:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson
Parents of the Bride
Ingredients 4 pork chops 4 medium potatoes, thinly sliced 2 tomatoes, chopped 4 carrots, thinly sliced 1 cup onion, chopped ½ cup green pepper, diced 1 tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper 2 chicken bouillon cubes 1 ½ cups boiling water 2 tbsp butter Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Trim fat from chops. Brown chops on both sides, season with salt and pepper to taste.
Butter a casserole dish and arrange the potatoes on the bottom. In a bowl, combine tomatoes, carrots, onions and green pepper.
Spread over potatoes and sprinkle with the salt and pepper.
Dissolve bouillon cubes in the boiling water. Pour over the vegetables. Dot with butter. Arrange chops on top. Cover and bake for 45 minutes, then uncover and bake for 30 minutes longer.
description: Warm, satisfying robust casserole with pork chops and medium potatoes that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Pork Chop and Vegetable Casserole

content:
Submitted by Kim and Raymond Roy
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 10 lasagna noodles 6 cups mashed potatoes (about 12 medium potatoes) 3 cups grated, medium cheddar cheese 1 tsp salt 1 ¼ cup butter or margarine 2 cups chopped onions bacon bits if desired Instructions Cook noodles according to directions on the package.
Line the bottom of a greased 9x13 inch pan with some of the noodles. In large bowl, mix mashed potatoes with cheese and salt. Place a layer of this mixture on top of the noodles. Put second layer of noodles over top and so on.
Melt butter in frying pan. Add onion and bacon bits. Sauté until onion is clear and soft. Spoon onions, butter and bacon bits over noodles.
Cover lightly with foil. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes before cutting. Serve with sour cream.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with lasagna noodles and mashed potatoes. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Perogy Lazy Boy

content:
Submitted by Joyce Roy
Aunt of the Bride
Ingredients 9 lasagna noodles 2 cups (1-500 gm container) of cottage cheese 1 egg 1/4 tsp onion salt 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese (old) 2 cups mashed potatoes 1/4 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 1/2 cup butter or margarine 1 cup chopped onions and bacon Instructions Line a 9x13 inch pan with cooked noodles. Mix cottage cheese, egg and salt together. Spoon over noodles.
Mix cheddar cheese with potatoes, onion salt and pepper. Spread over noodles. Cover with another layer of noodles.
Melt butter, add onions and bacon and sauté. Pour over noodles. Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
Let stand for 10 minutes. Serve with sour cream.
description: A bubbly, golden satisfying casserole with lasagna noodles and of cottage cheese — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Lazy Perogy Casserole

content:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau
Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
Ingredients 3 or 4 onions, chopped ¼ cup butter 1 jar of sauerkraut 1 cup of Uncle Ben&rsquo;s rice 2 cups of water chicken cubes for broth Instructions Cook onions in butter until soft. Mix chicken cubes in water. Add rice and cook for 20 minutes until water is absorbed.
In a casserole dish, mix rice, onions and sauerkraut and cook in a slow, 250 degree oven for about 1½ to 2 hours.
Very good with pork chops or sausages.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with or 4 onions and ¼ cup butter — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Kapusta

content:
Submitted by Judy and Lorne Pierce
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom
Ingredients 6 carrots, sliced (boil for about 10 minutes) 1 medium onion 1/4 cup margarine 1/8 cup flour 1 cup milk grated cheese (as much as you like) 1 cup cracker crumbs 3/4 tsp salt 1/4 tsp dry mustard 1/8 tsp celery salt 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions Cook onions in margarine in microwave for 5 minutes. Add flour and spices and mix. Add milk. Cook in milk until thick, about 5-10 minutes in microwave.
Layer cooked carrots, onion/milk mixture, then cheese, usually about 2 layers of each. Top with cheese and cracker crumbs.
Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Can be doubled.
description: Hearty rustic casserole packed with carrots, medium onion, and grated cheese — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Carrot Casserole

content:
Submitted by David Henri
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 2 cups white Minute Rice, cooked 1 can of cream of chicken soup ½ cup mayonnaise lemon juice to taste pepper to taste salt to taste ¼ tsp curry powder or more as per your taste 1 head of broccoli florets 2-3 skinless, boneless, cooked chicken breasts Instructions Place cut up, cooked chicken pieces on bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place broccoli florets on top of chicken. Mix the rice, soup, mayonnaise, lemon juice and spices together. If rice mixture seems dry, add more mayonnaise. Pour over chicken and broccoli.
Bake at 325 degrees for about 30 minutes or until top starts to brown.
