description: Warm, satisfying comforting casserole with white minute rice and of cream of chicken soup that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Broccoli Bake

content:
Submitted by Ruth Divver
Aunt of the Groom
Ingredients 1 cup plain yogurt 2 tsp baking powder 1 cup brown sugar ½ cup oil 1 egg 1 cup bran 2 cups flour ½ tsp vanilla 1 cup blueberries Instructions In a small bowl, mix yogurt andoneteaspoon of baking powder. Set aside.
In a large bowl, mix all other ingredients, including the second teaspoon of baking powder. Mix the yogurt mixture into the large bowl.
Grease muffin tins. Bake at 350 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes. Makes 12 muffins.
description: Tender, domed rich muffins bursting with plain yogurt, brown sugar, and ½ cup oil — the kind that make weekday mornings feel like a treat.
title: Yogurt Blueberry Muffins

content:
Submitted by Monique Racine-Theriault
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 2 cups firmly packed brown sugar 2 cups flour ½ cup butter, softened 1 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup milk 1 egg 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips ½ cup walnuts ¼ cup flaked unsweetened coconut Instructions Mix the brown sugar and flour together. Using a pastry cutter or two knives, cut in the butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Remove one cup of mixture and set aside.
To the rest of the mixture, add baking powder and salt. Using a fork, lightly beat in vanilla, milk and egg. Continue beating until smooth. Pour batter into a greased 9x13 inch baking pan.
Gently mix chocolate chips, nuts, and coconut together. Sprinkle reserved crumbs over top of batter. Sprinkle chocolate chip mixture over top of crumbs. Spread evenly over top of batter. Bake at 350 degrees for 35 minutes.
Cool bars completely before cutting. Cut into 24 bars. Store in air tight container for up to 5 days.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with firmly packed brown sugar and ½ cup butter. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Toffee-Topped Bars

content:
Submitted by Sherry and Paul Beaulieu
Cousins of the Bride
1/2 cup margarine 2 cups flour 2 tsp baking soda 1/2 tsp salt 1 1/2 cups white sugar 2 eggs 1-19 oz can of crushed pineapple Combine all ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch glass pan. Cook at 325 degrees for 45-55 minutes. Insert a toothpick in the centre of the cake to check if it’s ready.
Sauce:
Ingredients 1/2 cup margarine 1 cup brown sugar 2/3 cup Carnation milk 1 cup coconut 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Combine margarine, brown sugar and milk in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Continue boiling sauce for 5 minutes and then add coconut and vanilla.
Let cake cool a little bit and then pour sauce over top.
description: Homemade melt-in-your-mouth cake with can of crushed pineapple and brown sugar that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Texas Cake

content:
Submitted by Yvette and Gilles Guenette
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients 3 tasses de sucre brun 2 tasses de sucre blanc 5 tasses de crème 4 cuillers à soupe de cornstarch ¼ à ½ tasse de noix hachés Instructions Faire chauffer le sucre et la crème jusqu’à ébullition. Retirer du feu et ajouter le cornstarch. Diluer tout en brassant et ajouter les noix. Laisser refroidir avant de déposer dans vos tartes. Ces tartes ont un dessous et un dessus de pâte.
description: A delicious homemade comforting recipe with tasses de sucre brun and tasses de sucre blanc — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Sucre a creme

content:
Submitted by Louise Dubyk and Don Broughtor
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 2 cups flour 2/3 cup butter or margarine 2 tbsp sugar 2 cups brown sugar 4 tsp baking powder 6-8 tsp cinnamon 1 tsp salt 1/2 cup raisins 1/4 cup cold butter 1 cup cold milk Instructions Put flour, sugar, baking powder and salt in a large bowl. Cut in 1/4 cup of butter or margarine. Make a well in the centre. Pour milk into well. Stir to form soft dough (add a bit more milk if necessary).
Turn out on floured surface and knead 8-10 times. Roll into rectangle about 1/3 inch thick and 12 inches long.
Cream 2/3 cup of butter, brown sugar and cinnamon together. Drop 1 teaspoon or so into each of 12 greased muffin tins. Spread the remaining mixture over dough rectangle and sprinkle raisins over top.
Roll up like a jelly roll. Mark first, and then cut into 12 slices. Place cut side down into muffin pan.
Bake in 375-400 degree oven for 20-25 minutes. Makes 12. Recipe can be doubled and frozen.
Glaze:
To ½ cup of icing sugar, add enough milk or water to make a thin glaze and drizzle over rolls.
description: Freshly baked toasty bread with butter or margarine and brown sugar that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: SINamon Buns

content:
Submitted by Dean Pigeau and Karen Cosgrove
Cousin of the Bride
Cake:
1 1/2 cup flour 1 cup sugar 1 1/2 tsp soda 1/4 cup cocoa 1 cup mayonnaise 7/8 cup water Bake for 1 hour or less at 350 degrees.
Coffee Icing:
Ingredients 1/2 tsp coffee 1 tbsp margarine 2 tsp boiling water 1 tsp cocoa 1 cup icing sugar Instructions Mix coffee, margarine, water and cocoa until smooth. Slowly add icing sugar until creamy.
description: Homemade sweet cake with soda and cocoa that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Stock Club Cake with Coffee Icing

content:
Submitted by Christine and Graham Baker
Cousins of the Bride
A rich sour cream coffee cake which may be layered or simply topped with tart cranberries and spicy sweet streusel. Wonderful served warm with ice cream or whipped cream.
Streusel:
¾ cup lightly packed brown sugar 1 tsp cinnamon ½ cup all purpose flour ¼ cup butter Mix together sugar, flour, and cinnamon. Blend in butter until crumbly.
Cake:
Ingredients ½ cup soft butter 2 eggs 2 cups all purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 cup dairy sour cream 1 cup granulated sugar 1 tsp vanilla 1 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 2 cups cranberries, fresh or frozen Instructions Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, then vanilla. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture. Add sour cream.
Spread ½ batter in 10 inch spring form pan (or 9x13 inch pan). Sprinkle ½ streusel, then ½ cranberries. Spread remaining batter, cranberries and streusel. Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
description: This sweet cake with ¾ cup lightly packed brown sugar and cinnamon is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Sour Cream Coffee Cake with Strueusel

content:
Submitted by Lisa and Derry Thibeault
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 1 box of Ritz crackers, finely crushed 4 Skor chocolate bars, finely crushed 1 can of Eaglebrand 1 tub of chocolate icing Instructions Put ½ cup of Skor pieces aside for topping.
Combine Ritz crackers and Eaglebrand, mix well. Mix in remaining Skor pieces.
Pour into a greased 8x8 inch pan. Bake at 300 degrees for 20 minutes. Let cool for 20 minutes. Frost with chocolate icing and sprinkle remaining Skor pieces on top.
description: Sweet, satisfying sweet squares with box of ritz crackers and skor chocolate bars. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Skor Bars

content:
Submitted by Marie and Will Sayer
Cousins of the Bride
3 cups rhubarb, diced 1 1/3 cups whole wheat flour 1 1/3 cups all purpose flour 1 cup brown sugar 1 tsp baking soda 1/4 tsp salt 1 cup yogurt or sour cream 1/2 cup vegetable oil 2 eggs 1 cup chopped nuts Topping:
Ingredients 1/2 cup brown sugar 1/2 cup chopped nuts 2 tbsp butter 1 tsp cinnamon Instructions In a large bowl, combine rhubarb, flours, brown sugar, baking soda and salt.
In a separate bowl, blend together yogurt, oil and eggs. Stir into rhubarb mixture just until moistened. Fold in nuts.
Spoon batter into 24 greased muffin cups
Combine topping ingredients. Sprinkle a bit of topping mixture on each muffin.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25-30 minutes.
description: Fluffy decadent muffins with rhubarb and whole wheat flour that rise tall and taste even better than the bakery. Best eaten warm with a smear of butter.
title: Rhubarb Muffins

content:
Submitted by Shirley and Rob Webster
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Cake:
½ cup margarine 2 cups all purpose flour 1 ¼ cup white sugar 1 tbsp baking powder ¼ cup peanut butter ½ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup milk 2 eggs Cream margarine and sugar. Add peanut butter, vanilla and eggs. Beat well. In a separate bowl, blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to creamed mixture, alternating with milk. Mix well after each addition.
Pour batter into a greased 3L (9x13 inch) baking pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 30-35 minutes.
Cool. Leave in pan. When cool, spread with Peanut Butter Frosting. If desired, drizzle raspberry jam over top of cake. Carry to picnic in baking pan. Makes 18-24 pieces.
Peanut Butter Frosting:
Ingredients ¼ cup margarine ¼ cup peanut butter 1 tsp vanilla 2 ½ cups sifted icing sugar ¼ cup milk Instructions Cream margarine, peanut butter and vanilla. Add sugar, alternating with milk, beating until light and fluffy. Use additional milk if needed.
description: Homemade heavenly cake with ½ cup margarine and all purpose flour that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Picnic Cake

content:
Submitted by Colette and Mike Peplinskie
Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom
Ingredients 1 ½ cup all purpose flour 1 tsp salt 2 cups white sugar ½ cup cocoa 2 tsp vanilla 1 cup oil 4 eggs ¼ cup cold water ½ cup (or more) chopped walnuts Instructions Place all ingredients in a beater bowl in the order given, ending with walnuts. Beat on low speed or by hand until smooth. Do not beat for too long.
Grease a 9x13 inch pan. Smooth batter into the pan and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes, no longer. Ice if desired.
description: Homemade robust goodness with ½ cup all purpose flour and ½ cup cocoa — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Pep's Favorite Brownies

content:
Submitted by Chantal and Angus Muir
Cousins of the Groom
½ cup margarine or butter ¼ cup white sugar 3 tsp cocoa 1 egg pinch of salt ½ tsp vanilla Melt above ingredients together until smooth. Add 2 cups of graham wafer crumbs and 1 cup of coconut. Press firmly into a 9x9 inch pan.
Ingredients ¼ cup margarine or butter 2 cups icing sugar 2 tbsp hot milk ½ tsp peppermint extract Instructions Mix above ingredients and spread over first layer in pan. Pour a mixture of 3 melted squares of semi-sweet chocolate and 1 tablespoon of margarine or butter over top. Put in the fridge. Cut into squares before it gets too hard. Store in fridge.
This is a sweet tooth special!
description: Irresistible melt-in-your-mouth squares with ½ cup margarine or butter and ¼ cup white sugar — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Peppermint Squares

content:
Submitted by Fern Henri
Uncle of the Bride
Ingredients 1 square box of vanilla ice cream 1 container of Cool Whip Instructions Cut ice cream up into ½ to 1 inch thick pieces to fill the bottom of a 9 inch square pan or a 9x13 inch pan.
Crush OREO cookies until crumbly. Spread on top of ice cream. Pour chocolate syrup over cookies.
Spread Cool Whip over top. Sprinkle more crushed cookies over Cool Whip to decorate.
Cover with Saran wrap and place in freezer for at least a day before serving.
OREO cookies and chocolate sauce can be substituted for Skor chocolate bars and caramel sauce.
description: Perfectly sweet cookies studded with square box of vanilla ice cream and container of cool whip. Simple enough for a Tuesday, special enough for a holiday platter.
title: OREO Cookie Dessert

content:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell
Parents of the Groom
1 cup flour 1 tsp cinnamon 1 ½ cup shredded carrots ½ cup oil ½ tsp vanilla ¾ cup sugar ¼ tsp nutmeg 2 eggs, slightly beaten ¼ cup water icing sugar, optional ½ tsp each of salt, baking soda and baking powder With fork, in an 8 or 9 inch square pan, mix flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.
Mix briskly with fork until thoroughly blended. Bake in preheated, 350 degree oven for about 30 minutes or until pick inserted in centre comes out clean and cake pulls away from sides of pan.
Cool in pan on rack. Sprinkle with icing sugar or ice with cream cheese icing.
Cream Cheese Icing:
Ingredients ¼ cup softened butter 1 tsp vanilla dash of salt 2 ½ cups icing sugar 4 oz (125 gr) softened cream cheese Instructions Into cream cheese and butter, beat the vanilla and salt. Gradually add the icing sugar and blend well.
Note:This recipe makes a little more icing than “Mix-In-The-Pan Carrot Cake” needs. If you spread it on thin, you can probably ice two cakes.
description: A stunning rich cake with cinnamon and ½ cup shredded carrots — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Mix-In-The-Pan Carrot Cake

content:
Submitted by Claire and Marc Massicotte
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Cake:
1 cup sugar 1 egg 1 tbsp apricot jam 1 cup flour l tsp baking soda 1 tsp baking powder generous pinch of salt 1 tbsp butter 1 tsp white vinegar 1 cup milk Beat the egg and sugar well with a mixer. In a separate bowl, combine the jam, sifted flour, soda, baking powder and salt. In another bowl, melt butter and add the vinegar. Add liquids to egg mixture alternately with the flour.
Beat well and bake in aCOVEREDdish at 350 degrees for 45 minutes to 1 hour. It is very important to use a covered dish otherwise sauce won’t soak into cake. Corning wear dish works quite well.
Ingredients Sauce: 1 cup cream or table cream 6 ozs butter 1 cup sugar ½ cup hot water Instructions Melt together the ingredients for the sauce and pour over the pudding or cake as it comes out of the oven.
This is a delicious dessert, but RICH !! It doesn’t sound or look too good, but wait till you serve it, you will have a lot of oohs and aahs, I promise!! The texture is velvety and oh so smooth!!
description: A show-stopping luscious cake with apricot jam and l tsp baking soda. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Malva Pudding Cake

content:
Submitted by Lorna and Zoran Popovitch
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients 30 marshmallows, chopped fine 30 graham wafers, rolled fine 18 cherries, chopped ½ cup walnuts, chopped 1 can Eaglebrand Milk 1 cup coconut Instructions Mix the first four ingredients with the can of Eaglebrand. Butter a shallow pan and spread thick with half the coconut.
Spread the Eaglebrand mixture over the coconut, then spread another layer of coconut on top. Pack well and put in the fridge for 24 hours.
Enjoy! It&rsquo;s easy and yummy!
description: Homemade heavenly cake with marshmallows and graham wafers that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Ice Box Cake

content:
Submitted by Pat and Tom Sayer
Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
1 yellow Duncan Hines cake mix 1 small instant Jello vanilla pudding 4 eggs ½ cup Crisco oil ½ cup water ¼ cup White Rum Mix above ingredients together and bake in a greased bundt pan to which ¼ cup of chopped pecans has been added. Bake at 350 degrees for 55 minutes.
Topping:
In a sauce pan, combine the following:
Ingredients 1 cup of butter (use butter only) 1 cup of white sugar ½ cup of water Instructions Boil above ingredients for five minutes then add ½ cup of white rum.
When cake is cooked, remove from pan and place topping in the bundt pan. Return cake to the same pan. Let set for approximately 20 minutes until all topping has been absorbed by the cake.
Remove from the pan and enjoy.
description: A show-stopping luscious cake with yellow duncan hines cake mix and small instant jello vanilla pudding. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Granny Sayer's Rum Cake

content:
Submitted by Margaret and Walter Smith
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients ½ cup butter, room temperature 1 cup white sugar 2 cups graham crumbs 1 egg 1 cup medium coconut 1 cup milk 3 tbsp flour 1 tsp baking powder ½ tsp vanilla pinch of salt Instructions Cream butter and sugar together. Add beaten egg. Add other ingredients and mix well.
Place in a 9x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Serve with ice cream or whipped cream.
description: This decadent cake with ½ cup butter and graham crumbs is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Graham Coconut Cake

content:
Submitted by Kevin Roy
Cousin of the Bride
Base:
1 ½ cups vanilla or graham crumbs ½ cup chopped peanuts or walnuts ¼ cup melted butter 2 tbsp peanut butter Mix the above ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch pan. Save some of the mixture for the topping.
Filling:
Ingredients 250 gr cream cheese ½ cup white sugar ½ cup peanut butter 2 tsp vanilla 4 eggs, add one at a time 1 large container Cool Whip Instructions Mix the above ingredients and layer on top of the base.
Topping:
Reserved mixture from base of cake
5 tbsp chocolate sundae sauce
Sprinkle reserved topping on the cake. Heat the chocolate sauce in the microwave and swirl it on top of the cake with a knife.
description: Rich, tender melt-in-your-mouth cake with ½ cups vanilla or graham crumbs and ½ cup chopped peanuts or walnuts that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Drumstick Cake

content:
Submitted by Amanda McCabe and Jason Simard
Friends of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1 container of chocolate icing 1 box of graham crackers 1 pkg of instant vanilla pudding ½ cup of sugar 500 mL whipping cream Instructions Prepare vanilla pudding according to the directions on the package. Set aside.
Whip up whipping cream, slowly adding sugar until peaks form. Fold whipping cream into vanilla pudding.
Line an 8x10 inch baking dish with graham crackers. Pour in half of the pudding mixture. Cover pudding mixture with another layer of graham crackers. Pour second half of pudding mixture over graham crackers. Line mixture with graham crackers.
Let chill in the fridge for 20-30 minutes. Ice top with chocolate icing. It helps to warm up icing in microwave and then pour and spread over graham crackers.
Chill before serving.
description: Irresistible sweet squares with container of chocolate icing and box of graham crackers — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Chocolate Eclair Squares

content:
Submitted by Diane Laderoute
Cousin of the Bride
Ingredients 1 ¾ cup flour ¾ cup white sugar ¾ cup brown sugar 1 cup margarine or butter 2 egg yolks ½ tsp vanilla 1 pkg milk chocolate chips 1 cup slivered almonds Instructions Combine flour and sugars. Mix margarine in until blended. Add egg yolk and vanilla. Mix until smooth.
Press with floured fingers onto a 10x15 inch cookie sheet with sides. Bake at 350 degrees for 13-18 minutes or until light golden.
Cool slightly, about 20 minutes. Spread melted chocolate over top. Sprinkle almonds on top.
Cool until set. Cut into squares or triangles.
description: Crunchy, addictive satisfying snack mix with ¾ cup flour and ¾ cup white sugar — once you start munching, there's no stopping. Perfect for parties or movie nights.
title: Chocolate Butterscotch Almond Crunch

content:
Submitted by Diane and Harold Bell
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients ½ cup margarine 2 cups packed brown sugar 1 tsp vanilla 2 eggs 1 ½ cup all purpose flour 2 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 1 cup semi-sweet Chipits 1 cup coarsely chopped nuts Instructions Melt margarine in a large pot. Remove from heat. Add sugar and vanilla stirring until well blended. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to the pot and mix well.
Spread in a lightly greased 9x13 inch rectangular baking pan. Sprinkle Chipits and nuts over surface of cookie base. Press down lightly.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35-40 minutes. Cool thoroughly before cutting into bars.
description: Irresistible indulgent squares with ½ cup margarine and packed brown sugar — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: Chipits Chewy Nut Bars

content:
Submitted by Anne Mary and Jean-Paul Racine
Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
Bottom:
l cup sifted flour 1 cup rolled oats 1 cup brown sugar ¼ tsp salt ½ cup butter Mix flour, oats, brown sugar, soda and salt. Add butter and mix until crumbly, first with a fork, then with fingers. Place mixture into bottom of a greased 9x13 inch pan. Bake for 10 minutes.
Filling:
2 eggs 1 cup brown sugar ½ tsp almond extract 2 tbsp flour 1 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 1 cup coconut ½ cup pecan halves 1 cup well drained maraschino cherries, halved Beat eggs. Stir in brown sugar, almond flavoring, flour, baking powder, salt, coconut, and maraschino cherries and stir to blend. Pour over first mixture and spread evenly. Sprinkle with pecans. Return to oven and bake 25 minutes until lightly browned. Ice with Cherry Almond icing. Cut into squares or bars to serve. Makes 2 dozen 1½x2 inch bars.
Cherry Almond Icing:
Ingredients 3 tbsp soft butter (or more) 2 cups sifted icing sugar 2 tbsp maraschino cherry juice ¼ tsp almond extract Instructions Blend butter and sugar. Add cherry juice and almond extract, using enough juice to make icing easy to spread. Stir until smooth.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with l cup sifted flour and rolled oats. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Cherry Chews

content:
Submitted by Stella and Ben Daigle
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients 2 cups graham crumbs ½ cup melted butter 2 tbsp brown sugar 1-8 oz pkg of Philadelphia cream cheese 1 large pkg of Dream Whip 1 cup white sugar 1 can cherry pie filling Instructions Mix graham crumbs, melted butter and brown sugar together. Press into an 8x12 inch pan.
Mix Dream Whip according to directions on package. Add cream cheese and white sugar to whipped Dream Whip. Spread over graham crumbs. Top with cherry pie filling.
Refrigerate for 2 hours or more before serving.
description: A show-stopping luscious cake with graham crumbs and ½ cup melted butter. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Cherry Cheesecake

content:
Submitted by Judy and Roger Lafreniere
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients 2 tbsp butter 2 pears, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges 2 apples, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges ¼ cup brown sugar 5 cups french bread, cut into ½ inch cubes ½ cup raisins ½ cup pecans, coarsely chopped 2 ¼ cups real whipping cream 3 eggs 2 tsp vanilla 1 tsp nutmeg 1 tsp cinnamon Instructions In a large frying pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add fruit and cook , stirring occasionally until tender. Stir in brown sugar, increase heat and cook for 3 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in the bread, raisins and pecans. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk cream, eggs, sugar, vanilla and spices. Stir bread mixture into the cream mixture and let stand 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour mixture into a 9 inch greased baking pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 40-50 minutes.
description: Warm, homemade golden bread with pears and apples — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Caramelized Pear & Apple Bread Pudding

content:
Submitted by Sandra Stewart
Friend of the Bride and Groom
Ingredients 1 ready-to-use Graham pie crust 20 caramel squares 3 tbsp milk ½ cup chopped pecans 2 pkgs cream cheese, softened ½ cup sugar 2 eggs Instructions Microwave caramels and milk until melted (about 2 min; stir after 1 min). Stir in pecans and mix well. Pour into pie crust and chill for 10 minutes.
Beat (medium speed) softened cream cheese, sugar and eggs until smooth. Pour into chilled pie crust.
Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes or until set. Cool to room temperature. Chill for at least 3 hours. Decorate with sundae caramel sauce and pecans.
description: This sweet cake with ready-to-use graham pie crust and caramel squares is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Caramel Pecan Cheesecake

content:
Submitted by Joanne and Greg Craig
Cousins of the Bride
Crust:
½ cup butter 2 cups graham crumbs ¼ cup sugar Topping:
2 pkgs Dream Whip 1 pkg cream cheese ½ cup sugar Filling:
Ingredients ¾ cup sugar 1 tsp salt 3 tbsp cornstarch 1 tbsp butter ¼ cup water 1 tbsp lemon juice 4 cups fresh/frozen blueberries Note: Above filling can be substituted for 2 cans of blueberry pie filling.
Crust:Melt butter. Stir in sugar and graham crumbs. Press into 9x13 inch pan.
Filling:Combine sugar, salt and cornstarch into a pot. Add water. Add 2 cups of blueberries, stirring constantly over medium heat until mixture comes to a boil and is clear and thickened. Remove from heat. Add remaining blueberries, lemon juice and butter and stir. Let cool. Pour over top of
graham crust.
Topping:Prepare Dream Whip according to package directions. In separate bowl, blend cream cheese and sugar. Add to Dream Whip. Spread over top of filling, starting with the edges and working your way to the middle.
Sprinkle with graham crumbs and blueberries to garnish. Refrigerate for approximately 30 minutes before serving.
description: Rich, tender indulgent cake with ½ cup butter and graham crumbs that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Blueberry Cheesecake

content:
Submitted by Louise and Dan Pigeau
Aunt and Uncle of the Bride
Cake:
2 cups flour 1 1/4 cup sugar dash of salt 3 tsp baking powder 2 eggs 3/4 cup oil 1 tsp vanilla Mix first four ingredients together, then add remaining ingredients. Bake at 350 degrees for 30-45 minutes
German Icing:
Ingredients 1 egg, beaten 2/3 cup brown sugar 1/2 cup can milk 2 tbsp butter 1/4 tsp salt Instructions Boil until it starts to thicken and add 1 ½ cup of coconut and ½ cup of nuts.
description: Rich, tender sweet cake with dash of salt and brown sugar that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Birthday Cake with German Icing

content:
Submitted by Shirley and Dave Jodouin
Cousins of the Groom
Cake:
2 ½ cups flour 1 ½ tsp baking soda 1 ½ tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 1 ½ cup white sugar ¼ tsp cinnamon 2 eggs 1 cup mashed bananas 1 cup buttermilkor1 cup sourmilk (add 2 tbsp vinegar to your regular cup of milk) Throw all the ingredients into a big bowl and mix. Pour into a 3 L pan that has been greased, and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes or until a knife inserted in the middle comes out clean and dry.
Caramel Icing:
Ingredients 2 cups whipping cream 2 cups brown sugar Instructions Put these two ingredients in a large enough saucepan and bring to a boil. Start your stove at #8 until it boils, stir constantly, then turn your stove down until it reaches a simmering boil and let it boil down, stirring occasionally. This usually takes about 30 minutes. Remove from stove and let it sit on the counter until it cools down a bit, roughly 10-15 minutes.
description: A show-stopping luscious cake with ½ cups flour and ½ tsp baking soda. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Banana Cake with Caramel Icing

content:
Submitted by Heather Bell
Bride
Ingredients 32 Ritz crackers, crushed 1 ½ cups brown sugar ¼ cup flour ¼ tsp cinnamon ½ cup butter 4 cups chopped apples Instructions Mix all ingredients together except for the apples. Place half of the apples on the bottom of a baking dish. Add half of Ritz mixture on top of apples. Place remaining apples and then remaining Ritz mixture on top.
Bake at 375 degrees for 30 minutes.
description: A gorgeous melt-in-your-mouth dessert with ritz crackers and ½ cups brown sugar that looks like it came from a bakery but tastes even better because it came from your kitchen.
title: Apple Crisp

content:
Submitted by Patti and Ray Racine
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 3 eggs 2 cups sugar 1 cup oil 2 cups grated zucchini 1 tsp salt 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp cinnamon 2 tsp vanilla 3 cups flour Instructions Mix all ingredients together. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Makes 2 loaves.
description: Soft, golden buttery rolls with grated zucchini and cinnamon that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Zucchini Bread

content:
Submitted by Tiffany Breton and Matt Rainville
Friends of the Groom
Ingredients ¼ cup white wine vinegar 2 tbsp olive oil 1 tbsp fresh, chopped parsley 1 tsp each of sugar and dried basil ½ tsp dried dillweed ¼ tsp each of salt and pepper dash of hot pepper sauce 1 clove of garlic, minced 2 tomatoes, chopped 1 cucumber, chopped 1 french baguette (or bread) feta cheese for topping Instructions In a bowl, combine all ingredients (except cheese), stir, set aside.
Cut bread into ½ inch slices, place on baking sheet and broil, turning once until toasted. Spoon mixture over toast, top with cheese, broil for one minute.
Crack open your finest bottle of Stella Artois and enjoy!
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with ¼ cup white wine vinegar and olive oil. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Bruschetta and Brew

content:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson
Parents of the Bride
Ingredients 1 3/4 cups flour 2 tsp baking powder 1/4 tsp baking soda 1/2 tsp salt 1/3 cup shortening 2/3 cup white sugar 2 eggs, beaten 1 cup mashed bananas Instructions Blend flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt together. In another bowl, cream shortening and sugar together. Add beaten eggs and blend. Add dry ingredients alternately with bananas.
Beat after each addition until well blended. Pour into a loaf pan and let stand for 20 minutes. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Poke it in the middle and if it&rsquo;s still doughy-raw, then kick &rsquo;er back in for another 5 mins.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with mashed bananas that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Banana Bread

content:
Submitted by Billy Bell
Groom
Ingredients 3 cups self-rising flour 1 tbsp sugar 1 six-pack of beer 1 tbsp butter, melted Instructions Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.
In a large bowl, blend the flour and sugar. Quickly mix in one can of beer. Place the mixture in a 9x5 inch loaf pan. Wet your fingers and smooth the top.
Bake for 1 hour or until golden brown. Drink the other 5 beer while you’re waiting.
Brush the top of the bread with melted butter.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with self-rising flour and six-pack of beer that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Beer Bread

content:
Submitted by Danielle Beaudry
Niece of the Bride
2 pkgs Pillsbury Dough Croissant Rolls
Flatten on a cookie sheet and bake at 350 degrees for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool.
Topping:
½ cup Miracle Whip ½ cup Hellman&rsquo;s Mayonnaise 1 pkg Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (powder) 8 oz cream cheese Mix together and spread on cooled Croissant crust.
Sprinkle with chopped veggies:
Ingredients 1 cup broccoli 1 cup green pepper ½ cup green onions ½ cup olives 1 cup tomatoes Instructions Cover with saran wrap and pat lightly. Best if made the day before.
description: Homemade rustic pizza loaded with ½ cup miracle whip, ½ cup hellman's mayonnaise, and hidden valley ranch dressing. Golden crust, melty cheese, and the satisfaction of making it yourself.
title: Summer Pizza

content:
Submitted by Linda and Darrell Lamothe
Cousins of the Bride
Ingredients 2 pkgs Philadelphia cream cheese 1 pkg ranch style dry mix 8 large tortilla shells 1 jar of drained and finely chopped, jalapeno peppers red pimentos, drained and finely chopped onion to your taste, finely chopped black olives, finely chopped, optional Instructions Mix cream cheese with dry ranch mix. Spread evenly over tortilla shells. Sparingly top tortillas with the rest of the ingredients.
Roll shells up and wrap in Saran. Refrigerate for 24 hours. Slice into 1 inch slices and serve.
Very colorful and may be very hot depending on the amount of jalapenos you’ve added. Very nice for showers, parties and Christmas.
description: A delicious homemade satisfying recipe with philadelphia cream cheese and ranch style dry mix — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Ranch Style Tortilla Roll-Ups

content:
Submitted by Linda and Joe Albrechtas
Aunt and Uncle of the Groom
Ingredients 1 large tin tomato juice 1 can diced tomatoesor3-4 ripe, fresh tomatoes, chopped ½ English cucumber, unpeeled and finely chopped ½ red pepper, finely chopped (whole one if small) 2 cloves garlic, minced or crushed ½ red onion, finely chopped 1 tsp sugar ¼ cup red wine vinegar approximately 2 tbsp good olive oil dashes of Tabasco to taste salt and pepper to taste Instructions Best refrigerated for a couple of hours or overnight. Serve cold, garnished with croutons.
Makes about 8 cups.
description: Homemade robust goodness with large tin tomato juice and diced tomatoesor3-4 ripe — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Gazpacho

content:
Submitted by Nancy and Mike Potvin
Cousins of the Groom
8 oz light cream cheese, softened 2 tbsp grated onion 4.2 oz canned crab meat, drained and cartilage removed 2 tbsp fine bread crumbs 1/4 tsp salt 1/16 tsp garlic powder 1 lb. pkg wonton wrappers cooking oil for deep frying Mash cream cheese in a bowl. Work in onion, crab meat, bread crumbs, salt and garlic powder.
Place 1 tsp of mixture in center of each wonton wrapper. Dampen edges of wonton with water. Fold one corner at a time to centre, pressing to seal well. Cover with damp towel until ready to cook.
Deep fry in hot 375 degree cooking oil for about 2 minutes until browned. Drain on paper towel. Cool and refrigerate in covered container.
To serve, arrange on ungreased baking sheet. Heat in 450 degree oven for about 4 minutes until hot.
Makes 54 puffs.
1 puff:
Ingredients 52 calories 1 gr protein 3 gr total fat 3 gr carbohydrate 109 mg sodium
description: Crispy on the outside, melt-in-your-mouth tender inside — this comforting fish with light cream cheese and grated onion is weeknight fancy at its absolute best.
title: Crab Finger Puffs

content:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault
Friends of the Bride’s Family
Ingredients 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped 1 cup spinach, chopped 1/4 cup Dijon mustard 1/2 cup yogurt 1/2 tsp salt 1 tsp black pepper 1/4 cup lemon juice 4 large boneless, skinless chicken breasts 6 slices Swiss Cheese 4 thin slices smoked turkey breast 4 slices lemon (optional) Instructions In a bowl combine garlic, spinach, mustard, yogurt, salt, half the pepper and 1 tsp of lemon juice. Gently pound the chicken breasts flat between sheets of plastic wrap. Spread the sauce on the chicken. Top each breast with a slice each of the Swiss cheese and smoked turkey. Cut remaining slices of cheese in half and set aside.
Roll up the chicken and secure with a toothpick. Arrange in a non-stick baking dish and top each roll-up with a half slice of Swiss cheese and lemon slice.
Bake at 375 degrees for 20-25 minutes. Sprinkle remaining pepper and lemon juice and serve immediately. Serves four.
description: Juicy, flavorful comforting chicken with cloves garlic and spinach — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Cache Bay Chicken and Spinach Roll-Ups

content:
Ingredients 1 lb medium ground beef or pork 2 cloves garlic, crushed 1 large onion, chopped 1 14-oz tin tomato sauce ½ cup water 1 cup 5-minute rice 1 tsp paprika½ tsp dried dill weed ¼ tsp each salt and pepper ½ head cabbage sour cream Instructions In large, wide saucepan over medium heat, cook meat and garlic, stirring often with fork to keep meat crumbly. Add 1 or 2 tsp of vegetable oil if meat sticks.
Stir in onion and cook until meat loses its redness, a total of about 5 minutes. Stir in tomato sauce, water, rice, paprika, dill weed, salt and pepper. Cover and bring to a boil.
Meanwhile slice cabbage into 1 x 3 inch strips. Stir into meat mixture. Cover tightly and reduce heat to medium-low. Cook, stirring often, until rice and cabbage are tender, 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve with sour cream. Serves 4 to 6.
description: Fresh, vibrant bright vegetables with medium ground beef or pork and cloves garlic — cooked just right so they're tender but still have that satisfying snap.
title: Fast Cabbage Roll Skillet

content:
Ingredients 2 cups white sugar 1 cup water ½ tsp garlic 5 Tbsp soya sauce Instructions Wash and place wings in pan. Melt sugar in water, Add garlic and soya sauce. Marinade for 1 hour. Bake uncovered at 350 degrees for 2-1/2 to 3 hours till sauce is thick and bubbly.
Stir every ½ hour.
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with ½ tsp garlic and soya sauce cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Corey's Chicken Wings

content:
Ingredients 1 5-1/2 to 6 lb boiling fowl 8 cups water 5-6 medium-large carrots, chunked 12 whole medium onions 1 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions In large soup kettle, simmer the whole fowl with the water, covered, until tender – about 4 hours. Be sure it is beginning to fall apart. Left bird to utility tray and cut it into serving pieces discarding what skin and bones you readily can. Set this meat aside for the time being. Skim as much surplus fat as you can from the top of broth.NOTE;I usually do the above a day early so refrigerated fat is easy to scoop off the next day. Add the carrots, onions, salt and pepper and cook until vegetables are tender. Put chicken pieces in stew and when stew is boiling hard, put in dumplings.
DUMPLINGSI use Bisquick and follow the recipe and directions on the box. Also, dumpling recipe is included in this book under Miscellaneous.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with to 6 lb boiling fowl and medium-large carrots — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Chicken & Dumplings

content: Ingredients Chicken legs, breasts or whatever 1 tin beef consommé or broth ½ cup brown sugar ½ tsp ginger ½ tsp garlic salt 2 Tbsp soya sauce Instructions Heat consommé, brown sugar, ginger, garlic salt and soya sauce on stove. Put chicken in broiler pan and pour sauce over. Bake at 350 F. for 45 to 60 minutes.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned rustic chicken with chicken legs and tin beef consommé or broth — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Chicken A La Chuk

content:
Ingredients 3 tbsp salad oil or shortening 1 cup minced onions 1 cup sliced mushrooms 3 tbsp flour 4 franks, chopped 1-1/2 cups tomato juice 1 cup water 1.5 tbsp Worcestershire ¼ tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper 2 tbsp snipped parsley ¼ cup minced green pepper 1 8-oz pkg spaghetti Parmesan cheese Instructions In oil in skillet, sauté onions and mushrooms till golden. Stir in flour, chopped franks. Add tomato juice, water, Worcestershire, salt and pepper. Simmer, uncovered, 25 minutes. Add parsley, green pepper. Simmer 5 minutes longer. Meanwhile, cook spaghetti as package directs. Serve ring of spaghetti with sauce in center. Pass the cheese.
Makes 4 servings.
description: A comforting bowl of hearty pasta with salad oil or shortening and minced onions — saucy, satisfying, and ready to become a weeknight staple.
title: Career Girl's Spaghetti

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 4 pork chops (about 1 lb/500 gr.) 1 large red or green pepper,cut in strips 1 can Condensed Tomato with Basil &amp;Oregano Soup ¼ cup water 1 Tbsp vinegar 1 Tbsp packed brown sugar 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce 3 cups hot cooked mini potatoes Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add pork chops and cook until browned on both sides. Set pork chops aside. Add pepper strips and stir fry – about 3 minutes. Drain fat. Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in mixture of soup, water, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and brown sugar. Heat to a boil. Return pork chops to skillet.
Simmer cover, at medium-low heat until pork is cooked through – about 10 minutes. Serve with potatoes.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4
description: Tender, juicy savory pork with vegetable oil and pork chops — caramelized edges, unbelievable flavor, and the kind of aroma that makes the neighbors jealous.
title: Campbell's Zesty Tomato Pork Chops

content:
Ingredients 4 tsp vegetable oil 1 can Broccoli &amp; Cheese Soup 8 eggs 2 cups cooked, chopped broccoli dash ground black pepper grated Parmesan cheese Instructions Mix eggs gradually into soup with whisk or fork. Stir in half the broccoli and pepper.
Heat 1 tsp oil in non-stick 10” skillet or omelette pan, over medium heat
Add ¾ cup egg mixture. As it begins to set, lift edges allowing uncooked egg mixture to flow to bottom. Cook until set but still moist. Top one half with ¼ cup broccoli.
Fold omelette in half using spatula and slide onto plate. Sprinkle with cheese. Repeat for each of 4 omelettes. Serve with crusty bread.
Prep/cook time: 15 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Savory, colorful vibrant vegetables with vegetable oil and broccoli & cheese soup — proof that the humble veggie side can absolutely steal the show.
title: Campbell's Speedy Broccoli Cheese Omelettes

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil ¾ lb ground beef, cooked and drained ¼ cup diced onions ¼ cup diced celery 1 can Tomato w/Basil &amp; Oregano Soup ¾ cup water 5 cups hot cooked spaghetti ¼ cup grated Romano or Parmesan cheese Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet and stir fry onions and celery until tender.
Stir in mixture or soup, water and cooked ground beef. Bring to boil; reduce heat to low.
Simmer, covered, for 10 minutes. Serve over spaghetti or toss together. Sprinkle with cheese.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4
description: Homemade satisfying sauce with vegetable oil and ¾ lb ground beef — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Campbell's Shortcut Spaghetti & Meat Sauce

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil ½ cup chopped red or green pepper ½ cup chopped celery 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup 1/3 cup milk2 cups cooked chicken, cubed 1 cup frozen green peas dash ground black pepper, optional 4 cups hot cooked rice or pasta Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add pepper and celery and stir fry until tender crisp. Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in mixture of soup and milk Add chicken, peas and pepper. Heat through, stirring occasionally. Serve over rice or pasta. If desired, garnish with fresh parsley.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Succulent robust chicken with vegetable oil and ½ cup chopped red or green pepper that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Campbell's Shortcut Chicken a la King

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lb boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak, cut in strips 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup 1-1/2 cups frozen green peas 1 large onion, chopped ½ tsp Worcestershire saucee ¾ sup sour cream 1/8 tsp ground black pepper 4 cups hot cooked egg noodles Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add beef and cook until browned and cooked through. Add onions and stir fry until tender. Reduce heat to medium
Combine soup, peas and Worcestershire sauce and add to skillet. Heat to a boil. Simmer, covered – about 5 minutes. Reduce heat to low.
Stir in sour cream and pepper. Heat through. Serve with noodles.
Prep/cook time: 25 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Hearty, satisfying satisfying beef with vegetable oil and boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak — the kind of meal that brings everyone to the table before you've even finished plating.
title: Campbell's Shortcut Beef Stroganoff

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lb boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak, cut in strips 1 medium green pepper,cut in strips 1 medium onion, sliced 1 can Condensed Tomato Soup ¼ cup orange juice 2 Tbsp soya sauce 1 Tbsp vinegar 1 tsp garlic powder 4 cups hot cooked rice Instructions Heat half the oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add beef and stir fry until cooked through. Reduce heat to medium. Set beef aside.
Add remaining oil. Add pepper and onion and cook until tender-crisp.
Stir in mixture of soup, orange juice, soya sauce, vinegar and garlic powder. Return beef to skillet. Heat through, stirring occasionally. Serve over rice.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: A robust robust beef dinner with vegetable oil and boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak that's big on flavor and bigger on satisfaction. The entrée everyone remembers.
title: Campbell's Orange Beef Stir-Fry

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp all-purpose flour 1 Tbsp chili powder 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves (about 1 lb/500 gr.) 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lg red or green pepper, cut in strips 1 can Fiesta Tomato or Tomato Soup 1 cup corn niblets ¼ cup shredded Cheddar or Monterey 4 cups hot cooked rice Jack cheese Instructions Mix flour and chili powder. Coat chicken with flour mixture.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside, Drain fat.
Stir in mixture of pepper strips, soup and corn; heat to a boil. Return chicken to skillet. Reduce heat to low. Simmer, covered, until chicken is cooked through – about 5 minutes. Sprinkle with cheese and serve with rice.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with chili powder and boneless — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Campbell's Easy Santa Fe Chicken

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves,(about 1 pound/500 grams) 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup 2 tsp paprika (or to taste) ½ medium red pepper, cut in strips 2 cups hot cooked broccoli florets 1/3 cup sour cream or plain yogurt 4 cups hot cooked egg noodles Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside. Reduce heat to medium.
Add soup, paprika and red pepper strips to skillet. Heat to a boil, stirring occasionally. Return chicken to skillet; add broccoli. Reduce heat to low. Simmer, covered, until chicken is cooked through – about 5 minutes.
Stir in sour cream and heat through. Serve with noodles.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with vegetable oil and boneless — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Campbell's Easy Chicken Broccoli Paprikash

content:
Ingredients 3 cups hot cooked broccoli spears 2 cups cooked chicken, cubed* 1 can Cream of Broccoli Soup 1/3 cup milk ½ cup shredded Cheddar cheese 3 Tbsp dry bread crumbs 1 Tbsp margarine or butter, melted Instructions *For Easy Turkey &amp; Broccoli Divan, substitute turkey for chicken.
Arrange broccoli and chicken in 9-inch pie plate or 2 quart shallow baking dish. Combine soup and milk; pour over broccoli and chicken.
Sprinkle mixture with cheese.
Mix bread crumbs with margarine and sprinkle over cheese layer. Bake at 450 F. for 20 minutes, or until heated through. If desired, serve with rice.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Juicy, flavorful robust chicken with hot cooked broccoli spears and cooked chicken — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Campbell's Easy Chicken & Broccoli Divan

content:
Ingredients 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves 1 Tbsp oil 1 can Condensed Cream of Chicken Soup 1/2 cup water 3 Tbsp Dijon-style mustard 1 Tbsp honey Instructions Brown 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.
Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Chicken Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water, 3 Tbsp Dijon-style mustard and 1 Tbsp honey. Cover and simmer until chicken is cooked through.
Serves 4.
description: Chicken breasts simmered in a sweet and tangy honey mustard cream sauce
title: Campbell's 2-Step Honey Mustard Chicken

content:
Ingredients 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves 1 Tbsp oil 1 can Condensed Cream of Broccoli Soup 1/2 cup water 1 Tbsp lemon juice Lemon slices for garnish Instructions Brown 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.
Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Broccoli Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water and 1 Tbsp lemon juice. Cover and simmer until chicken is cooked through. Garnish with lemon slices.
Serves 4.
description: Tender, perfectly seasoned rustic chicken with boneless and condensed cream of broccoli soup — proof that a great chicken dinner never goes out of style.
title: Campbell's 2-Step Lemon Broccoli Chicken

content:
Ingredients 4 pork chops 1 Tbsp oil 1 can Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup 1/2 cup water 1/2 tsp garlic powder (optional) Instructions Brown 4 pork chops in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.
Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water. If desired, add 1/2 tsp garlic powder. Cover and simmer until pork chops are cooked through.
Serves 4.
description: Savory, golden comforting pork with pork chops and condensed cream of mushroom soup that's ridiculously easy to make and even more ridiculously delicious to eat.
title: Campbell's 2-Step Mushroom Pork Chops

content:
Ingredients 1 lb ground beef 1 can Tomato Soup 1 cup salsa Cheese for topping Rolls for serving Instructions Brown 1 lb ground beef.
Add 1 can Tomato Soup and 1 cup salsa. Heat through. Top with cheese.
Serve on rolls. Makes 8 sandwiches.
description: A robust robust beef dinner with ground beef and tomato soup that's big on flavor and bigger on satisfaction. The entrée everyone remembers.
title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Beefy Taco Joes

content:
Ingredients 4 boneless chicken breasts 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup 1/2 cup water Instructions Brown 4 boneless chicken breasts.
Add 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup and 1/2 cup water. Cover and simmer until done.
Serves 4.
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with boneless chicken breasts and cream of mushroom soup cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Chicken

content:
Ingredients 1 lb boneless chicken breasts, cut up 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup 1/2 cup water Instructions Brown 1 lb cut-up boneless chicken breasts.
Add 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend, 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup and 1/2 cup water. Simmer until done.
Serves 4.
description: Hearty comforting pasta with boneless chicken breasts and frozen vegetable pasta blend — the kind of meal you want to curl up on the couch with. Simple, satisfying, and just right.
title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Creamy Chicken & Pasta

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves,(about 1 lb/500 gr.) 1-1/4 cups Campbell’s Ready to UseBroth or 1 can Condensed ChickenBroth. 1 medium onion, chopped ¾ cup orange juice 1 cup uncooked regular long grain rice 1-1/2 cups fresh (cut-up) or frozen greenbeans 1 tsp grated orange peel 3 Tbsp chopped fresh parsley or l Tbsp dried parsley flakes Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside.
Stir in broth, orange juice and onion. Heat to a boil. Stir in rice beans and orange peel. Reduce heat to low. Cover and cook – about 10 minutes. Return chicken to skillet.
Simmer covered, until chicken is cooked through and rice has absorbed most of liquid – about 10 minutes. Remove chicken to platter. Stir parsley into rice mixture. If desired, garnish with orange peel.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Juicy, flavorful comforting chicken with vegetable oil and boneless — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Campbell's Citrus Skillet Chicken & Rice

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lb Boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in cubes 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup 1 cup water 1 pkg (6 oz/165 g) seasoned long-grainrice mix 2 cups thinly sliced carrots 2 green onions, sliced Instructions Heat oil at medium-high heat in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in mixture of soup, water, rice mix and carrots. Heat to a boil. Reduce heat to low.
Simmer covered, until chicken is cooked through and rice has absorbed most of the liquid (about twenty minutes), stirring occasionally. Add green onions for last 5 minutes.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4
description: Succulent comforting chicken with vegetable oil and boneless that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Campbell's Chicken & Vegetable Rice Skillet

content:
Ingredients 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts,cut in chunks 1 can Condensed Cream of Celery Soup 2-1/4 cups water2 cups fresh (cut-up) or frozen veggie mix ½ tsp dried basil leaves 2 cups uncooked rotini pasta Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and stir fry until cooked through.
Stir in mixture of soup, water, vegetables and basil; heat to a boil, stirring often.
Stir in pasta. Cook at medium-low heat, until pasta is tender, stirring often – about 15 minutes. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese, if desired.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Succulent hearty chicken with vegetable oil and boneless that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Campbell's Chicken & Vegetable Rotini

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 8 mini red-skinned potatoes, thinly sliced 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in strips 1 can Cream of Broccoli Soup ½ cup milk ¼ tsp garlic powder 2 cups fresh or frozen broccoli florets 1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese Instructions Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add potato slices and stir fry until tender crisp.
Add chicken and continue to stir fry until chicken is browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in mixture of soup, milk and garlic powder. Arrange broccoli on top; heat to a boil. Simmer, covered, over low heat until chicken is cooked through and broccoli is tender – about 5 minutes. Stir in Parmesan cheese.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: Succulent hearty chicken with vegetable oil and mini red-skinned potatoes that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Campbell's Broccoli Chicken Potato Parmesan

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 lb boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak, cut in strips 2 cups sliced mushrooms 1 medium onion, sliced 1 can Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup ½ cup water 2 Tbsp Dijon-style mustard 4 cups hot cooked rice Instructions Heat half the oil in large skillet over medium high heat. Add beef and brown until cooked through. Set beef aside. Reduce heat to medium.
Add remaining oil. Add mushrooms and onion and stir fry until tender.
Stir in mixture of soup, water and mustard. Heat to a boil. Return beef to skillet and heat through. Serve with rice. If desired, garnish with fresh parsley.
Prep/cook time: 25 minutes. Serves 4.
description: A robust robust beef dinner with vegetable oil and boneless beef sirloin or inside roundsteak that's big on flavor and bigger on satisfaction. The entrée everyone remembers.
title: Campbell's Beef & Mushroom Skillet

content:
Ingredients 1 egg ½ cup milk 1 cup Bisquick 2 tbsp yellow corn meal ¼ tsp paprika ½ tsp dry mustard 1/8 tsp cayenne pepper 1 lb (12) frankfurters Instructions Heat deep fat to 375 degrees. Blend egg and milk. Stir in dry ingredients. Dip frankfurters into batter. Fry until brown – 2 to 3 minutes
NOTE: Do not use previously frozen wieners as batter will not stick to them.
NEAT TRICK: Push wooden skewers into end of Batter Franks for eating with fingers.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with ½ cup milk and bisquick — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Batter Franks

content: Ingredients 4 lbs spareribs ¼ cup vinegar 1 cup sliced onions ¼ cup brown sugar 1 cup ketchup 2 tsp dry mustard 1 cup water 1 tsp paprika 2 tsp salt 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce Instructions Cut ribs into serving pieces. Brown them in frying pan. Combine the remaining ingredients and pour over ribs. Cover. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 1-3/4 hrs. Spoon sauce over the ribs two or three times during baking. Then uncover and cook for 15 minutes.
description: Rich, chewy sweet squares packed with spareribs, ¼ cup vinegar, and sliced onions. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Barbecued Spareribs

content: Ingredients ¾ lb lean ground beef 1 medium onion, chopped 1 28-oz tin diced tomatoes 1 10 oz tin beef broth 1 14 oz tin kidney beans 1 medium green pepper, chopped 1 tbsp chili powder ½ tsp cumin 2/3 cup long grain rice ¾ cup shredded cheddar cheese Instructions Brown beef with onion in large skillet. Drain off fat. Stir in tomatoes, ½ cup reconstituted beef broth (half broth, half water) with 28-oz tin tomatoes, kidney beans, green pepper, chili powder and cumin. Bring to a boil. Stir in rice. Pour into a 3-quart (3L) casserole. Cover. Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes. Uncover and stir. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake uncovered 10 minutes longer or until rice is tender.
description: A hearty pot of rustic chili with ¾ lb lean ground beef and medium onion that's perfect for game day, cold nights, or any time you need something warm and satisfying.
title: Baked Chili

content:
1 lb lean boneless pork chops, cut into thin strips 1 clove garlic, minced 1 tsp minced fresh ginger or ½ tsp powdered ginger 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 pkg Polynesian mix veggies Homemade Sauce: Ingredients 1 clove garlic, minced 1 tsp minced fresh ginger or 1/2tsp powdered ginger 1-1/2 Tbsp corn starch 1/2 tsp powdered ginger ½ cup fruit juice or broth 1 Tbsp each sugar and ketchup 2 Tbsp each vinegar and soya sauce Instructions Combine pork, garlic and ginger; season with salt and pepper to taste. Mix well. In large frying pan or wok over high heat, heat half of the oil. Add pork. Stir-fry 2 minutes. Remove pork from pan. Set aside.
Over high heat, heat remaining oil. Add garlic and ginger for homemade sauce. Add Polynesian mix. Cover. Cook 2 minutes stirring often. Combine remaining ingredients and pour over vegetables. Cook over medium heat, stirring until vegetables are CRISP-TENDER, about 3 minutes. Add pork. Heat through. Makes about 5 servings..
description: Succulent rustic pork with lean boneless pork chops and clove garlic — slow-cooked (or quickly seared) to melt-in-your-mouth perfection. Every bite is a flavor bomb.
title: Aloha Sweet & Sour Pork

content:
Ingredients 4 or 5 potatoes 1 to 1-1/2 cups flour Instructions Bake 4 or 5 potatoes in the skin until soft inside. Peel and discard the peel. Mash potatoes (Use a potato ricer to get a nice fine even texture. Combine 1 to 1-1/2 cups flour to every 2 cups of mashed potato and form a dough.
The author’s son likes to play with this dough just so he can eat the scraps. It has no raw egg in it and leaving it out for a bit, there is nothing in it to spoil. The color will gray after a while, but you add food coloring. When you make it, you won’t even notice. If you have extra, roll it into balls and press your thumb in one side and roll it across the back side of a fork. Cook in boiling water for 1 minute and serve either with tomato sauce or butter or even brown gravy.
description: Creamy, comforting rustic potatoes with or 5 potatoes and to 1-1/2 cups flour — the ultimate side dish that makes everything else on the plate better.
title: Potato "Playdough"

content:
PLAY DOUGH w/Kool-Aid
1 cup flour 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 pkg unsweetened Kool-Aid ¼ cup salt 2 Tbsp cream of tartar 1 cup water Mix flour, salt, cream of tartar and Kool-Aid in a medium saucepan. Add water and oil. Stir over medium heat 3 – 5 minutes. When mixture forms a ball in pan, remove from heat. Knead until smooth. Put in a plastic bag and refrigerate.
EDIBLE PEANUT BUTTER PLAYDOUGH
Ingredients ½ cup peanut butter ½ cup honey 1 cup powdered milk Instructions Knead until smooth
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with vegetable oil and unsweetened kool-aid that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Playdough

content:
BAKEABLE “SALT CLAY”
Stir together:
2 cups plain flour 1 cup salt 1 to 1-1/2 cups cold water 2 Tbsp Cooking oil Knead well until it forms a soft ball. Line a baking tray with aluminum foil, make the shapes you want, and bake in the oven at 300 degrees F or until hard (about one hour.)
You can use food coloring to color dough before you bake it, or paint it afterwards. A finishing coat of shellac or clear acrylic will make your salt clay products last longer.
Use cookie cutters or mold salt clay by hand, then bake in a 300 degree oven for use in creating refrigerator magnets, jewelry, ornaments, key chains, necklaces, and more.
CLAY ORNAMENT DOUGH – good for ornaments
Ingredients 4 cups flour 1 cup salt 1-1/2 cups water 1 tsp powdered alum Instructions It says to bake on 250 degrees for 30 minutes and flip for 1-1/2 hours. The author left hers sitting in the classroom for a few hours and then baked them for 30 minutes and they were already hard.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with plain flour and to 1-1/2 cups cold water — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Ornament Clay Dough

content:
FRONTIER KITCHEN CHEMICAL GARDEN
Mix:
Ingredients 6 Tbsp liquid bluing 6 Tbsp salt 6 Tbsp water Instructions Add 1 Tbsp ammonia
Mix well and pour slowly over 3 or 4 small rocks, clinkers or coal in an old pie plate (or a small fish bowl). Crystals start to grow very soon and will continue as long as the liquid stays.
If you want a little colour; add a few drops cake colouring to the rocks. Set the pan where you can watch it daily.
description: A delicious homemade satisfying recipe with liquid bluing — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Frontier Kitchen Chemical Garden

content:
BAKER’S CLAY INSTRUCTIONS – Brown Bag Cookie Art
You can make really nice ornaments out of Baker’s Clay. They are colorful, durable, easy, and fun to make. This is another project that is ideal to do with children.
4 cups unsifted all-purpose flour 1 cup table salt 1-1/2 cups cold water vegetable oil pastry brush toothpicks acrylic or tempera paints brushes* spray gloss polyurethane or shellac Ingredients narrow ribbons Instructions Mix the flour and alt together in a large bowl. Add the water and stir until the flour has been incorporated. Turn the dough out onto a smooth counter top and knead vigorously for about 5 minutes. If you don’t plan to use your Baker’s Clay right away, seal it in an airtight plastic bag so that the surface doesn’t dry and form a crust.
Preheat your oven to 225 degrees. Lightly oil your cookie molds. Break off a piece of Baker’s Clay and press it firmly into a mold, using the heel of your hand to make the back nice and smooth. Make the Baker’s Clay “cookies” on the thin side; they will cook more evenly.
To unmold the dough, hold the filled mold perpendicular to a wooden board and strike the edge sharply several times. If the dough doesn’t come right out, rotate the mold and strike the edge again a couple of times. The Baker’s Clay should peel out easily. Place the formed dough on a baking sheet, re-oil your mold, and continue until all of your dough is used.
Now take a toothpick and make a small hole in your Baker’s Clay cookies so that you will be able to string them with a ribbon after they are baked. Once baked, the ornaments will be too hard for you to be able to make a hole. If you forget, you can glue ribbons to the back of the ornaments with a hot glue gun, and hang them that way.
Bake the ornaments for 1 to 2 hours in the middle of the oven. They are done when they are dry and hard, and an even buff color. Cool on a rack.
Once the Baker’s Clay ornaments are completely cool, spray the lightly with polyurethane to seal them. Paint …
description: Warm, satisfying comforting casserole with unsifted all-purpose flour and table salt that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Baker's Clay Instructions

content:
Ingredients 6 cups water 2/3 cup sugar ½ cup packed brown sugar ½ cup pickling salt 1/3 cup soya sauce 2 cloves garlic, crushed 1 tbsp pepper 2 bay leaves, crushed Instructions In 5-qt glass or plastic container, combine brine ingredients. Stir until sugar and salt are dissolved. Add fillets to brine. Cover and refrigerate 12 hours or overnight. Drain; discard brine from fillets. Rinse with water. Pat dry with paper towels. Arrange fillets on cooling racks. Aair dry for 1 hour or until fillets are shiny and dry.
Place oven thermometer in smoker. Heat dry smoker for 20 minutes, or until temperature registers 100 F. Spray smoker racks with nonstick vegetable cooking spray. Arrange fillets on prepared racks, spacing at least ½ “ apart.
Smoke fillets according to smoker manufacturer’s directions (approx. 6 to 8 hours), or until fish is firm and opaque and internal temperature registers 180 degrees F. in flakiest part of fillets, adding wood chips as necessary to impart desired flavor and to maintain desired level of smoke. Store smoked fish, loosely wrapped, in refrigerator no longer than 2 weeks. Serve with crackers.
description: Crispy on the outside, melt-in-your-mouth tender inside — this comforting fish with ½ cup packed brown sugar and ½ cup pickling salt is weeknight fancy at its absolute best.
title: Smoked Whitefish

content:
Ingredients 15-1/2-oz tin canned salmon 1 cup liquid (liquid from salmon plus milk) ½ cup soft bread crumbs 1 tsp grated onions ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp lemon juice 1 tbsp chopped parsley 2 eggs, slightly beaten ½ tsp garlic salt Instructions Flake salmon and add salmon liquid and milk. Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.
Pour into a greased 8-inch pie pan and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 – 50 minutes or until center is solid when tested with a knife.
Makes 6 servings.
description: Flaky, golden crust cradling a rich filling with tin canned salmon and liquid — the kind of pie that earns a permanent spot on the holiday table.
title: Salmon Pie

content:
Ingredients 3 Tbsp butter 3-1/2 cups milk 1 onion, finely chopped 7-1/2 oz tin salmon of 1 qt canned lake trout 1 stalk celery, chopped salt and pepper 3 Tbsp flour Instructions Melt butter in a saucepan and sauté onion and celery. Add flour and stir well. Add milk slowly, stirring constantly. Add salmon and season with salt and pepper. Cook over low heat until creamy and smooth.
Reheat gently if served a t later date. Serves 4. Makes a hearty first course or a full meal when served with fresh biscuits and salad.
description: A soul-warming bowl of savory soup with onion and tin salmon of 1 qt canned lake trout — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Salmon Bisque

content: Ingredients 4 cups water 2 cups soya sauce 2 cups apple juice 1 cup packed brown sugar ½ cup pickling salt 2 tbsp whole black peppercorns 1 tsp garlic powder 1 tsp onion powder 3 lbs lake trout, or substitute fillets (8 oz each), 1 inch thick, skin on Instructions Same instructions as recipe for Smoked Whitefish.
description: Light, flaky hearty fish with soya sauce and apple juice — perfectly cooked with a golden crust and tender, moist flesh inside. Elegant made easy.
title: Mary's Guide Service Secret Smoked Fish Recipe

content:
4 large potatoes 1 tsp thyme 2 medium onions ¼ tsp basil 1 to 1-1/2 lb fish fillets, deboned ½ tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper SAUCE:
Ingredients ¼ cup butter ½ tsp salt ¼ cup flour Dash of pepper 2 cup milk ½ tsp dry mustard 1 cup grated Cheddar cheese (or Cheez Whiz) Instructions Boil 4 large potatoes until almost cooked. Slice and layer in a greased 9x13-inch dish. Slice a thin layer of onion over potato. Place a layer of thinly sliced fillet (uncooked &amp; deboned) over.
Sprinkle thyme and basil over fish as well as salt and pepper.
SAUCE: Mix thoroughly the butter and flour. Add milk and cheese. Add salt and pepper and ½ teaspoon dry mustard. Pour over top of fish. Bake, covered lightly with tin foil, for 1 hour at 350 degrees.
description: Delicate, flavorful bold fish with large potatoes and thyme that comes together quickly and tastes like a night out. Seafood done right.
title: Fish Scallop

content:
Ingredients 7 lbs chopped pike 1 cup vegetable oil 1 cup vinegar 1 cup tomato soup 1-1/2 tbsp salt 1 tbsp pepper garlic powder – optional Parsley – optional Instructions Mix last seven ingredients together. Pour over chopped fish and leave in refrigerator overnight.
In the morning, bake at 250 degrees for 5 hours. Pack in sterilized jars.
description: Delicate, flavorful rustic fish with chopped pike and vegetable oil that comes together quickly and tastes like a night out. Seafood done right.
title: Fake Salmon

content:
Ingredients 1 cup crushed cheese-flavored crackers ¼ cup sesame seed ½ cup butter or margarine 1 tbsp snipped fresh parsley 2-1/4 lbs catfish fillets, skin removed ½ tsp salt 2 tbsp shredded fresh Parmesan cheese ¼ tsp pepper ¼ tsp cayenne Instructions Heat oven to 400 F. In shallow dish, combine crackers, sesame seed, parsley, salt, pepper and cayenne. Set aside.
In 1-quart saucepan, melt margarine over medium heat. Pour into another shallow dish. Dip each fillet first in margarine and then in crumb mixture, pressing lightly to coat.
Arrange fillets in single layer in 13 x 9 baking dish. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until fish is firm and opaque and just begins to flake. Sprinkle fillets evenly with Parmesan cheese.
description: Warm, satisfying comforting casserole with crushed cheese-flavored crackers and ¼ cup sesame seed that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Cheesy Baked Catfish

content: Ingredients 1 lb fish fillets (Cod) 1/2 cup shredded Cheddar cheese 1 can cream of mushroom soup Dash of paprika Salt and Pepper Instructions Arrange fish in greased shallow baking dish and stir soup over fish. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cheese. Bake at 375 degrees for 45 minutes.
description: Hearty bold casserole packed with fish fillets, shredded cheddar cheese, and cream of mushroom soup — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Cheese Fish Bake

content:
Ingredients 1 tbsp paprika 2-1/2 tsp salt 1 tsp onion powder 1 tsp garlic power 1 tsp cayenne ¾ tsp white pepper ¾ tsp black pepper ½ tsp dried thyme leaves, crushed ½ tsp dried oregano leaves 2-1/4 lbs catfish, lake trout or substitute fillets (6 oz each) skin removed, cut in half crosswise. Instructions Build a campfire and allow it to burn down to glowing coals. In shallow bowl, combine seasoning ingredients. Set aside. Place cast-iron skillet on cooking grate over campfire. When drop of water flicked onto skillet dances across it, skillet is hot enough. Dredge both sides of each fillet in seasoning mixture.
Squarely drop fillets into skillet. Cook for 2 minutes on each side, or until fish is firm and opaque and just begins to flake.
NOTE: Dad dips the fish in melted butter, sprinkles the Cajun seasoning mixture on and then puts the fish in skillet is not greased. This recipe was taken from Doug’s cookbook.
description: Light, flaky savory fish with paprika and onion powder — perfectly cooked with a golden crust and tender, moist flesh inside. Elegant made easy.
title: Blackened Fish

content: Ingredients 1 cup buttermilk 1 egg 1 tsp salt 1 tsp white pepper 1-1/2 cup cornmeal ½ cup flour
description: Delicate, flavorful rustic fish with buttermilk and white pepper that comes together quickly and tastes like a night out. Seafood done right.
title: Batter For Fish

content: Ingredients ¼ cup mayo ¼ cup white wine l tbsp lemon juice l clove garlic, crushed l/4 tsp cayenne pepper Thyme and dill, optional
description: A zesty BBQ fish coating with mayo, white wine, lemon juice, and garlic — perfect for grilling up tender, flavorful fish with a hint of cayenne kick.
title: B. B. Q. Fish Coating

content:
Ingredients 3 lbs fish fillet Dairy buttermilk (enough to cover) 1 lemon, sliced 1 pint (8 oz) oil for frying Dry pancake mix (for dredging) 2 cups pancake mix 2-1/2 cups club soda Instructions Soak fish fillets in buttermilk. Place lemon slices over them, cover, and refrigerate 2–3 hours to remove &ldquo;fishy&rdquo; taste.
Heat oil in a heavy 2-1/2 quart saucepan.
Drain off milk and cut fillets in halves (triangle shape). Dredge in dry pancake mix.
In another bowl, combine 2 cups pancake mix and 2-1/2 cups club soda to the consistency of buttermilk.
Dip floured fillets into batter, let excess drip off.
Deep fry 4 minutes each side. Keep warm in a 250°F oven without stacking until all are fried.
description: Crispy deep-fried fish fillets inspired by the classic Arthur Treacher's recipe — golden battered goodness with a satisfying crunch in every bite.
title: Arthur Treacher-Style Fish

content:
Ingredients 12 oz uncooked ziti or other medium pasta 2 pkg (16 oz) frozen broccoli, carrots, and water chestnuts 2 Tbsp olive or canola oil 2 Tbsp non-hydrogenated margarine 2 tsp dried basil 2 tsp dried marjoram 1 tsp dried thyme 3/4 tsp salt 4 tsp corn starch 1 cup chicken broth 1/2 cup dry white wine or more broth 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese Instructions Cook pasta according to package directions. Meanwhile, in a saucepan, bring 1 inch of water to a boil; place vegetables in a steamer basket over water. Cover and steam for 5-7 minutes or until tender.
In a Dutch oven or large saucepan, combine the oil, margarine, basil, marjoram, thyme and salt. Cook and stir over medium heat for 1 minute. Combine cornstarch, broth and wine or additional broth until smooth; stir into the pan. Bring to a boil; cook and stir for 2 minutes or until thickened.
Drain pasta. Add pasta and vegetables to the sauce; toss to combine. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Yield: 8 servings.
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (1 cup) equals 181 calories, 9 g fat (3 g saturated fat), 13 mg cholesterol, 500 mg sodium, 18 g carbohydrate, 3 g fiber, 6 g protein. Diabetic Exchanges: 1 starch, 1 lean meat, 1 fat.
description: Fresh, vibrant fresh vegetables with uncooked ziti or other medium pasta and frozen broccoli — cooked just right so they're tender but still have that satisfying snap.
title: Ziti With Vegetables

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup no-salt added tomato sauce 2 tsp minced garlic ¼ cup plus 2 Tbsp lemon juice 1-1/2 tsp prepared mustard 1-1/2 Tbsp Brown Sugar Twin ¾ tsp ground red pepper 3 Tbsp cider vinegar ¼ tsp black pepper 2 Tbsp low-sodium Worcestershire sauce 6 (6-oz) skinned chicken breast halves Instructions Combine first 9 ingredients in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes. Pour 1 cup tomato sauce mixture into a large heavy-duty, zip-top plastic bag. Set aside remaining tomato sauce mixture. Add chicken to bag; seal bag, and shake until chicken is well coated. Marinate in refrigerator 8 hours, turning occasionally. Remove chicken from marinade, discarding marinade. Coat grill rack with cooking spray; place on grill over medium-hot coals (350 to 400 degrees). Place chicken on rack, and grill, covered, 8 minutes on each side or until chicken is done, basting frequently with reserved tomato sauce mixture.
Transfer chicken to a large serving platter.
Yield: 6 servings. Diabetic Exchange per Serving:
3 very lean meat, 1 vegetable. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 150, Carbohydrate 3.7 g, Protein 25.4 g, Fat 3.0 g, Cholesterol 69 mg, Fiber 0.4 g, Sodium 81 mg.
description: Rich, chewy decadent squares packed with no-salt added tomato sauce, minced garlic, and ¼ cup plus. Cut them small because they're decadent, or cut them big because life is short.
title: Zesty Barbecued Chicken

content:
Enjoy the richness of mayonnaise with only a fraction of the energy in our smooth mayonnaise-like dressing made with tofu (bean curd) as the base.
Ingredients 1 cup drained, mashed tofu 2 Tbsp vinegar 1 tsp Dijon mustard 1 tsp soy sauce 1 tsp dried parsley ½ tsp garlic powder ½ tsp salt Instructions Combine tofu, vinegar, mustard, soy sauce, parsley, garlic powder and salt in container of blender or food processor. Process about 1 minute until smooth (or beat ingredients together until smooth). Place in screw-top jar. Store, covered, in the refrigerator up to 4 weeks.
Makes 24 servings, l-1/2 cups.
Each serving: 1 Tbsp.
Counts as 1 EXTRA 1 g protein, 4 calories.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with drained and vinegar. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Tofu Mayonnaise

content:
Tofu supplies the protein, and tastes similar to chicken breasts, this quick-to-prepare, colorful vegetarian main dish.
Ingredients 8 oz tofu 2 tsp vegetable oil ¼ cup chicken or vegetable broth ¼ tsp ground ginger 1 cup sliced celery 1 small onion, coarsely chopped ¼ sweet red pepper, slivered 1-1/2 cups fresh bean sprouts, washed 1 Tbsp soy sauce well drained Freshly ground pepper and salt Instructions Drain tofu; cut into ¼ “ pieces, place between layers of paper towel and weight down with a dinner plate; Let stand 10 minutes to compress and remove excess water. NOTE: I found this wasted too many paper towels; I placed tofu between two dinner plates, put a heavy weight on top and kept tipping the plates to pour off the water. It didn’t take long.). Heat oil, 2 Tbsp broth and ginger in a frypan. Add celery, onion and red pepper; stir-cook over medium heat 3 min.
Add bean sprouts; continue stir-cooking 1 min. Stir in remaining broth, soy sauce and sprouts. Cook and stir gently over medium heat until vegetables are tender-crisp, and liquid is evaporated. Season to taste with pepper and salt.
Makes 2 servings. Each serving: about 1-1/4 cup. 13 g carbohydrate, 13 g protein, 10 g fat,
194 calories.
= 2 Protein Choices, 1 Fruits &amp; Vegetables Choice, 1 Fats &amp; Oils Choice
description: Succulent bold pork with tofu and vegetable oil — slow-cooked (or quickly seared) to melt-in-your-mouth perfection. Every bite is a flavor bomb.
title: Tofu Chop Suey

content:
