Ingredients 2 cans (l4-1/2 oz each) stewed tomatoes 1 can (19 oz) garbanzo beans or chickpeas, rinsed and drained 1 can (15-1/2 oz) great northern beans, rinsed and drained 1 can (15 oz) butter beans, rinsed and drained 1 cup finely chopped carrots 1 cup finely chopped onion 2 garlic cloves, minced 1 bay leaf 2 tsp dried parsley flakes 1 tsp dried basil ½ tsp dried thyme ½ tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions In an ungreased 3-qt baking dish, combine all ingredients. Cover and bake at 350 for 60-70 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally. Discard bay leaf before serving.
Yield: 9 servings. Nutritional Analysis: One serving (3/4 cup) equals 197 calories, 1 g fat (trace saturated fat), 0 cholesterol, 687 mg sodium, 41 g carbohydrate, 9 g fiber, 10 g protein.
Diabetic Exchanges: 2 starch, 2 vegetables
Note: High salt content – Could be from canned vegetables.
description: A thick, hearty satisfying stew with stewed tomatoes and garbanzo beans or chickpeas — slow-simmered until everything is fall-apart tender and the gravy is rich as velvet.
title: Three-Bean Cassoulet

content:
Ingredients 1-1/3 lbs red potatoes, unpeeled 1 small clove garlic ¾ tsp dried tarragon ½ cup no-oil Italian dressing ½ tsp dried parsley flakes 2 Tbsp minced green onions ½ tsp dry mustard ¾ tsp lemon juice Instructions Cook potatoes in boiling water to cover 15 minutes or until tender. Drain well, and cool. Cut into ¼” slices, and set aside. Combine tarragon, parsley, mustard and garlic in container of an electric blender. Cover and process 30 seconds or until pureed. Add Italian dressing and process until blended. With blender running, slowly add lemon juice, and continue to process dressing mixture until smooth. Add dressing mixture to cooked potato slices; add minced green onions, and toss well to coat. Cover and chill. Yield: 8(1/2 cup) servings.
Diabetic exchange: 1 starch. Nutritional Value per serving: Calories 84, Carbohydrate 16 g,
Protein 2g, Fat 1g, Cholesterol 1 mg, Fiber 2g, Sodium 124 mg.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with red potatoes, small clove garlic, and ¾ tsp dried tarragon. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: Tarragon Potato Salad

content:
Ingredients 3 cups cold fat-free milk 3 cups sliced fresh strawberries 2 pkg (1 oz each) sugar-free instant white chocolate pudding mix 1 prepared angel food cake (14 oz), cut into 1” cubes 3 cups fresh raspberries 3 whole strawberries, quartered 1 carton (8 oz) reduced-fat frozen whipped topping, thawed Instructions In a bowl, whisk milk and pudding mix for 2 minutes or until slightly thickened. Place a third of the cake cubes in a trifle bowl or 3-1/2- qt serving bowl. Top with a third of the pudding, 1 cup sliced strawberries, 1-1/2 cups raspberries and a third of the whipped topping. Layer with a third of the cake and pudding, 1 cup strawberries and a third of the whipped topping. Top with remaining cake, pudding, strawberries, raspberries and whipped topping. Garnish with quartered strawberries. Serve immediately or refrigerate.
Yield: 14 servings.
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (1 cup) equals 170 calories, 3 g fat (2 g saturated fat), 1 mg cholesterol, 289 mg sodium, 32 g carbohydrate, 3 g fiber, 4 g protein. Diabetic Exchanges: 1 starch, 1 fruit.
description: Silky, decadent pudding with cold fat-free milk and sliced fresh strawberries — pure comfort in a bowl. The kind of dessert that makes everyone feel like a kid again.
title: Strawberry Raspberry Trifle

content:
Ingredients 6 cups chicken broth 6 medium potatoes, peeled &amp; shredded 4 medium carrots, shredded 4 celery ribs 1 large onion, chopped 1 large green pepper, diced 1 large sweet red pepper, diced 1 small sweet yellow pepper, diced ½ cup all-purpose flour 1-1/2 tsp salt 1 tsp Italian seasoning ¼ to ½ tsp pepper 1 cup water 4 cups 2% milk Instructions In a Dutch oven or soup kettle, combine the broth, potatoes, carrots, celery and onion; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Add the peppers; return to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until vegetables are tender. In a bowl, combine the flour, salt, Italian seasoning, Pepper and water until blended. Stir into the vegetable mixture. Bring to a boil, cook and stir for 2 minutes until thickened. Reduce heat. Stir in milk, heat through (do not boil).
Yield: 20 servings.
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (1 cup) equals 92 calories, 1 g fat (1 g saturated fat), 4 mg cholesterol, 500 mg sodium, 18 g carbohydrate, 2 g fiber, 5 g protein. Diabetic Exchanges: 2 vegetable, ½ starch.
Note: To reduce sodium content, boil your own chicken without salt to make broth.
description: Steaming bowls of robust soup packed with chicken broth, medium potatoes, and medium carrots. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Sweet Pepper Chowder

content:
These vegetables-filled bread cups can be refrigerated up to 2 hours before baking without getting soggy.
Ingredients 9 (1/2 oz) slices very thinly sliced whole wheat bread ½ cup light process cream cheese, softened Vegetable cooking spray 1 (2-oz) jar diced pimento, drained 1 (10-oz) pkg frozen chopped spinach, thawed 1 small clove garlic, minced 2 tsp reduced-calorie margarine 1 tsp hot sauce ½ cup finely chopped onion 1/8 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions Trim crusts from bread; reserve crusts for another use. Flatten bread slices, using a rolling pin. Cut slices into quarters. Press into miniature (1-1/3”) muffin pans coated with cooking spray. Bake at 350 degrees for 5 to 7 minutes or until lightly browned. Drain spinach, press between paper towels to remove excess moisture. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add margarine. Place over medium heat until margarine melts. Add onion, and sauté 4 to 5 minutes or until tender. Stir in spinach; cook 2 to 3 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Combine cream cheese and next 5 ingredients in a bowl; add spinach mixture, stirring well. Spoon into bread cups (2 tsp per cup). Bake at 350 degrees for 5 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
Yield: 3 dozen appetizers.
Diabetic Exchange per Appetizers: Free. Per Serving: Calories 19, Carbohydrate 2.3 g, Protein 0.9 g, Fat 0.8 g, Cholesterol 2 mg, Fiber 0.4 g, Sodium 51 mg
description: Simple, delicious zesty vegetables with slices very thinly sliced whole wheat bread and ½ cup light process cream cheese. Sometimes the best meals are built around what's growing in the garden.
title: Spinach Cups

content:
Ingredients Vegetable cooking spray ½ lb firm tofu, drained and crumbled 1-1/2 cup finely chopped onion 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes with puree, undrained 1-1/3 cup finely chopped green pepper 3 cloves garlic, minced 2 (15 oz) can red kidney beans, drained 1 Tbsp chili powder 7 oz wagon wheel pasta, uncooked 1 tsp ground cumin ¼ plus 3 Tbsp (1-3/4 oz) shredded reduced-fat Cheddar cheese ½ tsp dried crushed red pepper Instructions Coat a Dutch oven with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add onion, green pepper, and garlic; sauté until tender. Add chili powder and next 2 ingredients; sauté 1 minute. Stir in tofu and tomatoes. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, 15 minutes. Add beans; cook 10 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Cook pasta according to package directions, omitting salt and fat. Drain. To serve, place pasta in individual bowls; top with bean mixture and cheese.
Yield: 7 servings.
Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 3 starch, 2 vegetable, 1 fat. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 281, Carbohydrate 53.2 g, Protein 10.9 g, Fat 4.1 g, Cholesterol 1 mg, Fiber 5.1 g, Sodium 228 mg.
description: Rich, slow-simmered satisfying chili with vegetable cooking spray and ½ lb firm tofu. Top it with cheese and sour cream and let the warmth spread from your belly outward.
title: Spicy Chili Mac

content:
Ingredients Vegetable cooking spray ½ tsp ground cumin 1 lb ground round ¼ tsp salt 1 cup chopped onion ¼ tsp pepper 2 cloves garlic, chopped 1/8 tsp ground red pepper 1 (16 oz) can red kidney beans, rinsed and drained Dash hot sauce ½ can canned no-salt- added beef broth, 1 (14 oz) can no-salt- added whole undiluted tomatoes, undrained and chopped 2 tsp cornstarch 1-1/2 cup no-salt- added tomato juice 1/4 cup plus 2 Tbsp (1-1/2 oz) shredded 40% less-fat Cheddar cheese 1 tsp ground coriander Instructions Coat a Dutch oven with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add ground round; cook until meat is browned, stirring to crumble. Remove meat from pat; drain and pat dry with paper towels. Wipe drippings from pan with a paper towel. Coat Dutch oven with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic; sauté 5 minutes or until onion is tender. Return meat to Dutch oven. Add beans and next 6 ingredients; bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Combine beef broth and cornstarch, stirring well; add to beef mixture. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, and cook 1 minute or until thickened and bubbly. Ladle into individual bowls, sprinkle with cheese. Serve immediately.
Yield: 6 (1 cup) servings.
Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 2 lean meat, 1 starch, 1 vegetable. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 210, Carbohydrate 20.9 g, Protein 21.3 g, Fat 5.7 g, Cholesterol 46 mg, Fiber 2.0 g. Sodium 198 mg
description: A hearty pot of rustic chili with vegetable cooking spray and ½ tsp ground cumin that's perfect for game day, cold nights, or any time you need something warm and satisfying.
title: Spicy Beef Chili

content:
Ingredients Vegetable cooking spray ¼ tsp salt 1 lb freshly ground raw turkey 1-1/2 (6 oz) cans no-salt added tomato paste 3 Tbsp minced onion 6 oz spaghetti, uncooked 2 cup plus 3 Tbsp water 1 Tbsp freshly grated Parmesan cheese 2 tsp dried Italian seasoning ¼ tsp garlic powder Instructions Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add turley and onion; cook over medium heat until turkey is lightly browned, stirring to crumble. Drain and pat dry with paper towels. Wipe drippings from skillet with a paper towel. Add turkey mixture, water, and next 4 ingredients to skillet. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes or until thickened, stirring occasionally. Cook spaghetti according to package directions, omitting salt and fat; drain. Place on a serving platter; top with sauce. Sprinkle with cheese.
Yield: 6 (1-1/2 cup) servings.
Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 3 very lean meat, 2 starch. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 237, Carbohydrate 28.6 g, Protein 22.0 g, Fat 3.8 g, Cholesterol 44 mg, Fiber 1.4 g, Sodium 173 mg.
description: Tangy, savory satisfying sauce with vegetable cooking spray and ¼ tsp salt. Drizzle it, dip it, pour it — this one makes everything taste better.
title: Spaghetti With Savory Sauce

content:
Ingredients 14 fresh snow pea pods, trimmed 28 large cooked, peeled, and deveined shrimp Instructions Arrange pea pods in a vegetable steamer over boiling water. Cover and steam 1 minute or until crisp-tender. Remove pea pods and chill.
Separate pea pods lengthwise into 2 pieces. Wrap a pea pod half around each shrimp and secure with a pick.
Yield: 28 appetizers.
Diabetic Exchange: Free. Calories 7, Carbohydrate 0.1 g, Protein 1.4 g, Fat 0.1 g, Cholesterol 12 mg, Fiber 0 g, Sodium 14 mg.
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with fresh snow pea pods and large cooked — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Shrimp Dippers

content:
Ingredients 8 oz uncooked linguine 2 medium tomatoes, chopped 6 green onions, sliced ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese ¼ cup minced fresh basil or 4 tsp dried basil 2 garlic cloves, minced 1 tsp salt (reduce this, if necessary) ½ tsp pepper 3 Tbsp non-hydrogenated margarine Instructions Cook pasta according to package directions, Meanwhile, in a large serving bowl, combine the tomatoes, onions, Parmesan cheese, basil, garlic, salt and pepper. Drain pasta and toss with margarine. Add to tomato mixture, toss to coat.
Yield: 6 servings.
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (1 cup) equals 2ll calories, 9 g fat (5 saturated fat), 22 mg cholesterol, 680 mg sodium, 26 g carbohydrate, 2 g fiber, 8 g protein. DIABETIC EXCHANGES: 2 fat, 1-1/2 starch.
description: Tender pasta tossed with uncooked linguine, medium tomatoes, and green onions in a bold sauce that coats every strand. This is the dinner everyone asks for twice in one week.
title: Linguine With Fresh Tomatoes

content:
Ingredients 3 (2-oz) turkey breast cutlets ¼ tsp salt 2-1/2 Tbsp all-purpose flour ¼ tsp freshly ground pepper 2 tsp olive oil, divided 2 Tbsp lemon juice Instructions Place cutlets between two sheets of heavy-duty plastic wrap; flatten to 1/8-inch thickness, using a meat mallet or rolling pin. Dredge in flour; set aside. Heat 1 tsp oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat until hot. Add half of cutlets, and cook 3 minutes on each side or until browned. Transfer cutlets to a serving platter; keep warm. Repeat procedure with remaining 1 tsp oil and cutlets. Sprinkle cutlets with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Yield: 4 servings.
Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 4 very lean meat, ½ fat. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 179, Carbohydrate 4.5 g, Protein 26.8 g, Fat 5.2 g, Cholesterol 61 mg, Fiber 0 g, Sodium 203 mg.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with turkey breast cutlets and ¼ tsp salt that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Lemon Turkey Cutlets

content:
Flavor these dumplings with herbs like dill, caraway and basil for unique taste variations. These dumplings are not doughy at all, but become light and fluffy as they steam over simmering stew or soup.
Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour 2 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 1 Tbsp margarine or butter 1/3 cup 2% milk Instructions Combine flour, baking powder and salt. Cut in butter until mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add milk; stir until just moistened. Divide into 6 portions and spoon onto bubbling stew or soup. Cover tightly and simmer 15 minutes without lifting the lid.
Makes 6 small dumplings.
Each serving: 1 dumpling.
1 Starchy Choice. 14 g carbohydrate, 3 g protein, 2 g fat, 86 calories
description: A delicious homemade comforting recipe with ½ tsp salt and margarine or butter — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Fluffy Dumplings

content:
Call this a waist-watcher gravy. There are only 16 calories in one serving. Serve it over meatloaf, roast meat and mashed potatoes.
Ingredients 1 cup beef broth and defatted roast drippings combined 2 tsp all-purpose flour 1 tsp cornstarch 1 tsp ketchup Pinch each of salt, freshly ground pepper and basil Instructions Combine beef broth, flour and cornstarch in a screw-top jar, cover and shake until well combined. Pour into saucepan, stir in ketchip, salt, pepper and basil. Stir-cook over medium heat about 2 minutes until gravy thickens.
Makes 4 servings, 1 cup.
Each serving: ¼ cup – or 1 EXTRA 3 g carbohydrate, 1 g protein, 16 calories
description: A rich, flavorful hearty sauce with beef broth and defatted roast drippings combined and cornstarch that transforms anything it touches. The secret weapon every kitchen needs.
title: Fat-Free Gravy

content:
If you’d like to serve this as an appetizer, divide the crab mixture evenly into 16 small patties, These smaller versions will have about 31 calories each.
Ingredients 2 (6oz) cans lump crabmeat, drained 1 tsp pepper ½ cup finely chopped water chestnuts 1 tsp dark sesame oil ¼ cup soft breadcrumbs 3 Tbsp reduced-calorie margarine 1 Tbsp plus 1 tsp dried parsley flakes Vegetable cooking spray Instructions Combine first 7 ingredients in a small bowl; stir well. Shape into 8 patties. Coat a large. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add crabmeat patties and cook 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly browned on both sides, turning once.
Yield: 4 servings. Diabetic Exchange per serving: 2 lean meat, ½ starch. Nutritional Analysis per serving: Calories 129, Carbohydrate 6.6 g, Protein 13.7 g, Fat 5.0 g, Cholesterol 68 mg, Fiber 0.3 g, Sodium 283 mg
description: Rich, tender indulgent cake with cans lump crabmeat and ½ cup finely chopped water chestnuts that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Far East Crab Cakes

content:
Ingredients 1 250 gr tub spreadable cream cheese ½ to ¾ cup sour cream 1 cup salsa 1-1/2 cups grated Cheddar cheese 1 cup cubed cooked chicken (use left-over chicken or grill up a chicken breast) 1 bag bite-size Nacho chips Instructions Preheat oven of 350 degrees. Blend cream cheese with sour cream until mixture is spreadable. Spread mixture into a 6-cup oven-safe serving dish.
Spread salsa over mixture, using enough salsa to cover evenly. Arrange chicken, then the cheese evenly over salsa.
Bake until cheese melts and starts to bubble, about 25 minutes.
Serve with Nacho chips.
From the Ontario Chicken Lover “Wing” magazine – Number 12 in A Series.
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with tub spreadable cream cheese and ½ to ¾ cup sour cream — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Dip'n Chicken

content:
Ingredients 1 (16 oz) carton 1% low-fat cottage cheese ½ tsp dried dillweed 2 Tbsp tarragon vinegar 1 Tbsp skim milk 1 Tbsp finely chopped green onions Dash of coarsely ground pepper 1 Tbsp dried parsley flakes Fresh parsley sprigs (optional) Instructions Place cottage cheese and vinegar in container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine cottage cheese mixture, green onions, and next 4 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well. Transfer cheese mixture to a small serving bowl; cover and chill. If desired, garnish with parsley sprigs and serve with Shrimp Dippers.
Yield: 1-3/4 cup Diabetic Exchange: Free Nutritional Analysis: Calories 13, Carbohydrate 0.5 g, Protein 2.1 g, Cholesterol 1 mg, Fiber 0 g, Sodium 70 mg
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with carton 1% low-fat cottage cheese and ½ tsp dried dillweed — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Dilled Garden Dip

content:
Ingredients 2 egg whites, lightly beaten ¼ tsp salt 2 Tbsp water ¼ tsp pepper ¼ cup cornmeal 6 (4 oz) orange roughy fillets ¼ cup Parmesan cheese 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour ½ tsp dried oregano Vegetable cooking spray ½ tsp dried parsley flakes 1 Tbsp vegetable oil Instructions Combine egg whites and water in a shallow dish. Combine cornmeal and next 5 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well. Sprinkle each side of fillet with flour, Dip fillets into egg white mixture, and dredge in cornmeal mixture. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add oil. Place skillet over medium-high heat until hot. Add fillets, and cook 4 to 5 minutes on each side or until golden and fish flakes easily and flakes easily when tested with a fork.
Yield: 6 servings. Diabetic Exchanges per Serving: 2-1/2 lean meat, 1 vegetable. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 148, Carbohydrate 7.2 g, Protein 19.6 g, Fat 4.0 g, Cholesterol 25 mg, Fiber 0.7 g, Sodium 233 mg
description: Delicate, flavorful rustic fish with egg whites and ¼ tsp salt that comes together quickly and tastes like a night out. Seafood done right.
title: Crispy Orange Roughy (Fish)

content:
Ingredients ¼ cup plus 2 Tbsp frozen egg substitute, thawed ¼ tsp garlic powder ¼ tsp salt 1 Tbsp water ¼ tsp pepper 1 cup crispy rice cereal, crushed 1 (3-lb) broiler-fryer, cut up and skinned 1/3 cup toasted wheat germ ¼ all-purpose flour 1 Tbsp instant minced onion Vegetable cooking spray ½ tsp salt-free herb and spice blend Instructions Combine egg substitute and water in a shallow dish. Combine cereal and next 6 ingredients in a shallow dish. Place chicken and flour in a large zip-top plastic bag; seal bag, and shake until chicken is well coated. Dip chicken in egg substitute mixture; dredge in cereal mixture. Place chicken on rach of a broiler pan coated with cooking spray. Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 1 hour or until chicken is tender.
Yield: 6 servings. Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 3 very lean meat, 1 starch. Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 209, Carbohydrate 14.0 g, Protein 27.3 g, Fat 4.2 g, Cholesterol 76 mg, Fiber 1.3 g, Sodium 253 mg.
description: Succulent hearty chicken with ¼ cup plus 2 tbsp frozen egg substitute and ¼ tsp garlic powder that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Crispy Oven-Fried Chicken

content:
Ingredients 1 cup ketchup ½ cup reduced-fat mayonnaise 3 Tbsp cider vinegar 3 Tbsp honey 2 Tbsp water 1 Tbsp olive or canola oil 1 tsp lemon juice ½ tsp ground mustard ¼ tsp salt Salad greens &amp; vegetables of your choice Instructions In a blender or food processor, combine the first nine ingredients; cover and process until blended. Serve over salad. Refrigerate leftovers. Yield: 1-3/4 cups
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (2 Tbsp dressing) equals 70 calories, 4 g fat (1 g saturated fat), 3 mg cholesterol, 318 mg sodium, 10 g carbohydrate, trace fiber, trace protein.
Diabetic Exchanges: ½ starch, ½ fat
description: A tangy, homemade bright dressing with ketchup and ½ cup reduced-fat mayonnaise that elevates any salad from side dish to star. So much better than bottled.
title: Creamy French Dressing

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup light sour cream 1/3 cup light mayonnaise ¼ cup 1% milk ¼ cup Splenda Granular 2 Tbsp vinegar 1 Tbsp lemon juice 1 tsp bottled horseradish (Optional) Generous ¼ tsp salt (Optional) Instructions Per ¾ cup Serving of Coleslaw: Calories 80, Fat 4.5 gr (.5 saturated), Carbohydrate 8 gr, Fiber 2 gr, Protein 1 gr, Sodium 210 ml
Diabetic exchange = 1 Vegetable, 1 fat
WW = 2 points
Credit to the Splenda Cookbook.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with light sour cream and light mayonnaise — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Creamy Coleslaw

content:
Ingredients 4 medium round red potatoes, peeled and sliced ½ tsp salt 1 small onion, peeled and sliced 1 clove garlic, minced 4 green onions, coarsely chopped 1 cup skim milk 2 (20 oz) cans no-salt added chicken broth, undiluted 1/8 tsp ground white pepper 1/8 tsp ground nutmeg Instructions Combine first 5 ingredients in a 3-quart saucepan; cover and simmer 20 minutes or until potato is tender. Transfer potato mixture in batches to container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine pureed mixture with milk and remaining ingredients. Serve warm or chilled Yield: 9 (3/4 cup) servings.
Diabetic Exchange: 1 starch
Nutritional Value per Serving: Calories 65, Carbohydrate 14 g, Protein 2 g, Fat trace, Cholesterol trace, Fiber 1 g, Sodium 144 mg
description: Rich, comforting rustic soup with medium round red potatoes and ½ tsp salt that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Creamed Potato Soup

content:
Ingredients 3 green onions 2 Tbsp soya sauce 2 carrots 2 tsp Brown Sugar Twin 2 Tbsp sesame oil 1 Tbsp dry sherry 1 clove garlic, minced 3 lb whole pickerel, gutted &amp; cleaned 1 tsp finely grated ginger root (I (Mom) used ginger powder – about ¼ tsp) Instructions To prepare sauce, cut the green onions and carrots into 2-inch pieces. Then thinly slice these pieces into very thin julienne strips, set aside. Heat oil in a small frying pan. Add garlic and ginger and sauté for a minute or two. Add the remaining ingredients, except fish and stir until sugar is dissolved and mixture is heated through. Remove from heat. Stir in vegetables.
At this point, I had to guess what Mike Eliuk, Wildlife Management Officer and Conservation Officer from Sioux Lookout did with his pickerel.
I used Pickerel fillets, placed them in the pan with the veggies, spooned a bit of the mixture on top of them, covered the pan and, over low heat, finished cooking the veggies and fish. The flavor is excellent!
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with green onions and soya sauce. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Chinese-Style Whole Pickerel

content:
Ingredients 3-1/2 cup water 1 cup sliced carrot 1 Tbsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules 1 (8-oz)can mushroom stems and pieces, 1 (14-1/2 oz) can no-salt added whole drained tomatoes, undrained and chopped 1 cup diced zucchini ¼ cup dried minced onion 1 cup cooked chicken breast (skinned before cooking and cooked without salt or fat) 1 tsp dried basil 1 tsp paprika ¾ tsp minced garlic 2 Tbsp dry red wine Instructions Combine first 7 ingredients in a Dutch oven. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes. Add carrot; cover and simmer 10 minutes. Add mushrooms and remaining ingredients; simmer, uncovered, 8 minutes. Yield: 7 (1 cup) servings.
Diabetic Exchange per Serving: 1 very lean meat, 2 vegetable.
Nutritional Analysis per Serving: Calories 83, Carbohydrate 10.5 g, Protein 7.5 g, Fat 1.6 g, Cholesterol 16 mg, Fiber 1.2 g, Sodium 492 mg
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with sliced carrot, chicken-flavored bouillon granules, and can mushroom stems and pieces. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Chicken Vegetable Soup

content:
Ingredients 1 cup shredded cabbage ¼ cup vinegar 1 cup shredded red cabbage ¼ cup commercial no-oil Italian dressing ½ cup shredded carrot ¼ cup Splenda or Brown Sugar Twin ½ cup chopped green pepper 1/8 tsp salt ½ cup chopped sweet red pepper 1/8 tsp pepper ¼ cup chopped onion 1-1/2 tsp minced fresh parsley Instructions Combine first 7 ingredients in a large bowl, stirring well. Set aside. Combine vinegar and remaining ingredients in a jar; cover tightly, and shake vigorously. Pour dressing mixture over vegetables, tossing gently to coat. Cover and chill. To serve, stir coleslaw lightly, and serve with a slotted spoon. Yield: 8 (1/2) cup servings.
Nutritional Analysis: Per serving: Calories 22, Carbohydrate 4 g, Protein trace, Fat 1g, Cholesterol trade. EXCHANGE PER SERVING: 1 vegetable.
description: Crunchy, refreshing vibrant salad tossed with shredded cabbage, ¼ cup vinegar, and shredded red cabbage. Light enough for summer, hearty enough to stand on its own.
title: California Coleslaw

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cups water ¼ tsp ground mace 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen chopped broccoli, thawed 1 Tbsp cornstarch 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen cauliflower, thawed 3 cups skim milk, divided 1/3 cup chopped onion ½ tsp salt 2 tsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules 1/8 tsp pepper Instructions Combine first 5 ingredients in a Dutch oven; cover and bring to a boil. Cook 5 minutes or until (do not drain); stir in mace. Transfer in batches to container of an electric blender; cover and process until smooth. Return to Dutch oven. Dissolve cornstarch in ½ cup milk; stir into vegetable mixture. Add remaining 2-1/2 cups milk, salt and pepper; cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly, stirring frequently. Yield: 15 (1/2 cup) servings.
Diabetic Exchange: 1 vegetable Nutritional Analysis: Calories 32, Carbohydrate 5 g,
Protein 3 g, Fat trace, Cholesterol 1 mg, Fiber 1 g, Sodium 274 mg
description: Rich, comforting rustic soup with ¼ tsp ground mace and pkg frozen chopped broccoli that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Broccoli-Cauliflower Soup

content:
Ingredients 2 celery ribs, chopped 2 medium carrots, finely chopped 7 green onions, chopped 4 garlic cloves, minced 1 Tbsp non-hydrogenated margarine 1-1/2 cups uncooked medium pearl barley ½ tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 4-1/2 cups chicken broth, divided 2 Tbsp minced fresh parsley ½ cup slivered almonds Instructions In a large ovenproof Skillet, sauté the celery, carrots, onions and garlic in margarine until tender. Stir in the barley, salt and pa=epper. Stir in 2-1/2 cups broth. Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
Stir in Parsley and remaining broth. Sprinkle with almonds. Bake, uncovered, 30-40 minutes longer or until liquid is absorbed and barley is tender. Yield: 8 servings.
Nutritional Analysis: One serving (3/4 cup) equals 222 calories, 7 g fat (1 g saturated fat), 4 mg cholesterol, 711 mg sodium, 35 g carbohydrate, 8 g fiber, 7 g protein. Diabetic Exchanges: 2 starch, 1 lean meat.
Note: Sodium content will be greatly reduced if you boil your own chicken without salt to make broth.
description: A pan of melt-in-your-mouth squares with celery ribs and medium carrots that's dangerously easy to make and even more dangerous to leave unattended on the counter.
title: Barley Casserole

content:
Ingredients ½ cup vegetable oil 2 tsp baking powder ¼ cup granulated sugar substitute ½ tsp baking soda 1 egg ½ cup quick-cooking oats, uncooked ½ cup unsweetened applesauce ½ cup currants 1 tsp vanilla extract 1 tsp ground allspice ½ cup whole wheat flour Vegetable cooking spray ½ cup all-purpose flour Instructions Combine oil and sugar substitute in a bowl. Add egg; beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until fluffy. Stir in applesauce and vanilla. Combine whole wheat flour and next 6 ingredients; add to oil mixture, stirring well. Cover; chill 15 minutes. Drop by heaping teaspoon onto baking sheets coated with cooking spray. Bake at 375 degrees for 10 minutes.
Remove from sheets; let cool on wire racks. Yield: 26 cookies. Diabetic Exchanges per
Cookie: ½ starch, 1 fat. Nutritional Analysis per Cookie: Calories 75, Carbohydrate 7.6 g,
Protein 1.2 g, Fat 4.7 g, Cholesterol 8 mg, Fiber 0.6 g, Sodium 31 mg.
description: Decadent cookies packed with ½ cup vegetable oil, ¼ cup granulated sugar substitute, and ½ tsp baking soda — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Applesauce Currant Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup all-purpose flour ½ cup whole wheat flour 3 tsp baking powder ½ tsp cinnamon 1/4 tsp nutmeg ¾ cup rolled oats ¼ cup Brown Sugar Twin ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional) 1 egg ¼ cup oil 1/3 cup milk 2/3 cup applesauce Instructions Mix dry ingredients well with a fork.
Beat egg, then add oil and milk.
Stir in applesauce.
Stir this into the dry ingredients, mixing only until moistened.
Spoon into greased muffin cups and bake at 375 degrees for 20 minutes.
description: Golden-topped heavenly muffins loaded with ½ cup all-purpose flour, ½ cup whole wheat flour, and ½ tsp cinnamon. Mix the batter in one bowl, bake, and watch them vanish.
title: Applesauce Oatmeal Muffins

content:
Ingredients 1 cup All-Bran (Cereal) ¼ cup milk 1 cup applesauce 1/3 cup oil 1 egg ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional) ¾ cup all-purpose flour ¾ cup whole wheat flour 3 tsp baking powder ½ tsp baking soda 1 tsp cinnamon 1/3 cup Brown Sugar Twin Instructions Stir All-Bran, milk, applesauce, oil and egg together.
Stir together the dry ingredients.
Add bran mixture to dry ingredients, stirring just to moisten.
Bake at 375 degrees for 15 – 20 minutes.
description: Tender, domed rich muffins bursting with all-bran, ¼ cup milk, and applesauce — the kind that make weekday mornings feel like a treat.
title: Applesauce Bran Muffins

content: Ingredients 2 pkg cherry Jello 2 cups boiling water 1 14-oz can of crushed pineapple 1 can cherry pie filling Instructions Mix and let set. Top with Light Cool Whip.
description: Warm, luscious dessert with cherry jello and boiling water — golden and bubbling on top, tender and fruity underneath. Best served with a scoop of ice cream.
title: Lisa's Light Dessert

content:
Ingredients 2-1/2 Tbsp cornstarch 1 cup apple juice ¼ cup Ontario Maple Syrup 1 Tbsp lemon juice 1-1/2 tsp cinnamon 6 Ontario apples, unpeeled, cored, sliced 1/3 cup raisins ¼ cup milk 1 egg 1-1/4 cup biscuit mix 1 tsp sugar Instructions Combine cornstarch, apple juice, maple syrup, lemon juice and 1 tsp of cinnamon. Place apples and raisins in a 2-1/2 quart shallow baking dish. Pour juice mixture over top. Bake at 400 F. (200 C.) for 20 minutes.
With fork, blend milk and egg. Stir into biscuit mix just until moistened. Drop from large spoon into 6 mounds on top of fruit. Combine sugar and remaining cinnamon, sprinkle over biscuit. Bake 20 to 25 minutes longer or until browned and bubbling.
Serve warm. Makes 6 servings.
NOTE: Good made with McIntosh apples.
description: This sweet dessert with cornstarch and apple juice is proof that simple ingredients, done right, create something truly magical.
title: Maple Apple Cobbler

content:
A Favorite dessert in Quebec.
Ingredients 2 Cups all-purpose flour 4 tsp baking powder 1 tsp each salt &amp; ground nutmeg (I (Mom) would cut this down to ½ tsp each) 2 Tbsp white sugar 2 Tbsp cooking oil ¼ cup whole milk 1 egg 2 cups maple syrup (recipe for Mock Maple Syrup on Miscellaneous page) Instructions Sift flour with baking powder, salt, nutmeg and sugar. Stirring constantly to avoid lumps,gently add oil, then milk and, finally, egg.
Bring maple syrup and 1 cup of water to a boil in a wide saucepan or frying pan. Add dough a tablespoonful at a time, then lower heat, cover pot and gently simmer 10 minutes.
Skim off dumplings and keep them warm. Reduce maple syrup and water by half and serve as a sauce on the dumplings.
Serves 6.
description: Homemade satisfying goodness with each salt & ground nutmeg would cut this down to ½ tsp each) and cooking oil — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Grand-Peres

content: Ingredients 2 Cups of Flour 1 Cup of Brown Sugar 1 1/4 Cups of Shortening 1/2 Tsp Salt 1 1/2 Lbs of Dates 2 Cups of Rolled Oats 1/2 Cup of Coconut 1/2 Tsp Baking Soda 1 Tsp Baking Powder Instructions Rub first 8 ingredients until crumbly. Put 2/3 of the mixture into the bottom of a 9x12 cake pan. Cook dates with 1 1/2 cups of water until soft. Add 1/4 - 1/2 cup of white sugar and 2 Tbsp of butter. Mix well. Spread date mixture over crumbs in bottom of pain. Top with remaining crumb mixture. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
description: Sweet, satisfying luscious squares with of flour and of brown sugar. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Date Squares

content:
DUMPLINGS:
1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour ½ cup sugar 2 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt ½ cup milk 2 tbsp butter or margarine – softened 2 tsp vanilla extract ½ cup coarsely chopped peeled apple, optional SAUCE:
Ingredients 4 Tbsp butter or margarine 1-1/2 cups packed brown sugar 1-1/2 cups water Instructions In a skillet, heat the unsoftened butter, brown sugar and water to boiling.
Meanwhile, mix together all dumpling ingredients. Drop by tablespoonfuls into the simmering sauce. Cover tightly and simmer 20 minutes. Do not lift lid. Serve warm with cream or ice cream, if desired.
Yield:- 6 – 8 servings.
description: Sweet, homemade rich candy with ½ cup sugar and ½ tsp salt — the kind of treat that makes you feel like a confectioner. Wrap them up for gifts or keep the whole batch.
title: Caramel Dumplings

content:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Mix together:
Ingredients 1 cup all-purpose flour 1 cup rolled oats 1 cup lightly-packed brown sugar 1 teaspoon cinnamon Instructions Cut in ½ cup butter or margarine. Set aside.
Wash, peel and slice apples to make 4 cups.
Mix about ¼ of the crumb mixture with the apples and spread in a buttered 8” x 12” baking dish. Cover with remaining crumbs and pat down slightly.
Bake in the preheated oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until apples are tender.
Serve hot or cold, with cream.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
VARIATION: QUICK APPLE CRISP Prepare as directed above, replacing sliced apples with a 20-oz can of apple pie filling. Bake in a preheated 350 degree oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Credit to the Purity Cookbook.
description: A gorgeous melt-in-your-mouth dessert with rolled oats and lightly-packed brown sugar that looks like it came from a bakery but tastes even better because it came from your kitchen.
title: Apple Crisp

content:
Ingredients 6 to 8 baking apples, such as McIntosh ½ tsp cinnamon ½ cup coarsely chopped dried apricots or raisins (optional) 1 cup all-purpose flour 2/3 cup brown sugar ½ cup brown or granulated sugar ½ cup healthy margarine 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour 2/3 cup blanched slivered almonds (optional) Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Peel, core and slice apples. Place in a large bowl with apricots or raisins, if using. Stir ½ cup sugar with 3 Tbsp flour and cinnamon. Sprinkle over apples and toss to coat evenly.
Place in a 9-inch glass pie plate and pat down. Stir flour and brown sugar together. Using a pastry cutter or fork, cut margarine into flour/sugar mixture until crumbly. Mix in almonds, if using. Spread over apples.
Bake in center of preheated oven until apples are fork-tender, 45 to 50 minutes.
Serves 8 to 10.
1 Serving:- 322 Calories, 2 gr Protein, 10 gr Fat, 58 gr Carbohydrate
TIP: For a nice crumbly topping, freeze margarine for 10 minutes to make cutting in easier.
description: A gorgeous decadent dessert with to 8 baking apples and ½ tsp cinnamon that looks like it came from a bakery but tastes even better because it came from your kitchen.
title: Amazingly Easy Apple Crisp

content:
Ingredients 1 cup butter or margarine 1-1/2 cups brown sugar 2 eggs ½ cup milk ½ tsp white vinegar (I (Mom) didn’t use this) 1-3/4 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp cinnamon 3 cups quick-cooking rolled oats ½ cup raisins (optional) ½ cup chopped walnuts or sliced almonds (optional) Instructions Cream together butter and brown sugar. Add the vinegar to the milk and set it aside for one minute. Add eggs to the butter and sugar mixture one at a time, mixing until light and fluffy. Add the milk with vinegar and mix until well incorporated.
In a separate bowl, sift together flour, soda, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. Stir into the creamed mixture. Stir in rolled oats and raisins and nuts if desired.
Drop from tablespoon, two inches apart on a greased cookie sheet. Top with a walnut half if desired. Bake in a 350 degree (180 C) oven for approximately eight to 10 minutes (I (Mom) used 12 minutes) – more if you like your cookies crispy, less if you like your cookies soft and chewy.
Cool slightly and remove from cookie sheet. Makes about 60 cookies.
description: Decadent cookies packed with butter or margarine, brown sugar, and ½ cup milk — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Yummy Oatmeal Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ lb butter ½ lb margarine ½ cup cornstarch 1 cup sifted icing sugar 3 cups sifted flour Instructions Cream butter and margarine gradually with cornstarch and sugar. Add flour, a little bit at a time, and whip mixture until it looks like cream. The more they are beat – the better they are.
Drop by spoonfuls on cookie sheet and bake in a 325 degree oven for 22 minutes.
description: Buttery, golden rich cookies with ½ lb butter and ½ lb margarine that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Whipped Shortbread

content:
Ingredients ½ cup butter 1 cup sifted flour ¼ cup brown sugar Nuts, chopped finely or corn flakes crushed 1 egg, separated Jelly 1 tsp flavouring Instructions Cream butter. Add sugar gradually and mix well. Add well Beaten egg yolk, flavouring and flour. Shape into balls about the size of a walnut. Dip in unbeaten egg white, then in finely chopped nuts, almonds, walnuts or soya bean flakes or corn flakes. Dent in center with a thimble and bake in a moderate oven at 350 degrees for 5 minutes and dent again. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes more. Fill with jelly or jam while still hot if planning not to freeze them.
Otherwise, fill them only when you’re ready to serve them.
description: Buttery, golden rich cookies with ½ cup butter and sifted flour that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Thimble Cookies

content:
Ingredients 3/4 cup Golden Crisco 1-1/4 cup lightly packed brown sugar 1 egg 2 Tbsp milk 2 tsp vanilla 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp salt 3/4 tsp baking soda 1 cup chocolate chips 1 cup coarsely chopped pecans or walnuts Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Cream Golden Crisco and brown sugar in large bowl until light. Add egg, milk and vanilla, beating 1 minute or until well blended.
Combine flour, salt and baking soda. Add to creamed mixture gradually, just until blended.
Stir in chocolate chips and nuts.
Drop dough by heaping teaspoonfuls on ungreased baking sheet, leaving 3 inches between cookies.
Bake at 375 degrees for 8-10 minutes. Cookies will still appear moist when baked.
Cool 2 minutes then remove to cooling racks.
Makes about 30 cookies.
description: Decadent cookies packed with golden crisco, lightly packed brown sugar, and chocolate chips — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: The Ultimate Chocolate Chip Cookie

content:
Ingredients 1 cup shortening 1 cup sugar 2 eggs 2 tsp vanilla 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 2 tsp cream of tartar 1 tsp salt Raisins Fine sugar Instructions Heat oven to 375 degrees. Blend shortening and sugar. Beat in eggs and vanilla. Blend in sifted dry ingredients. On lightly floured board, pat or roll dough to ¼-inch thickness. Cut in 3-inch rounds. Arrange on greased cookie sheet. Press 3 raisins in center of each cookie.
Sprinkle with fine sugar. Bake 8 to 10 minutes. Cool slightly, remove from pans and cool on cake rack.
description: A classic indulgent cookie recipe with cream of tartar and raisins — chewy centers, golden edges, and that irresistible homemade smell that fills every room in the house.
title: Star Sugar Cookies

content:
Ingredients 3 cups flour 1 cup butter or shortening 1 cup white sugar 2 eggs 1-1/2 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla or lemon extract 1 tbsp milk Instructions Cream butter, sugar, eggs and milk. Add vanilla. Stir in sifted dry ingredients and blend well.
Roll out dough ¼” thick and cut in desired shapes. Bake at 375 degrees until done, about 8 – 10 minutes.
description: Warm from the oven and loaded with butter or shortening, ¼ tsp salt, and vanilla or lemon extract, these luscious cookies are the ones everyone reaches for first at the bake sale.
title: Rolled Cut-Out Sugar Cookies

content: Ingredients 1 cup shortening 1 cup white sugar 1 cup brown sugar 2 eggs 1 tbsp vanilla 1 cup peanut butter 3 cups flour 2 tsp baking soda 1 tsp salt Instructions Cream shortening, sugar and eggs. Add peanut butter and vanilla. Add flour, salt and baking soda. Form into small balls and press down with a fork dipped into milk. Bake at 350 degrees for 12 to 15 minutes.
description: Homemade heavenly cookies with brown sugar and peanut butter that snap, crumble, and melt exactly the way a great cookie should. Pure comfort in every bite.
title: Peanut Butter Cookies

content:
1 cup margarine or butter 1-1/2 cup brown sugar 1 egg Mix above ingredients well, then add:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup flour 1 tsp baking soda 1-1/4 cup oatmeal Instructions Mix well and bake in 350 degree oven ‘til golden brown.
This recipe came from Florence Burton, who had gotten it from Roland Lejambe.
description: Decadent cookies packed with margarine or butter, brown sugar, and oatmeal — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Oatmeal Cookies

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup butter or margarine, softened 2/3 cup packed brown sugar 2 eggs 1-1/2 old-fashioned rolled oats 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda ½ tsp salt 1 6-oz pkg sweetened dried cranberries, option 2/3 cup white or semi-sweet chocolate chips, optional Instructions Preheat oven to 375 F. Using electric mixer, beat better and sugar together in a medium mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Add eggs and mix well.
In a separate mixing bowl, combine oats, flour, baking soda and salt. Add to butter mixture in several additions, mixing well after each addition.
Stir in cranberries and chocolate chips, if using. Drop by rounded l Tbsp onto ungreased cookie sheet. Bake for 10 minutes or until golden brown.
Makes 2 dozen large cookies.
description: Buttery, golden melt-in-your-mouth cookies with butter or margarine and packed brown sugar that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Oatmeal Snack Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup Canadian Corn Starch ½ cup icing sugar 1 cup sifted all-purpose flour ¾ cup butter (do not substitute) Instructions Sift cornstarch, icing sugar and flour together. Blend in butter with a spoon, mixing until a soft, smooth dough forms. Shape dough into 1” balls. Place about 1-1/2” apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten with a lightly floured fork.
Bake in 300 F. (slow oven) for 20 – 25 minutes or until edges are slightly browned.
NOTE: If dough is too soft to handle, cover and chill for 1 hour.
May be used in a cookie gun.
Credit to Frontier Kitchen.
description: Perfectly sweet cookies studded with ½ cup canadian corn starch, ½ cup icing sugar, and sifted all-purpose flour. Simple enough for a Tuesday, special enough for a holiday platter.
title: Never Fail Shortbread

content:
Ingredients 1 lb butter 1 cup fruit sugar 4 cups flour Instructions Cream butter. Add sugar. Mix well. Add flour a little at a time, mixing well each time. Roll out ½ inch thick. Cut with cookie cutter. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 325 degree oven.
Bake 15 minutes. Turn cookie sheet. Continue baking for another 15 minutes.
Credit to Lorry Bernard.
description: Warm from the oven and loaded with fruit sugar, these luscious cookies are the ones everyone reaches for first at the bake sale.
title: English Shortbread

content: Ingredients 2 8-oz pkgs shredded coconut 1 15-oz (1-1/3 cup) Eagle Brand 2 tsp vanilla Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Mix ingredients. Drop from teaspoon onto well-greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes. Cool slightly. Remove to rack. Makes about four dozen.
description: A classic indulgent cookie recipe with pkgs shredded coconut and eagle brand — chewy centers, golden edges, and that irresistible homemade smell that fills every room in the house.
title: Easy Macaroons

content:
Ingredients 1 cup light or dark brown sugar, packed 1 large egg, slightly beaten 1 tsp baking soda 1 cup chocolate chips Instructions Mix all ingredients together till well mixed. Drop by teaspoonful on a greased cookie sheet 2” apart.
Bake in a 350 degree oven till puffed and golden brown – about 10 minutes.
description: Perfectly sweet cookies studded with light or dark brown sugar, large egg, and chocolate chips. Simple enough for a Tuesday, special enough for a holiday platter.
title: Easiest Chocolate Chip Cookies

content:
Ingredients 2 cups raisins 1 cup water 1 cup lard or shortening 2 cups sugar 3 eggs, beaten 1 tsp vanilla 3-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda ½ tsp salt Instructions Cook raisins in water for five minutes. Cool. Cream lard or shortening and sugar. Add eggs, raisins and vanilla. Add sifted dry ingredients. Drop by spoonfuls about 1-1/2” apart on greased cookie sheet. Bake in 350 degree oven for 12 to 15 minutes.Oven grate on top – pan of water under.Remove cookies from pan immediately.
Makes four dozen large cookies.
description: A classic indulgent cookie recipe with raisins and lard or shortening — chewy centers, golden edges, and that irresistible homemade smell that fills every room in the house.
title: Dad's Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup butter 1 cup flour 1 cup water 4 eggs, unbeaten Instructions Heat butter and water. When butter is melted, add flour all at once and stir vigorously until mixture no longer sticks to sides of pan. Remove from stove, cool slightly; add eggs one at a time, beating after each egg is added. Drop by heaping tablespoons onto a well-greased baking sheet. Place 2 inches apart and bake in hot oven, 450 degrees F. for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 325 degrees F., and bake about 20 minutes longer. Remove from baking sheet and cool.
When ready to serve, cut open on one side, and fill with Whipped Cream.
Credit to Heart of the Settlement Cookbook.
description: A tried-and-true robust recipe with ½ cup butter that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Cream Puffs

content:
Ingredients 1 cup peanut butter (plain or chunky) 1 cup sugar 1 egg Instructions Mix egg and sugar together. Add peanut butter. Spoon onto greased cookie sheet. Press with a fork. Use small teaspoon and note that they really flatten and spread.
Bake at 350 F. for l0 minutes.
description: Buttery, golden rich cookies with peanut butter that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Colette's Skeanut Butter Cookies

content:
Ingredients 3 pkg (200 gr ea) flaked coconut 1 can (300 ml) Eagle Brand Milk 2 tsp vanilla extract 1-1/2 tsp almond extract Instructions Combine coconut, Eagle Brand, vanilla and almond extracts; mix well.
Drop by rounded spoonfuls onto well-greased cookie sheets.
Bake 12 at a time on middle rack of preheated 325 F. oven, 10 – 12 minutes or until browned around the edges. Remove immediately to wire racks, cool completely.
Makes about 4 dozen.
For Chocolate Macaroons; Omit almond extract. Add 4 squares (1 oz or 28 grams each) unsweetened chocolate, melted. Proceed as above.
For Chocolate Chip Macaroons; Omit almond extract. Add 1 cup (250 ml) mini chocolate chips. Proceed as above.
description: Buttery, golden rich cookies with flaked coconut and eagle brand milk that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Coconut Macaroons

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup sweetened condensed milk 1 Tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder 1 tsp vanilla 3 cups flaked coconut 2 egg whites 8 oz. Semi-sweet chocolate, melted (optional) Instructions In large bowl, blend together condensed milk, cocoa and vanilla; stir in flaked coconut until well mixed. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form; fold into coconut mixture.
Drop by tablespoonfuls onto lightly greased or parchment paper-lined baking sheet; shape into logs about 3” long. Bake at 300 degrees F for 30 minutes or until golden brown and firm. Let cool on wire racks. Dip one end of each log into chocolate (if using). Let stand on waxed paper until chocolate is set.
Makes about 20.
description: Melt-in-your-mouth cookies packed with sweetened condensed milk, unsweetened cocoa powder, and flaked coconut — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Chocolate Coconut Macaroon Logs

content:
Ingredients 2 eggs ¼ cup white sugar ¾ cup brown sugar 1 cup margarine or butter 2 cups chocolate chips 1 tsp vanilla 1 pkg vanilla or chocolate instant pudding 1 tsp baking soda 2-1/4 cups flour Walnuts (optional) Instructions Mix egg, butter, sugars, vanilla.
Add instant pudding and the rest of the dry ingredients.
Bake for 10 minutes at 375 degree F. oven.
description: Homemade heavenly cookies with ¼ cup white sugar and ¾ cup brown sugar that snap, crumble, and melt exactly the way a great cookie should. Pure comfort in every bite.
title: Chocolate Chip Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ lb butter ½ cup brown sugar 2 cups flour Instructions Mix the ingredients together; roll out ½”thick.
Cut out with metal cookie cutters.
Bake 325 F. or 20 minutes on an ungreased cookie sheet.
NOTE FROM COLETTE PEPLINSKIE: I use a pastry cutter, then knead into a ball
description: Buttery, golden rich cookies with ½ lb butter and ½ cup brown sugar that come out of the oven soft in the middle and crispy at the edges. The cookie jar never stays full for long.
title: Butterscotch Shortbread Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup butter ½ cup coconut 1-1/2 cup icing sugar 1 tsp milk 1 tsp vanilla Pinch of salt Cherries Instructions Cream butter, mix all ingredients and make into balls around cherries. Roll in graham wafers.
NOTE: If using maraschino cherries, drain them well and dry them or icing will stick to them.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with ½ cup butter and ½ cup coconut — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Cherry Surprises

content: Ingredients 1 cup soft butter 2 eggs 1 cup peanut butter (Smooth or chunky) 2-1/2 cups flour 1 cup white sugar 1 tsp baking powder 1 cup brown sugar ½ tsp salt Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Use a wooden spoon or electric mixer to cream the butter in a large bowl. Add peanut butter and mix until well blended. Add white and brown sugars, a little at a time, mixing until they are blended in. Break an egg into a small bowl and stir. Add to the peanut butter mixture and mix well. Do the same with second egg. Sift flour, baking powder and salt over mixture; stir until well blended. Form into 1 to 1 inch balls. Place on greased cookie sheet. Flatten with a fork. Bake for 10 minutes. Makes 36 large cookies.
description: Homemade melt-in-your-mouth cookies with soft butter and peanut butter that snap, crumble, and melt exactly the way a great cookie should. Pure comfort in every bite.
title: Best Peanut Butter Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup softened butter ¾ cup sugar 1 Medium or Large egg (not Extra large or Jumbo) 1 Tbsp milk or cream 2 cups flour ¼ tsp salt ¼ tsp nutmeg or cinnamon Instructions Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add sugar and mix until fluffy. Beat in the egg and then the milk.
In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter. Knead the dough for just a minute. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.
Yield: 5 to 8 cookies, depending on size of mold.
description: Decadent cookies packed with ½ cup softened butter, ¾ cup sugar, and medium or large egg — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Beautiful Sugar Cookies

content:
Ingredients ½ cup softened butter ½ cup packed dark brown sugar ¼ cup molasses 1 medium or large egg (do not use extra large or jumbo) 2-1/4 cups flour ½ tsp cinnamon 2 tsp ginger 2 dashes cloves 1 dash cayenne pepper Instructions Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add brown sugar and molasses, then the egg. In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter mixture. Knead the dough briefly. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 375 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.
Yield: 5 to 8 cookies depending on size of mold.
description: Warm from the oven and loaded with ½ cup softened butter, ½ cup packed dark brown sugar, and ¼ cup molasses, these luscious cookies are the ones everyone reaches for first at the bake sale.
title: Beautiful Ginger Cookies

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup honey 3 Tbsp soy sauce 2 cloves garlic 1 tsp ground ginger or 1” fresh ginger root 1 tsp (any kind) vinegar ¼ cup water 2 lb drumsticks (about 12) Instructions Put the honey, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, vinegar and water into the container or a blender and whirl it around till smooth. Arrange drumsticks in 9” x 13” pan and pour sauce on top.
Bake at 350 F. for about an hour, basting from time to time with the sauce and turning the legs over about halfway through.
Serve with rice or something to soak up the delicious sauce.
Makes about 4 servings.
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with honey and soy sauce cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Streamlined Honey Garlic Drumsticks

content:
Prep: 10 min. Total Time: 55 min.
Ingredients 2 tsp vegetable oil 6 small boneless skinless chicken breast halves (about 1-1/2 lbs.) ½ cup Kraft Russian Dressing ½ cup Kraft Pure Apricot jam ½ pkg dry onion soup mix (about 3 Tbsp.) Instructions PREHEAT oven to 350 F. Heat oil in large skillet on medium-high heat. Add Chicken; cook 4 min. on each side or until browned. Remove chicken from skillet; place in 3-L baking dish.
MIX dressing, jam and soup mix in small bowl. Pour over chicken.
BAKE 45 min. or until chicken is cooked through.
Makes 6 servings.
Credit to Kraft What&rsquo;s Cooking.
description: Crispy-skinned, robust chicken with vegetable oil and small boneless skinless chicken breast halves cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Russian Chicken Breasts

content:
Ingredients Preparation: 5 minutes Cooking: 30 minutes
1 can condensed tomato soup 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce ¼ cup vinegar 1 tsp garlic powder ¼ cup vegetable oil ¼ tsp hot pepper sauce (optional) 2 Tbsp packed brown sugar 4 chicken breast halves (about 2 lbs) Instructions In a small bowl, combine soup, vinegar, oil, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder and hot sauce.
Remove skin from chicken. On rack in broiler pan, arrange chicken bone side up. Brush chicken with sauce. Broil 6” from heat for 25 to 30 minutes or until no longer pink, turning once and brushing often with sauce during cooking. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
Variation: On grill rack, arrange chicken over medium coals. Grill uncovered 20 minutes, turning often. Brush chicken with sauce, grill another 20 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink, turning chicken often and brushing with sauce.
description: Sweet, satisfying melt-in-your-mouth squares with condensed tomato soup and worcestershire sauce. The recipe that turns a simple baking pan into a crowd-pleasing masterpiece.
title: Oven Barbecued Chicken

content:
Ingredients 1 lb chicken pieces Flour (for coating) A few Tbsp olive oil Handful of sliced peppers Handful of sliced mushrooms 1 can (19 oz) Aylmer Accents Italian Stewed Tomatoes 2 cups cooked spaghetti noodles Parmesan cheese for topping Instructions Coat chicken pieces in flour and brown in a few tablespoons of olive oil.
Add sliced peppers and mushrooms; cook until tender, stirring occasionally.
Pour in one can of Italian Stewed Tomatoes, cover and simmer for 30 minutes or until done.
Serve over cooked spaghetti noodles and top with Parmesan cheese.
Serves 4.
TIP: Uncooked chicken can be kept frozen in an airtight bag for up to 6 months; cooked chicken for up to 3 months.
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with chicken pieces and a few tbsp olive oil that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: One Pan Cacciatore Dinner

content:
Ingredients Preparation Time: 10 minutes - Cook Time: 25 minutes
2 Tbsp cornstarch 2 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 tin condensed chicken broth 4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves ¼ cup apple juice 1 large carrot cut into thin strips 1 Tbsp honey 1 medium green pepper, cut in thin strips 2 tsp Dijonaise mustard Instructions In small bow, stir together cornstarch, broth, apple juice, honey and mustard – set aside.
In large skillet, heat half of the oil. Brown chicken 5 minutes on each side. Remove chicken from skillet – set aside.
In same skillet, heat remaining oil. Stir-fry carrot and pepper until tender-crisp.
Stir in reserved cornstarch mixture. Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to low. Cover, simmer 5 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink.
Serve with noodles, if desired. Makes 4 servings.
description: Tangy, savory robust sauce with cornstarch and vegetable oil. Drizzle it, dip it, pour it — this one makes everything taste better.
title: Glazed Chicken With Julienne Veggies

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp soya sauce 2 Tbsp hoisin sauce 2 Tbsp liquid honey 2 Tbsp Ketchup 2 minced garlic cloves ½ tsp sesame oil 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts Instructions In small saucepan, combine soy sauce, hoisin, honey, ketchup and garlic. Cook over medium heat for 2 minutes, stirring often. Remove from heat: stir in sesame oil. Remove ¼ of the sauce for basting; set remaining sauce aside.
Place chicken on greased grill over medium heat; close lid and cook for 2 minutes. Turn and brush with sauce, cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side or until no longer pink inside. Serve with reserved sauce. Makes 2 servings.
Some of the extra sauce is delicious spooned over the stir-fried vegetable dish.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with soya sauce and hoisin sauce — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Honey Hoisin Chicken

content:
Preparation: 10 minutes Baking time: 30 minutes
Ingredients 2-1/2 lb chicken wings ½ cup dry bread crumbs ¼ cup flour 1 Old El Paso Chili Seasoning Mix (35gr pkg) Instructions Heat oven to 350 degrees. Line a baking sheet with foil, grease lightly. With a sharp knife, remove wing tips, discard. Cut through wings at joint to yield two pieces each. Place bread crumbs, flour and chili seasoning mix in large plastic bag, shake to combine. Shake chicken wings in mix to coat thoroughly.
Place wings on baking sheet and bake 25 to 30 minutes or until cooked through.
Serve with Mexi-Veggie dip if desired.
Makes about 30 wings.
description: Rich, slow-simmered satisfying chili with chicken wings and ½ cup dry bread crumbs. Top it with cheese and sour cream and let the warmth spread from your belly outward.
title: Chili Flappers

content:
Ingredients 1 cup rice 4 tbsp butter 3 – 4 onions, chopped 1 28-oz can tomatoes 1 clove garlic, minced 1 red or green pepper, chopped 1 tsp paprika 2 tsp salt Instructions Saute onions, garlic and pepper in butter. Add remaining ingredients and cook slowly on top of stove for 1 hour.
YIELD: Four servings.
description: Fluffy, fragrant hearty rice with rice and – 4 onions — every grain perfectly cooked and packed with flavor. The side dish that steals the spotlight.
title: Spanish Rice

content:
Ingredients 2 Tbsp olive oil 1 lb hot Italian sausage 1 medium onion, thinly sliced 2 lg green peppers, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips 1 680-ml Hunt’s Italian tomato sauce 1 cup water 2/3 cup uncooked small pasta 5 sprigs each: mint &amp; basil tied together Salt and ground black pepper to taste Instructions In a large skillet, heat oil; add sausage and onion. Cook until sausage is brown on all sides and onion is tender.
Add peppers; cook 2 minutes. Add remaining ingredients; stirring to submerge pasta. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium-low. Cover, simmering about 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sausages are cooked thoroughly and pasta is tender. Sausages may be cut into slices before serving.
15 minutes preparation time: 25 minutes cooking time: 4 servings.
description: Rustic rustic stew loaded with olive oil, hot italian sausage, and medium onion, simmered until the kitchen fills with the most incredible aroma. Serve it with crusty bread and call it a perfect night.
title: Sausage and Pepper Stew

content:
Ingredients 2 large onions 2 medium potatoes 1 lb ground beef ½ cup long grain rice 1 cup chopped celery salt and pepper 1 tin tomato soup 1 soup can boiling water Instructions Peel onions and slice over bottom of 2-quart casserole. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Slice peeled potatoes over onions. Salt and pepper potatoes. Pat ground beef over. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Sprinkle rice on next, followed by celery. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Mix soup and water together. Pour over top. Bake covered in 350 f oven for 2 hours until vegetables are tender. Serves 4.
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food with large onions and medium potatoes. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Shipwreck

content: Ingredients 1 lb hamburg 1 cup rice ½ tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper 1 tin tomato soup 1 tin water 3 onions Instructions Mix together uncooked hamburg, rice, salt, pepper, soup and water. Slice onions on top. Bake in a covered casserole in a 350 degree oven for 2 hours.
description: Hearty, satisfying hearty beef with hamburg and rice — the kind of meal that brings everyone to the table before you've even finished plating.
title: Ruth's Beef and Rice Supreme

content:
Empty 5-lb. bag of beans into pot.
Cover with water.
Boil for 1 hr. after water begins to bubble.
Drain beans and save the water.
Put 1/2 the beans in a dish and ½ in a pot.
To the beans in the pot, add:
Ingredients ½ lb. shortening ( break in chunks). ½ cup syrup. a good handful of brown sugar. 1 lb. of bacon, cut in small pieces. Instructions Put the other ½ of beans on top and mix with a large spoon.
Next day (or whenever, add the reserved water until it covers the beans 1-1/2 to 2”(add
more water if necessary.
Bake in 350 degree oven for 3-1/2 to 4 hours.
description: Warm, satisfying comforting casserole with ½ lb. shortening . and ½ cup syrup. that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Rob's Baked Beans

content: Ingredients 2 cups uncooked long grain rice 1 tbsp soya sauce 2 10-oz cans consommé soup ½ cup oil 2 10-oz cans mushrooms &amp; liquid Celery, onions and green pepper – to taste, chopped fine 1 envelope onion soup mix Instructions Mix all ingredients in small roaster and bake in 350 degree oven for 1-1/2 hours.
description: Fluffy, fragrant robust rice with uncooked long grain rice and soya sauce — every grain perfectly cooked and packed with flavor. The side dish that steals the spotlight.
title: Oven Fried Rice

content:
Get supper on the table in a flash using only six ingredients. Toss a salad, warm up an Italian bread stick and you have dinner fit for royalty in about 30 minutes.
Ingredients 1 lb broad egg noodles 1 19-oz can tomato sauce 4 cups ricotta cheese ¼ cup sliced green onion 1 tsp dried basil 1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese Instructions Butter a 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Set aside. Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain well and return to pot. Stir tomato sauce, ricotta cheese, green onion and basil into pasta.
Spoon pasta mixture into prepared baking pan.
Sprinkle Parmesan cheese evenly over casserole. Bake in a 350-degree oven for 25 minutes or until hot and bubbly.
Makes 8 servings.
description: A comforting bowl of hearty pasta with broad egg noodles and can tomato sauce — saucy, satisfying, and ready to become a weeknight staple.
title: Noodles Al Forno Napolitana

content:
I&rsquo;ve made this one so many times, I can recite it from memory. A favorite of Pep&rsquo;s (and pretty much anyone else). So good that it convinced my girls that tomatoes aren&rsquo;t the devil in stewed vegetable form.
Ingredients 5 handfuls of elbow macaroni (Yep, handfuls. Not sure how, but my hand-size is the same as Mom&rsquo;s and everyone else&hellip;but it still somehow works!) 1 brick of medium cheddar cheese (I think they&rsquo;re 400g bricks, but those long 2x4 sized ones) Margarine Salt/Pepper to taste 1 Can of Stewed Whole Tomatoes Water Instructions In a pot, boil enough water to boil 5 handfuls of macaroni. I throw a bit of salt and olive oil in there (for taste and so the noodles won&rsquo;t stick).
Grate the entire brick of cheese while you&rsquo;re waiting for the noodles to cook.
Boil the noodles till they&rsquo;re &ldquo;el dente&rdquo; (whatever that means&hellip;I think it&rsquo;s Italian for &ldquo;however you like them&rdquo;).
Drain the noodles and put them back in the pot.
Melt enough margarine in with the noodles so they&rsquo;re good and greasy. Usually around a tablespoon or so.
Dump the can of whole tomatoes in there, but chop them up. I use a potato masher to make them as small as possible so the girls don&rsquo;t pick them out, but Mom used to chop them by hand with a paring knife.
Put a handful of the grated cheese in with the noodle mixture and mix it in (the noodles will be cooler now, so it won&rsquo;t melt - it&rsquo;s for extra yummy gooey yum-ness when it cooks later on).
Sprinkle some salt and pepper in there to taste (can always be added later when it&rsquo;s in your bowl, so your call).
Dump the noodle mixture into a 9x13 casserole and cover it with the rest of the shredded cheese. Make sure to cover all the noodles or they&rsquo;ll be exposed and get hard when you bake them.
Bake in a 300 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 mins.
description: Hearty comforting pasta with handfuls of elbow macaroni and brick of medium cheddar cheese — the kind of meal you want to curl up on the couch with. Simple, satisfying, and just right.
title: Mom's Macaroni & Cheese Casserole

content:
Ingredients 1 lb ground beef 1 tin mushroom soup ½ cup chopped onions 1 tbsp parsley flakes 1 tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper ½ tsp sage 3 tbsp ketchup 1 tbsp barbecue sauce ½ tsp garlic powder ½ cup rolled oats Instructions Combine all ingredients in large bowl. Mix well. Pat down in 1-1/2 qt casserole. Bake
covered in 350 degree F oven for 50 minutes. Remove cover and bake about 15 minutes to
brown. Serves 4.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with ground beef and tin mushroom soup — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Maritime Madness

content:
Ingredients 2 lbs. navy beans 1 onion, cut in two ¼ lb. salt pork ¾ cup brown sugar ¼ cup catsup 3 Tbsp. dry mustard 4 Tbsp. molasses Salt &amp; Pepper to taste ¼ cup shortening, in chunks Instructions This is sort of the recipe for baked beans that I (Mom) started with. They do not have to be boiled beforehand. This recipe can be altered to many ways – every cook puts his/her own touch to it.
I (Mom) have lost the knack of doing beans because my best “pal” (Dad) took over the job and did much better job of baking beans. Also, if you want good beans, look up Rob’s/Bob’s baked beans – they are especially delicious.
description: Hearty satisfying casserole packed with navy beans, onion, and ¼ lb. salt pork — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Mom's Baked Beans

content:
Ingredients ½ cup raw long grain rice 1-3/4 cup peeled and chopped tomatoes (or use one 14-oz can) 1 small mild onion, chopped 1 green pepper, chopped 1-1/2 tsp salt – divided 3 thick loin pork chops 1 cup hot water (or 1 can consommé) 2 tbsp chicken bouillon cordial – see below Instructions Measure raw rice into large shallow casserole. Stir tomatoes into it. (If time permits let stand 1
hour to soak rice.) Cover with layer of the onions then layer of the green pepper. Sprinkle with
1 tsp salt. Place chops on top.
Mix bouillon cordial with hot water and pour over all. Sprinkle remaining ½ tsp salt over all.
(If desired use one 10-oz can consommé instead of cordial and hot water) Cover, using foil if
you do not have a lid. Bake at 350 degrees 1 hour, removing cover during last ½ hour to
brown top. The rice is tender and the combination of vegetables delicious.
VARIATIONS: 1) Use ½ to ¾ pound sausages instead of chops. 2) Use ½ to ¾ pound ground
chuck made into three meat cakes instead of chops. 3) You could cover the vegetables with a
layer of ground chuck omitting the copes, to make this cheaper and quicker.
THIS SERVES THREE; DOUBLE THIS QUANTITY TO SERVE 6.
description: A bubbly, golden hearty casserole with ½ cup raw long grain rice and peeled and chopped tomatoes — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Mabel's Easiest Casserole

content:
Ingredients 15 lasagna noodles 250 ml grated medium cheddar cheese 500 ml cottage cheese 500 ml mashed potatoes 1 egg ¼ tsp salt ¼ tsp onion salt 1/8 tsp pepper 1 cup butter or margarine 1 cup chopped onions Instructions *Add ½ - 1 lb crumbled cooked bacon to potato-cheese mixture
Cook noodles as directed on package. Drain. Line bottom of 9 x 13 inch pan.. In medium size
bowl, mix cottage cheese, egg and first amount of onion salt together. Spoon over noodles and
spread. Cover with layer of noodles.
In same bowl, mix cheddar cheese with potato, salt, second amount of onion salt and pepper.
Spread over noodles. Cover with layer of noodles.
Melt butter in frying pan. Saute onions slowly until clear and soft. Pour over noodles. Cover.
Bake 30 minutes in 350 degree F. oven. Let stand 10 minutes before cutting. Serve with sour
cream. Make 8 large servings.
description: A bubbly, golden satisfying casserole with lasagna noodles and grated medium cheddar cheese — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Lazy Perogy Casserole

content:
Ingredients 15 medium-sized potatoes 400 gr cheddar cheese 1 small pkg Velveeta cheese 1 lb bacon, diced 3 large onions, diced 1 pkg lasagna noodles Sour cream Shred 2 pkg of cheeses together and set aside. Instructions Dice bacon. Fry until almost cooked. Add diced onion and fry until golden brown. Set aside
to keep warm.
Peel and cook potatoes. When cooked, drain well. Add shredded cheeses while potatoes are
still hot. Cover and let stand for a few minutes, so cheeses melt a bit. Then mash until creamy.
Set aside to cool.
Boil lasagna noodles until cooked. In a square pan, put ½ of onion and bacon. Add layer of
noodles. Spread potatoes and cover with noodles. Then add rest of onion. Set in low oven 30
minutes. Serve with sour cream.
Credit to R. Simpson for this recipe!
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with medium-sized potatoes and cheddar cheese. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Lazy Man's Perogies

content:
Ingredients 1 lb lean ground beef (Mom used 1-1/2 lb ground moose) 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped 1 tsp sugar 1 tsp salt (Mom omitted salt and it tasted just fine) 1/4 tsp pepper 2 (8-oz) tins tomato sauce 4 cups dry egg noodles (1/2 lb) 5 scallions, chopped (Mom used regular onions) 1 pkg (3 oz) cream cheese, room temp 1 (8-oz) container sour cream 1/2 cup or more shredded cheddar cheese Instructions In a large skillet, cook meat over medium high heat, breaking up clumps with wooden spoon, until browned, 5 minutes. Carefully drain off drippings. Stir in garlic, sugar, salt, pepper and tomato sauce into meat. Cover, simmer 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, in large, deep pot of lightly salted water, cook noodles just until al dente, firm yet tender, following package directions. Drain.
In small bowl, stir together scallions, cream cheese and sour cream until well blended.
Lightly coat bottom of shallow 2-1/2 quart baking dish (9 x 13 Pyrex works well) with non-stick cooking spray. Arrange noodles over bottom of dish. Spoon meat sauce evenly over noodles. Carefully spread sour cream mixture over top. Sprinkle with cheddar cheese. (Can be made up to 24 hours ahead and refrigerated, covered.)
Heat oven to 350 degrees. Bake casserole until heated through and bubbly, about 30 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes before serving.
NOTE: Mom would put a little butter in the noodles.
description: A bubbly, golden savory casserole with lean ground beef and cloves garlic — layers of comfort baked until the top is crispy and the inside is meltingly good.
title: Husband's Delight Casserole

content: Ingredients 2 Cups of mashed potatoes, hot or cold 1 250g package of cream cheese, softened 1 small onion, finely chopped 3 Tbsp of flour Salt &amp; Pepper to taste Instructions Add everything together. Beat until light and fluffy. Spoon into greased casserole dish. Bake at 300 degrees for 35 mins (maybe more).
description: Warm, satisfying robust casserole with of mashed potatoes and g package of cream cheese that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Fluffy Potato Casserole

content:
Ingredients ½ cup butter 2 green peppers 2 onions 2 cups celery mushroom pieces and stems 1/2 cup soya sauce 2 tins consommé soup 4 cups minute rice Instructions Saute diced green peppers, chopped onions, chopped celery, mushroom in butter til onions are
transparent. Add soya sauce and soup. Bring this mixture to a boil and stir in minute rice.
Cover and cook on low heat for 1/2 hour.
Cooked chicken chunks, or ham, or egg can be added when rice is added.
description: Succulent hearty chicken with ½ cup butter and green peppers that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Chuk's Chicken Fried Rice

content: Ingredients 1 lb hamburg 1 cup uncooked rice 1-1/2 tsp salt ¼ tsp pepper 1 tsp chili powder 2 minced onions 1 28-oz tin canned tomatoes 1-1/4 cup water 1 tin mushroom (optional) Instructions Mix well and bake at 350 degrees F. for 2 hours, covered.
description: Rich, slow-simmered robust chili with hamburg and uncooked rice. Top it with cheese and sour cream and let the warmth spread from your belly outward.
title: Chili Beef Bake

content:
Ingredients 2 cups chopped onion 1/2 cup chopped green pepper 2 lbs ground beef 2 Tbsp fat 1 (28-oz) tin red kidney beans 1 (28-oz) tin tomatoes 1–3 Tbsp chili powder 1 Tbsp salt 1/2 tsp pepper 2 Tbsp vinegar Instructions Brown onion, green pepper and ground beef in 2 Tbsp fat.
Add kidney beans, tomatoes, chili powder, salt, pepper and vinegar.
Simmer one hour, stirring frequently.
Approx. 10 servings.
description: A hearty pot of rustic chili with chopped onion and chopped green pepper that's perfect for game day, cold nights, or any time you need something warm and satisfying.
title: Chili Con Carne

content:
Ingredients 3 cups frozen vegetable blend with broccoli, carrots, cauliflower 1 pkg Kraft Dinner Macaroni &amp; Cheese ¾ cup skim milk ¼ cup Kraft Calorie-Wise Zesty Italian Dressing 2 tins (170 gr each) Clover Leaf Chunk White Tuna, drained 1 cup Kraft Double Cheddar Shredded Cheese Light, divided Instructions PREHEAT oven to 375 F. Place vegetables in colander in sink. Cook Macaroni as directed on
package. Pour cooked Macaroni over frozen vegetables in colander to drain and quickly thaw
vegetables.
RETURN macaroni and vegetables to same saucepan. Add Cheese Sauce Mix, milk and
dressing; mix well. Add in tuna and ½ cup of the cheese; mix well. Spoon unto a 2-L round
casserole dish; cover with foil.
BAKE 35 minutes or until heated through. Uncover; top with remaining ½ cup cheese. Bake an
additional 3 to 4 minutes, or until cheese is melted.
Makes 4 servings, 1-3/4 cups (425 ml) each. Prep: 10 min. – Total 50 min.
Cal 410, Fat 10 gr (sat 5 gr), Chol 55 mg, Sodium 1090 ml, carb 49 gr, Fibre 3 fr, Sugars 9
gr,Protein 33 gr, Vita A 60% DV, Vita C 50% DV, Calcium 35% DV, Iron 35% DV
Credit for this recipe goes to Kraft What&rsquo;s Cooking.
description: A comforting bowl of satisfying pasta with frozen vegetable blend with broccoli and kraft dinner macaroni & cheese — saucy, satisfying, and ready to become a weeknight staple.
title: Cheesy Tuna Noodle Casserole

content:
Ingredients 1 lb ground chuck beef 1 tbsp cooking oil 1 chopped onion 1 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 3 tbsp raw long grain rice 1 10-oz can tomato soup 1 soup can of water 3 cups coarsely chopped cabbage (packed) Instructions In large frying pan stir and break up the ground beef in oil for a minute or two then add onion,
salt, pepper and rice. Mix well while gently sautéing for 2 or 3 minutes, then add soup and
water and mix well. Turn cabbage into greased baking dish or casserole which may be carried
to the table. Pour meat mixture evenly over the raw cabbage. DO NOT STIR. Bake covered
