1-1/2 hours at 325 degrees F.
NOTE: this reheats excellently so if you are so inclined, make and bake two of these and serve
one for the current meal and cool and chill and cover and store for a meal three or four days
hence.
description: Soft, golden buttery rolls with ground chuck beef and cooking oil that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Casserole Cabbage Rolls

content:
Ingredients 1 can Cheddar Cheese Soup ½ soup can milk dash ground black pepper 4 cups hot cooked elbow macaroni, (about 2 cups uncooked) 1 medium tomato, chopped 1 green onion, sliced 3 Tbsp dry bread crumbs 2 Tbsp margarine or butter, melted Instructions Combine soup, milk and pepper in 1-1/2 quart baking dish.
Stir in macaroni, tomato and green onion. Mix bread crumbs with margarine and sprinkle over macaroni mixture.
Bake at 400 F. for 20 minutes or until heated through.
Prep/cook time: 30 minutes. Serves 4.
description: A comforting bowl of savory pasta with cheddar cheese soup and ½ soup can milk — saucy, satisfying, and ready to become a weeknight staple.
title: Campbell's Easy Macaroni and Cheese

content:
Ingredients 2 lb stewing beef 1 pkg dry onion soup mix 1 10-oz tin cream of mushroom soup 1 cup red table wine Instructions Combine ingredients in casserole. Cook slowly for 2 – 3 hours in a 250 degree oven. Serve on a bed of egg noodles.
YIELD: Six to eight servings.
description: Warm, satisfying robust casserole with stewing beef and dry onion soup mix that feeds a crowd and tastes even better the next day. The definition of comfort food.
title: Beef Casserole

content:
Ingredients 3 potatoes, sliced thin 1 onion, sliced thin 3 carrots, sliced thin 1/4 cup uncooked long grain rice 1 cup frozen green peas 12 sausages or wieners, sliced into l-inch pieces 1 l0-oz tin tomato soup 1 tin water Instructions Put everything in casserole in order given. If using sausages, boil for l0 minutes first. Mix
soup and water and pour over top. Cover and bake at 350 degrees F for 2 hours.
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with potatoes and onion — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: 7-Layer Dinner

content:
Ingredients 1 cup butter 5 eggs 1-1/4 cup sugar 2 cups flour 1 cup red glazed cherries 1 cup green glazed cherries 1 cup chopped walnuts Instructions Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time. Add flour. Mix well. Add cherries and nuts. Mix well. Bake in greased angelfood tin or a pan lined with well-greased brown paper.
Bake for 1-1/2 – 2 hours at 300 degrees.
description: This decadent cake with red glazed cherries and green glazed cherries is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: White Christmas Cake

content: Ingredients ½ cup shortening 1 cup sugar 1 egg 2 cups flour 1 tsp salt 1 tsp cinnamon ½ tsp nutmeg ½ tsp cloves 1 tsp baking soda 1 tin tomato soup 1 cup walnuts 1 cup raisins or dates Instructions Cream together shortening and sugar. Fold in beaten egg. Add soup. Mix well. Stir in flour, spices, salt, baking soda, sifted together. Add raisins and nuts last. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Ice with cream cheese frosting if desired.
description: A stunning rich cake with ½ cup shortening and cinnamon — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Tomato Soup Cake

content:
Ingredients 1 box chocolate cake mix 1 cup Hellmann’s Mayonnaise 1 cup water 3 eggs 1 tsp ground cinnamon (optional) Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
Blend all ingredients.
Bake as directed on the box.
description: A stunning rich cake with box chocolate cake mix and hellmann’s mayonnaise — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Super Moist Chocolate Mayo Cake

content:
Yield: 24 muffins Baking Time: 20-25 minutes
Preparation Time: 15 minutes Oven Temperature: 350 degrees
Ingredients 1 cup sugar ¾ cup margarine 3 eggs 1 tsp soda 2 cups sifted flour 1 tsp cinnamon 1 tsp nutmeg 1/3 cup sour milk ¾ cup jam Instructions Cream sugar and margarine. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition.
Sift dry ingredients together. Add dry ingredients alternately with sour milk. Fold in ¾ cup jam. Fill muffin tins ¾ full (grease if not using liners).
Bake 20-25 minutes. At 350 degrees.
NOTE: Any flavour jam, or jellied or whole cooked cranberries can be used.
description: Golden-topped indulgent muffins loaded with ¾ cup margarine, soda, and sifted flour. Mix the batter in one bowl, bake, and watch them vanish.
title: Strawberry Jam Muffins

content:
Ingredients ½ cup shortening ½ cup white sugar ½ cup light brown sugar 2 eggs 1-3/4 cup flour ½ tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt ½ tsp vanilla ½ cup crushed pineapple including some syrup ½ cup water Instructions Into beater bowl measure shortening and sugars and beat until creamy. Beat in eggs. Remove beaters.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt. Combine pineapple, water and vanilla. Stir flour mixture into creamed mixture alternately with the pineapple-water mixture until blended. Line the muffin pans with pleated paper cups and carefully spoon in batter to fill 7/8 full. Bake at 350 degrees 20 – 25 minutes. Cool in paper cups on cake rack.
When cool, frost with icing made by beating together 3 tbsp butter, 3 tbsp solid parts of crused pineapple, 1-1/2 cups icing sugar and ½ tsp lemon juice.
description: A stunning rich cake with ½ cup shortening and ½ cup white sugar — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Pineapple Cupcakes

content:
3 cups sliced fresh rhubarb, ½” pieces 1 Tbsp unsalted butter, room temperature ¼ cup strawberry or seedless raspberry jam 1 cup sugar 1-1/2 cup all-purpose flour 2-1/2 tsp baking powder ½ tsp cinnamon 2/3 cup butter, room temperature 2 eggs ¼ tsp each vanilla &amp; almond extract 1/3 cup milk Put rhubarb in a medium bowl. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Generously coat a deep dish pie plate with margarine.
Put jam in a small bowl. Microwave on medium until melted – about 30 seconds. Pour jam over rhubarb and stir to coat evenly. Ingredients Evenly sprinkle ¼ cup sugar in pie plate. Spoon rhubarb and jam in pie plate. In a medium bowl stir flour, cinnamon and baking powder. In a large bowl, with an electric mixer whip ¾ cup butter until creamy. Beat in remaining ¾ cup sugar until well mixed. Instructions Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in almond and vanilla extracts &amp; mix well. Gradually beat in 1/3 of the flour mixture and about ½ of 1/3 cup milk. Repeat until flour &amp; milk are used up. Batter will be thick so spoon carefully to coat all the fruit. Bake in a 350 degree oven 40 – 45 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes – scrape edges with a knife and invert on a serving plate. Credit to June Brayshaw for this recipe!
description: A show-stopping luscious cake with sliced fresh rhubarb and unsalted butter. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Rhubarb Upside-Down Cake

content:
Ingredients 2/3 cup yellow cornmeal 1 cup stirred but unsifted cake flour 5 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 1 cup milk 1 egg 2 Tbsp corn or other cooking oil 1/3 cup corn syrup 1 Tbsp butter or drippings Instructions Turn the oven to 425 degrees. Next put the sifter over a bowl and into it measure the cornmeal, flour, baking powder and salt. Sift into bowl. At this stage measure the butter or drippings into a 9” x 9” pan and put it in the oven to heat up to sizzling point. To dry ingredients add egg, milk, oil and corn syrup and stir just enough to mix. Carefully remove hot pan from oven and pour batter into sizzling hot fat. Return to oven at 425 degrees and bake 20 minutes.
Cut into squares and serve as a hot bread with your main course OR serve as dessert with butter and maple or corn syrup. Your kids will drool over this.
description: This sweet cake with yellow cornmeal and stirred but unsifted cake flour is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Nugget 1942 Johnny Cake

content:
1 large lemon or any flavour cake mix 1 3-3/4 pkg Jello Lemon Instant pudding 1 cup water ½ cup oil 4 eggs Mix all together.
TOPPING;
Ingredients 2 tbsp cinnamon ½ cup brown sugar ½ cup chopped walnuts Instructions Grease tube pan. Sprinkle half topping mixture. Pour half of cake mixture. Add rest of topping mixture. Pour rest of batter on top.
Bake in a 350 degree oven for 50 minutes. (I use a large angel food cake tin.)
Credit to Stella Pep&rsquo;s Mom for this recipe!
description: This decadent cake with large lemon or any flavour cake mix and jello lemon instant pudding is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Nancy Lemon Coffee Cake

content:
FILLING:
1 14-oz fruit cocktail 1 small carton sour cream Sugar to taste 1 – 2 tsp. Drain cocktail. Set out on paper towel to dry off. Mix with sour cream. Set aside.
MIX:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup graham wafer crumbs ¼ cup sugar ¼ cup melted butter Instructions Press in 8”x 8”or 6”x 11”pan. Bake until light brown. Cool. Pour filling on cool shell. Sprinkle remaining crumbs on top. Store in refrigerator several hours or overnight.
description: A stunning rich cake with fruit cocktail and small carton sour cream — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Mock Cheese Cake

content:
Ingredients 1 cup flour ¾ cup sugar 1 tsp cinnamon 1/4 tsp nutmeg ½ tsp each salt, baking soda, baking powder 1-1/2 cups shredded carrots 2 eggs, slightly beaten 1/2 cup oil 1/4 cup water 1/2 tsp vanilla icing sugar, optional Instructions In 8”or 9”square pan with fork mix well flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.
Mix briskly with a fork until thoroughly blended. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 30 minutes or until pick inserted in center comes out clean and cake pulls away from sides of pan. Cool in pan on rack. Sprinkle with icing sugar or ice with cream cheese frosting.
description: Rich, tender indulgent cake with ¾ cup sugar and cinnamon that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Mix-in-the-pan Carrot Cake

content:
2 cups flour 2 cups white sugar 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp vanilla 1 cup walnuts 2 eggs 19-oz tin crushed pineapple Mix and bake in a 9”x 9” greased pan in a preheated 350 degree oven for 50 – 60 minutes.
Ice with cream cheese frosting:
Ingredients 8 oz cream cheese (250 gr.) ¼ cup butter 1 tsp vanilla 1 lb icing sugar
description: A stunning melt-in-your-mouth cake with walnuts and tin crushed pineapple — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Mexican Wedding Cake

content: Ingredients 2 cups flour 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp cinnamon ¾ tsp nutmeg ¼ tsp salt ¼ cup cocoa 3 eggs 2 cups sugar ½ cup oil ¾ cup buttermilk OR 2 Tbsp vinegar 1 tsp vanilla 2 cups grated zucchini Instructions Mix all together. Bake in 350 degree F. oven for 40 – 60 minutes.
description: Rich, tender indulgent cake with cinnamon and ¾ tsp nutmeg that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Mirsh's Chocolate Zucchini Cake

content:
First Step:
1 cup white sugar 4 tbsp cocoa 1-3/4 cup all-purpose flour 2 tsp baking soda Pinch of salt Second Step:
Ingredients 1 cup mayonnaise 1 cup cold water 1 tsp vanilla (Beat well) Instructions Sift dry ingredients together. Add to liquid mixture and beat for 2 minutes. Bake in 9”x 9” cake pan for 40 minutes at 350 degrees. - 25 minutes for cupcakes.
description: Homemade heavenly cake with cocoa and pinch of salt that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Hundred Dollar Cake

content:
Ingredients 2 large eggs 1 cup white sugar ¼ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla ½ cup milk 3 tbsp margarine or butter 1 cup all-purpose-flour 1 tsp baking powder Instructions Into beater bowl break eggs and beat until fluffy then slowly add sugar until very light and doubled in bulk. Beat in salt &amp; vanilla. In a pan heat together the milk and margarine OR butter to just below boiling point. While very hot pour into egg mixture BEATING ALL THE TIME. Remove beaters.
Sift the flour &amp; baking powder over and FOLD in. Pour into greased 8 x8 or 9 x 9 pan &amp; bake on center rach in 350 degree oven for 30 min. or until when touched with fingertip dent does not remain. Cool in pan on rack for a few minutes. Then if desired, cover with:
TOPPING
1/2 cup soft margarine
½ cup light brown sugar
½ cup fine coconut
Cream those together. Spread evenly &amp; carefully over slightly cooled cake and heat under hot broiler – 4” from burner – until it bubbles but DO NOT LET IT BURN. Cool.
ALSO CALLED “HOT MILK SPONGE CAKE – use 9 x 13 pan same instructions as above.
4 eggs 2 cups white sugar
2 cups flour 2 tsp baking powder
¼ tsp salt 1 tsp vanilla
1 cup hot milk
description: Homemade heavenly cake with large eggs and ¼ tsp salt that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Hot Milk Sponge Cake

content: Ingredients ½ cup butter or margarine 1 cup white sugar 2 eggs 2 cups pastry flour (1-3/4 cup all-purpose) 1 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt 1 cup milk 1 lb cooking gumdrops 1 lb seedless raisins Instructions Cream sugar and butter; add eggs. In separate bowl, dredge gumdrops and raisins with ½ cup of the flour. To the creamed mixture, add 1 cup flour and ½ cup milk, beating well after each addition. Add remaining flour with baking powder and salt. Beat well. Add remaining milk and the floured raisins and gumdrops. Mix thoroughly. Bake in a well-greased loaf pan in a 350 degree oven for 1 hour.
description: A show-stopping sweet cake with ½ cup butter or margarine and pastry flour. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Gumdrop Cake

content:
Ingredients 2 tbsp butter 1/3 cup white sugar 2 eggs 3 tbsp milk 1 cup flour 1 tsp baking powder Pinch of salt Instructions Cream butter and add sugar little by little, then add the eggs and salt. Sift flour with baking powder. Add to egg and sugar mixture alternately with milk. Beat well. Pour into a well-greased and floured flan pan. Bake 15 minutes in a 325 degree oven. Knock firmly on bottom of pan when you take it out of the oven to loosen it from pan. Cool. Invert on a place and fill it with fruit or filling of your choice. Top with whipped cream if desired.
I (Mom) have fond memories of a dear friend, Mrs. Ilsa Preuss, every time I look at this recipe.
description: Rich, tender sweet cake with pinch of salt that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: German Fruit Cake

content:
300 degrees - 50 – 60 minutes
Ingredients for Cake:
2 eggs 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar 2 cups flour 2 tsp Baking Soda 1 – 14 oz tin fruit cocktail (drained but reserve juice) – Slightly crush fruit w/a fork Ingredients for Topping:
Ingredients 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar ½ cup pure Carnation Milk ½ cup butter or margarine Instructions Directions for Cake: Beat eggs with Sugar. Put juice &amp; Baking Soda in eggs &amp; beat. Add Fruit Cocktail &amp; beat. Add flour &amp; beat. Pour into a 13” x 15” well-greased pan. Bake for 50 - 60 minutes in a 300 degree oven. Test w/a toothpick for doneness.
Directions for Topping: Put topping in a saucepan &amp; boil it for about 3 minutes. (It should get a little thicker.) Do this just about 10 minutes before cake is finished baking.
After cake is done, loosen the edges and prick holes in the cake w/a fork. While cake is still warm, pour hot topping over cake.
This is called Nova Scotia Fruit Cocktail Cake and credit to Alda Sivret for this one!
description: This decadent cake with w. sugar and – 14 oz tin fruit cocktail – slightly crush fruit w/a fork is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Fruit Cocktail Cake

content:
2 cups white sugar 4 eggs 1-1/2 cups oil 2 Tbsp cinnamon 2 cups flour 1 Tsp salt 2 tsp baking powder 3 cups grated carrots 2 tsp baking soda 1 cup chopped nuts Blend sugar and oil. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each one. Beat well. Sift together dry ingredients. Add gradually to first mixture. Add carrots and nuts last.
Bake in 9” x 15” pan 45 minutes approx. at 350 F.
Icing For Carrot Cake:
Ingredients 1 8-oz pkg cream cheese ¼ lb butter 1 lb icing sugar 2 tsp vanilla Instructions Mix Well.
description: A stunning rich cake with cinnamon and grated carrots — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Frontier Kitchen Carrot Cake

content:
Ingredients 1 package chocolate pudding mix (cooked type) 1 package chocolate or devil&rsquo;s food cake mix 1 cup chocolate chips Instructions Prepare chocolate pudding mix according to package directions. Blend dry chocolate or devil&rsquo;s food cake mix into hot pudding.
Pour into greased and floured 13&quot; x 9&quot; pan.
Sprinkle with chocolate chips. Bake at 350°F for 30–35 minutes.
description: Rich, tender melt-in-your-mouth cake with chocolate pudding mix and chocolate or devil's food cake mix that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Frontier Kitchen Triple Fudge Cake

content:
All who attended Creation Unique’s Camp Wanna-sew enjoyed Edna’s Lemon Cake.
1 Lemon Cake Mix 1 Jello Instant Pudding Mix Add:
Ingredients 4 eggs 1 cup of water ½ cup oil Instructions Mix well. Pour into pan and bake for 1 hour at 325 degrees.
For the glaze, Edna stirred lemon juice with lemon juice.
description: Homemade heavenly cake with lemon cake mix and jello instant pudding mix that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Edna's Lemon Cake

content:
Ingredients 1 pkg strawberry cake mix 1 pkg strawberry Jello (4 servings) Instructions Dissolve Jello in ¾ cup boiling water. Add ½ cup cold water; set aside. Mix and bake cake as directed in a 9”x 13” pan. Cool 20 – 25 minutes. Poke deep holes about 1”apart in cake (still in pan) with a meat fork or large nail.
Pour Jello into holes.
Refrigerate cake while preparing Quick and Easy Icing made with whipped topping and instant pudding.
description: This decadent cake with strawberry cake mix and strawberry jello is the one people request by name. Tender, flavorful, and gorgeous enough for a celebration (or just a really good Tuesday).
title: Double Strawberry Sheet Cake

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup cornmeal ½ cup sugar (use more than this) ½ cup flour 4 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 1-1/2 cup milk 1 egg, beaten ½ cup oil or shortening Instructions Heat oven to 425 F. Mix corn meal, sugar, flour, baking powder and salt. Add milk, eggs and oil stirring only to mix well. Pour into a 9” baking dish or casserole. Bake until light brown or about 25 minutes. Test till it comes out clean.
Makes about 6 servings. Double recipe for a 9x13 pan. My Mom (Memere) used to call this Johnny Cake and served as a dessert with maple syrup.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with cornmeal and ½ cup sugar that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Corn Bread

content:
Ingredients 2 cups sugar 1 cup oil 2 cups flour 2 tsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp salt 2 tsp cinnamon 4 eggs, beaten 3 cups grated carrots 1 cup finely chopped walnuts (optional) Instructions Mix all together. Do not overbeat. Bake for 1 hour at 350 F. Check after 35 minutes. Do not overbake.
Use a greased angelfood cake pan or a 9 x 13 pan.
Credit to Colette Tremblay for this recipe!
description: A stunning rich cake with cinnamon and grated carrots — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Carrot Cake

content:
This fruitcake is an easy- to-make classic holiday offering. If you like, make it ahead, wrap and freeze for entertaining and gift-giving.
Ingredients 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 2 eggs, lightly beaten 1 jar (750 ml) mincemeat 1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 2 cups chopped mixed glace fruit 1 cup chopped walnuts Whole glace cherries Instructions Grease two 9 x 5 loaf pans – set aside.
In bowl, stir together flour and baking soda. In separate large bowl, combine eggs, mincemeat, condensed milk, chopped fruit and walnuts. Add flour mixture; mix well. Divide batter between prepared pans.
Bake in 300 F oven for 80 to 85 minutes or until cake tester inserted in center comes out clean. Let cool in pans on racks for 15 minutes. Remove from pans, let cool completely. Garnish with glace cherries.
Makes 2 cakes – 16 to 20 servings.
description: A stunning rich cake with mincemeat and tin sweetened condensed milk — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Cherry Walnut Fruitcake

description: Rich, tender indulgent cake with to 1 cup icing sugar and pkg cream cheese that hits all the right notes. This is the cake that turns an ordinary day into a celebration.
title: Cherry Cheese Cake

content: Ingredients 2 medium-sized carrots, peeled &amp; grated ½ tsp salt ½ cup raisins ¾ tsp cinnamon ½ cup healthy margarine ¼ tsp each ground ginger, nutmeg, allspice 1 cup all-purpose flour ¾ cup dark brown sugar 1 cup whole wheat flour 1 egg 2-1/2 tsp baking powder ½ cup buttermilk 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Coat 12 muffin cups with a little margarine. Measure 1 cup grated carrot into a bowl. Add raisins. Melt ½ cup margarine in microwave or saucepan. Let cool to room temperature.Place flours, then baking powder, soda, salt and spices into large bowl. Add sugar and stir with a fork until evenly blended. Make a well in center. Place egg, buttermilk and vanilla in another bowl. Whisk with a fork. Whisk in cooled margarine. Pour into well. Stir just until dry ingredients are moistened. Add carrots and raisins. Stir until evenly distributed.Immediately spoon into muffin cups. Do not press down.Bake in center of oven until a skewer inserted in center of muffin comes out almost clean, 17 to 20 minutes. Cool on a rack. Makes 12 muffins.1 muffin – 235 Calories, 4 gr Proteins, 9 gr Fat, 36 gr Carbohydrate
description: Tender, domed rich muffins bursting with medium-sized carrots, ½ tsp salt, and ½ cup raisins — the kind that make weekday mornings feel like a treat.
title: Carrot Cake Muffins

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 Tbsp baking powder 1 Tbsp white sugar ¼ tsp salt 1 cup shredded old cheddar cheese 1 pkg onion soup mix 1 cup milk 1 egg ¼ cup oil Instructions Stir together dry ingredients and stir in the cheese
Combine soup mix and milk. Let stand 5 min.
Beat egg, milk mixture and oil and add to dry ingredients mixing lightly just to combine.
Spoon into well-greased muffin cups and bake 375 degrees for 15- 20 minutes.
Serve hot!
description: Tender, domed rich muffins bursting with ¼ tsp salt, shredded old cheddar cheese, and onion soup mix — the kind that make weekday mornings feel like a treat.
title: Cheese and Onion Muffins

content: Ingredients ¼ cup shortening 1/2 cup firmly packed brown sugar 1/4 cup molasses 2 eggs, unbeaten 1 cup milk 1 cup all-purpose flour 1-1/2 tsp baking powder 1/2 tsp baking soda ¾ tsp salt 1-1/2 cup Quaker Natural Wheat Bran (Green Box) 1/2 cup raisins Instructions Cream shortening and sugar together. Add molasses and eggs and beat together well. Add milk and then bran. Combine flour, baking power, soda and salt together. Add to liquid ingredients. Add raisins if desired. Place in greased muffin pans. Bake in 400 degree oven for l8 – 20 minutes. Makes l dozen large muffins.
description: Fluffy sweet muffins with ¼ cup shortening and firmly packed brown sugar that rise tall and taste even better than the bakery. Best eaten warm with a smear of butter.
title: Bran Muffins

content:
Ingredients 2-1/3 cups Bisquick 3 Tbsp melted butter ½ cup milk 1 quart strawberries, sliced and sweetened 3 Tbsp sugar Instructions Heat oven to 425 F. Stir all ingredients except strawberries until soft fough forms.
Drop by 6 spoonfuls onto ungreased baking sheet.
Bake 10 – 12 minutes. Split; fill and top with strawberries and whipped cream, if desired.
description: Homemade sweet cake with bisquick and melted butter that rises tall, stays unbelievably moist, and tastes like it took way more effort than it actually did.
title: Bisquick Strawberry Shortcake

content:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Blend or sift together:
2 cups all-purpose flour ¼ cup sugar 4 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt Chopped walnuts (optional) Make a well in center of the dry ingredients.
Combine and add:
Ingredients 1 beaten egg 2/3 cup milk 1 cup well-mashed bananas 2 Tbsp vegetable oil Instructions Stir only until combined. (The batter should be lumpy.) Fill greased muffin cups 2/3 full.
Bake in preheated oven for approx. 20 minutes.
Yield: 12 medium-sized muffins – for approx. 20 minutes.
42 very small muffins – for 10 minutes.
Note: I (Mom) iced mine with cream cheese frosting.
description: Golden-topped sweet muffins loaded with ¼ cup sugar, ¼ tsp salt, and chopped walnuts. Mix the batter in one bowl, bake, and watch them vanish.
title: Banana Muffins

content:
Combine together:
2 beaten eggs 2 cups buttermilk 2 cups brown sugar 2 cups All-Bran (coarse) 1 cup Mazola Oil 1 tsp vanilla Sift together, then add to the above:
Ingredients 2 cups flour ¼ tsp salt 2 tsp baking soda 1 cup raisins 2 tsp baking powder Instructions Fill medium-sized paper cups ¾ full. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes in a 375 degree oven until golden brown.
BB - I have no idea why this recipe has &ldquo;No. 2&rdquo; in its name. Likely the 2nd iteration of this, or just the 2nd Bran Muffin recipe she had. Regardless, I thought it was funny and topical with the type of recipe, so I left it in.
description: Golden-topped heavenly muffins loaded with beaten eggs, buttermilk, and brown sugar. Mix the batter in one bowl, bake, and watch them vanish.
title: All-Bran Muffins - No. 2

content:
3 level Tbsp meringue powder 500 gr (or 1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar 3-1/2 oz warm water ½ tsp cream of tartar Combine ingredients, mixing slowly. Then beat at medium speed for 7 – 10 minutes. Utensils have to be grease-free. Icing dries rapidly so keep it covered with a damp cloth. To restore texture, re-beat slowly. Makes 3-1/2 cups.
OR:
Ingredients 3 egg whites 500 gr. (1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar 1 tsp almond flavoring Instructions Whip at low speed till desired working consistency is reached. Keep covered with damp cloth while working flowers. Store tightly covered in fridge. Whip again before using.
description: Silky, sweet decadent frosting with level tbsp meringue powder and sifted icing sugar that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Royal Icing

content: Ingredients ¼ cup lightly salted butter 1-1/2 cups dark brown sugar 1/3 cup milk 1 tsp vanilla extract 3 cups icing sugar Instructions Melt butter in small pan and stir in brown sugar.Stir in milk and bring mixture just to a boil.Cool to lukewarm. Stir in vanilla and icing sugar and beat until creamy.Credit to Country Living for this recipe!
description: Sweet, homemade decadent candy with ¼ cup lightly salted butter and dark brown sugar — the kind of treat that makes you feel like a confectioner. Wrap them up for gifts or keep the whole batch.
title: Simple Caramel Frosting

content: Ingredients 2 squares unsweetened chocolate 1 cup icing sugar 1 egg, beaten 2 Tbsp butter 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Melt chocolate in top of double boiler. Stir in icing sugar, beaten egg, butter and vanilla. Remove from heat at once and stir. Cool a little before spreading on brownies.
description: Silky, sweet decadent frosting with squares unsweetened chocolate and icing sugar that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Rich Chocolate Icing

content:
Ingredients ¼ cup butter 2-1/2 or 2-3/4 cup sifted icing sugar 1 tsp vanilla 1 egg Instructions Cream butter well; stir in egg. Add sugar, a little at a time, beating until light and fluffy. Stir in vanilla.
Yield: enough for 2 pans.
description: Silky, sweet indulgent frosting with ¼ cup butter and or 2-3/4 cup sifted icing sugar that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Rich Butter Icing

content: Ingredients 1 envelope whipped topping mix (2 to 2-1/2 cup yield) 1 pkg instant pudding mix (4 serving size) 1-1/2 cup cold milk 1 tsp vanilla, if desired Instructions In a chilled bowl, blend and whip topping mix, instant pudding, cold milk and vanilla until stiff (3-8 min.). Immediately frost cake and store in refrigerator. Serve chilled. Frosted cake may be frozen for storage
description: Silky, sweet rich frosting with envelope whipped topping mix and instant pudding mix that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Quick and Easy Icing

description: Silky, sweet sweet frosting with butter or margarine and vanilla. that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Miracle Icing

content:
Ingredients 1 cup granulated sugar 1/3 cup water Pinch of cream of tartar Pinch salt 2 egg whites 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Combine sugar, water, cream of tartar and salt in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, stir to dissolve sugar and continue to cook, without stirring, until mixture reaches soft ball stage of 240 F./115C.
Place egg whites in a bowl and beat with an electric mixer until white and frothy. Continue to beat, adding sugar mixture very slowly, until very light and fluffy. Beat in vanilla.
Credit to Bonnie Stern for this recipe!
description: Silky, sweet decadent frosting with granulated sugar and pinch of cream of tartar that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Marshmallow Frosting

content: Ingredients 2 cups icing sugar ¼ tsp salt 6 tablespoons butter ½ tsp vanilla 4 squares bitter chocolate ½ cup milk 2 eggs Instructions Blend sugar, milk, eggs, salt and vanilla in a bowl over ice water. Melt chocolate and butter together. Add while warm to first mixture and beat until desired consistency.
description: Rich, creamy heavenly frosting with icing sugar and ¼ tsp salt — the finishing touch that turns good baking into something spectacular.
title: Fluffy Chocolate Frosting

content:
Ingredients 2 egg whites, unbeaten 1-1/4 cups sugar 1 pkg Jell-O (any flavour) Dash of salt 1/3 cup water 2 tsp light corn syrup Instructions Combine all ingredients in top of double boiler. Beat 1 minute to mix thoroughly. Place over boiling water and beat constantly with rotary beater (or at high speed of electric mixer) 7 minutes, or until frosting will stand in stiff peaks. (Stir frosting up from bottom and sides of pan occasionally.) Remove from boiling water. Beat 1 minute, or until thick enough to spread.
Makes 4-3/4 cups frosting, or enough to cover tops and sides of two 9-inch layers, 2 dozen cupcakes, or a 10-inch tube cake, or to fill and frost a 9-inch tube cake that has been split horizontally into 2 layers.
description: Rich, creamy sweet frosting with egg whites and jell-o — the finishing touch that turns good baking into something spectacular.
title: Jell-O Seven Minute Frosting

content:
Ingredients 5 Tbsp flour 1 cup milk 1 egg ½ cup butter ½ cup shortening 1 cup white sugar 1 tsp lemon extract or vanilla Instructions Mix flour, milk and egg and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens.
Cool. Combine sugar, butter and shortening, and then blend in cooled mixture. Beat until smooth and fluffy; mix in flavoring and spread on cake.
description: Silky, sweet sweet frosting with ½ cup butter and ½ cup shortening that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Dot's Flour Icing

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup butter, shortening or margarine ¼ tsp salt ½ tsp lemon juice 1 tsp (1/2 of a small packet) Strawberry unsweetened instant soft drink mix 3-1/2 cups sifted icing sugar ¼ cup milk Instructions Combine Crisco, salt, lemon juice, dry strawberry instant drink mix and 1 cup sugar.
Add milk and remaining sugar alternately and mix until smooth. Add milk or sugar as necessary for good spreading consistency.
description: Rich, creamy heavenly frosting with ¼ tsp salt and ½ tsp lemon juice — the finishing touch that turns good baking into something spectacular.
title: Creamy Strawberry Icing

content: Ingredients 1 4-oz pkg cream cheese 2 cups icing sugar 2 tbsp milk ¼ tsp salt Instructions In beater bowl beat cheese until creamy then add sugar and salt and beat until smooth, adding milk slowly until of smooth almost runny spreading consistency.
description: Rich, creamy melt-in-your-mouth frosting with pkg cream cheese and icing sugar — the finishing touch that turns good baking into something spectacular.
title: Cream Cheese Frosting

content:
Ingredients 1 cup Crisco (or ½ cup Crisco + ½ cup white margarine) 1 kg (2.20 lbs) icing sugar ½ cup water (I use a wee bit more – in icing I use to ice the cake – it’s easier to spread) 1 tsp almond flavoring (I use 2 – 3 tsp of almond or coconut flavor) Instructions This icing is the easiest recipe for doing cakes. It has a creamy texture and is great to practice making flowers. And for the borders – just add a few drops of water.
Cream shortening, gradually add icing sugar. Cream again. Add flavoring while still creaming. Add water slowly also while you are creaming until you reach the desired texture. (IMPORTANT THAT YOU DO NOT CREAM YOUR ICING AT TOO HIGH A SPEED FOR IT CAUSES AIR BUBBLES IN YOUR ICING – THEREBY MAKING IT DIFFICULT TO HAVE YOUR ICING SMOOTH ON THE CAKE.)
description: Silky, sweet indulgent frosting with crisco and kg icing sugar that spreads like a dream and makes any cake or cupcake irresistible.
title: Buttercream Icing

content: Ingredients ½ cup wild rice 2 Tbsp granulated sugar ½ cup warm water 1 pkg dry yeast (or 1 Tbsp ) 5 cups (approx.) all-purpose flour ¼ cup fancy molasses 2 Tbsp shortening 1 tsp salt ½ cup natural bran ½ cup raw sunflower seeds ¾ cup 7-grain cereal ¾ cup rolled oats Instructions In saucepan, cook wild rice in 2 cups water (500 ml) water for about 45 minutes or until tender; drain and let cool.Meanwhile, dissolve 1 tsp (5 ml) of the sugar in warm water. Sprinkle in yeast; let soften, stirring together until dissolved. Stir in ¼ cup (50 ml) of the flour to make a thin paste; set in warm place for 10 minutes or until bubbly.In large bowl and using electric mixer, combine molasses, remaining sugar, shortening, salt and 2 cups (500 ml) warm water. In order, beat in bran, sunflower seeds, yeast mixture, wild rice, 7-grain cereal and rolled oats. With wooden spoon, beat in as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make firm dough. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic, kneading in as much of the remaining flour as necessary..Place in large greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap; let rice in warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours. (NOTE: I tried the plastic wrap and found the dough stuck to it even if it was lightly greased; I choose instead to make sure that there is flour on the surface and then cover it with a clean cloth.)Punch down dough; turn out onto lightly floured surface. Divide into thirds. With rolling pin, flatten each to about 7-1/2-inch (19 cm) square; firmly roll up each into log. Place each in greased 8- x 4-inch pans. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 hours.Bake in center of 375 F (190 C) oven for 15 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom. Remove from pans; let cool on racks. Makes 3 loaves, 12 slices each.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with ½ cup wild rice and granulated sugar that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Wild Rice Many-Grain Bread

content:
Ingredients ½ cup lukewarm water 1 tsp sugar 3 cups (2 boiling hot &amp; 1 cold) water 2 tbsp sugar 1 tbsp salt 1 tbsp dry yeast or 1 envelope 7 – 8 cups all-purpose flour (6 measured out to start with, then technique determines the rest - see below) ¼ cup shortening or margarine Instructions Pour 2 cups boiling water on salt, 2 tbsp sugar and shortening in bowl. When shortening has melted, add 1 cup cold water. Prepare yeast (see NOTE below).
Pour softened yeast into water mixture, add flour – about 6 cups to start with – and stir. Keep adding flour until dough seems thick enough to work by hand. Then put dough on well-floured board and knead and mix in more flour until dough feels nice and rubbery and no longer sticks to your hands – about 10 – 15 minutes.
Place in a bowl, cover and let double in size. (about 1.5 hours) Then punch down and shape into 4 small loaves (9 x 4-1/2 pans) and let rise again until doubled (about 1.5 hours).
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: The bread dough needs a warm(ish) environment to rise nicely in. A helpful trick I found was to turn the oven light on beforehand and let your dough rise in the closed oven (with the light still on). This last time, I even put a little space heater in my laundry room and had the temperature around 25 degreec Celcius in the room. That seemed to work well. Whe Mom used to make this at the lake, Dad would keep the place as hot as Satan&rsquo;s arse crack, so I&rsquo;m trying to mimic that.
Bake for 25 minutes in a preheated 375 degree oven. (Billy&rsquo;s Note: Make sure to use the bottom rack! I burnt the tops of the friggers last time, even on the middle rack.) . (Also, set it for 20 mins and give it a look. With my oven, I think 25 would be too long.)
For a soft crust, butter top while hot.
NOTE:To prepare yeast:
- 1tsp. Sugar (Billy&rsquo;s Note: I like my bread &ldquo;airy&rdquo;, so I put an extra 1/2 tsp of sugar in for the yeast - so 1.5 tsp total)
- ¼ cup boiling water – combine with another ¼ cup of cold …
description: Homemade buttery bread with ½ cup lukewarm water and dry yeast or 1 envelope that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Water Bread

content: Ingredients 1 French bread, 12 to 15”, cut into 1”cubes ½ cup butter or margarine, softened Parsley flakes Grated Parmesan cheese Garlic powder Instructions Spread both sides of bread with butter. Place, cut side down, on a cookie sheet. Sprinkle one side with cheese, parsley and garlic powder. Broil 4 inches from the heat until light golden brown. Turn slices and repeat with other side. Serve immediately.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with french bread and ½ cup butter or margarine that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Toasty Garlic Bread

content:
Ingredients 2 cups stirred all-purpose flour 2 tsp sugar 4 tsp baking powder ½ cup butter of shortening ½ tsp cream of tartar 2/3 cup milk ½ tsp salt Instructions Sift together into mixing bowl the flour, baking powder, cream of tartar, salt and sugar. Cut in butter (or fat of your choice) with pastry blender until size of peas. Add milk all at once and quickly stir in.
Turn out onto flour sprinkled board. Pat smoothly to ¾ inch thickness and cut with 2-inch floured cutter. Bake on ungreased baking sheet at 450 degrees F. for 10 minutes.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with stirred all-purpose flour and ½ cup butter of shortening that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Tea Biscuits Supreme

content:
Ingredients 8 cups sifted all-purpose flour 5 Tbsp baking powder 2 tsp salt 1 cup skim milk powder 1-1-2 cup shortening Instructions TEA BISCUIT MIX: Combine first 4 ingredients. Mix in shortening until it resembles fine bread crumbs. May be stored in covered container in refrigerator or cool place. Makes about 12 cups.
TEA BISCUITS: Combine Tea Biscuit Mix and water. Knead gently on floured board. Roll 1/3 inch thick; cut into triangles. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet for 10 minutes at 450 F.
Makes 16 biscuits.
Credit to Angela McOrmond for this recipe!
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with sifted all-purpose flour and skim milk powder that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Tea Biscuit Mix

content:
Ingredients 1/3 cup butter or margarine ½ cup chopped celery ¼ cup chopped onion 5 cups soft bread cubes 1 cup chopped walnuts 1 tsp salt ½ tsp poultry seasoning ¼ tsp pepper ½ cup chopped parsley ¼ cup chicken broth 1 egg, well beaten Instructions In a skillet, melt butter. Saute celery and onion until tender. Combine remaining ingredients and shape mixture into eight balls. Place on a greased baking sheet. Bake at 375 degrees for about 20 minutes.
Yield: 8 servings.
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food with butter or margarine and ½ cup chopped celery. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Stuffing Balls

content:
2-1/2 cups Bisquick baking mix 4 Tbs cold butter or margarine 1 heaping cup grated cheddar cheese ¾ cup cold whole milk ¼ tsp garlic powder TOPPING:
Ingredients 2 Tbs butter ½ tsp garlic powder ¼ tsp dried parsley flakes Pinch of salt Yield: 1 dozen biscuits
Combine Bisquick with cold butter in medium bowl, mixing with pastry cutter or fork until remaining chunks of butter are size of peas. Add cheddar cheese, milk and ¼ tsp garlic powder. Mix by hand until combined but don’t overmix.
Drop ¼-cup portions of dough onto an ungreased cookie sheet using an ice cream scoop. Bake for 15 to 17 minutes, or until tops begin to turn light brown.
While you remove biscuits from oven, melt 2 Tbs butter in small bowl in microwave. Stir in ½ tsp garlic powder and dried parsley flakes. Use brush to spread garlic butter over tops of all biscuits.
description: Homemade toasty bread with bisquick baking mix and tbs cold butter or margarine that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Red Lobster Cheddar Bay Biscuits

content:
A Tea Biscuit dough should be soft but not sticky. Little kneading improves the quality of the biscuits, but should be done with a gentle touch as too much handling tends to make them tough.
Preheat oven to 450 F. Blend or sift together:
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 4 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt Instructions Cut finely 1/3 cup shortening (or ¼ cup oil).
Add ¾ cup plus 2 Tbsp milk (200 ml).
Stir with a fork to make a soft dough. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently 8 to 10 times. Roll or pat to desired thickness, (biscuits all be doubled in height when baked). Cut out with a floured cutter.
Place biscuits on an ungreased baking sheet, close together for soft-sided biscuits or about 1” apart for crusty-sided biscuits. Bake in a preheated oven for 12 to 15 minutes.
YIELD: 18 to 20 – 1-3/4” biscuits.&gt; &amp;nbs𰒤�+
description: Homemade toasty bread that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Purity Tea Biscuits

content:
Ingredients ½ cup lukewarm water 1 pkg or 1 Tbsp yeast 1 tsp sugar 3 cups tepid water (2 boiling, 1 cold) ¼ cup shortening ½ cup sugar 2 tsp cinnamon ½ tsp salt 2 beaten eggs 1 cup lightly floured raisins Flour Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Dissolve sugar and yeast in 1stquantity of water
Melt ½ cup sugar, shortening and salt in 2 cups boiling water, then cool mixture with the one cup of cold water.
Beat the eggs and cinnamon. Add yeast mixture, egg mixture and the raisins to the water mixture.
Stir and add flour gradually until you have a soft dough that leaves the sides of the bowl.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured board and knead for about 10 minutes, adding flour as needed, until dough is smooth and springy. Shape into a ball and put into a large bowl, cover with a towel and put in a warm place until dough has doubled in volume. (About 1-1/2 to 2 hrs.) Punch dough down, shape into 4 small loaves (8-1/2” x 4-1/2” well-greased pans), cover again and let double in volume again.
Bake 30 – 45 Minutes at 350 degrees. Because of higher content of sugar and raisins, bread seems to brown fast so you should keep an eye on it for the last 5 or 10 minutes.
description: Soft, golden toasty rolls with ½ cup lukewarm water and or 1 tbsp yeast that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Raisin Bread

content:
Blend together:
1-1/2 cups flour 1 – 2 tbsp sugar 3 tsp baking powder 1/8 tsp salt Combine:
Ingredients 1 beaten egg 1-3/4 cup milk 2 tbsp vegetable oil or melted shortening Instructions Add liquids to the dry ingredients. Beat only until combined. Bake in a hot skillet slightly greased until edge of pancake begins to dry and lose its gloss. Turn and bake until golden brown.
NOTE: If batter thickens, add a little more milk.
description: Fluffy, golden decadent stacks with – 2 tbsp sugar and beaten egg — griddle-kissed and ready for a cascade of syrup. Weekend mornings were made for these.
title: Purity Pancakes

content: Ingredients 1-1/2 cup Bisquick 1/3 cup very hot water Instructions Heat oven to 450 degrees. Grease cookie sheet or 12-inch pizza pan. Mix 1-1/2 cups Bisquick baking mix and 1/3 cup very hot water; beat vigorously 20 strokes. Turn dough onto surface well dusted with baking mix. Knead about 60 times or until smooth and no longer sticky. With hands dusted with baking mix, press dough into 13-inch circle on cookie sheet or press in pizza pan; pinch edge, forming ½ inch rim. Top pizza to taste. Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until crust is brown and cheese is melted and bubbly.
description: Classic heavenly pie with bisquick and very hot water — every slice is a perfect balance of flaky pastry and luscious filling. Save room for seconds.
title: Pizza Pie Dough

content:
Ingredients ½ cup vegetable oil 1 minced onion 1 cup spaghetti sauce or 8-oz tomato sauce 1 lb grated cheddar cheese 1 tin mushroom pieces, drained 1 tsp oregano ½ tsp salt Dash pepper Dash garlic powder Instructions MIX. Spread on buns and broil till bubbly.
Sauce keeps for a month.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with ½ cup vegetable oil and minced onion that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Pizza Buns

content: Ingredients 3 eggs 1 cup oil 2 cups white sugar 1 tsp vanilla 2 cups shredded raw zucchini 3 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp each salt, baking powder 1 tsp cinnamon or 2 tsp nutmeg 1/2 tsp baking soda 1/2 cup chopped nuts Raisins, if desired Instructions Line two 4”x 8”loaf pans (or one large pan) with greased waxed paper. Combine first 5 ingredients; add mixed dry ingredients and stir well. Pour into loaf pans and bake at 325 degrees for approx. l hour. Test to make sure loaf is baked right through.
description: Simple, delicious zesty vegetables with shredded raw zucchini and each salt. Sometimes the best meals are built around what's growing in the garden.
title: Pearl's Zucchini Loaf

content:
Ingredients 4 cups lukewarm water 4 tsp sugar 3 pkg or 3 Tbsp yeast 3 eggs ½ cup melted shortening ½ cup plus 3 tsp sugar 9 cups (more or less) flour Instructions Add water and sugar together then sprinkle yeast over top. Let rise.
Mix eggs, shortening and 2ndamount of sugar together. Add yeast mixture and flour.
Knead. Let rise l to 1-1/2 hours. Place two finger-sized pieces of dough in muffin tin.
Bake at 350 F. for 15 minutes.
Credit to Terry Cavanaugh for this recipe. (NOTE: Terry is Rose Lejambe’s sister-in-law.)
description: Soft, golden toasty rolls with lukewarm water and or 3 tbsp yeast that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Parker House Buns

content:
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour (sifted) 1/3 cup St Lawrence Oil 3 tsp baking powder 2/3 cup milk 1 tsp salt Instructions Sift together flour, salt &amp; baking powder. Add oil and milk. Stir with a fork until all the dry ingredients are moistened. Place the dough on a piece of wax paper. Dampen the working surface so that the paper won’t slip. Cover the dough with wax paper and roll it with a rolling pin to ½” thickness. Peel off wax paper. Cut into biscuits. Place on an ungreased baking sheet. Bake in a very hot oven 475 degrees F. for 10 to 12 minutes. Yields about 15 med biscuits.
For CHEESE TEAC BISCUITS add ½ cup grated cheese to the flour mixture.
For SALAD TEA BISCUITS add 2 tbsp MAGIC FRENCH DRESSING and decrease the amount of Corn Oil to ¼ cup.
For CINNAMON ROLLS roll out the dough between wax paper to ¼ inch thickness. Spread with a mixture of 1/3 cup BEE HIVE GOLDEN CORN SYRUP and 1 tsp cinnamon. Roll up. Cut crosswise into 1 inch rolls. Place cut side down in oiled muffin tins. Bake at 475 degrees F for 12 minutes.
description: Warm, homemade golden bread with st lawrence oil — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Minute Magic Tea Biscuits

content:
Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing (Stuffing) Ingredients ½ cup chopped onion ½ cup butter or margarine 1 tsp poultry seasoning ½ tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 8 cups dry bread cubes1 cup chicken broth or water NOTE: (I (Mom) use 1 tsp chicken bouillon powder in 1 cup boiling water.)
Saute onions in margarine. Add all other ingredients and mix well. This amount stuffs a 10-lb turkey or can be heated in a greased foil-covered bread pan in a 325 degree oven for 40-45 minutes if it has been refrigerated, or at a lower temperature for a longer period.
description: Warm, homemade golden bread with ½ cup chopped onion and ½ cup butter or margarine — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing

content:
Combine in a cup:
1/2 cup lukewarm Water 2 tsp sugar Sprinkle over the top:
2 pkg dry yeast Let soften for about 15 minutes.
Scald in a saucepan over boiling water:
2/3 cup Milk Mix in &amp; Set aside to cool:
½ cup St. Lawrence Oil ½ cup Bee Hive Corn Syrup Measure and sift together:
Ingredients 5-1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp salt ¼ cup sugar Instructions Combine with the milk, corn oil and syrup mixture …5 beaten eggs and 2 cups of the sifted flour, sugar, salt mixture. Mix to give a thick batter. Beat vigorously and stir in yeast.
Add remaining flour and stir until smooth. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with wax paper and a cloth and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size. Punch down, knead lightly on a floured board and divide the dough into 2 parts.
This basic sweet rich dough may be used for making your own coffee cakes or sweet rolls. Try the following variation – on the following sheet.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with lukewarm water and dry yeast that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Magic Sweet Yeast Bread

content:
Roll out one portion of the Sweet Yeast Bread (recipe foundHERE) to a large rectangle 14” x 10”. Spread with the following mixture to within about ½” of the edge.
Ingredients ¼ cup Bee Hive Corn Syrup 2 tbsp St. Lawrence Oil ¼ cup brown sugar 1 tsp cinnamon Instructions Roll up tightly starting at the long side of the rectangle. Into each of 12 muffins pour 1 tsp St. Lawrence Oil and 1 tsp
Bee Hive Corn Syrup. Place 3 to 5 half pecans or walnuts and half a maraschino cherry in the bottom of each tin.
Cut the roll into 1-1/2” slices and place cut side up in muffin tin. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in size and then bake in a moderate oven, 375 degrees F for 15 minutes. Remove from tins almost immediately after they are taken our of the oven. Makes 12 Chelsea buns.
description: Soft, golden buttery rolls with ¼ cup bee hive corn syrup and st. lawrence oil that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Magic Chelsea Buns

content:
Ingredients 1 cup flour 2 Tbsp sugar 4 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 1 cup cornmeal 1 egg 1 cup milk 1 Tbsp melted butter Instructions Mix and sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Add cornmeal. Beat egg and milk and add to the flour mixture. Mix thoroughly and add melted butter.
Bake in a greased cake pan 8-inch square at 350 F. for 30 minutes.
description: A show-stopping melt-in-your-mouth cake with ½ tsp salt and cornmeal. Every forkful is pure, sweet indulgence — the recipe you'll be asked to share again and again.
title: Johnny Cake

content:
2 cups flour 1 cup shortening 3 tsp baking powder 2 tbsp sugar MIX LIKE PASTRY AND THEN ADD;
Ingredients 1 egg 1 tsp vanilla ½ cup milk Instructions Mix well. Roll out on floured board. Divide evenly and place in muffin tins. Put about 1 tbsp jam in center of each and fold corners together. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 minutes.
description: Warm, homemade golden bread with ½ cup milk — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Jam Buns

content:
Ingredients 2 eggs ¾ cup white sugar 1 cup milk 3 cups cake or pastry flour OR 2-5/8 cups All-Purpose flour 1 Tbsp baking powder 1-1/2 tsp salt (I use ½ tsp) ½ tsp nutmeg 1 Tbsp melted margarine or butter Instructions BEAT eggs and sugar in beater bowl until pale – about 5 minutes. Sift together flour, baking powder, salt and nutmeg. Add them alternately with the milk to the bowl and beat until blended. Beat in melted butter or margarine.
HEAT fat to between 365 – 375 degrees F. – no hotter or it will burn the doughnuts before they are cooked through.. 1-1/4” of oil should do.
ROLL batter into balls about 1” in diameter, carefully place balls in fat and let cook for about 2 minutes. IF they do not turn over themselves give them a little nudge with your slotted spoon. When rich gold, skim out onto paper-towel-lined plates. When all are done, sprinkle generously with powdered sugar.
MAKES about 48 and they are superb.
description: A delicious homemade satisfying recipe with ¾ cup white sugar and cake or pastry flour or 2-5/8 cups all-purpose flour — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Drop Doughnuts (Revolving Spudniks)

content:
Ingredients ½ cup shortening 1 cup sugar 2 eggs 1 grated rind of lemon or 2-3 tsp flavor 1-1/2 cup flour ½ cup milk ½ tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder Instructions Cream sugar and shortening. Add eggs and beat well. Add rind. Mix in dry ingredients with milk. Pour into greased bread pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 – 60 minutes until golden brown and toothpick comes out clean. While loaf is still hot, poke holes and spread glaze on loaf.
Glaze: Juice of ½ lemon (or 1 tbsp ReaLemon) and ¼ cup icing sugar.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with ½ cup shortening and grated rind of lemon or 2-3 tsp flavor. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Darryl's Lemon Loaf

content:
Ingredients 1 cup sugar 3 eggs ½ cup less 1 tbsp soft butter 6 tsp (2 Tbsp) baking powder 4 cups sifted all-purpose flour 1 tsp salt ½ tsp nutmeg 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup milk Instructions Into beater bowl measure the sugar, eggs and butter and beat until blended and smooth. Stir and measure the flour, add the baking powder, salt and nutmeg to it and sift again Add to first mixture along with the vanilla and milk and beat until smooth. Scrape down sides of bowl. Cover snugly and refrigerate four hours or overnight. Cut dough into quarters, scoop out ¼ with rubber scraper and out on generously floured board to ¼” thickness. (Keep remainder of dough in refrigerator.
Cut out with floured doughnut cutter, transferring doughnuts and cut-outs to board on which you can carry them to the stove. Incidentally, this dough is highly satisfactory to handle. Heat fat or oil to 390 degrees F (or hot enough to brown cube of bread in 40 seconds). Fat should be at least 1” deep or deeper if you are using a deep-fat fryer. Lift doughnuts on wide spatula and carefully slip into heated fat without crowding. As soon as they rise to surface and show gold colour on bottom, turn over. Stand over them and do not overbrown. At once list from fat with slotted spoon and drain on paper toweling
Continue with all remaining dough until it is used up, piling leftover scraps up and rolling and cutting them out too. I had 48 doughnuts and 48 hole cut-outs.
P.S. You can use this recipe to make Revolving Sputniks (Drop Doughnuts) by dropping 1” balls of dough from a teaspoon into the hot fat.
description: Flavorful, satisfying satisfying comfort food with ½ cup less 1 tbsp soft butter and sifted all-purpose flour. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Doughnuts

content:
Ingredients 1-1/2 cup sifted all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp ground ginger ¼ tsp salt ½ cup butter, softened ½ cup sugar 1/3 cup whole-berry cranberry sauce 1 egg ½ cup unsulfured molasses ½ cup boiling water Instructions Grease and flour an 8” x 8” baking pan. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Sift together flour, baking soda, ginger and salt. Set aside. Using an electric mixer in a large bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
Beat in cranberry sauce and egg. Blend in molasses. Gradually stir in dry ingredients and beat thoroughly. Stir in water. Turn into the baking pan.
Bake gingerbread for 40 minutes, or until toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool in pan for 20 minutes. Remove and cool on rack. Decorate cake, if desired, by sifting confectioners’ sugar over a paper doily placed on the gingerbread.
Makes 10 pcs.
description: Warm, homemade toasty bread with sifted all-purpose flour and ground ginger — crusty on the outside, pillowy soft on the inside, and impossible to eat just one slice.
title: Cranberry Christmas Gingerbread

content:
Ingredients 1 cup flour, sifted ½ cup yellow corn meal 3 tsp baking powder 1 cup milk ½ tsp salt 1 egg, beaten ½ cup sugar 1 Tbsp melted shortening Instructions Mix and sift flour, baking powder, salt and sugar. Stir in corn meal. Add milk to beaten egg and stir into first mixture. Add shortening and blend. Turn into shallow greased 8-inch pan. Bake at 400 degrees for 20 minutes.
Credit for this recipe goes to the Temagami Cookbook.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with ½ cup yellow corn meal and ½ tsp salt that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Corn Bread

content:
Credit for this recipe goes to Table Treasures.
Ingredients ½ cup chopped onion 1 Tbsp butter 1 egg ½ cup milk 1-1/2 cup Bisquick ½ cup grated cheese Instructions Saute onion in butter. Mix egg in milk. Add Bisquick and grated cheese, then onion. Stir only until mixed well. Pour into buttered 1-1/2 quart pan. Top with more cheese or poppy seed if desired. Bake at 400 F. for 20 – 25 minutes.
Good warmed over; best when served warm.
description: Homemade fragrant bread with ½ cup chopped onion and ½ cup milk that's surprisingly simple and outrageously delicious. Proof that the best things in life are warm and carb-filled.
title: Cheese Bread

content:
Ingredients 1 Pkg or 1 Tbsp Yeast ¼ cup lukewarm water 2 eggs, plus enough scalded &amp; cooled milk (or cooled boiled water) to make 2 cups (beat this slightly) ½ cup (1/4 lb)melted butter or margarine 1 tsp salt ½ cup sugar 6 cups all-purpose flour Instructions In large bowl sprinkle yeast &amp; add warm water &amp; stir until dissolved. Add eggs, milk (or water), butter, salt, sugar and stir until well blended.
STIR in 3 cups flour, beating until smooth after each addition. Add the 4thcup; beat until dough is smooth &amp; elastic. Stir in 5thcup of flour to make stiff dough.
MEASURE the 6thcup &amp; sprinkle about half of it on a board. Turn the dough out onto the heavily floured board &amp; with well floured hands knead dough until it is smooth &amp; elastic (about 5 – 7 minutes) using remaining flour as needed. (NOTE) I don’t usually measure the flour: I’m just copying this quantity as it is in the recipe.)
PLACE the dough in a well buttered bowl &amp; butter the top lightly. Cover &amp; let rise in a warm place until almost doubled in bulk. (1-1/2 - 2 hrs).
WHEN doubled, punch down, squeezing the air bubbles with hands. Divide dough into 4 parts, wrap each portion in plastic film &amp; refrigerate. (This last step can be eliminated but rolls are easier to shape when chilled.)
FOR BUTTERHORNS – roll dough to 8” circle, ¼” thick. Brush with butter. Cut into wedges: roll each toward point. Place in greased pan; point down. FOR CRESCENTS – bend into a curve. NOTE – for dinner rolls – pinch off pieces of dough about 1” in diameter &amp; form into balls. Arrange balls in greased round or 8” or 9” square baking pans, allowing about 1” spaces between them.
LET RISE in warm place until doubled in size.
BAKE at 375 degrees F. for about 11 – 13 minutes (depending on oven).
description: A tried-and-true bold recipe with or 1 tbsp yeast and ¼ cup lukewarm water that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Butterhorns

content:
Credit for this recipe goes to Clara Therrien
Ingredients 1 cup All-Bran ½ cup sugar ½ cup raisins 2 tsp baking powder 1 cup milk 1 cup flour ½ cup butter Pinch salt 1 egg Instructions Mix all together and put into muffin tin. Bake at 400 F. for 15 to 20 minutes.
description: Fluffy sweet muffins with all-bran and ½ cup sugar that rise tall and taste even better than the bakery. Best eaten warm with a smear of butter.
title: Bran Muffins

content:
Ingredients 1 cup flour 7/8 cup milk 4 tsp baking powder 2 Tbsp milted shortening OR melted bacon fat ½ tsp salt 4 Tbsp brown sugar ½ cup corn kernels 1 cup corn meal 6 bacon slices 1 egg Instructions Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Line an 8x8x2-inch pan with bacon slices.
Sift flour, baking powder, salt and brown sugar. Add corn meal and stir to blend.
Beat egg, add milk, melted fat and corn kernels and stir to blend.
Pour liquid ingredients over dry ingredients and stir just enough to blend.
Pour batter into pan over bacon slices.
Bake in preheated oven from 25 to 30 minutes or till light brown on top.
To serve, unmould same as an upside-down cake.
Serve hot.
description: Soft, golden warm rolls with milted shortening or melted bacon fat and ½ tsp salt that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Bacon Corn and Cheddar Bay Biscuits

content: Ingredients 3 bananas, mashed 1 cup white sugar 1 egg, beaten 1-1/2 cup flour 1 tsp each baking soda, baking powder 1/8 tsp salt 2 tsp melted butter or oil 1 tsp vanilla Instructions Mash bananas and mix with white sugar. Add beaten egg. Sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Add to first mixture. Beat well. Add melted butter and vanilla. Bake in a greased loaf pan at 350 degrees for 35 to 45 minutes. Test to make sure it is baked right through.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with bananas and each baking soda that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Banana Bread

content:
Ingredients 1 10-oz pkg spinach 1 cup mayonnaise 1 cup sour cream 1 pkg dry Knorr vegetable soup mix 2 green onions 1 small can water chestnuts Instructions Cook spinach just lightly; drain and chop. Chop onions and water chestnuts. Mix all ingredients together and serve with small pieces of bread.
NOTE: You can use a large round load of pumpernickel bread. Cut bread from the center and use this to serve your dip. The bread taken from the center is used as small pieces to dip.
description: Freshly baked golden-brown bread with pkg spinach and mayonnaise that fills the kitchen with the most incredible aroma. The kind of recipe that makes you wonder why you ever bought store-bought.
title: Spinach Bread Dip

content: Ingredients 1 cup (250 gr) sour cream 4 oz cream cheese, softened 1 tsp onion salt 1 tsp horseradish ½ tsp Worcestershire sauce 1 tsp lemon juice 1 tbsp ketchup 4 oz shrimp, chopped Instructions Combine and mix all ingredients except shrimp until blended. Add shrimp. Refrigerate. Serve with crackers and/or sliced vegetables.
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with sour cream and cream cheese — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Shrimp Dip

content:
Ingredients 500 ml sour cream 440 gram jar salsa shredded medium cheese chopped fresh tomatoes chopped mild onions chopped lettuce Instructions Layer cheese/sour cream/salsa mix, chopped tomatoes, chopped onions and chopped lettuce. Top with more grated cheese.
Makes 2 12”round dishes. (My low green Pyrex dish) (BB - Left this in because it made me laugh. I&rsquo;m guessing we don&rsquo;t have that &ldquo;low green Pyrex dish&rdquo; anymore?)
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with sour cream and jar salsa — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Salsa Dip

content:
Ingredients 1 pkg (8 oz) cream cheese, softened 1 tin (7-1/2 oz) salmon, drained &amp; flaked 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce ¼ tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper Snipped parsley Snack crackers Instructions Mix cream cheese, salmon, Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper until well blended, about 1 minute. Shape into a ball. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Sprinkle with parsley. Garnish with sliced olives if desired. Serve with crackers.
Option:- Substitute 1 tin (6-1/2 oz) tuna, drained &amp; flaked, for the salmon.
About 6 servings.
description: Delicate, flavorful rustic fish with cream cheese and tin salmon that comes together quickly and tastes like a night out. Seafood done right.
title: Salmon Cheese Ball

content: Ingredients 1 (16 oz ) tin red salmon ¼ or ½ lb butter ¼ tsp dill weed (or 1 tsp fresh) 2 Tbsp Miracle Whip Salt &amp; pepper to taste Instructions Blend for a few minutes, then chill.
description: Crispy on the outside, melt-in-your-mouth tender inside — this robust fish with tin red salmon and ¼ or ½ lb butter is weeknight fancy at its absolute best.
title: Salmon Pate

content:
Ingredients 1 Pumpernickel 1 300 gr pkg of Chopped Spinach 1 40-gr Knorr Vegetable Soup Mix 1 500 mL tub of Sour Cream Instructions Defrost chopped spinach &amp; squeeze out excess water.
Hollow out center of pumpernickel.
Cut center of pumpernickel into cubes or slices. In a small bowl combine sour cream soup mix and spinach.
Stir until thoroughly mixed. Place in center of pumpernickel
Refrigerate for 1 hour
Include bagels as an option for dipping.
description: Smooth, tangy garden-fresh dip with pumpernickel and pkg of chopped spinach — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Pumpernickel Spinach Dip

content:
Ingredients 1 lb 5” wieners (12 to a pound) OR equivalent in smoked or Polish sausage or pepperoni or knockwurst 1-1/4 cups white vinegar ½ cup water 2 tbsp sugar 2 tsp pickling spices 1 garlic clove, chopped Instructions Cut wieners in thirds crosswise. If other smoked type sausages are used, cut them into large bite-sized pieces. Measure all remaining ingredients into shallow saucepan, bring to a boil, add cut wieners, reduce heat, cover and SIMMER gently 30 minutes. Remove from heat and pour into preheated, broad-mouthed jar with lid. Cool then refrigerate for 3 or 4 days before using. During the marinating period, shake jar to make sure all wiener pieces are covered with marinade.
Serve as appetizers on toothpicks or as a snack with sliced rye bread. These will keep in the refrigerator for a week.
description: Old-fashioned hearty pickles with ” wieners or equivalent in smoked or polish sausage or pepperoni or knockwurst and white vinegar — crisp, tangy, and made the way grandma did it. Fill the pantry and count the days until they're ready.
title: Pickled Wieners

content:
Ingredients 2 lbs chicken wings 1/3 cup honey 2 tbsp Oxo chicken bouillon liquid 2 tbsp soya sauce 1 tbsp lemon juice ½ tsp garlic powder ¼ tsp ground ginger Instructions Cut wings in two sections and remove tips. In a large baking pan, place wings in a single layer. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and raise temperature to 475 degrees. Drain fat from pan.
Combine Oxo with remaining ingredients and pour over wings. Bake 25 minutes until golden and glazed with sauce. Reserve sauce for dipping.
Makes 6 appetizer servings.
description: Juicy, flavorful robust chicken with chicken wings and honey — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Oxo Honey Garlic Chicken Wings

content: Ingredients ½ lb grated med. Cheddar Cheese (about 2 cups) ½ cup milk 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce 8 oz softened cream cheese ½ tsp garlic salt 3 oz crumbled blue cheese ¾ cup finely chopped walnuts Instructions Mix in a 2-quart bowl, cheeses, milk, Worcestershire sauce and garlic salt. Chill if necessary before shaping into a ball. Roll in walnuts. Chill until firm (about 3 – 5 hours). Serve with crackers.
description: A tried-and-true satisfying recipe with ½ lb grated med. cheddar cheese and ½ cup milk that proves the best meals don't need to be complicated — just made with care.
title: Nutty Cheese Ball

content:
Ingredients 2 cups sour cream (500 ml) 1 pouch Loney’s Onion Soup Mix 2 Tbsp green pepper, finely chopped ½ tsp Tabasco spicy sauce 2 tsp tomato ketchup ½ tsp chili powder Instructions In a medium-sized bowl mix Loney’s Onion Soup Mix with sour cream. Add remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly. Serve immediately with an assortment of raw vegetables.
For a Mexican-style dip, use twice the amount of spicy sauce. Serve with tortilla chips.
description: Creamy, addictive bright dip with sour cream and pouch loney’s onion soup mix — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Loney's Express Dip

content:
Ingredients 1 (1-oz) packet Original Ranch Party Dip 1 pint sour cream 1/4 cup bacon bits 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese Instructions Mix Ranch Party Dip with sour cream.
Add bacon bits and shredded cheddar cheese.
Mix and serve.
description: A rich, flavorful crisp dip loaded with packet original ranch party dip, pint sour cream, and bacon bits. The appetizer that everyone parks themselves next to and never leaves.
title: Hidden Valley Bacon & Cheddar Dip

content: Ingredients 1 8-oz pkg cream cheese 1 can (6 oz) crabmeat 1 tsp horseradish 1 tsp milk 1 tsp minced onion Instructions Combine all ingredients. Serve cold or bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees. Serve with cocktail rye bread.
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with pkg cream cheese and crabmeat — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Exquisite Crab Dip

content:
Ingredients 2 lbs chicken wings ½ cup melted butter or margarine ¼ tsp garlic powder 2 tbsp chopped parsley 1 cup dry bread crumbs ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese ½ tsp salt Instructions Cut chicken wings in two pieces, discarding the tips. In a small shallow bowl, combine butter and garlic powder. In another bowl, combine remaining ingredients. Dip chicken into butter mixture, then into crumb mixture. Place on greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 – 60 minutes or until done.
Yield: about 20 appetizers.
description: Juicy, flavorful robust chicken with chicken wings and ½ cup melted butter or margarine — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Crispy Chicken Wings

content: Ingredients 1 250 gr pkg cream cheese ½ jar Cheez Whiz cheese 1 cup shredded cheese 1-1/2 tsp onion flakes 1-1/2 tsp Worcestershire sauce Instructions Mix well. Refrigerate till firm. Makes 2 balls. Roll in parsley flakes or crushed walnuts.
description: A delicious homemade robust recipe with pkg cream cheese and ½ jar cheez whiz cheese — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Corey's Cheese Ball

content: Ingredients 1 7-oz tin crab meat 1 pkg. (250 gr) cream cheese 1 tsp lemon juice Sprinkling of parsley Instructions Mix all ingredients. Seafood sauce on top for flavor and color (optional)
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with tin crab meat and cream cheese — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Colette's Crab Meat Dip

content: Ingredients 1 cup sour cream 1 cup mayonnaise 1 pkg frozen chopped spinach 1 pkg Knorr vegetable soup mix Instructions Drain spinach well, then mix all ingredients together. If desired, put dip into pumpernickel bread.
description: Smooth, tangy zesty dip with sour cream and mayonnaise — simple to throw together but guaranteed to be the most popular thing on the snack table.
title: Colette's Spinach Dip

content:
Ingredients Quantity desired of bacon strips Quantity desired of water chestnuts Sugar Soya Sauce Instructions In one bowl place granulated sugar and in second bowl place soya sauce. Dip chestnuts firs in soya sauce and then into the sugar coat.
Partly cook bacon to remove some of the grease but bacon is still limp. Roll prepared chestnuts in bacon and secure with a toothpick.
Place on a cookie sheet. Just before serving, place in oven or under broiler to crisp bacon.
description: Succulent rustic pork with quantity desired of bacon strips and quantity desired of water chestnuts — slow-cooked (or quickly seared) to melt-in-your-mouth perfection. Every bite is a flavor bomb.
title: Chestnut & Bacon Roll-ups

content:
This recipe came from Pat Johnston, who is Yolande&rsquo;s Friend
Ingredients 1 cup Butter or Margarine 1 Pkg (1/2 lb.) Imperial Cheese (Red) Dash Cayenne Pepper Pinch of salt ½ tsp Worcestershire Sauce 1-1/2 cups Flour 4 cups Rice Krispies Instructions Combine all ingredients by hand or with a pastry blender. Form into small balls. Place on cookie sheet and flatten with fork. Bake 15 – 20 minutes at 350 degrees.
description: A beautiful decadent pie with butter or margarine and imperial cheese with a buttery crust and a filling so good you'll want to eat it by the spoonful.
title: Cheese Krispies

content:
Ingredients 1 28-oz or 796 ml can diced tomatoes 1 medium onion, chopped 1 Tbsp dried basil 2 Tbsp olive oil 1 tsp garlic powder 1 loaf French bread 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese Instructions COMBINE tomatoes, onion and basil in large skillet. BRING to boil, simmer, uncovered, over medium heat until thickened – about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
COMBINE oil and garlic powder. SLICE bread in half lengthwise, place cut side up on large baking sheet. PLACE under broiler to toast bread. BRUSH bread with garlic oil. SPREAD tomato mixture evenly over bread; sprinkle with cheese (optional). PLACE under broiler until cheese melts, about 2 minutes.
CUT into 2 inch pieces and serve immediately.
Preparation Time: 10 minutes.
Cooking Time: 20 minutes
Makes about 16 appetizers or luncheon servings. Diced tomatoes are convenient and flavourful to use in this simple version of the classic Italian appetizer.
description: A delicious homemade comforting recipe with or 796 ml can diced tomatoes and medium onion — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Bruschetta Fast and Easy

content:
Ingredients 7 oz tuna or chicken, drained and flakes 1 hard-boiled egg, chopped ¼ cup mayonnaise 1 tsp lemon juice 2 6-oz pkg sliced mozzarella cheese 2 8-oz pkg sliced party salami Instructions Mash together tuna or chicken, egg, mayo and lemon juice; set aside.
Cut each cheese slice into 4. Arrange a piece of cheese on each slice of salami, trim cheese to fit.
Spread a tsp. of tuna or chicken mixture on cheese. Roll up and secure with 3 wooden picks. Chill. To serve, cut each roll in 3. Makes 8 dozen appetizers.
NOTE: I (Mom) would experiment with different cheeses.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with tuna or chicken and hard-boiled egg — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Antipasto Roll-ups

content:
3 cups water 2 cups tomato juice 1 large onion, diced 1 cup diced carrots 1 cup diced celery 1 cup diced yellow turnip 1 cup diced peeled potato 1 tsp salt 1/4 tsp pepper Combine all 9 ingredients in large saucepan. Bring to a boil on medium. Reduce heat. Cover. Simmer for about 20 minutes until vegetables are tender. Makes about 7 cups.
NOTES:
Ingredients The &ldquo;20 minute simmer&rdquo; took longer for me. I like my veggies mushy in stuff like this though. Very good, though. An easy 4 out of 5. Credit to Company&rsquo;s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.
description: Rich, comforting rustic soup with tomato juice and large onion that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Vegetable Soup

content:
1/2 cup margarine (or butter), softened 4 oz. block of cream cheese, softened 1/4 tsp onion powder 1/4 tsp garlic powder 1/8 tsp cayenne powder 1 cup grated Asiago (or Romano) cheese 3 tbsp chopped fresh parsley 1 large French bread loaf, cut into 3/4 inch slices Beat first 5 ingredients in medium bowl until well combined.
Add Asiago cheese and parsley. Stir.
Spread cheese mixture on both sides of each bread slice. Lay out long piece of foil on counter. Keeping slices in order, reshape loaf towards 1 end of foil. Wrap foil over loaf. Folder ends to seal completely. Bake in 375 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 minutes until cheese it melted and bread is warmed through. Makes about 15 slices.
NOTES:
Ingredients This was great. We used mozzarella cheese instead of Asiago because we had mozzarella and have no idea what an Asiago is. 4 out of 5 Credit to Company&rsquo;s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with block of cream cheese and onion powder. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Tasty Cheese Loaf

content:
1 1/4 cups all purpose flour 1/2 cup cornstarch 1/2 cup icing sugar 1 cup butter, softened pinch of salt Preheat oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium bowl sift together cornstarch, icing sugar, and flour. Add butter; stir until smooth dough is formed.
Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Place two inches apart on cookie sheet. Flatten with your fingers or fork. Decorate with seasonal sprinkles.
Bake for 20-22 minutes, until edges are lightly brown.
TIP: I like to put 1/3 cup of mini semi-sweet chocolate chips in half of the dough. To mix it up a little.
NOTES:
Ingredients These were really good! 4.5 out of 10 Credit to The Happy Baker cookbook.
