description: Warm from the oven and loaded with all purpose flour, cornstarch, and icing sugar, these luscious cookies are the ones everyone reaches for first at the bake sale.
title: Shortbread Cookies #2

content:
1 cup soft butter 1 1/2 cups flour 1/2 cup cornstarch 1/2 cup icing sugar 1 tsp vanilla or almond flavouring Roll out or cookie press. Decorate with small pieces of red and green cherries. Bake in 325 degree Fahrenheit oven for around 15-20 mins.
NOTES:
Ingredients I used margarine once and butter the next. MUCH better with real butter. 4 out of 5 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Decadent cookies packed with soft butter, cornstarch, and icing sugar — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: Shortbread Cookies #1

content:
1/3 cup all-purpose flour 1 tsp paprika 3/4 tsp seasoned salt 3/4 tsp salt 1/4 tsp pepper 12 chicken drumsticks, skin removed Combine first 5 ingredients in large resealable freezer bag. Dampen chicken with water. Add a few pieces to bag. Seal bag. Shake until chicken is well coated. Arrange chicken in single layer on greased baking sheet. Repeat with remaining chicken and spice mixture. Bake in 425 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 minutes. Turn chicken. Bake for about 15 minutes until juices run clear. Serves 4.
NOTES:
Ingredients This was pretty good. Could use a bit more salt though. 3.5 out of 5. Credit to Company&rsquo;s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.
description: Hearty rustic casserole packed with paprika, seasoned salt, and chicken drumsticks — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Oven-Baked Chicken

content:
3/4 brown sugar 1/2 cup sugar 3/4 cup butter 2 eggs 1 tsp vanilla 1 1/2 cups all purpose flour 1 tsp baking soda 1/2 tsp salt 1/2 tsp cinnamon 2 cups quick cooking oats 1 cup raisins Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium sized bowl blend together sugars and butter until light and fluffy.
Add eggs and vanilla; mix thoroughly.
In a separate bowl sift together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon; stir into wet mixture. Add oatmeal and raisins; stir until mixed.
Drop by heaping tablespoonfuls, two inches apart onto lined cookie sheet. Bake for 10-12 minutes.
NOTES:
Ingredients These were awesome! 5 out of 5 Credit to The Happy Baker cookbook.
description: Perfectly sweet cookies studded with brown sugar, all purpose flour, and cinnamon. Simple enough for a Tuesday, special enough for a holiday platter.
title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookies

content:
1/2 cup butter 1 cup white sugar 2 eggs, beaten 1 1/2 cups flour 1/2 tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 1/2 cup milk grated rind of 1 lemon Cream butter and sugar. Add rind. Blend in dry ingredients alternately with milk. Bake in greased loaf pan at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 minutes.
Mix juice of 1 lemon and 1/4 cup icing sugar and drizzle over top while still hot.
NOTES:
Ingredients Was a bit dry. Maybe cook for 5-10 mins less next time. 3.5 out of 5 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Homemade rustic goodness with grated rind of 1 lemon and was a bit dry. maybe cook for 5-10 mins less next time. — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Lemon Loaf

content: Ingredients 2 Medium baking potatoes, unpeeled 1 can (10 fl oz.) chicken broth 1 cup milk 3 slices of cooked bacon (can be the ready-to-serve microwave stuff) 3/4 cup of shredded cheddar cheese, divided 2 Tbsp sliced green onions, divided 1/4 cup sour cream Instructions Pierce potatoes; microwave on HIGH 5 mins., turning over after 2 1/2 mins. Cut potatoes in chunks. Combine potatoes, broth, and milk in large microwaveable bowl. Microwave on HIGH 6 mins., stirring after 3 min. Slightly crush potatoes with a potato masher. Reserve 2 Tbsp. each of the bacon and cheese and 1 Tbsp. of the onions for topping; set aside. Add remaining bacon, cheese, and onions to soup. Serve topped with reserved bacon, cheese, onions, and sour cream.
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with medium baking potatoes and chicken broth. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Loaded "Baked Potato" Soup

content:
Ingredients 3/4 Cup brown sugar 2 Tbsp cornstarch 3/4 Cup water 3 Tbsp soya sauce 1 Tbsp honey 2 Cloves of garlic, chopped very fine Instructions Combine all ingredients in a pot. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Real good slathered on chicken wings, ribs, or in a stir fry.
Billy&rsquo;s Notes - So good! Restaurant quality.
Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Homemade bold sauce with brown sugar and cornstarch — so much better than store-bought that you'll never go back to the bottle.
title: Honey Garlic Sauce

content:
10 to 12 chicken wings, cut into 2 pieces 3/4 cup brown sugar 2 tbsp corn starch 3/4 tbsp soya sauce 1 tbsp honey 2 cloves garlic, chopped very fine (or use a garlic press) Brown chicken wings or spareribs in frying pan. Drain fat and place in casserole dish. In a separate pot, combine sauce ingredients. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Pour sauce over wings in casserole dish. Cook in microwave for 10 to 12 minutes on power level 6 or bake in oven at 325 to 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.
NOTES:
Ingredients Can also use 4 Lbs. of spareribs for this recipe. We&rsquo;ve also used it for just chicken strips and breasts as well. It&rsquo;s as good as restaurant honey garlic sauce. I&rsquo;d give this one a 5 out of 5. Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Succulent hearty chicken with to 12 chicken wings and brown sugar that's simple enough for a weeknight but impressive enough for company. Every. Single. Time.
title: Honey Garlic Chicken Wings

content:
1.5 lbs. of lean hamburg 3 small onions (chopped fine) Brown meat and onions, then drain well.
Combine all other ingredients in a large pot with meat and mix well.
Ingredients 1 28 oz. can of tomatoes 3 cans of beef consommé 4 carrots (chopped fine) 1 bay leaf (optional) 1/2 cup of pearl barley pepper to taste 2 cups of water 1 can of tomato soup 3 sticks of celery (chopped fine) 1/2 tsp. of thyme 1/2 tsp. of parsley Instructions Simmer covered at least 2 hours or all day if possible.
description: Rich, comforting rustic soup with of lean hamburg and small onions that's like a warm hug on a cold day. One pot, minimal effort, maximum satisfaction.
title: Hamburger Soup (Jenny)

content:
1 lb lean ground beef 2 onions, chopped 2 garlic cloves, minced 1 jar spaghetti sauce 2 tsp dried oregano 1 tsp dried basil Pepper to taste 6 lasagna noodles 2 cups skim or 2% cottage cheese (we use ricotta) 2 cups shredded partly skimmed mozzarella cheese 1 egg, lightly beaten 1/2 cup freshly grated parmesan In a large nonstick skillet or Dutch oven, cook beef over medium heat for about 5 minutes, or until browned, breaking up with a fork. Pour off fat. Add onions and garlic; cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until softened. Add spaghetti sauce, oregano and basil; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a large pot of boiling water, cook lasagna noodles for 10 to 12 minutes, or until tender but firm. Drain and rinse under cold water; drain well. Combine cottage cheese, mozzarella cheese, egg and 1/2 of the Parmesan cheese. Cover bottom of a 13x9 inch baking dish sparingly with some of the tomato sauce. top with a layer of lasagna noodles. Cover with 1/2 of the cheese mixture. Cover with 1/2 of the remaining tomato sauce, noodles and cheese to make 2 layers of each, then a final layer of tomato sauce. Sprinkle with remaining Parmesan cheese. Bake, uncovered, in 350 degree Fahrenheit oven for 45 minutes, or until hot and bubbly. Remove from oven and let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving. Makes 8 servings.
NOTES:
Ingredients I grated a bunch of mozzarella on it for melty goodness and tuned our really good! 4.5 out of 5 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Tender pasta tossed with lean ground beef, onions, and garlic cloves in a bold sauce that coats every strand. This is the dinner everyone asks for twice in one week.
title: Family Favourite Lasagna

content:
Ingredients 2 cups margarine 1 cup brown sugar 2 tbsp cinnamon Instructions Stir in a large bowl. Spread on hot toast.
NOTES: This really tastes like the store-bought stuff! I&rsquo;d give it a 4.5 out of 5.
Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: A delicious homemade comforting recipe with brown sugar and cinnamon — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Cinnamon Toast Spread

content:
3 bacon slices, diced 1/4 cup finely chopped onion 1/4 cup finely chopped carrot 2 cups milk 1 tbsp chicken bouillon powder 2 tbsp all-purpose flour Pepper, sprinkle 2 cans of cream style corn (10 oz., 284 mL, each) 1/2 cup fresh (or frozen, thawed) kernel corn Chopped fresh chives, for garnish Fry bacon in large saucepan on medium-high for 4 to 5 minutes until crisp. Remove to paper towel to drain. Drain, leaving 1 tbsp (15 mL) drippings in saucepan.
Add onion and carrot to reserved drippings. Sauté on medium for about 5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Combine milk and bouillon powder in small bowl until bouillon powder is dissolved.
Sprinkle flour over onion mixture. Stir. Add milk mixture and bacon. Add next 3 ingredients. Heat and stir until mixture is boiling and thickened.
Sprinkly chives over individual servings. Makes 6 cups (1.5 L)
NOTES:
Ingredients This is one of my favorite recipes. Easily a 5 out of 5! Credit to Company&rsquo;s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.
description: A soul-warming bowl of comforting soup with bacon slices and finely chopped onion — the kind that simmers low and slow and makes the whole house smell incredible.
title: Cream Corn Chowder

content:
Dry Mixture:
2 cups flour 1 tsp baking soda 1 tsp salt 1/2 cup chocolate chips Wet Ingredients:
1/2 cup margarine 3/4 cup white sugar 2 eggs 1 tsp vanilla 1 cup ripe bananas, mashed Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and prepare muffin cups. Mix flour, baking soda, and salt. Stir in chocolate chips. Cream together margarine and sugar with electric mixer. Beat in eggs 1 at a time. Mash bananas and add bananas and vanilla to wet ingredients. Add wet ingredients to dry mixture all at once. Stir (do not use mixer) until mixture is moist but not lumpy. Fill muffin cups 3/4 full with batter. Bake 25 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Yield: 12 muffins.
NOTES:
Ingredients These were pretty good! 4 out of 10 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Tender, domed rich muffins bursting with chocolate chips, ripe bananas, and these were pretty good! 4 out of 10 — the kind that make weekday mornings feel like a treat.
title: Chocolate Chip Banana Muffins

content: Ingredients 1 chicken breast 1 brick of cream cheese, softened 1/2 cup of ranch dressing 1/2 cup (or more) of Franks Red Hot 1 cup cheddar cheese, shredded Salt to taste Instructions Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and chili flakes. Bake in the oven or a pan. Shred the chicken and add the Frank&rsquo;s Red Hot to it. Mix it up. (This can be left in the fridge overnight or can go to the next step right away). In a separate bowl, combine the cream cheese and ranch dressing. Mix until mostly smooth. Add in the chicken/hot sauce mixture with a 1/2 cup of the cheddar cheese and mix till combined. Put the dip in a baking pan (8x8 works well) and cover with the remaining cheese. Bake at 350 for about 20 mins or until it starts bubbling.
description: Creamy, addictive fresh dip with chicken breast and brick of cream cheese — set it out with chips or veggies and watch it disappear before the party even starts.
title: Buffalo Chicken Dip

content:
1 can (12 oz.) beer 1 chicken (3 1/2 to 4 lbs) 2 tbsp or your favourite chicken rub (or Basic Barbecue Rub) Pop the tab off the beer can. Using a church key-style can opener, make a few more holes in the top of the can. Pour out half the beer into your mouth and enjoy!
Set up the grill for indirect grilling (ie: hot side not where you&rsquo;re putting the bird) and preheat to medium. If using a charcoal grill, place a large drip pan in the centre.
Remove the packet of giblets from the body cavity of the chicken and set aside for another use. Remove and discard the fat just inside the body and neck cavities. Rinse the chicken, inside and out, under cold running water and then drain and blot dry, inside and out, with paper towels. Sprinkle 2 teaspoons of the rub inside the body and neck cavities of the chicken. Rub the bird all over on the outside with 2 teaspoons of the rub. If you have the patience, you can put some of the rub under the skin.
Spoon the remaining rub through the holes into the beer in the can. Don&rsquo;t worry if it foams up. This is normal. Insert the beer can into the body cavity of the chicken and spread out the legs to form a sort of tripod. tuck the wing tips behind the chicken&rsquo;s back.
Stand the chicken up in the centre of the hot grate, over the drip pan and away from the heat. Cover the grill and cook the chicken until the skin is a dark golden brown and very crisp and the meat is cooked through (about 180 degrees Fahrenheit on an instant-read meat thermometer inserted in the thigh). 1-1/4 to 1-1/4 hours should do it. If using a charcoal grill, you&rsquo;ll need to add 12 fresh coals per side after 1 hour.
Using tongs, carefully transfer the chicken in its upright position on the beer can to a platter and present it to your guests. Let rest 5 minutes, then carefully remove the chicken from the beer can. Take care not to spill the hot beer or otherwise burn yourself.
NOTES:
Ingredients That last tip is a beauty. The chicken is so tender …
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with beer and chicken cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Beer-Can Chicken

content:
1/3 Cup soy sauce
3 Tbs Brown Sugar
2 Tsp Worcestershire Sauce
1/4 Tsp Onion Powder
1/4 Tsp Ground Ginger
1/4 Tsp Garlic Powder
1/4 Tsp Liquid Smoke
1/2 Tsp Table Salt
1 Lb of Lean Beef, fat removed (about 1 inch thick)
Mix first 8 ingredients in bowl. Set aside.
Cut meat across the grain into 1/2 to 1/4 inch (3 to 6 mm) thick slices. Do not cut too thick or center won&rsquo;t dry. Using partially frozen meat makes this easier to do. Add beef strips to ingredients in bowl. Stir to coat. Cover. Let stand in refrigerator overnight. Draw strips across edge of bowl to remove excess marinade. Place wire cooling tracks in baking pans with sides. Arrange single layer of beef strips over and rearrange pans at half time. Fills 2 tall, narrow jars. Store airtight in cool location or in refrigerator. Freeze for long-term storage. Makes 1/2 pound (225g).
NOTES:
Ingredients I make this with a food dehydrator. Really depends on thickness of the meat, but typically takes 6-8 hours in there. I like it tough and chewy. This is a really tasty recipe. I&rsquo;d give it a 4.5 out of 5. I also like to throw in around 1 Tsp of crushed red pepper to spice it up a bit. Credit to the Company&rsquo;s Coming Preserves recipe book I have at home.
description: Rich, savory bold beef with soy sauce and tbs brown sugar — fork-tender and packed with deep, beefy flavor. Comfort food at its finest.
title: Beef Jerky

content:
2 lb chicken breasts 2 tbsp shortening 1 can tomato soup 1/2 cup chopped onion 1/2 cup chopped celery 2 tbsp brown sugar 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce 2 tbsp lemon juice 2 tsp dry mustard Brown chicken in shortening. Stir in other ingredients. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes until chicken is tender. Serve on a bed of cooked rice.
NOTES:
Ingredients Considering all of the strong flavoured ingredients in this, it didn&rsquo;t turn out as strong as I thought it would. Still was pretty good though. 4 out of 5 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with chicken breasts and tomato soup cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: BBQ Chicken

content:
1 Cup Mashed Banana
1 Cup Canned Crushed Pineapple
2 Tsp. Lemon Juice (Fresh or Bottled)
2 Tbsp. Chopped Maraschino Cherries
3 1/2 Cups Granulated Sugar
3 oz. Liquid Pectin
Measure first 5 ingredients into large saucepan. Heat and stir constantly as you bring to a rolling boil. Boil 1 minute. Remove from heat.
Sitr in pectin. Sitr and skim for 5 minutes so fruit won&rsquo;t float. Pour into hot sterilized half pint jars to within 1/4 inch (0.5 cm) of top. Place sterilized metal lids on jars and screw metal bands on securely. For added assurance against spoilage, you may choose to process for 5 minutes in a boiling water bath. Makes 4 half pints.
NOTES:
Ingredients This was really good. 4.5 out of 5. Credit to the Company&rsquo;s Coming Preserves cookbook.
description: Homemade rustic goodness with mashed banana and canned crushed pineapple — made from scratch with real ingredients and a whole lot of love. This one's a keeper.
title: Banana Butter

content:
From the cookbook that came with our waffle iron.
Ingredients 2 cups all-purpose flour 2 tablespoons granulated sugar 1 tablespoon baking powder 1/2 teaspoon baking soda 1/2 teaspoon salt 1 1/3 cups reduced-fat milk 6 tablespoons of vegetable oil 2 large eggs Instructions Place ingredients in large mixing bowl and combine until well blended and smooth. Let batter rest 5 minutes before using. Preheat your Waffle Iron on setting #3 (pretty specific to mine, so use your own judgement).
When preheated, green indicator light will illuminate. Pour 1/3 cup of batter onto the centre of the lower grid; spread batter using a heatproof spatula to within 1/2 inch of the edge of the grid. Close lid of waffle iron - green indicator light will turn off. When green indicator light turns on again, waffle is ready.
description: Fluffy, golden melt-in-your-mouth stacks with granulated sugar and reduced-fat milk — griddle-kissed and ready for a cascade of syrup. Weekend mornings were made for these.
title: Basic Waffles

content:
Ingredients Phase 1: For the Steamer
2 cups Long-grain rice (Basmati or Jasmine, rinsed) 3 cups Broth (Chicken or Vegetable) or water 6 cloves Garlic, smashed 2 tbsp Butter or Olive oil 2 pinches of Sea salt Phase 2: The Fresh Components
3 Roma tomatoes, diced 1 cup Fresh basil, sliced into ribbons 1 cup Shredded cheese (Mozzarella or Italian blend) 4 tbsp Balsamic vinegar (or 1 tbsp Balsamic Glaze) Instructions
Instructions Steam: Place all Phase 1 ingredients into the rice steamer. Start the normal cook cycle. The garlic will soften and mellow as the rice cooks. Prep: While the rice is steaming, dice the tomatoes and toss them with a tiny pinch of salt in a small bowl. Slice the basil ribbons and set aside. The Garlic Mash: Once the steamer finishes, find the softened garlic cloves. Mash them into a paste with a fork and fold them back into the rice. Season: Drizzle the balsamic vinegar over the hot rice and fluff thoroughly with a fork until the color is consistent. Melt: Sprinkle the shredded cheese over the rice. Close the steamer lid for 1–2 minutes (with the power off) to allow the residual steam to melt the cheese. Garnish: Fold in the salted tomatoes and fresh basil ribbons. Serve immediately. Billy&rsquo;s Note: For a Gemini-generated recipes, this was pretty amazing!
description: A delicious homemade savory recipe with long-grain rice and broth or water — simple enough for any night of the week but special enough to remember.
title: Balsamic "Caprese" Rice Bowl

content:
8 bacon slices, diced 1/2 cup finely chopped onion 1/2 cup grated medium Cheddar (or Swiss) cheese 1 unbaked 9 inch (22 cm) pie shell 3 large eggs 1/2 cup milk 1/2 cup evaporated milk 1/2 tsp salt 1/8 tsp pepper 2 tbsp grated parmesan cheese Fry bacon and onion in frying pan on medium-high for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until bacon is golden. Drain. Cool.
Sprinkle Cheddar cheese in bottom of pie shell. Sprinkle bacon mixture over cheese.
Beat eggs in medium bowl until frothy. Add both milks, salt, and pepper. Beat until mixed. Pour over bacon mixture.
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese. Bake on bottom rack in 350 degree Fahrenheit oven for 40 to 45 minutes until knife inserted in centre comes out clean. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving. Cuts into 6 wedges.
NOTES:
Ingredients Maybe use less salt next time and MORE cheese! 4 out of 10 Credit to Company&rsquo;s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.
description: Homemade luscious pie with bacon slices and finely chopped onion. Golden crust, gorgeous filling, and the kind of satisfaction only a from-scratch pie can deliver.
title: Bacon and Cheese Quiche

content:
2 cups sugar 1 cup vegetable oil 3 eggs 2 tsp vanilla 3 cups flour 1 tsp salt 1 tsp baking soda 2 tsp cinnamon 4 cups raw apples, chopped Mix ingredients by hand in order given. Pour into 13x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.
Combine 1 cup of brown sugar, 1/4 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter. Boil for 4 minutes. Pour over baked cake and return to oven for a few minutes. This is scrumptious!
NOTES:
Ingredients I split the uprights and baked it for 35 mins and it turned out great. 4.5 out of 10 Credit to Camp Lau-Ren&rsquo;s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook
description: A stunning rich cake with vegetable oil and cinnamon — moist layers, rich flavor, and the kind of crumb that makes you close your eyes mid-bite.
title: Apple Dabble Cake

content:
Ingredients 1 Medium onion, finely chopped 4 Garlic Cloves, minced 1/2 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes. (or more if you like it with a bit more zing) 45 oz of canned Black Beans (3 15 oz cans) 14 1/2 oz of chicken broth (or vegetable broth) 1 10 oz can of tomatoes with green chilies (such as Rotel Tomatoes) 1 11 oz can of corn Instructions Coat bottom of large stockpot with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring frequently, until onions are soft but not brown, about 5 minutes. Place one can of beans in blender; add sautéed onion mixture, and red pepper flakes. Cover and blend on high until smooth, about 30 seconds. Pour mixture back into stockpot. Place second can of beans and broth in blender and puree until smooth; add to stockpot. Stir third can of beans (do not puree first), chicken stock, tomatoes and green chilies, and corn into stockpot. Bring to a boil, lower heat to medium and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes. Garnish with sour cream. Yields about 1 1/2 cups of soup per serving. Billy&rsquo;s Notes:I couldn&rsquo;t find 15 oz cans of black beans, so I just dived all my black beans into thirds, for how the recipe plays out.
Credit for this recipe goes to Weight Watchers:
Spicy Black Bean Soup | Healthy Recipes | WW CanadaEnjoy a tasty and healthy recipe. Learn how to make Spicy Black Bean Soup.
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with medium onion, garlic cloves, and red pepper flakes.. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Spicy Black Bean Soup

content:
Ingredients 1 can of black beans, drained and rinsed 1 can of corn, drained 1/2 cup of salsa (no sugar added) 2 Tbsp non-fat Greek yogurt (or sour cream) salt and pepper to taste Optional ingredients I&rsquo;ve tried:
Cilantro (chopped up) - added a good lemony and more of a sunny vacation-y flavour to it Chopped up jalapenos - little more zing is never a bad idea! Pour the beans + corn into a bowl.
Instructions Then add salt and mix it well.
Add salsa + greek yogurt. Mix.
Serve immediately or store in an air-tight container for up to 4 days.
Billy&rsquo;s notes: This was really good to eat just on its own or I even put it on eggs and a bed of salad for a homemade burrito bowl. Might toss some cut up jalapenos in it next time.
description: A bright, crisp bright salad with of black beans and of corn — fresh, colorful, and bursting with flavor. The side dish that steals the show.
title: Mexican Black Bean and Corn Salad

content:
Ingredients 4 boneless chicken breasts 1 cup Greek yogurt (or mayo if you&rsquo;re going a little less healthy) 1/2 cup parmesan cheese 1/2 tsp seasoned salt 1/2 tsp black pepper 1 tsp garlic powder Instructions Mix mayonnaise or yogurt, cheese and seasonings. Spread mixture over chicken breasts and place in baking dish. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes. Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:
Melt-In-Your-Mouth Chicken Breasts Recipe - Food.comThe mayonnaise make these chicken breast very moist and tender&ndash;enjoy!
description: Crispy-skinned, satisfying chicken with boneless chicken breasts and greek yogurt cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Melt In Your Mouth Chicken Breasts

content:
Ingredients Note: Make the sauce below for 1 cup of pre-cooked rice. Just double or triple it for however many cups of rice you have.
Make the rice however you make your rice. This isn&rsquo;t a rice recipe - it&rsquo;s more of a sauce recipe.
2 Tbsp low sodium soya sauce 2 Tbsp rice vinegar 1.5 Tbsp pineapple juice (NOTE: don&rsquo;t be a dope like me and buy pineapple juice. Just use the juice from the can of pineapple, noted below) 1 Tbsp tomato paste 1 Tbsp brown sugar 1 Tsp corn starch 1 Tbsp water (for corn starch slurry) 1 Medium carrot, julienned 1 Medium red bell pepper, cut thinly 1 Medium green bell pepper, cut thinly 1/2 cup of pineapple chunks (optional, but really, there&rsquo;s pineapple juice in it for cryin&rsquo; out loud. If you don&rsquo;t like pineapple, don&rsquo;t make this) Instructions Cook your rice ahead of time however you cook rice. In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, pineapple juice, tomato paste, and brown sugar to create the sweet and sour sauce. Stir until well combined. In a separate small bowl, dissolve the cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of water to create a slurry. Set aside. Heat a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the julienned carrots and sliced bell peppers. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they are slightly softened but still have some crunch. Pour the sweet and sour sauce into the skillet with the vegetables. Stir well to coat the vegetables evenly. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Gradually add the cornstarch slurry while stirring to thicken the sauce. Allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce reaches the desired consistency. If using, gently fold in the pineapple chunks and cook for an additional minute to warm them through. Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and serve it onto plates or bowls. Spoon the sweet and sour vegetable mixture over the rice. Credit for this recipe goes to Snap Calorie:
Low Fat Sweet and Sour Rice RecipeLooking to enjoy a guilt-free, flavor-packed meal? …
description: Fluffy, fragrant savory rice with low sodium soya sauce and rice vinegar — every grain perfectly cooked and packed with flavor. The side dish that steals the spotlight.
title: Low Fat Sweet and Sour Rice

content:
Ingredients 1/2 cup unsweetened Greek yogurt 3/4 cup unsweetened almond milk 1 tsp stevia (optional) 1 cup of fresh fruit (or drained canned fruit or frozen fruit). Feel free to mix it up. 1 tbsp chia seeds 2 tbsp oatmeal Instructions Chuck it all into a blender. Drink it.
Weight Watchers Points: 2
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: The points are pretty much the chia seeds and oatmeal. If you don&rsquo;t want the benefits those 2 things bring with them, this is a 0 point smoothie.
description: Mix up this robust beverage with unsweetened greek yogurt and unsweetened almond milk and watch it become everyone's favorite. Simple to make, impossible to stop sipping.
title: Greek Yogurt Smoothie

content:
Ingredients 3 bananas 2 eggs 3 Tbps rolled oats Olive oil Instructions Whisk both eggs in a bowl. Mash up just 1 of the 3 bananas on a plate, using a fork. Add to eggs and mix. Add the rolled oats to the egg/banana mix as well. Cut up the other 2 bananas in slices. In a pan (that you can cover), drizzle some olive oil to make sure the bottom is barely covered. Lay down all of the banana slices evenly around the pan, then pour the egg mixture over them. Even it out with a spoon or your spatula. Cover and cook on low for 10 mins. Flip it over and cook on low for another 5 mins (still covered). Pro-tip: Saw a neat trick to hold a plate upside down over your pan, tightly, then flip the pan and plate right over - to flip your pancake. Worked like a charm! Cut it up and put some syrup or whipped cream or whatever you want to jazz it up! Weight Watchers Points: 1 point for 1/3 of the pancake.
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: This was pretty good! Ate 1/2 of it and was full in the morning. Great protein and super healthy. Used sugar free syrup on it but I&rsquo;m not even sure it needed syrup. Also, we only had one banana so I used that in the batter and just used strawberries and frozen blueberries instead of the other 2 bananas. It came out as kind of a french toast meets banana bread type of thing.
description: Fluffy, golden rich stacks with bananas and tbps rolled oats — griddle-kissed and ready for a cascade of syrup. Weekend mornings were made for these.
title: Fruit Protein Pancake

content: Ingredients 4 cups chicken broth 3 eggs 1/3 cup cold water 3 tbsp corn starch Instructions Mix the corn starch slurry (the corn starch and the water) in a bowl Bring the chicken broth to a simmer in a medium pot Slowly stir the corn starch slurry into the chicken broth Let it simmer for around 2-3 mins, stirring occasionally Whisk the eggs in a separate bowl Slowly stir the eggs into the soup, while stirring. The more you stir, the finer the egg &ldquo;strands&rdquo; will be. Think the same technique as making scrambled eggs. Lots of stirring, little chunks. Less stirring, big chunks. Let that simmer for a few mins and enjoy. Billy&rsquo;s Note:I put a couple of cups of kimchi in this and it gave it some nice color, texture, and spice. I&rsquo;m going to put chicken in it next time to get more protein in it.
description: Hearty, hearty soup loaded with chicken broth, cold water, and corn starch. Ladle it into big bowls, grab some crusty bread, and settle in for the evening.
title: Egg Drop Soup

content:

2 lbsstewing beef, cut in 1 inch cubes (or cut up pot roast) 1 teaspoonsalt 1 teaspoonpepper 1 1⁄2tablespoonsfresh garlic, minced 3 tablespoonsbutter 2 mediumonions, cut in chunks 2 mediumcarrots, cut in thick slices (or I use whole baby carrots) 4 red potatoes, cut in chunks (whatever you consider a decent bite size) 2 stalkscelery, cut in thick slices 1 quartchicken broth(I use the Campbells 900 ml ready-to-serve carton) 1 quartbeef broth 1(156 ml) cantomato paste 2bay leaves 1 teaspoon maggi seasoning sauce or 1 teaspoon bouquet garni liquid seasoning (no clue what this was and didn&rsquo;t use it) DIRECTIONS
Instructions In a large soup pot, melt butter over medium high heat. Brown the beef chunks in the butter. Add salt, pepper, garlic, broth, vegetables and tomato paste. Bring to a boil. Add bay leaves and Magi. Reduce heat to medium-low (so soup is simmering). Cover and cook for 1 1/2 hours (until potatoes are done but not overcooked.). Here&rsquo;s a link to the actual website. content:
Ingredients 1 beaten egg 1/2 cup Stove Top stuffing mix 1 Tbsp dried onion 1 Tsp chicken bouillion powder 1/4 cup water a squirt ofWorcestershire sauce (optional) 1 lb ground turkey 1 cup Stove Top stuff mix (crushed fine) Instructions In bowl combine egg 1/4 cup water; stir in 1/2 cup stuffing mix, dried onion, a shot of Worcestershire sauce, and bouillon. Let stand for five minutes. Add turkey and mix well; shape into 1/2 inch patties. (This made 5 patties for me) Coat with crushed stuffing mix. (Make sure you crush it well enough or it won&rsquo;t stick!) Cook patties in skillet coated with oil about 10 minutes per side until thoroughly cooked. Billy&rsquo;s Notes:These were fantastic! We&rsquo;re going to try them with some kind of cranberry mayo or something next time. Super good!

content:
Ingredients 1 egg 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper 1 pound ground beef 1/2 cup fine dry bread crumbs Instructions Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat and lightly oil grate. In a medium bowl, whisk together egg, salt and pepper. Place ground beef and bread crumbs into the mixture. With hands or a fork, mix until well blended. Form into 4 patties approximately 3/4 inch thick. Place patties on the prepared grill. Cover and cook 6 to 8 minutes per side, or to desired doneness. Billy&rsquo;s Notes: While I like burgers with a lot of stuff in them, I also like plain jane patties. This did the trick. Just enough extra stuff to make sure they don&rsquo;t fall apart, these ones are pretty good.
Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipes.com:
The Perfect Basic Burger RecipeNothing beats a simple hamburger on a warm summer evening! Ground beef is blended with an easy to prepare bread crumb mixture. Pile these burgers with your favorite condiments, pop open a cool drink and enjoy!

content:
Ingredients 1 Loaf French or ciabatta type bread - sliced in half, horizontally 1/2 Cup butter - softened 1/2 Cup mayonnaise 1/4 Cup cheddar cheese - shredded 1/4 Cup mozzarella cheese - shredded 1 1/2 Tbsp garlic - minced 1/4 Tsp oregano to finish Instructions Preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Mix all of the ingredients together, except for the oregano. Slice a loaf of French bread horizontally and spread 1/2 the mix on the bread. Bake for 10 mins, or until the cheese is toasted and browned. Spread the other 1/2 of the mix on the bread and let it melt in there (don&rsquo;t put it back into the oven!) Sprinkle with oregano to finish. Compliments to The Sassy Foodie for this recipe (although I tweaked it and made it better!):
Keg Copycat Cheesy Garlic ToastIf you have ever had garlic cheese toast from The Keg, you want this keg copycat cheesy garlic toast in your life, NOW! Cheesy, garlicky and delicious!
description: Flavorful, satisfying hearty comfort food with loaf french or ciabatta type bread - sliced in half and butter - softened. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.

content:
Ingredients Crust:
2 cups all purpose flour 1 cup butter, softened 1/2 cup white sugar Filling:
1 1/2 cups white sugar 1/4 cup all-purpose flour 4 eggs 2 lemons, juiced Instructions Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. To make the crust: Blend 2 cups flour, softened butter, and 1/2 cup sugar in a medium bowl until well combined. Press crust into the bottom of an ungreased 9x13-inch pan. Bake crust in the preheated oven until firm and golden, about 15 minutes. Meanwhile, make the filling: Whisk remaining 1 1/2 cups sugar and 1/4 cup flour in a medium bowl. Whisk in eggs, then lemon juice until smooth. Pour filling over the baked crust. Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Set the pan aside to cool completely; the bars will firm up as they cool. When cooled, cut bars into uniform squares. Credit for this recipe goes toAllrecipes.com:
The Best Lemon BarsThese lemon bars are easy to make with fresh lemons and simple ingredients you probably already have for a deliciously rich and tart treat.
description: Irresistible rich squares with all purpose flour and lemons — layer upon layer of sweetness that cut into perfect little treats for sharing (or not sharing).
title: The Best Lemon Bars

content:
Ingredients 1 cupsalted buttersoftened 1 cupgranulated sugar 1 cuplight brown sugarpacked 2 teaspoonspure vanilla extract 2 large eggs 3 cupsall-purpose flour 1 teaspoonbaking soda ½ teaspoonbaking powder 1 teaspoonsea salt 2 cupschocolate chips(14 oz) Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. Line three baking sheets with parchment paper and set aside. In a medium bowl mix flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Set aside. Cream together butter and sugars until combined. Beat in eggs and vanilla until light (about 1 minute). Mix in the dry ingredients until combined. Add chocolate chips and mix well. Roll 2-3 Tablespoons (depending on how large you like your cookies) of dough at a time into balls and place them evenly spaced on your prepared cookie sheets. Bake in preheated oven for approximately 8-10 minutes. Take them out when they are justbarelystarting to turn brown. Let them sit on the baking pan for 5 minutes before removing to cooling rack. Billy&rsquo;s Notes: These are fantastic! Not only are they pretty easy to make (not gummy dough, so easy to roll in balls&hellip;heh, heh, balls), but they are moist and taste kickass. Keep an eye on them in the oven though. I&rsquo;d only done 7 mins and they were perfect.
Credit for this recipe goes to Joy Food Sunshine:
The Best Chocolate Chip Cookie Recipe EverThe best chocolate chip cookie recipe ever! No weird ingredients, no chilling, etc. Just an easy, straightforward, amazingly delicious, doughy yet still fully cooked, chocolate chip cookies!
description: Decadent cookies packed with cupsalted buttersoftened, cupgranulated sugar, and cuplight brown sugarpacked — the kind you bake a double batch of because the first one disappears before they've cooled.
title: The Best Chocolate Chip Cookie Recipe Ever

content:
Ingredients 1 tablespoon olive oil 2 carrots, chopped 1 onion, chopped 1 poblano or bell pepper, chopped 1/2 pound ground beef 2 tablespoons tomato paste 2 15-ounce cans black beans, rinsed 1 tablespoon chili powder kosher salt and black pepper 1/2 cup corn kernels (from 1 ear, or frozen and thawed) 1/2 cup grated Cheddar (2 ounces) 2 scallions, sliced How to Make It Step 1
Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the carrots, onion, and poblano and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes.
Step 2
Add the beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until no longer pink, 3 to 5 minutes.
Step 3
Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring, until it is slightly darkened, 1 minute.
Step 4
Stir in the beans, chili powder, 3 cups water, ½ teaspoon salt, and ¼ teaspoon pepper.
Step 5
Simmer over medium heat until the vegetables are tender, 8 to 10 minutes. Stir in the corn.
Step 6
Divide the chili among bowls and top with the Cheddar and scallions.
Here&rsquo;s a link to the actual website. Credit to Realsimple.com:
Southwestern Beef Chili With CornPacked with flavor but not too spicy, this chili gets its southwestern flare from poblano peppers and chili powder. After cooking a base of onion, carrots, and peppers, you’ll brown the meat and stir in beans, tomato paste, and more. Serve the chili on its own, with hot sauce at the table, or with tortilla chips or a dollop of sour cream. Another plus: Our Southwestern Beef Chili with Corn freezes well. Double or even triple the recipe when you cook, then freeze the extra servings in separate containers to thaw and enjoy later in the season; on a cold, busy night, your future self will thank you.
description: A hearty pot of bold chili with olive oil and carrots that's perfect for game day, cold nights, or any time you need something warm and satisfying.
title: Southwestern Beef Chili with Corn

content:
Ingredients Spicy Chicken
1 cup shredded chicken (canned or rotisserie) 3 ½ tablespoons mayo, more to taste 1 tablespoon Sriracha Sushi
2 sheets seaweed, cut in half 1 ½ cups cooked rice (short grain preferred) Rice seasoning 1 Persian cucumber, sliced (or regular cucumbers) 1 large avocado, sliced NOTE: There are a lot of recipes to make the actual sushi rice, but I&rsquo;ve usedTHIS ONE(made in the Instant Pot). It turns out pretty good.
Instructions Mix together the shredded chicken with mayonnaise and Sriracha. Set the bowl aside for now. Place a half sheet of seaweed on a cutting board. Place about ¼ - ⅓ cup of warm rice on the left side of the half sheet of seaweed. Lightly wet your fingers to prevent the rice from sticking. Spread the rice across the seaweed in an even layer. Sprinkle rice seasoning over the rice. Now, flip the seaweed and rice over to the other side so that rice side is faced down. Line spoonfuls (about 3 tablespoons) of the spicy chicken in a thin layer along the bottom of the seaweed. Add slices of cucumber and avocado to the spicy chicken. Once all the ingredients are placed, take the bottom edge of the seaweed rice and roll the seaweed and ingredients up until you reach the other end. The rice side should be on the outside now. Take the bamboo sushi rolling mat and place it in a Ziploc bag. Place the Ziploc-ed sushi mat over the sushi roll. Gently press against the bamboo sushi mat where the sushi roll is. This helps press the rice back against the sushi and smoothes out any area where the rice might start to fall off. Carefully remove the mat from the sushi roll. Take a sharp knife and cut the roll in half by sawing back and forth. You don’t want to press down into the sushi roll because that will cause the roll to break apart. By sawing back and forth, the roll remains intact. Place the two halves next to each other, and make two to three more slices to create 6-8 pieces total. Continue to make these rolls until all the ingredients are …
description: Crispy-skinned, bold chicken with shredded chicken and ½ tablespoons mayo cooked until golden and irresistible. This is the recipe that makes chicken exciting again.
title: Spicy Chicken Sushi Roll

content:
Outback Steakhouse Potato Soup Ingredients Potatoes (4 large or up to 8 small potatoes) Kosher Salt Vegetable Oil 2 sticks of butter 1 Onion, diced 3/4 cup of all purpose flour 48 oz of chicken broth 2 cups water Celery salt to taste 1/2 tsp ground black pepper 1/4 tsp dried basil 1/4 tsp sugar 1 1/2 cups heavy cream 4-6 slices of cooked bacon, chopped Chopped chives (depends on your preference) 2 cups shredded Monterrey Jack / Cheddar cheese blend. Method Preheat your oven to 350 degrees
Rinse and scrub the skins of the potatoes and dry with a towel and then pierce each potato several times with a fork.
Place a small amount of oil in your hands and coat each potato and then sprinkle each one with Kosher salt.
Place in the oven, directly on the rack (no cookie sheet and NO foil!)
Bake at 350 for approximately 60 minutes
In a large pot, cook your bacon, turning it frequently until just crispy. Leave the grease from the bacon for the next step (yes, it&rsquo;s unhealthy, I know).
In the same pot, melt the butter with the bacon grease, over a low to medium heat, being careful not to burn it.
Add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the flour and stir while it cooks for about 3 minutes, using a rubber spatula to loosen any bits of bacon stuck to the bottom (yum!)
Slowly add the chicken broth, water, salt, pepper, basil and sugar, bringing to a boil and stirring often with a whisk.
Gradually add the heavy cream until you&rsquo;ve reached the proper consistency.
Chop the potatoes into bite sized cubes and add to the soup and simmer for approximately 5 minutes to bring the potatoes to temperature (remember, they&rsquo;re already cooked.. so don&rsquo;t overdo it).
Ladle the soup into individual bowls and garnish with chopped bacon, chives and the cheese blend.
Notes:
Ingredients This is a REALLY good soup. It kind of eats like a meal. I&rsquo;d rate it around 4.5 out of 5. Instructions Here&rsquo;s the actual recipe website. Credit to Chef Pablo&rsquo;s kitchen!
Outback …
description: Steaming bowls of bold soup packed with potatoes, kosher salt, and vegetable oil. The recipe you reach for when the weather turns and comfort is non-negotiable.
title: Potato Soup (Outback Steakhouse)

content:
Ingredients 2 lb mushrooms 6 garlic cloves 1/2 cupwhite vinegar5% acidity 2 cup water 1 tbspsugar 1 tbspsalt 6bay leaves 8 whole peppercorns 6 tbsp vinegar Instructions Clean and wash mushrooms, and put them in a pot. Fill with enough water to cover the &lsquo;shrooms. Add the 1/2 cup of vinegar to the water, bring it to a boil and cook for 10 mins. (The recipe says 15, but I wanted my &lsquo;shrooms with a bit of body to them, so I&rsquo;ll try it with 10 mins next time). At 15 mins, they came out more like the consistency of canned mushrooms. When mushrooms are cooked, drain the liquid. Now, the marinade: In a separate pot, place the 2 cups of water, salt, sugar, bay leaves, and peppercorns. Bring them to a boil. Once the sugar and salt have dissolved, add the 6 tbsp of vinegar and remove from the heat and set aside. Clean and dice the garlic into small pieces. Place the diced garlic on the bottom of the jars. Fill the jars with the cooked mushrooms. Pour marinade over the mushrooms. Cover, using lids and store them in the refrigerator. They can be stored in the fridge up to several weeks. Credit for this recipe goes to Mom&rsquo;s Dish:
Pickled Mushrooms (So Easy)Pickled mushrooms are super effortless to make. From an accompaniment on charcuterie boards to a steak topper, these vinegary shrooms are best kept handy!
description: Homemade satisfying preserves with mushrooms and garlic cloves that capture the season in a jar. Better than anything on the store shelf and made with love.
title: Pickled Mushrooms

content:
First start out by letting the steaks rest at room temperature for 30 minutes (this brings them a little closer to room temp to cook more evenly). Heat vegetable oil in a 12-inch cast iron skillet over medium-high heat (you want to let it get hot, preheating should take about 3 minutes if using a gas stove then more for electric.). I like to turn on vent fan above stove. Dab both sides of steak dry with paper towels then season both sides with salt and pepper (some people might recommend to wait until the end on the pepper because it can burn but I’ve never tasted burnt pepper). Place steaks in skillet and using metal tongs press down across top surface of steaks to ensure the entire bottom surface is making direct contact with skillet. Let cook until browned on bottom, about 3 minutes. Flip and continue to cook until steak is about 10 degrees away from desired doneness (see notes below for temps) approximately 3 minutes longer. Ingredients Reduce heat to medium-low, add garlic butter on top of steak in pats. I used the recipe found here -Garlic Butter Recipe. Using an oven mitt grasp pan and tilt pan so butter pools to one side, spoon butter over steaks and continue to cook until steaks registers temperature of desired doneness, about 1 minute longer. Transfer to a plates, let rest 5 minutes before slicing. Credit for this method goes to Cooking Classy:
How to Cook Steak {Pan Seared with Garlic Butter} - Cooking ClassyAn easy, straight forward method that uses basic ingredients to cook an excellent steak! A recipe everyone in the whole family will love.
description: Rich, savory satisfying beef with first start out by letting the steaks rest at room temperature for 30 minutes . and heat vegetable oil in a 12-inch cast iron skillet over medium-high heat . i like to turn on vent fan above stove. — fork-tender and packed with deep, beefy flavor. Comfort food at its finest.
title: Pan Seared Steak with Garlic Butter

content:
Ingredients 1 pound of spaghetti (I just guesstimated and used my own fettucini noodles)
2 tbsp of salt
1/4 cup + 2 tbps of extra-virgin olive oil
8 garlic cloves, minced
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes (less if you want it a bit less spicy - 1/4 tsp was good for the girls)
2 tsp of lemon juice
2 tsp of parsley
1/2 tsp of salt (or more to taste)
grated parmesan (to taste)
Instructions 1)Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add in 2 tablespoons salt to the water and cook the pasta according to package directions.
2)In a small saucepan, pour in 1/4 cup olive oil and heat over low heat for 60 seconds. Add in garlic and red pepper flakes. Stir and cook 60 seconds. Remove from heat.
3)Pour olive oil mixture over the cooked and drained pasta. Add in additional 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Pour in lemon juice and parsley and toss until pasta is coated.
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: This was good! Definitely want to use the parmesan and go a little heavier on the salt once you taste test it. Was a little bland to start, but salt tuned it up.

Olive Oil PastaThis Super Easy Olive Oil Pasta is a simple side dish that is quick to make and easily customizable to become a full meal. Just add meat and veggies!

content:
Montreal Style Bagels Dough
1 cup (227g) lukewarm water 2 teaspoons maple syrup 2 teaspoons instant yeast or active dry yeast 3 1/4 to 3 1/2 cups (390g to 418g) King Arthur Unbleached Bread Flour 1 teaspoon salt Topping
3/4 cup (106g) sesame or poppy seeds Boiling Liquid
3 quarts (2722g) water 1/3 cup (113g) honey or 1/4 cup (85g) barley malt syrup For the dough: Combine all of the ingredients, using the lesser amount of flour, and mix until a firm dough forms. Use the remaining 1/4 cup flour as needed to knead the dough by hand for 10 to 12 minutes, or at medium speed in your mixer with the dough hook for 8 minutes. The dough should be smooth and elastic and not stick to your fingers. When poked with your finger, it should bounce back. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 1/2 hours, until almost doubled in bulk. Line a baking sheet with parchment. Place the sesame (or poppy) seeds in a shallow bowl. After the first rise, deflate the dough and divide into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a 6&quot; rope. Wrap the rope around your fingers and overlap the ends. Roll your palm over the seam to seal. Place them on the baking sheet, cover, and let rise while you set up the boiling liquid and preheat your oven with a baking stone in it to 475°F. To boil: Fill a large saucepan with 3&quot; of water. Add the honey (or barley malt syrup). Place over medium-high heat and bring the water to a boil. The bagels are ready to cook when they&rsquo;ve risen very slightly (the surface should still bounce back when lightly touched). Reduce the water to a simmer, then slip two or three bagels into it. Cook on one side for a minute, then flip over and cook for another minute. Remove with a slotted spoon and dredge in the seeds. Flip the baking sheet over and put the parchment on the back; place the boiled bagels on the parchment. Once all of the bagels are boiled, slide the parchment with the bagels on it onto the baking stone and bake for 18 to 23 minutes, …
description: Flavorful, satisfying comforting comfort food with lukewarm water and maple syrup. The kind of recipe that gets dog-eared in the cookbook and passed down through generations.
title: Montreal Bagels

Ingredients 2cups chopped cooked chicken breasts 2hard-boiled eggs, sliced 1⁄2cup thinly slicedcarrot 1⁄2cupfrozen green pea 1(10 3/4 ounce) can cream of chicken soup 1cupchicken broth salt and pepper (optional) 11⁄2cups instantbiscuit mix 1cupmilk 1⁄2cup meltedbutter Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. In a greased 2-quart casserole, layer the chicken, eggs, carrots, and peas. Mix the soup, chicken broth, and season with salt and pepper, if desired; pour over the layers. Stir together the biscuit mix and milk, and pour this over the casserole. Drizzle butter over the topping. Bake until the topping is golden brown, 30 to 40 minutes. Hurry up Chicken Pot Pie (Paula Deen) Recipe - Food.comThis is a quick chicken pot pie recipe from Paula Deen. It is so easy to make, and you could modify this recipe in so many ways.

content:
Maya&rsquo;s Cookie Brownie Recipe (Blondies) Ingredients ½ cup unsalted butter 2 teaspoons vanilla ¼ teaspoon to ½ salt ½ cup packed brown sugar ½ cup granulated sugar 1 large egg 1 cup all-purpose flour ½ cup of chocolate chips Instructions Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350F degrees. Line an 8x8 inch square baking pan with aluminium foil leaving an overhang on each side and spray lightly with non-stick cooking spray. Or line the pan with parchment paper. Set aside.
In a medium sized sauce pan on medium heat, melt the butter. After the butter has melted, begin whisking it gently as you allow it to crack and pop while bubbling slightly. The butter will begin to turn brown in color as milk solids form, and you will smell a nutty aroma. Once the butter has begun to brown, remove from the heat. Be careful not to let the butter brown too long or it will burn.
Let the butter cool for 5-10 minutes. Stir in the vanilla &amp; salt. Then add in the sugars followed by the egg while mixing with a large wooden spatula or wooden spoon. Make sure the butter has cooled so that the egg doesn&rsquo;t scramble. Then fold in the flour with your wooden spoon/rubber spatula, and once combined stir in the chocolate chips.
4.Pour the batter into your prepared pan and bake for about 18-22 minutes. The top should look set and the edges should just be starting to pull away at the sides. Allow the blondies to cool for at least 15 minutes before cutting in.
description: Perfectly sweet cookies studded with ½ cup unsalted butter, ¼ teaspoon to ½ salt, and ½ cup packed brown sugar. Simple enough for a Tuesday, special enough for a holiday platter.
title: Maya's Cookie Brownie Recipe

content:
This is a mish-mash of a few different recipes for this I&rsquo;ve seen on the web, along with a few different techniques. No single one had all the ingredients and info in the same spot, so I figured I&rsquo;d just write it up myself. If it sucks, I&rsquo;m sorry. :(
Ingredients 2 cups of flour (I just use regular flour, but the internet nerds say to use semolina and some other weird stuff. Keep it simple!) 2 eggs 1/2 tsp olive oil 1/4 tsp salt 1/2 cup of water (just keep it handy - DO NOT just dump it in!! See the recipe below for the deets) NOTE: This is a doubled-up recipes that makes around 4 good portions with enough left over for lunch the next day.
In a big mixing bowl, add the flour and salt and make kind of a &ldquo;well&rdquo; in the middle of it. Just a little dent that&rsquo;ll fit 2 eggs in it. Crack the 2 eggs and the olive oil into the well. (A lot of recipes say to do this right on the counter, which I get to, but this first step is a bit gummy so it&rsquo;s better to make the initial mess in a bowl than your counter)
With a fork, start working the edges of the well so the egg and flour come together. It&rsquo;s going to seem like it&rsquo;s not coming together at all, but keep at it and it&rsquo;ll start kind of coming together. It won&rsquo;t all come together and it&rsquo;ll seem like a big crusty mess for a bit. Just add a splash of water (NOT THE WHOLE THING) here and there to have it come together better. I even it&rsquo;s better to just wet my hands with the water, rather than just splashing it in. This is where a bit of technique comes in. Gotta play with it a bit.
When it&rsquo;s MOSTLY together (and it won&rsquo;t all be beautiful at this point), dump the contents of your bowl onto the counter. Wipe the counter first, of course. Don&rsquo;t do like me and have half of Maddy&rsquo;s Nutella toast stuck in your noodles.
This is where a bit more technique comes in. It&rsquo;s still going to be a bit of a mess on your counter, but the goal is …
description: Tender pasta tossed with of flour, olive oil, and of water in a bold sauce that coats every strand. This is the dinner everyone asks for twice in one week.
title: Homemade Pasta/Noodles

content: Ingredients 4 boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces 1 tablespoon olive oil 1 large onion, chopped 2 cloves garlic, minced 1 red bell pepper, chopped 1 zucchini, chopped 1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatoes, undrained 1/2 cup chicken broth 1 tablespoon tomato paste 1 teaspoon dried Italian seasoning 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley, for garnish Instructions Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken:Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper. Add them to the hot skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside. Sauté the vegetables:Add the chopped onion and red bell pepper to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the chopped zucchini and cook for an additional 3-5 minutes, until slightly tender. Build the sauce:Stir in the diced tomatoes (with their juice), chicken broth, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, and smoked paprika. Bring the sauce to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Return the chicken:Add the cooked chicken pieces back into the skillet with the vegetable and tomato sauce. Stir to combine and heat through for a couple of minutes. Serve:Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
description: Juicy, flavorful savory chicken with boneless and olive oil — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Hearty Chicken and Vegetable Skillet

content:
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: I figured I&rsquo;d mention, before this recipe, that I didn&rsquo;t make it like the recipe states and it turned out really good. I&rsquo;m sure the recipe is good, but I wanted garlic butter that I could put in the fridge and use whenever (not a sauce). I mixed all the ingredients together as there recipe states, but then formed it into a log in parchment paper and put it in the fridge to harden. It freezes well and then you can just cut off chunks as you need them
Ingredients 1/2 cup Unsalted Butter 4 cloves garlic - minced 2 tsp garlic powder 1/2 tsp salt 1 tbsp fresh lemon juice - plus more to taste 1-2 tbsp fresh parsley - minced (optional) Instructions Melt 1-2 tablespoons of butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the garlic and saute for approximately 30-60 seconds, stirring constantly. Do not let the garlic burn, reduce the heat to low if needed. Add the remaining butter, garlic powder, and salt to the pan. Stir well to combine. Once the butter has melted, add the lemon juice and continue to cook for an additional 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Remove the butter from the heat and stir in the minced parsley, if using. Best served hot.
Billy&rsquo;s Notes: Once again, that&rsquo;s not what I did. :)

Ingredients 3 large ripe bananas 3/4 cup of sugar 1 egg, slightly beaten 1/3 cup of melted butter 1 cup of blueberries 1 tsp of baking soda 1 tsp of baking powder 1/2 tsp of salt 1 1/2 cups of flour Instructions Mash bananas in a large mixing bowl. Add sugar and egg. Add butter and blueberries. Combine dry ingredients and gently stir into banana mixture. Pour into 12 well-greased muffin cups. Bake at 375 degrees for 20 minutes. content: Ingredients 1 1/2 cups warm water (about 100 degrees F) 1 packet yeast (active dry, instant, or quick rise- 2 1/4 teaspoons) 1 ½ tsp salt 3 1/4 cups all-purpose flour Instructions In a large bowl(or container), mix together the warm water(1 1/2 cups), yeast(1 packet/2 1/4 teaspoons), and table salt(1 1/2 teaspoon), until yeast and salt are fully or almost dissolved(I like using a whisk for this). Add the flour(3 1/4 cups)to the bowl all at once and stir together until a sticky dough forms. Don&rsquo;t worry about mixing it too much, just make sure everything is uniformly wet. It WILL be messy and sticky. You can scrape what&rsquo;s left on the spoon with a silicone spatula. Cover the bowl with a kitchen towel(or place the container to the lid on ajar).Leave it for 2-3 hours to rise in a somewhat warm place until it&rsquo;s doubled in volume. 30 minutes to an hour before you are ready to bake, preheat your oven to 450 degrees F. Place your Dutch oven in the oven, with the lid ON, while the oven is preheating. Once the oven reaches 450, keep it preheating for another 20 minutes, so the Dutch oven gets good and hot. Scrape the dough into the edge of a piece of parchment paper dusted with flour. Make it into as much of a &ldquo;loaf&rdquo; shape as you can by folding the edges up on top of it(a silicone spatula works well for this). Then, use the edges of the parchment paper to flip the loaf over so the floured side is on top and the loaf is in the center of the parchment paper. Don&rsquo;t worry about it looking beautiful or smooth on top. Also doesn&rsquo;t matter if your dutch oven is round or oval. Use a very sharp knife or clean razor blade to cut a few slits across the top of the loaf about 1/2&quot; deep, across the entire top. I did this in a cross shape for mine. Take out the preheated Dutch oven and take off the lid, carefully! Grab the parchment from the sides and place the loaf in your Dutch oven. Place the cover back on the Dutch ovenand place in the …
description: Soft, golden toasty rolls with warm water and packet yeast that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: Dutch Oven No-Knead Bread

Ingredients 3 or 4 lb. venison roast 6 beef bouillon cubes 1 onion, diced 1/2 tsp. garlic powder 5-6 carrots, thick sliced 5-6 med. potatoes, wedged Instructions Wash meat, trim fat. Place in crock pot with bouillon cubes, cover with water (about 2 quarts). Add onion and garlic powder. Cook on High overnight about 8 hours. In the A.M. add carrots, potatoes and continue to cook on High 4 to 5 hours until potatoes are tender. Serve as is or may be thickened with cornstarch.

Ingredients 2 large cucumbers, peeled and thinly sliced 1 sweet onion, thinly sliced 1 tablespoon sea salt Dressing
1 ½ cups mayonnaise, or more to taste 2 tablespoons vinegar 1 tablespoon white sugar 1 teaspoon dried dill weed 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1 teaspoon ground black pepper Instructions Mix cucumbers, onion, and salt together in a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit for 15 to 30 minutes. Turn cucumber mixture into a colander set over a bowl or in a sink. Let drain, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid and salt has drained, 15 to 30 minutes. Transfer drained cucumber mixture to a large bowl. Whisk mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, dill, garlic powder, and pepper for dressing together in a bowl until smooth. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours before serving. Billy&rsquo;s Notes: Found this one on the internet and it was really good. I know I&rsquo;d tried a few of these but this was easily the best one I&rsquo;ve tried.

Dad&rsquo;s Creamy Cucumber SaladThis creamy cucumber salad pairs crisp cucumber slices with a creamy mayo and vinegar dressing. It&rsquo;s sure to be a family-favorite this summer!

title: Dad's Creamy Cucumber Salad

content:
Ingredients Dozen chicken wings 1 tbsp baking powder ½ tsp salt 2 tsps garlic powder Pinch of pepper Instructions Adjust oven rack to upper-middle position and preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminium foil and set a heat-proof wire rack inside.
Pat dry chicken wings liberally with paper towels, squeezing out as much moisture as you can. Transfer them to a large bowl.
In a small bowl, combine the baking powder, garlic powder, salt and pepper together, whisking well to combine, and sprinkle the mixture over the wings. Toss wings through the baking powder mixture until evenly coated.
Arrange on rack, leaving about 1-inch of space between each wing.
Bake for 30 minutes; flip and continue to cook until crisp and golden brown, (about 15 minutes longer), until golden browned and crispy.
Credit for this recipe goes to Cafe Delites:
Crispy Buffalo Chicken Wings (BAKED)Crispy Buffalo Chicken Wings are exactly what they claim to be. CRISPY without deep frying, using one special ingredient you have in your kitchen pantry!
description: Juicy, flavorful robust chicken with dozen chicken wings and ½ tsp salt — golden on the outside, tender all the way through. The kind of dinner that earns a round of applause.
title: Crispy Chicken Wings

content:
Ingredients 6 large russet potatoes, rinsed 1/4 cup olive oil 1 tablespoon minced garlic, (or 3 teaspoons garlic powder) 1/2 teaspoon onion powder 2 teaspoons salt, (adjust to your tastes) 1 teaspoon paprika 1/2 teaspoon black cracked pepper 2/3 cup finely grated or shredded parmesan cheese, divided 2 tablespoons fresh chopped parsley Instructions Preheat oven to 390°F (200°C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper; set aside. Cut each potato in half lengthways, then cut each half in half lengthways again, and then cut each half in half lengthways again until you have 8 wedges. (Make sure they are about the same thickness and size). In a small jug, combine the oil, garlic, onion powder, salt, paprika annd pepper. Pour the oil mixture over the potatoes and toss together to coat evenly. Arrange potato wedges in a single layer on a large baking tray, skin-sides-down. Sprinkle with half of the parmesan cheese and extra salt if needed. Bake for 35 minutes, turning wedges half way through baking time, until golden, crisp and cooked through when tested with a fork. Sprinkle with parsley and remaining parmesan cheese to serve. Billy&rsquo;s Notes: These were great! Some of the best home-made potato things like this I think I&rsquo;ve tried.
Credit for this recipe goes to Cafe Delites:
Crispy Garlic Baked Potato Wedges - Cafe DelitesCrispy Garlic Baked Potato Wedges are soft pillows on the inside, and crunchy on the outside with a good kick of garlic and parmesan cheese!
description: Hearty bold casserole packed with large russet potatoes, olive oil, and minced garlic — the ultimate one-dish dinner. Pop it in the oven and let it do all the work.
title: Crispy Garlic Baked Potato Wedges

Scrape out the seeds if desired and slice into ¼″ slices. Combine all dressing ingredients and toss with cucumbers. Refrigerate 1 hour before serving. Also known as custard or English creme, this stuff is really good when you dip fruit it in it or put it on a waffle.
Ingredients 1 small box of instant Jell-O Vanilla Instant Pudding 1 cup of 35% cream 2 cups of milk Instructions Add the Instant Pudding mix and the milk together in a medium-sized bowl. Mix together until thick.
In another bowl, thoroughly beat 1 cup of 35% cream. Add to the bowl of pudding and
mix well. Transfer to a container, cover and set in the fridge to chill.
Enjoy with your favourite dessert or on a crêpe filled with banana slices or fresh strawberries.

Ingredients 1 1/4 cups butter, softened 2 cups white sugar 2 eggs 2 teaspoons vanilla extract 2 cups all-purpose flour 3/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder 1 teaspoon baking soda 1/2 teaspoon salt 2 cups semisweet chocolate chips Directions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla. Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt; stir into the creamed mixture. Mix in the chocolate chips. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets. Bake 8 to 9 minutes in the preheated oven. Cookies will be soft. Cool slightly on cookie sheet; remove from sheet onto wire rack to cool completely. Here is a link to the actual website. content:
Buffalo Wild Wing has to be one of the best chicken wing joints going. Here are a handful of their cooking secrets and sauce recipes.
How to fry your wings:
Heat oil or shortening in your fryer to 350°F. Fry wings for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove wings and dry on a rack or paper towels briefly. Place in plastic container with a sealable lid. Add 1/4 to 1/3 cup of your preferred sauce (see below) to the container. Close lid and shake well to coat wings. Buffalo Wild Wing Sauce Recipes:
NOTE: For ALL of these sauce recipes, do the following:
Combine all ingredients in a blender. Simmer in a saucepan for 20-30 minutes. Honey BBQ:
1 cup ketchup 1/3 cup white vinegar 1/4 cup molasses 1/4 cup honey 1 tsp liquid smoke 1/2 tsp salt 1/2 tsp ground black pepper 1/4 tsp paprika 1/4 tsp chili powder 1/4 tsp onion powder 1/4 tsp garlic powder 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper Parmesan Garlic:
1/2 cup butter, melted 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1/2 teaspoon onion salt 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground 1/2 cup parmesan cheese, grated Spicy Garlic:

6 fresh habanero peppers (with seeds) 5 lemon drop peppers (or other small hot pepper) 8 jalapenos (with seeds) 2-3 garlic cloves 2 cups white vinegar 1/4 teaspoon ground oregano 1/4 teaspoon ground cumin 1/2 teaspoon onion …

content:
Ingredients Ingredients Juice of 1/2 lemon 1 medium avocado, peeled, pitted, and sliced into 1/4-inch thick pieces 4 sheets nori (dried seaweed paper) Sushi rice - Recipe follows (or use this great Instant Pot recipe I use) 1/3 cup sesame seeds, toasted 1 small cucumber, peeled, seeded, and cut into matchstick-size pieces 4 crabsticks, torn into pieces Pickled ginger, for serving Wasabi, for serving Soy sauce, for serving Instructions Directions Squeeze the lemon juice over the avocado to prevent browning.
Cover a bamboo rolling mat with plastic wrap. Cut nori sheets in half crosswise. Lay 1 sheet of nori, shiny side down, on the plastic covered mat. Wet your fingers with water and spread about 1/2 cup of the rice evenly onto the nori. Sprinkle the rice with sesame seeds. Turn the sheet of nori over so that the rice side is down. Place 1/8 of the cucumber, avocado and crab sticks in the center of the sheet. Grab the edge of the mat closest to you, keeping the fillings in place with your fingers, and roll it into a tight cylinder, using the mat to shape the cylinder. Pull away the mat and set aside. Cover with a damp cloth. Repeat until all of the rice has been used. Cut each roll into 6 pieces. Serve with pickled ginger, wasabi and soy sauce.
Sushi Rice:
Place the rice into a mixing bowl and cover with cool water. Swirl the rice in the water, pour off and repeat 2 to 3 times or until the water is clear.
Place the rice and 2 cups of water into a medium saucepan and place over high heat. Bring to a boil, uncovered. Once it begins to boil, reduce the heat to the lowest setting and cover. Cook for 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and let stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
Combine the rice vinegar, sugar and salt in a small bowl and heat in the microwave on high for 30 to 45 seconds. Transfer the rice into a large wooden or glass mixing bowl and add the vinegar mixture. Fold thoroughly to combine and coat each grain of rice with the mixture. Allow to cool to room …
description: Soft, golden buttery rolls with juice of 1/2 lemon and medium avocado that pull apart like a dream. Perfect alongside any meal or slathered with butter straight from the oven.
title: California Rolls (Sushi)

content:
Ingredients 6 slices day-old bread 2 tablespoons butter, melted 1/2 cup raisins (optional) 4 eggs, beaten 2 cups milk 3/4 cup white sugar 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon 1 teaspoon vanilla extract Billy&rsquo;s Note:I didn&rsquo;t have raisins once, so I subbed in 2 apples cut up in little pieces. I drizzled caramel sauce on it to make a caramel apple bread pudding. Worked great!
Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Break bread into small pieces into an 8 inch square baking pan. Drizzle melted butter or margarine over bread. If desired, sprinkle with raisins. In a medium mixing bowl, combine eggs, milk, sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla. Beat until well mixed. Pour over bread, and lightly push down with a fork until bread is covered and soaking up the egg mixture. Billy&rsquo;s Note:I let it soak up all that eggy milkness for around 15 mins here before putting it in the oven. Bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top springs back when lightly tapped. Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipes.com. Here is the actual link to their page:
Bread Pudding II RecipeThis lightly spiced bread pudding is made with simple pantry items like bread, eggs, milk, and sugar. Use a rich egg bread or a moist white loaf.
